be a very long time before you see him in another professional game cheese junglers are perhaps the most fun junglers to play in the game whatever chi strat you're running if you can get it off it feels well like you're cheating at the game there are several very fun cheese junglers in league of legends fiddlesticks nunu and shaco are among the most popular but cheese strategies just tend not to work for professional players and this is due to the fact that professional players actually go through every single one of their replays while it might not be the professional players themselves doing each and every one pro teams have analysts that go through all of the vods that they play and if you're playing a champion with a chi strategy that analyst will simply look at what your route is and then tell the pro players next game exactly what you're gonna do chi strategies don't last very long in professional league of legends and they only ever tend to work for a single game as discussed before pros like consistency they don't really want to play games where their jungler could be completely useless just because they made one mistake when it comes to champions like nunu and shaco they do the same thing they do level 2 cheese ganks in order to get a lead for a certain lane one of the reasons that junglers are considered slaves to their teams is because the jungle roll overall is weaker in a fives comp when you're in a team of five you can immediately communicate with each other pro players are very familiar with each and every champion's ideal jungle pathing they also make sure to get good level 1 invades in order to get vision on which buff the enemy jungler will be starting they figure out exactly which camps the jungler has taken and they can make a very good educated guess as to which side of the map the jungler is going to be on when it comes to ganks there's not many surprises after cheese ganking one lane on nunu or shaco the other members know exactly where you are they can very easily figure out what lane you're going to cheese gank next what's worse about these types of junglers is that even when you get extremely far ahead it doesn't actually really do anything in this game shaco was up 5 kills on the enemy team he was by far the most fed member but in a straight up team fight he doesn't contribute anything other than damage in solo queue players get tilted players get upset if you're gonna get ganked five times and die five times to an enemy shaco when your jungler hasn't touched your lane a single time you might tilt and go afk but pro players aren't gonna go afk on stage they understand i'm playing the weak side of the map they understand that even if they're down two kills three kills or 50 cs the other side of the map is prospering meanwhile my jungler is farming and when it comes to late game team fights he will carry me later on make no mistake pro players do tilt they get upset they get irrational and they play worse because of these feelings but they're pros this is their profession they're still gonna show up to work even if they're upset so what are the qualities that make a mid laner viable for pro play a good mid laner should be like a good pan gender person you have to be able to identify as more than one role take azir for example azir identifies as a burst mage combo mage long range mage control mage and dps mage in pro play you're going to be going up against a lot of different people and you don't have a lot of choice in who you get to [ __ ] lane you can't be picky you have to be able to [ __ ] everyone or you're going to get [ __ ] yourself azir's early game might be a little bit weak but as time goes on the team will play with him and around him and he'll be able to adapt and fit into whatever gender role his team needs him to be if they need a manly engage for their combo he can go in with the sharima shuffle if they need him to be a huge [ __ ] then he can play his max range and just sit back and hit stuff whichever role you need azir for he is able to do it that cannot be said for most other mid laners velkas xerath lux ziggs assassin's mid have to get ahead otherwise they start falling off as the game progresses short-range burst mages need flash to be able to engage whatever the case may be specialties are generally not very valuable in pro draft if your champion is specifically good at one thing then there's likely another champion that's specifically good at shutting you down champions like azir syndra and orianna are all very safe picks but this isn't the only reason that these champions are so valuable the biggest reason that a lot of artillery mages are generally avoided in pro play is because artillery mages have a lot of skill shots and you might be thinking wait pro players are good at hitting skill shots so shouldn't they be playing them more yes pro players are generally better at hitting skill shots but they're also better at dodging them as well azir orianna and syndra all have sort of aoe abilities they are skill shots in a sense but they're not really skill shots when it comes to azir you can't really just sidestep all of his damage you can dodge it with things that are similar to flash but the thing is is that you have to burn flash if you're good enough artillery mages will never be able to hit a single skill shot on you pro players don't want their main source of magic damage to be side-steppable this is why when you pick an artillery mage you have to draft around that mage all of these guys need some sort of setup from the rest of their team in the form of crowd control in order to be able to get the maximum amount of damage off champion reliance on single skill shots is also another big reason as to why other champions are not played in the bottom lane as well if mage supports are played in the bot lane it's generally going to be champions like annie or zyra they have a lot of aoe and a lot of their damage isn't avoidable champions like pike and blitzcrank suffer from the same problem if your entire champion is based off of the hope that you're able to hit a single straight line skill shot well pro players are not going to let you be able to get away with that you have to work so much harder in order for your champion to be able to be strong alistar is picked in one in every three professional games one of the biggest reasons that is is because his wq combo isn't something that you can just sidestep the more skillful your opponents the less consistent skill shots become in other words if you're going to play something like blitzcrank in a professional game then you have to be a god at hitting gods with one of the most telegraphed abilities in the entire game faker versus ryu's z1v1 is probably the most well-known outplay in league of legends history assassins are fun to watch fun to play and extremely unfun to play against this is the main reason as to why riot usually doesn't make assassins broken enough to be viable in professional play assassins unfortunately have all the weaknesses that we have discussed so far in the video consistency need for resources and inability to fit into a lot of different roles this outplay also took place in season 3 when plating wasn't a thing before if you left your lane for a roam you would only be giving up one wave for a kill now you could potentially be giving up two plates and a wave which makes the goal difference pretty much even in theory assassins should never be viable in pro play unless they're so broken that they can one shot someone with their basic combo yet the most played champion in professional league of legends is an assassin and that champion is leblanc let's take a look at what leblanc has that other assassins don't at first glance leblanc has something very different about her she has 525 range she is a ranged assassin leblanc is the only ranged assassin in the mid lane every other assassin in the game is a melee ranged champion this is a huge difference for her it means she doesn't get bullied in the early stage by mages if you trade with leblanc at level 1 she will trade back with you if you want to make any champion in the game instantly more broken just add 200 or 300 range on their auto attacks leblanc's kit used to have waveclear in it as well her q would proc on most of the minions in lane and you could then detonate them in order to clear the way faster this meant that leblanc could take away the mage's only real advantage against an assassin in this matchup which was waveclear if you want to roam as an assassin you're supposed to shove your wave in first most assassins don't have safe wave clear where they won't be taking harass from a ranged mage these days mid lane is just about who's managing the wave better and who's going to roam first to a fight if your champion doesn't have waveclear then you can never really choose when you can go back to base when you want to roam or when your jungler can go for a crab pro games usually focus around farming more than fighting this means that carries will have more gold to spend which means they'll get their core items faster so that they'll be able to get safety items afterwards to be able to shut assassins down if you're a champion like talon for instance who's got a lot of damage packed into their ultimate and you only blow up a zhonyas or ga during a fight that's not good enough to win the team fight leblanc's ultimate on the other hand is only 30 seconds at level 16. she has the very real possibility of using her full combo more than once during a team fight and of course on top of all of that most of leblanc's abilities are not dodgeable by sidestepping the main reason that faker was able to outplay ryu was because he dodged ryu's shurikens you can't dodge the first two cues of leblanc's combo and you can't really dodge her w either assassins thrive off of low vision isolated targets and uncoordinated team fighting all of which don't really happen as much in professional play even though most viewers including myself would love to see assassins return to pro play if riot ever intends to do that then assassins would be absolutely disgusting in solo queue some champions don't fit into any of the categories that we've talked about so far there are a handful of champions that are viable but just not the best meta picks in pro play when they do get picked up it's not because players always think their champion is the strongest it's not because they saw it in another region and it's definitely not because their team requested that they play it these unique pocket picks come out because the player simply loves the champion a lot of people seem to forget this but professional players are human too they don't necessarily want to play their 10 000th game on azir even if it's the most safe pick they want to try something different they want to show the world their own personality through their own unique play style my favorite instance of a great pro pocket pick was clg's huhi on aurelion soul no one plays this champion not in solo queue or in pro play but who he brought it out against the coup tigers in the 2015 world championship coup tigers ended up second place overall in the tournament losing to skt and clg was quickly written off because they lost the game against one of the wild card regions but during this game who he absolutely went off and this disgusting performance decimated coup tiger so hard that aurelian soul was permanently banned against clg for the remainder of their games at worlds so at the end of the day what really decides what comes out in the draft is how much the player enjoys playing that champion thanks for watching big thanks to fly diamond and liquid coach hacks defender for taking the time to answer all of my questions about professional drafts you can find their twitter's in the description below if you guys want more updates on my videos you can hit the bell button to get notifications on when i'm posting community polls to my audience you can follow me on discord and twitter as well for more updates like share and subscribe and that's it bye

titre: Which Champions Are Stronger - Early Or Late Game? | League of Legends
transcription: lee sin a fighter with a story history for his versatility skill expression mobility skirmishing and early game pressure regardless of the meta how strong or how weak he is his fervently loyal fan base will insta-lock him no matter what and his theoretical consistency is what cemented him as one of league's most iconic champions to this day vayne a marksman known for her simple yet ruthlessly effective playstyle with virtually unparalleled damage of time in single combat she's able to tear through anyone standing in her way no matter their durability her incredible combat mobility and outplay potential allow her to remain a constant threat growing even stronger as time goes on both characters have been a mainstay throughout the game's timeline ever since their inception in completely different ways though while they ultimately converge on the same end result what is the main difference between these two the former is at his best in the early stages of the game while the latter is at her best in the mid to late stages of the game champions are heavily defined by how well their pressure maintains with every passing minute in a game as a result two crashing philosophies have been at odds with one another for as long as i can remember and while one group has been mutually agreed on as the superior one for quite some time the recent durability changes in 12.10 have surfaced the age-old question once more so for today i want to share my own thoughts on the notion of scaling what stage of the game is more important early game or late game and by extension which group of champions is stronger over the other but first a word from our sponsor nordpass as i'm sure you're well aware by now cyber security is a must-have in this day and age is data compromisation is happening more and more often fortunately nordpass is a password manager created by the same team that made nordvpn what nordpass does is basically generate encrypted passwords using cutting-edge encryption algorithms and remembers them for you so you don't have to memorize whatever gibberish is made for each one it can store account passwords or even things like credit card information syncing them across all devices platforms and browsers for better accessibility while browsers offer the feature to automatically store your autofill information it may not be as protected against data breaches as you might think on the subject of data breaches with nordpas premium one of its best features is a data breach scanner letting you know which passwords for which platforms are compromised and giving you the option to make a brand new one very useful to have on deck all in all nordpass is a simple password manager that can give you a lot of peace of mind and not getting hacked so if you're interested head on over to nordpass.com vars or use the code vars for a two-year premium plan and one month additional for free if you decide to change your mind for whatever reason they do have a 30-day money-back guarantee thanks again to nordpast for sponsoring the video but for now let's get back into it in the past i've gone over scaling several times as one of the most influential elements in the game it plays a great deal in establishing every champion's identity typically when you're evaluating the strengths and weaknesses of someone something along the lines of they fall off late game or they have a weak laning phase is thrown around and for good reason it's important to know when a champion is at their best and worst so you know when to be aggressive and when to be cautious scaling in league is defined as one's arbitrary progression of value usually in comparison to others normally every champion goes stronger over time with every level of every new item purchased every objective secured their various parameters increase but the rate at which you grow stronger as the game transitions from one phase to another differs from champion to champion thus adding dynamically fluctuating results in every game there's no universal constant that determines this it can originate from an endless number of factors a champion can have a very strong early game by virtue of high base damage like rek'sai or strong neutral like jace they can have a strong early game through a very dangerous saw than like olaf or short burst trades like pantheon the same holds true for the other end of the spectrum a champion's late game efficiency can be a result of a lot of things they can excel in pressuring enemies from a distance like xerath staggering damage output like vein smothering zone control and pressure like azir or increased utility and protection like sonar regardless of the method it's commonplace for champions with a strong early game to gradually diminish in power the longer the game lasts and vice versa leaking champions start out noticeably weaker in relative pressure to account for this steeper climb upwards try to imagine it like a battery versus a solar panel batteries start with full energy capacity that slowly drains over time before running out solar panels begin a game empty but amass power over time of course a handful of champions are known for being relevant for the entirety of a match with a strong early mid and late game albeit not to the same extent as one dedicated in either field as mentioned before an essential part of a champion's identity is how well they scale it plays a huge role in deciding who you're going to play in every game if you see a team consisting of aatrox top lease in jungle draven bottom pike support you're staring down a very early and mid-game-centric lineup as the mid laner are you doubling down on hitting the enemy team hard and fast by picking a strong early game champion or do you believe it wise to have an insurance policy in the event things don't go as planned and select a late game scaler at first it might sound like an easy answer for many of you for the past 4 to 5 years the average game length has been steadily decreasing and prior to version 12.10 we were more or less at a point where once 30 minute games are now 25 minutes occasionally less that would give the impression that legume champs are losing equity in light of that late game not being an assured outcome anymore and while there is a grain of truth to that there are way too many aspects to the contrary that make that claim not as substantiated as one would expect the interesting thing about this debate is that even though lee gets almost two dozen balance patches every year and champions are frequently rotated in and out of the meta the arguments in favor of either side remain mostly the same and that's after you factor in how much the game has changed in recent years with the advent of elemental drakes dragon souls elder dragon tower plates objective bodies menta gardens runes reforge etc it's a very delicate balance that riot has been and will continue trying to stabilize until the end of time this brings us to the topic of the video which is more important in league of legends early game or late game scaling is it better for a player and their team to focus on one or the other or should there be an equal bounce moreover how does the champion scaling influence their viability in the long term figuring that part out demands a few questions answered first what even is early mid and late game everyone has no definition for it but for me i like to section them off based on a specific event rather than a set time frame so the early game is whatever happens prior to tower plates falling off at 14 minutes because that's when objective bodies begin as well around this point most champions assuming they're not egregiously ahead or behind are around level 10 i think they finish their first item in boots and are well on their way to item number two that's the early game generally when a champion has a strong early game it refers to this part the mid game is from that point until the dragon soul is acquired which can be as early as 21 minutes or as late as you know forever if not the team cares about it reason being the dragon soul does two things first it's a permanent buffer whichever team secures it that's strong enough to decide the momentum of the batch going forward second it opens up elder drake and most of the time whichever team secures that is guaranteed to win or at least acquire a significant advantage in objectives be it turrets baron inhibitors what have you that's when we enter the late game other people tend to decide early mid and late game based on how many items the average player has which is also fine early game is one completed item and boots mid game is the second and third finish item and late game is the fourth and final one point being it's not a hard line in the sand when it comes to the early game the main reason why so much focus is placed on it is because it's always present in every game no matter what each round of solo queue opens up a laning phase there's never been a point ever in the history of the game's what 12 year life span where you skip laning phase and went straight into 5v5 team fights and no level 1 invades do not count in essence the early game is inevitable therefore champions like lee sin pantheon rek'sai talon misfortune they always have their strongest moments since that strongest moment is the first phase of every game scaling takes place in a set order early gamers will have their moment to shine before late gamers full stop in a way you can imagine it like a turn-based game early gamers are glass canyon sweepers like weavile zeraora or dragopold more often than not they're gonna hit you first but since they're squishy if you survive their burst combo you can smash them to pieces right back but if you spend any amount of time playing competitive pokemon you'll know that sweepers are impossible to stop once they get going due to the game's snowballing nature the best defense is a strong offense if you build up enough momentum with an opening strike you can prevent your opponent from responding even if they have the means to do so early successes and failures have lasting consequences for the rest of the game hypothetically if your team consists of renekton rengar katarina draven and some early game support against the enemy team's gwen kindred orion and kog'maw and lulu yes they have a superior late game but that doesn't matter if you build up a lead substantial enough to neutralize that possibility before it happens going by that it makes perfect sense and we have the empirical data to back it up a lot of early and mid game champions have better average win rates in solo queue and even to some extent pro play conventional late game 1v9s like ryze azir gangplank and kaiser tend to have lower overall win rates and a lot of that stems from players not knowing how to play the early and mid game properly leading to them falling so far behind their hyper scaling is canceled out by the massive gold deficit this scenario is more prominently displayed in the jungle champions who dominate the early game perform much better in win rate the higher the elo in patch 12.10 wreckside of 53 when raiden dive and plus red guard as well kha'zix was at 51. if you go down to silver rek'sai and rengar's win rates drop all the way to 48 and kha'zix down to 49. conversely master yi had almost a 54 win rate in silver but a 48.5 win rate in diamond plus i understand these are specific instances and may not reflect the entire jungle roster's performative data but there is evidence proving early game's importance that being said those very same win rate metrics also reinforce the point against early game champions while they're able to snowball games really fast the same can happen in the opposite direction as well first impressions are lasting impressions if you make a strong first impression it should carry you all the way to victory but if you fail to seize the opportunity you will rarely if ever have a second chance that's not to say an early game champion is completely useless later on but the amount of stuff they can do at any point in time falls off a cliff early gamers don't scale well usually from not gaining anything from items outside of the raw stats they provide take a look at renekton for instance his q damages all enemies around him and heals him his w is a single target point-and-click stun that strikes a few times his e is two short-range dashes and his ultimate is in hp and fury steroid alongside a magic damage aura building attack damage only increases his damage and healing it doesn't intrinsically alter the properties of any of his abilities in any way the scope of his pressure stays within the same range of boundaries so to speak make no mistake underestimate him and he will tear you a new one but he doesn't get anything beyond that now look at someone like vayne auto attackers naturally scale well in light of there being more than one way to enhance their damage first you have standard ad increasing how much damage you deal with each hit but then there are other factors like on-hit effects crit chance and attack speed so you can not only increase the damage of your hits but the frequency of them an example of an ability caster with good scaling would be vladimir vladimir skills better than renekton because building ability power does more than boost his damage it also grants bone's health thanks to his passive and it increases the healing from his q and ultimate as well making him grow into her ability on top of defense vice versa building health increases his damage to a degree so if renekton wanted to be a late gamer he would need more scaling elements say his armor shred on empowered dice lasts longer the more id he gets or the bonus health on his ultimate is based on his maximum health stuff like that but i digress early gamers rely more on their base numbers considering no one has any items at the start of a game around mid-to-late game is where base damage falls off in relevance and items start to matter a lot more you have a limited amount of time to win the game or build up a gold lead that can buy you some time from the moment you spawn on the rift it's an uphill battle as relay gamers their biggest point is that the game is more of a marathon than 100 meter dash early gamers would win every single time his solo queue matches at a time limit that is if there was some contingency in place like if by 25 minutes the game isn't over whichever team has a higher gold lead or more turrets or something wins but there isn't a time limit so a match can theoretically go on forever in other words for late game skillers time is on their side if they can make it past the 25 minute mark without the enemy team having an insurmountable lead it only gets easier for them and honestly even if they fall far behind the game is designed in a way that puts more pressure on the team winning early on in the form of shutdown gold and as in season 12 we now have objective bodies adding even more trump charity shout outs to mario party fans mind you the significance of comeback factors have gotten more meaningful recently with objective vines as i said earlier and now the durability update but i would argue it's always been like this league of legends is designed to be a scaling game dragons towers farming etc all of those help you get further and further into the game therefore it makes practical sense to have at least a few late gamers in your team since that's inherently the direction you're gonna go solo queue results also adhere to this we have the four horse women on top lane fiora camille riven and irelia who all have very strong mid to late games and of course let's do forget there's an entire role centered around scaling the bot lane even early to mid game adc's like miss fortune draven and lucian scale pretty dang well by nature of attack speed and crits additionally contrary to popular belief there aren't that many games where you fall so far behind that you have a snowball's chance in hell of winning solo queue mentality tries to convince otherwise but only like 5 out of every 100 games are genuinely unwinnable so if you're patient you most certainly can be successful with late gamers especially if you know how to play the early game correctly unfortunately that's a lot easier said than done while the game progresses in favor of scaling champions the means in which you can achieve your power spike requires you to be near an enemy for a sustained period of time to do something thus making you vulnerable more than any late game chamber like to be unlike other competitive games camping is not a thing here you can't simply afk form for 30 minutes because to scale you have to fight or at the very least be in a situation that forces you to interact just as how late gamers have a chance to reach the strongest point early gamers have a chance to stop you from doing so and they will always have an advantage over you in the early game should you play defensively and focus only on staying alive your lane opponent can use that priority to roam around the map either to fight somewhere else or take objectives causing you to slowly but surely bleed out in resources basically you're thrusted into a very uncomfortable position where you don't want to get involved in fights because you're at your weakest point but you don't want to give the enemy team full control over the entire map or else they'll out macro you and win that and a major source of frustration from playing late gamers is that you don't actually get to play the game for 15 to 20 minutes making them less desirable over early gamers who forfeit delayed gratification for instant gratification so as you can tell there are pros and cons to both sides but there is a clear winner in my opinion despite being someone who enjoys early game champs like jace pantheon and renekton i firmly believe that even before objective bunnies and version 12.10 were added late gamers are more sustainable long term in matches and in your solo queue journey like i said the game is designed to scale and it's always better to play someone who can scale reasonably well if not really well then again this is largely based on opinion so what i'm gonna do is leave a straw poll in the description of this video for you to vote on what do you believe is more important early game or mid game just for the sake of this experiment assume mid game is split into two halves early to make game and mid to late game so there's no actual mid game we're going to add things off here though so if you enjoyed the video please be sure to leave a like and subscribe don't forget to follow me on twitter join my discord server and check out my other discussion episodes if you haven't yet but for now thanks so much for watching and i look forward to seeing you again soon take care [Music] foreign

titre: Bel’Veth Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription:
Mes semblables s'abattront sur Runeterra
et détruiront tout. Nous vous consumerons, nous vous remplacerons. Pour chaque ville qui tombera,
dix seront construites. Pour chaque vie que nous prendrons,
mille engeances du Néant naîtront. Le Néant vous engloutira tous. Froide, calculatrice, et sans-cœur. Elle a déjà fait savoir ses intentions et les raisons
de sa présence, donc pas besoin d'en savoir plus. Bienvenue dans le Focus sur Bel'Veth. La compétence passive de Bel'Veth est Mer de lavande,
et elle est divisée en deux aspects. Tout d'abord, lorsque Bel'Veth élimine
des champions ou des grands monstres, elle cumule des effets Lavande,
ce qui lui confère de la vitesse d'attaque permanente. Il n'y a pas de limite à la vitesse d'attaque qu'elle peut gagner,
donc plus Bel'Veth consomme, plus vite elle triomphera. Je purifierai ce monde. Ensuite, après avoir utilisé une compétence,
Mer de lavande confère à Bel'Veth un bonus de vitesse d'attaque pour les deux prochaines attaques de base qu'elle lancera.
Elle peut en cumuler jusqu'à six. Charge du Néant permet à Bel'Veth de faire une ruée
et d'infliger des dégâts à tous les ennemis qu'elle traverse. Elle applique aussi les effets à l'impact à la première cible qu'elle touche. Grâce à Charge du Néant, Bel'Veth peut faire une ruée dans quatre
directions différentes, et chaque direction a son propre délai de récupération. Le délai de récupération de Charge du Néant
dépend de la vitesse d'attaque, donc veillez à bien tirer parti de Mer de lavande
afin d'accélérer le destin inévitable de tous vos ennemis. Mon monde attend. Si votre estomac est vide, Charge du Néant
ne vous permet pas de traverser les murs avec votre ruée. Plus de détails à suivre. En utilisant son Z, Projection cinglante, Bel'Veth donne un coup de queue
au sol, ce qui blesse, projette dans les airs et ralentit les ennemis. Si elle touche un champion, Projection cinglante réinitialise le délai
de récupération de Charge du Néant dans la direction de ce champion, ce qui donne à Bel'Veth l'opportunité de se rapprocher et d'achever sa proie.
- « Disparais. » Avec son E, Maelström impérial, Bel'Veth s'immobilise
et canalise une tempête de coups autour d'elle qui cible l'ennemi ayant le moins de PV
(cela comprend aussi les sbires et les monstres). Mais sachez que Bel'Veth
ne sera jamais une cible facile. Le E octroie une réduction des dégâts et du vol de vie, tout en faisant
de plus en plus de dégâts en fonction des PV manquants de la cible. Au même titre que le délai de récupération de Charge du Néant,
le nombre d'entailles dépend de sa vitesse d'attaque. Bel'Veth n'est pas UNIQUEMENT monstrueuse ; elle a aussi
une allure phénoménale et se comporte comme une véritable impératrice. Qui sait, peut-être nous invitera t-elle
à un délicieux banquet ou- Mais qu'est-ce que c'est que ça ? Ha ha. Je suis en danger. Terminons ce Focus sur le champion
avant de finir en nourriture pour poisson. L'ultime de Bel'Veth est Banquet infini. Sa compétence passive permet à Bel'Veth d'infliger des dégâts bruts
toutes les deux attaques de base qu'elle lance sur la même cible, peu importe la forme dans laquelle elle se trouve. Cette compétence passive est cumulable à l'infini : les joueurs qui gardent un œil sur leur objectif
et abattent les ennemis un par un sont donc récompensés. Si vous changer de cible, alors votre cumul se rompra,
donc faites en sorte de choisir judicieusement vos proies. Comme vous l'avez déjà remarqué,
lorsque Bel'Veth élimine des champions ou des monstres épiques, cela génère des coraux du Néant,
qu'elle peut consommer pour prendre sa forme ultime. Je saurai faire bon usage de toi. Une fois qu'ils ont été consommés,
les coraux exploseront et infligeront des dégâts aux ennemis proches,
tandis que Bel'Veth recevra un effet Lavande. Quand elle se trouve dans sa forme ultime elle gagne,
des PV max, de la portée d'attaque, de la vitesse d'attaque totale, et de la vitesse de déplacement
en dehors des combats. Comme on l'a laissé entendre tout à l'heure,
après que Bel'Veth a rempli son estomac et révélé sa vraie forme, son A - Charge du Néant,
lui permettra de traverser les murs. Tu n'as nulle part où te cacher. Vous vous demandez peut-être : est-ce que les autres
êtres du Néant sont à l'abri de Bel'Veth ? Eh bien, non ! Si Bel'Veth élimine un monstre épique du Néant
et consomme les coraux, elle reçoit alors un ultime renforcé qui lui permet
de garder sa vraie forme plus longtemps. Grâce à ces coraux, elle pourra aussi ressusciter
les sbires qui meurent autour d'elle en rémoras du Néant, prêts à avancer sur la voie et dévorer les ennemis. Manger plusieurs coraux
prolongera la durée de Banquet infini. Consumer, grandir, recréer. Cependant, si Bel'Veth reprend sa forme précédente, alors elle perd
tous ses bonus et sa Charge du Néant renforcée. Lorsque vous jouez Bel'Veth,
soyez aussi calculateur qu'elle. Même si elle détient des millions d'années de connaissances,
son corps physique est tout nouveau dans ce monde. Bel'Veth est plutôt faible en début de partie, mais elle gagnera
en force et en puissance au fur et à mesure qu'elle se nourrit. Je m'en souviendrai. Quand vous êtes dans la jungle, il vous faut dévorer autant de grands monstres
que possible pour accumuler un maximum d'effets Lavande. La forme ultime de Bel'Veth lui confère un puissant bonus de durabilité :
elle est plus forte dans cette forme que dans sa forme normale. N'hésitez pas à vous concentrer
sur le farm jusqu'au niveau 6. Je suis sûr que vos équipiers
ne vous en voudront pas ! Laisse-moi goûter à ta peur. En milieu de partie, essayez d'abattre en priorité
le héraut de la Faille, ou même le baron, car ils rendront Bel'Veth particulièrement forte
puisque son ultime durera plus longtemps, et elle aura des rémoras du Néant à ses côtés
qui accroîtront sa puissance de push. Chaque parcelle de ce monde
alimentera notre métamorphose. Difficile de se faire des amis
quand on a pour ambition de dévorer le monde, il n'est donc pas surprenant que Bel'Veth n'excelle pas dans les combats d'équipes,
surtout lorsque Banquet infini n'est pas activé. Cherchez des occasions pour faire du split-push ou des escarmouches
en 1 contre 1, où vos proies, enfin... vos ennemis, sont seuls. Si vos ennemis ont peur de se mesurer à l'impératrice, pourchassez-les
avec Charge du Néant et arrêtez-les à l'aide de Projection cinglante, ce qui réinitialisera le délai de Charge du Néant
et vous aidera à vous rapprocher si vous en avez besoin. Poursuivez votre massacre avec des attaques, afin de profiter
au maximum du passif de Banquet infini, et une fois que votre cible n'a plus beaucoup de PV, lancez
Maelström impérial pour achever votre festin, puis passez au suivant. Si vous être en difficulté (non pas que Bel'Veth
puisse l'être un jour), mangez ! Ton courage perdurera... à travers moi. Bel'Veth représente tout ce que le Néant
a jamais dévoré. L'existence de Bel'Veth marque le début
de notre déclin, et sa victoire est assurée. Êtes-vous prêts à vous noyer
dans ses eaux de lavande ? Votre impératrice vous attend. Apprenez-en plus sur Bel'Veth
en cliquant sur les liens ci-dessous. Le Néant exige que j'existe, alors me voilà.

titre: Graves: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Bienvenue dans ce focus de League of Legends consacré à Graves, le Hors-la-loi. Graves est un DPS physique à distance capable de foncer en mêlée pour infliger des dégâts bonus. Sa compétence passive est Nerfs d'acier. En lançant le combat, son armure et résistance magique augmentent. Chevrotine tire trois balles en cône, chacune blessant les ennemis touchés. Les balles peuvent toucher un ennemi plusieurs fois. Je monte Chevrotine au max. Écran de fumée ralentit tous les ennemis à portée et camoufle la zone pendant quelques secondes. Les ennemis dans la zone d'effet sont ralentis et ne voient pas en dehors sauf s'ils sont attaqués. J'investis un point et j'en reste là pour le moment. Décharge le fait foncer dans une direction et il gagne un bonus de vitesse d'attaque pendant quelques secondes. Décharge récupère plus vite chaque fois que Graves fait une attaque normale. J'investis un point au niveau 2 et le monte au max au niveau 13. Son ultime est Dégâts collatéraux. Il tire d'abord un obus explosif qui blesse le premier champion qu'il touche. Au bout de sa portée ou après avoir frappé un champion ennemi, l'obus explose en cône de feu et blesse les ennemis à l'intérieur. J'investis des points aux niveaux 6, 11 et 16. Graves peut infliger d'énormes dégâts à faible niveau. Je fais Décharge vers Sona, je bloque Kog'Maw avec Écran de fumée et je la touche avec Chevrotine. Je m'éloigne avant que Kog'Maw ne puisse contre-attaquer. Graves peut aussi se sortir de situations difficiles. Je me fais ganker par derrière par ce malin de Twisted Fate. J'utilise Décharge pour arriver au contact, je lance Chevrotine et je fais Saut éclair. Je lance Ecran de fumée devant mes poursuivants. Ils ne peuvent plus me toucher et je m'en sors avec des soins de Soraka. Écran de fumée peut aussi leurrer les ennemis. Là je me fais ganker par Udyr et je dois fuir. Je lance Écran de fumée quand Warwick vient ganker. Udyr ne peut plus voir alors on se jette sur lui. Udyr tombe sous nos dégâts et on force le passage, forçant Sona et Kog'Maw à se retirer. La mobilité de Graves lui permet de se jeter sous une tourelle. Je marche jusqu'à Kog'Maw, je lance Chevrotine, une attaque normale et je fais Décharge pour sortir de la portée de la tourelle. Soraka le tue avec Infusion alors que j'échappe à Surprise d'Icathia. Combat d'équipe à la rivière du bas. Je lance Fatigue sur Sion pour l'éloigner de Soraka. J'entre à courte portée avec Décharge et je tire Chevrotine pour l'abattre facilement. Quand il meurt je passe à une cible proche et j'attaque Udyr. Je n'ai pas beaucoup de mana mais, dès que je peux, je fais Décharge pour augmenter la vitesse d'attaque et je le tue avant de me faire descendre par Kog'Maw et Twisted Fate. Durant le combat mes PV ont beaucoup chuté et monté alors j'ai réussi à survivre au combat grâce au vol de vie de Soif-de-sang. En ayant Maillet gelé assez tôt, je peux encaisser des dégâts et les récupérer avec Soif-de-sang. Je joue agressivement contre l'équipe adverse, qui se tourne vers moi. Saut éclair pour filer, Dégâts collatéraux sur l'équipe ennemie, retour avec Décharge. On tue Sona et on les repousse de l'inhibiteur pour le détruire. Les capacités à effet de zone et la mobilité de Graves en font un bon défenseur dans Dominion. L'adversaire essaie de capturer la carrière, je fais Dégâts collatéraux et Chevrotine pour dégager le point et j'entre avec Saut éclair. Je repousse Lee Sin, Écran de fumée sur Xerath, je les tue tous les deux et je saute sur Garen avec Décharge puis Chevrotine. Il s'en sort mais doit fuir et me laisse capturer l'ossuaire. Sa mobilité est aussi utile en combat en équipe dans Dominion. Quand je commence à combattre Xerath, je reste près des sbires pour garder les effets de Épée sanglante et Nerfs d'acier cumulés. Quand Shaco apparaît, je vais aider à le tuer avant de revenir sur Xerath. Nocturne fait l'erreur de s'approcher trop près, mais au lieu de poursuivre Xerath, je reste avec la vague de sbires pour garder les statistiques à fond et capturer la foreuse. Quand je commence à capturer le point et quand Xerath vient m'en empêcher, je m'approche avec Décharge, je le ralentis avec Écran de fumée et je le tue avec des attaques de base. Chevrotine aide bien Graves pour les parties axées sur la mêlée. Shaco fait Tromperie sur le mur, j'avance avec Décharge, je lance Écran de fumée et je fais de gros dégâts avec Chevrotine. Il fait Hallucination, j'amorce Dégâts collatéraux pour le tuer lui et son clone. Graves a une interaction spéciale avec Nocturne. Lancez-lui Écran de fumée, vous verrez. "Je les tiens, tes ténèbres" "Que dis-tu de mes ténèbres ?" Niveau runes : marques de pénétration d'armure, sceaux d'armure fixe, glyphes de résistance magique fixe et quintessences de dégâts fixe. Comme ça, je peux dominer en début de partie et résister correctement dans les combats en mêlée. Mes maîtrises sont 21/0/9, maîtrises de dégâts physiques de base en attaque et, en utilitaire, un point en durée de sortilège neutre. Je prends Fatigue et Saut éclair et je monte le dernier au max dans l'arbre. En classique je commence avec une ou deux Lames de Doran et j'ajoute Jambières du berzerker. Mes deux premiers objets sont presque toujours Soif-de-sang et Danseur fantôme. Je prends aussi Maillet gelé et Lame d'infini et j'ajoute Dernier souffle quand l'armure pose problème. Dans Dominion j'utilise une config similaire, juste Lame du prospecteur au lieu des Lames de Doran et Lame sanguine au lieu de Soif-de-sang. Et je prends Dernier souffle un peu plus tôt. Merci d'avoir regardé le focus sur Graves. N'oubliez pas de vous abonner à la chaîne YouTube de RiotGames et de faire J'aime juste en dessous de la vidéo !

titre: The NEW BEST SOLO CARRY CHAMPIONS on PATCH 25.22 - League of Legends
transcription: Leblanc and Volibear drop out of the top three due to their nerfs. So, we've got a new look for jungle and mid in 25.22 as we're ready to set you guys up with three of the best champions for every single role. But first, some hard truth. YouTube is built for the algorithm, not your LP. That means clickbait titles, junk food topics, and stretching simple tips for watch time. We're part of that system, too, just like every guide and YouTube channel. And because every video is randomly surfaced to you as a singular guide and topic, advice often contradicts what you just watched. No context, no plan, more confusion. On our website at Skillcap, however, we're free from all that, allowing us to focus on what actually matters. We teach in the most efficient way to really rank you up fast with the same premium editing and challenger level coaches you see here, just without the algorithms hoops. Start with our essentials courses for every role, updated for season 15. These are meticulously planned step-by-step guides that build your fundamentals in the right order. Once you've finished essentials, move into the advanced courses, deep dive, wave control, advanced mechanics, and proven bronze to diamond climb paths, and literal world record jungle clears for each jungler. All structured so lessons stack instead of contradict. While you follow these learning paths, we also release our popular challenger walkthroughs, new sessions every week showing how to abuse the best meta champs for your ELO, supported with an AMA section below each video, which we always respond to. We back it with a climb five divisions improvement guarantee while you're actively using SkillCat, plus a 30-day refund change of mind policy on top of that. So, there's literally no risk. Stop trying to mix and match random guides and start following a structured path far away from all the noise. Click the limited time discount link below to join Skillcap today. So, a top lane champ who got better last patch and making his way into the top three for 25.22 is Urgot. With Doran Shield and Second Wind getting nerfed, it's great for a champion like Urgot who has one of the best level ones for any top lane champ. If you can get a good trade-off at level one, then it's more rewarding now when up against the Dorans with second wind champion. So, your ability to score early solo kills has become better. You should never be AFK at level one when playing Urgot, as running straight for brush control up top lane is what you want to do. You can get so many advantages and win lanes solely off good level one trades. So, it's something you really want to focus on when picking Urgot. What's really nice about Urgot is that he doesn't have any hard losing matchups in meta right now. Olaf is one champ who can be more annoying to play into, but Olaf is picked very sparingly, so banning him out doesn't really feel that worth it. However, there aren't any other champs you have to worry about as much as him. The optimal build for Urgot revolves around a black cleaver rush and a Sterex gauge second and then either jock show or overlord's blood mail third. Press the attack is always the best keystone rune for Urgot along with inspiration for secondaries. A top lane pig that everyone below diamond should be spamming for ELO right now is Malight. Malight was just buffed a few patches ago and is far and beyond the best tank top laner that you can play. Even if you only use Malight as a counter pick, he's as close to a free win champion as you're going to get in specific matchups. Champ has literally been up above a 55% win rate in the Atrox, Jax, Jace, Trindir, Urelia, Fiora, Nar, Quinn, Riven, and Camille matchups, which is absolutely unheard of. Usually, counter matchups end up with the counter winning 52 or 53% of the time, but the fact Mal is currently up above 55% is just an absurd stat line. Mal can struggle more into other tanks like Scion and Mundo along with AP bruisers like Silus and Morichiser as those are the only matchups you do want to avoid [music] if possible. Silus is the must ban out of those four as his team fight impact with being able to steal Malight's ultimate is just way too broken. The standard build for Malight consists of a Sunfire or hollow radiance rush into situational tank items. For the keystone rune, you should be taking Grasp when in those melee matchups and then comet if against ranged or if you are into a more spell-casting reliant top lane pick like Atrox or Illaoi. And then for our third solo carry top lane pick of the patch, the best fighter top laner right now is Garen. For how simple Garen is to play, he's a phenomenal pickup for 25.22 with how powerful of a spot he's in. Although not quite as powerful as Urgot, Garen's level one if he starts E is really strong, so it's something you want to take advantage of. Sitting in the far brush in top lane and then waiting for the enemy to walk to lane and fighting them like this is a great way to generate an advantage right from the jump. Solo Q is all about taking control of your own lane and outright winning so that you don't have to rely on your team. So aggressive level one trades is a great way to do that. So that this strategy is most impactful. You can run bone plating in runes. Most Garen players take the sorcery tree for secondaries, but opting for bone plating if you are going to try and sneak into the top lane brush and go for a level one trade is most optimal. For your build with Garen, it's going to be a Stridebreaker Rush into Phantom Dancer second and either Spear of Shojan or Infinity Edge third. Shojun will be most optimal in games where the enemy team is more durable and you won't be able to one-shot them with one rotation of spells, while IE works great if the enemy team is entirely squishy. Matchup wise for Garen right now, Camille and Kale are always two tougher lanes, so using your band on either of them is never a bad idea. So, a jungler that's been on our radar the past few patches who's making his way into the top three for 25.22 is Ramis. Ramis has been a very sleeper jungle pick as of recent, and it all stems back to his buffs from 25.13. Ramis saw huge buffs to his W resist going from 27 all the way up to 47 in max rank. Ever since then, he's been a top 10 win rate jungler. But now that Volibear is nerfed and Diana was just recently nerfed, it gives even more incentive to give the Armadillo a go. Even with this W buff, though, a Q max first into E second and W last is what's performing best. W max isn't terrible, but there's so much value in maxing Q because you get the cooldown of the spell down from 12 to 6 seconds, while W cooldown stays the exact same regardless of rank. Matchup wise for Ramis, he's really good if you can pick him into stuff like Yei, Viego, and Graves, but will struggle more against an AP jungler like Lilia. So, she's a good band to consider. For the build, it's a thorn mail rush into Sunfire or Hollow Radian second and dead mans or jock show third. The most ideal situation for Ramis is if the enemy team is full AD and he can go thorn mail into Sunfire and Dead Man's. But if the enemy comp does have significant magic damage threat, that's when hollow radiance and jock show can be considered. So, it was initially looking like Riot were going to slap Jacks with a few nerfs for this patch, but they didn't end up going through. So, he's back in the top three for jungle. If you're emerald or below right now and all you play for 25.22 is Ramis and Jax, then you're putting yourself in a really good spot. Jax is the best pure carry jungler for the lower ranks right now, as he's super easy to succeed with if you get a lead. The majority of Jax's damage is point and click. So, when it comes down to winning out in skirmishes, if you got the item advantage, there's pretty much no way for you to mess it up. And this has been amplified even further due to the resist buffs that Jack's got in his R a few patches ago. you're just super tanky and deal a mass amount of guaranteed damage. So, there are few junglers who can be as impactful as Jax without needing any sort of mechanical skill. For your build on Jax, two core items in every game should be Trinity Force and Sundered Sky, while items like Za, Sterox, and Wit's End can follow. Lethal Tempo is the best keystone rune as it allows for Jax to thrive in extended [music] fights. And then in regards to Jax's matchups, he wants to avoid playing into champs who don't rely on auto attacking at all to win out in skirmishes. So, Lilia is a good champ to try and avoid. And then continuing to be the best AP jungler that you can play for solo Q is Diana. So Diana got nerfed last patch, but the nerf, as we expected, did not do nearly enough to drop her power level significantly. Diana is a really good jungler for solo Q because of the fact she has a very clear power spike in her level six. The champ farms really well and can motor through a full clear because of all the AoE damage potential. So being consistent with your clear, cycling camps on respawn, and only looking for ganks when there's downtime will allow you to be really impactful on the pick. Once you reach level six is where you can be more aggressive in your decision-making and skip camps, especially if you have your flash up, as a Diana with flash and R has some of the best gank potential in the game. Unless you are 100% sure you're going to score a kill on a gank pre six, you should pretty much always prioritize clearing your camps over a gank because hitting that level six as soon as possible is so vital. And if a gank doesn't work out, then you're just delaying your win condition. In regards to builds on Diana, you have a few options as there's the durable Leandre into Rift Maker that works best into tankier enemy comps while Nasher's Rush can be considered if the enemy team is more squishy. Magic-wise for Diana, Jax is one jungler in meta who most Diana players are really struggling against right now. So, he's a good band to consider. Diana needs to be able to weave auto attacks in order to proc her passive and win out in skirmishes during the early to mid game. And with Jax's dodge mechanic combined with the tankiness he gets from his R, it makes winning out in skirmishes very difficult for Diana when against Jax. So, with Leblanc nerfed for this patch, taking her spot in the top three for mid lane is going to be Anivvia. Anivia was just recently buffed and is currently the best mid lane mage that you can play for solo Q. Our challenger mid laner Shri agrees that Anivia is a really good pick for the lower end of the ladder and he's got a new full game commentary for the pick up on our website where he walks you through exactly how to abuse the champ for free ELO. When you get level six with Anivvia, she has the ability to acquire lane priority super easily with her R and can then look for roam plays. Anivvia has a really underrated roam at level six because her R and W and then Q combo is such a great catch tool and will prevent the enemy from escaping for a very long duration unless they flash or dash away. In order for this combo to be most effective, movement speed is really key on Anvia because as long as you can get in range to slow the enemy with your R, then that's all you need to consistently land the combo. Swiftness boots are extremely underrated on the pick and have the highest win rate for any boots on Anvia. However, only 24% of Anivvia players actually build them. It's also why Relentless Hunter is the most popular rune in that third row of the domination tree. Something that's also quite interesting on Anivvia is maxing your W [music] out second instead of Q. W max second has a 2% better win rate than Q, and this could partially be due to Anivvia one tricks being the only players running this skill max, but it's definitely something to try out if you haven't already. Increasing the size of Anivvia wall for the Midame can be huge for blocking off choke points. And as long as your team is with you, it doesn't really matter if you're lacking a bit of damage from Q if you can block the enemy's escape path with the added width of W. So, there are a lot of ways to optimize Anivvia that many players are missing out on right now, which is kind of crazy considering she's already one of the best midlaners with people playing her wrong. The build for Ania is the one thing that's super standard as you want Rod of Ages into Archangels for every game. Matchup wise for Anivia, she doesn't like playing into longer range mages, so Hay or Syndra are two good bands to consider. For our second mid-lane selection of the patch, she kind of plays in a similar fashion to Anivia as we have Morana. So, just like Anivvia, Morgana has the ability to shove out waves super fast and acquire priority in lane, but she can do this a little bit earlier as you can begin one-shotting the range minions at level five. In solo Q, being able to have the push in lane is so vital to succeed because moving first when a fight breaks out in the jungle can single-handedly win you a game. And you know that both junglers are going to be fighting tooth and nail to secure those scuttle crabs. So, if you can have the enemy shoved under tower and then be ready to rotate for your jungler, you set yourself up so well. Just being able to rotate on every play your jungler is making will tilt the ever living daylight out of the enemy jungler as well. If you're the one moving first for every single play while the enemy mid is stuck under tower, then the enemy jungler is going to absolutely hate this mid laner. The best build from Morgana mid lane consists of a Blackfire Torch Rush into Leandre's or Horizon Focus second, while Zas is generally the best third purchase. If the enemy team is more tanky, then going Leandre second for the percent health burn makes sense. But into squishier comps where you just want to be sitting back further and tossing out QES, Horizon Focus is great as it provides the 25 ability haste and more upfront burst potential. Mobility is what's going to counter Morgana the hardest as it makes landing her Q much more difficult. So banning out Katarina or Zed is good value. And then a new addition to the top three for this patch who has been criminally underrated as of late is Nefury. So, over the past few patches, there's a new build that has been picked up by Nefury players, and it's giving her a lot more power. On top of this, Halo Blaze is buffed for 25.22, and it's been Nefir's best performing keystone rune in recent patches. So, she's getting indirectly better while already being in a phenomenal spot. Back to the build though, it's Voltaic Cyclloor that has really caught steam on Nefury and is a great alternative to Eclipse. The passive from Voltaque just works incredibly well with Nefury because the slow allows for Nefuri to easily land her Q while the energized attack refreshes faster when you dash, which means when Nefir uses her R or her E, she regenerates that energized attack much quicker. So the core build working the best right now consists of Voltaic into opportunity second and Edge of Knight third. A good band to consider when picking Nefury is Yaso as his wind wall and mobility can do a really good job at limiting your strength in the 1v one as landing Q consistently is super difficult. So, moving on to the bot lane now. A champ who has started seeing more play as of late and who's such a reliable bot lane carry to climb with is Ziggs. We've seen Ziggs pulled out at Worlds this year and has been a super impactful champ and he's arguably even better for solo Q. There are a couple of reasons for Ziggs being such a great bot lane pick. Starting with the fact that he can dominate lane against most traditional ADCs. You take teleport as your secondary summoner spell on Ziggs. So, the game plan is to try and poke away at the enemy ADC whenever they are going for last hits. whittle them down low enough and then if you drop low on mana, you just take a quick base, teleport back to lane and continue to pressure relentlessly. With Zigs being able to shove in most ADCs, it allows him to chip away at tower plates really easily and amass a huge gold lead solely off of getting a CS lead and collecting plate gold. You don't even need to get kills in lane with Ziggs to come out way ahead of the enemy, which is a big reason to why he's so consistent. You're also way less reliant on your support to be good in order to succeed with Zigs because as long as you can hit your long-range poke, that alone can win you lane without your support even needing to interact. For the build on Zigs, it's going to be a Luton's rush into Horizon Focus second and Robon's third as the core, while Comet will slot in as the keystone rune. Matchup wise for Ziggs, avoiding champs who have heals is ideal so that your poke sticks better and therefore banning a meta support like Nami is good value. For the second solo carry ADC of the patch, it's going to be Ash. Ash is such a good ADC for solo Q right now because you don't have to worry about running into a bad ADC matchup. Because of the range advantage that Ash has, it allows her to thrive in lane and she has a lot more agency over how lanes play out than most other ADCs. It also helps a lot that Mio is one of the most played supports in the game right now, as the fact Mio's W increases Ash's auto range even further makes her incredibly oppressive, and that duo has over a 54% win rate currently. It really doesn't matter what support you pair up with Ash, though, as she can do well with melee supports or enchanters. Ash provides enough CC on her own, so if she's with an enchanter, then she can just make plays happen. But if she's with a melee engage support, then the chain CC lockdown potential is pretty disgusting and makes for a ridiculously strong level six gank assist. All in all, just one of the best blind pick champs you can play down in bot lane right now, who doesn't go by the name of Ziggs. Build for Ash consists of a Kraken Slayer Rush into Phantom Dancer second and Infinity Edge third, while Lethal Tempo is the best keystone rune. The only weakness of Ash is the fact that she lacks a gap closer. So using your ban on a meta dive champ like Jarvin is never a bad idea. And then a staple in the top three for ADC who's back again for another patch is Jinx. With the buffs she received a few patches ago. No other ADC has been buffed enough to overtake Jinx's spot in meta. So she's been a really great pickup for a while. There's just no better ADC when it comes down to thriving in mid-ame team fights than Jinx. Jinx passive providing all that bonus attack speed and movement speed allows her to chain kills in fights and completely take over games. This makes her incredibly potent in the chaotic skirmishes that define solo Q. With enchanter supports being favored more than melee supports right now, it makes Jinx's team fight potential even more devastating as her main weakness is getting dove on and blown up right away. But if you have someone like Lulu or Mlio by your side, then your survivability in fights increases drastically. Of course, you can't always rely on your support to provide you with good peel though, so banning out a meta dive champ like Zed or Jarvin is always good value. For the core build on Jinx, you want to be rushing Yal into Infinity Edge second and Hurricane third. While Lethal Tempo is the best keystone rune, it would be really nice if Riot did something to shake up the support meta because there's just simply no reason for us to take Millio out of the top three for support if they don't. Mio has been a staple in the top three for quite some time now, and that won't be changing for 25.22. So, that's two indirect buffs for Poppy as we head into this patch, which should land her in a really powerful spot. And then back in the support top three for a second patch in a row, and one of the best team fight supports is Amumu. Amumu's roaming potential is exceptional for a support. His Q gives him strong gank setup for mid lane, and his ultimate can turn any skirmish in the river or jungle into a one fight for your team. In solo Q, where supports often need to impact other lanes to carry, Amumu's roaming tools are top tier. Amumu can be a little more vulnerable preix because he doesn't have a reliable poke tool or way to provide pressure besides going allin. So banning out a range support like karma or morgana is a good idea. titre: Most POWERFUL Characters in League of Legends Lore Explained
transcription: Fiddlesticks. Fiddlesticks is the primordial demon of fear. He is not a spirit or a ghost. He is the concept of fear itself. He is probably one of the oldest beings on this list, being born when the first breath of life screamed. He is known as the first of 10, the 10 being the 10 primordial demons. They are some of the most mysterious beings in Runeterra. Other demons like Evelynn or Atakhan have certain goals or desires, whereas Fiddlesticks is just a force of entropy. He can manipulate space and manifest anything that someone fears. Apart from this, there are no actual feats that show how powerful Fiddlesticks is. He hasn't killed any gods, Darkin, or aspects. There is one feat though that I find really interesting. In the game, Fiddlesticks has unique interactions with every champion. His voice lines are references to something that the champions fear. For example, when he interacts with Garen, this is what he says. GAREN, BROTHER, >> [groaning] >> PLEASE DON'T [clears throat] HATE ME. The reason why he says this is because in Demacia, mages are despised. They are usually executed or thrown in prison. Garen's uncle was also killed by mage, so Garen hates mages. The conflict here comes from Garen's little sister, Lux. He loves her more than anything, but she was born a mage. So, with this, we can see that Fiddlesticks knows what everyone fears the most. So, the voice line that I find particularly interesting is the one that he has with Kindred. Let's listen. Gray man. We will cover Kindred separately later in the video, but for now, the gray man that Fiddlesticks mentions refers to death, Kindred's initial form. Death comes for everyone, and the thing that it fears the most is being alone. If everyone dies, then death has no choice but to be alone. Let's listen to one of Kindred's voice lines now. Lamb, tell me a story. There was once a pale man with dark hair who was very lonely. Why was it lonely? All things must meet this man, so they shunned him. Did he chase them all? He took an axe and split himself in two, right down the middle. So, he would always have a friend. So, he would always have a friend. The significance of Fiddlesticks' interaction with Kindred is that it shows us that even death has its own fear. This means that the extent of Fiddles' power can also touch death in a way, something that no other character on this list can do. Lissandra. Lissandra is the ice witch. She lives in the Freljord and is thousands of years old. She has two sisters, Avarosa and Serylda. At one point, Lissandra was attacked and blinded by Volibear. This, however, was a blessing in disguise. Lissandra was no longer able to see, but was able to access the dream plane. This is how she established a connection with the watchers. These are extremely powerful extra-dimensional beings from the void. Lissandra was able to make a deal with them. In exchange for granting her and her sisters immortality, they would prepare Runeterra for the arrival of the void. Her sisters thought that this is actually not the really good deal. If the watchers came, they would destroy the world. There's no point in being the ruler of nothingness. So, they asked the demigod Ornn for a favor, to build a prison for the watchers. This is how the Howling Abyss came into existence. In the end, Lissandra also thought it's not a good idea to bring the watchers into the world, so she betrayed them, sealing them in true ice under the Howling Abyss. She now keeps watch over the watchers, ironically, and is preventing their release and the end of the world. So, what are Lissandra's powers? Well, she is a true iceborn, which makes her immortal and able to resist any type of cold. She is also capable of wielding and manipulating true ice. True ice is magical and can never melt. Lissandra is also a powerful and knowledgeable witch. She can enslave other beings with her will and enter their dreams. This way, she can manipulate memories and even rewrite the history that people remember. This may not sound like much, but she can enter the dreams of the watchers, which is no small feat. Speaking of the watchers, the true power that Lissandra holds is that without her, all of Runeterra would get destroyed by them. She now seeks to find the heart of blue, a mysterious artifact that can help create more true ice and keep the watchers at bay. Bard. Bard is the wandering caretaker. Bard has existed since before the creation of the universe. He is a wandering celestial who drifts like a note in an endless song. To some, he is the great caretaker. To others, he is a strange omen wrapped in floating bells and laughter, but his purpose never changes. Preserve balance, protect creation, and intervene only when the cosmic itself falls out of tune. He appears in places where reality grows unstable, where forgotten relics hum with too much power, or where reckless mortals awaken forces they cannot contain. Bard does not warn, negotiate, or threaten. He simply acts. A simple gesture or a quiet adjustment, and fate itself shifts back into harmony. Bard also has meeps that follow him around. These are celestial spirits that were created from the residual inspiration used to create the stars. They carry his tools, alter the world in his direction, and guide those who stumble into his path. Entire cultures have myths about Bard, the spirit who fixes what should not break, the wanderer who mends the stars, the protector who comes before disaster. Bard is not a warrior or a conqueror. His strength is quiet, cosmic, and precise. He can warp reality and avert catastrophes before mortals ever realized they were moments away from extinction. If Fiddlesticks is fear incarnate, then Bard is balance. Where the universe falters, Bard appears. When the balance is restored, he simply drifts away, humming the eternal song only he can hear. Kindred. Kindred is the living embodiment of death in Runeterra. Not a villain, not a savior, but the inevitable balance that everyone must face. They appear as a duo entity, Lamb and Wolf. When a soul reaches its final moment, Kindred offers a choice, acceptance or denial. If you come willingly, Lamb's arrow will end your life swiftly and peacefully. If you deny though, Wolf will hunt you down violently. Kindred's presence weaves into the mythologies of many cultures. To some, they are poetic and inevitable. To others, terrifying and unavoidable. Kindred does not kill indiscriminately. [clears throat] Instead, they respect the natural order, closing the cycle of life and death. They are drawn to souls who are meeting their end and those who are desperately trying to flee from it. When someone meets their end, whether on a battlefield or from a dying heart, Kindred arrives. When Lamb's arrow is loose or Wolf's jaw snaps shut, the soul moves on. And in the act, Kindred maintains the balance of life and death across Runeterra. Power-wise, Kindred's dominion is absolute. Death is not a power to be challenged indefinitely. In a world of war, magic, and chaos, Kindred stands as the ultimate constant. Soraka. Soraka is the star child. She is a celestial being who abandoned immortality to walk among mortals. She saw suffering and needless pain in the mortal world, and rather than judge from above, she descended to heal it herself. Villages tell stories of a gentle horned healer arriving on moonlit nights, leaving peace and life in her footsteps. To the dying, she is hope. To the desperate, she is light. To the wicked, she is forgiveness, even when they do not deserve it. Unlike other celestials, Soraka does not impose order or chase grand cosmic goals. She cares about the small things, a wounded traveler, a starving child, or a lost warrior who has forgotten kindness. Her compassion is her power, and it radiates like a quiet star in the darkness. Her magic is pure restoration. Each time she heals, she uses her own celestial essence. This is why in game, she loses health whenever she heals someone. Beneath her gentleness though, lies something vast. She has seen worlds rise and fall, watched stars be born and die. Still, she chooses to care for fragile mortal hearts. Soraka's strength is not destructive, it's transformative. She changes people by showing them a better way. In a world of gods, monsters, and endless war, Soraka stands as a reminder. Sometimes, the greatest power is simply choosing to heal. Aurelion Sol. Aurelion Sol is the star forger. Long before Runeterra ever existed, Aurelion Sol was sculpting the heavens. He is a celestial born in the beginning of creation. He can create stars and form entire galaxies. To him, stars are not miracles. They are art. Even though we know him as a big celestial dragon, his true form is something unimaginable that cannot be described. Once upon a time, Aurelion Sol returned to one of his earlier creations, but noticed that a world he had not created was orbiting it. This world was Runeterra. So, who created Runeterra? Well, that's a topic for another video. Let me know in the comments if you'd like to see that. Anyway, the aspects of Targon invited him to Runeterra to guide its inhabitants. Flattered by the offer, Aurelion descended in the form of a dragon. The Targonians named him Sol after their sun. To thank him for honoring their request, they gave him a golden crown. As soon as he touched it, though, he realized that it was not a gift, but a prison. The crown was imbued in magic so powerful that even he couldn't fight against. Now, with Aurelion Sol's power in their hands, the Targonians used it to channel power into a man-made structure, the Sun Disc. Aurelion Sol decided that when he escaped, he would take revenge on the aspects and destroy Runeterra for detaining him. To anger the Star Forger is to challenge the cosmos itself, and the cosmos rarely loses. Zoe. Zoe is the aspect of twilight. She looks like a little girl, but is actually thousands of years old. She was born as a human and chosen by the aspects to be one of their hosts. As the aspect of twilight, she heralds change. She embodies the moment between what was and what will be, a moment of upheaval. Just like in the real world, twilight is the moment before the day turns into night. Her arrival often marks turning points in history. Old orders crumble, new threats awaken, and cosmic forces shift. Essentially, if you see her, it means that something big is about to happen. Zoe is also chaotic and childish. She is friends with Aurelion Sol, and even though he is one of the most powerful beings in the universe, she treats him as a puppy. She can create miniature stars and blast them into her enemies. She can also teleport and travel between dimensions. Behind her playful grin lies undeniable truth. The universe bends around her whims. She is both harmless and utterly terrifying, a paradox wrapped in sparkles and laughter. Volibear. Volibear is ancient, older than mortal kingdoms, older than Freljord tribes. He is storm made flesh, the primal fury of nature incarnate. To many, he is the thunder's roar, the great storm, the primal god of war and survival. Legend says he and his demigod kin shaped the land long before mortals walked it. With a swipe of his claws, he carved fjords. In a titanic battle against the magma serpent, he forged rivers and mountains from its blood and corpse. For countless generations, the tribes of the Freljord worshipped him, offering blood and strength. Volibear led them into war, equipped with runic armor forged by his brother, the Divine Smith. But time and civilization softened many. Fields replaced hunts, walls replaced wild roaming. Volibear saw this as decay, weakness born out of comfort. He cast aside armor, discarded restraint, and embraced pure elemental rage. Now, he calls for a return to the old ways. He seeks to strip civilization away and resurrect the primal spirit of the land. To him, strength, blood, and struggle are what makes life meaningful. Civilization is a cage. Wilderness is the truth. In terms of power, Volibear is as close as Runeterra gets to an avatar of nature's fury. Storm magic, godlike strength, and near immortality so long as the old beliefs endure. The stronger the belief in Volibear, the stronger he gets. He does not just fight armies. He embodies unstoppable force, a tempest given form, and brings war into the bones of the land itself.
