titre: A New Champion Teaser Just Dropped...
transcription: Ta-da, happy New Year and stuff like that. All right, so in exactly 24 hours from now, we will get the new cinematic. This time it is called Salvation and it is these cinematics from the beginning of the year that are the biggest and most hyped pieces. We're talking about The Call, Still Here and everyone freaking about Kindred. And just like it happened with the previous cinematics, it is still made by the same studio who keeps making them awesome. And so, we should all expect something amazing. But what's interesting is that right before this cinematic is about to drop, Riot also sent out packages. Now, of course, because League of Legends does have a marketing team, it is part of a promotion and so creators usually get these packages. However, the interesting thing is that this is not the first time this happened. In fact, because we know what happened last time we can have a good assumption about what is happening right now. And the fact is that what is happening right now is legitimately teasing the next champion. And I don't know how it's possible, but against all odds, nothing managed to leak about them. So, all we have right now are these teases and speculations. Which is exactly what we also have about today's sponsor, me. Did you know that I'm making a game on Steam? I should be promoting it more. It is a unique twist on a rage game. It has pretty cool physics and it is story driven because I am the lore guy, I guess. I still have no idea when I'll release it. It's probably going to be like March or May. I don't know, but you can wish list it right now. Now, back to the video. So, after our little package from Riot arrived, after you opened it, the first thing you saw was I'm kind of late to the party, I guess. Yes, it is now January the 6th, so I'm 1 day late. Everyone else already opened theirs. I mean, it got Nikki to join the corporeal realm. However, I did avoid spoilers, so I still don't know what's inside. Anyway, this is what the package looks like. I guess I'll show you a closer images. From what I can see, there are silver wings all over the this place. They are on the decorations. They are on the front and back kind of reminds me of Xin Zhao a little bit, but the pattern on top is probably what is part of the leaked Sona. And yes, the pattern actually looks kind of the same. Anyway, after sliding the cover off, well, now you can see the beautiful Demacian sigil on the front, which means I guess it is time to open the stuff the thing up. Also, how the there's another silver wing on the side. I just noticed it. Uh, and there is no latch, so do I just pry it open? I don't want to break this. All right, I immediately noticed something and that's exactly what we have to talk about. So, the last time creators got one of these packages, it was actually um this one. So, yes, the one we got before was actually this giant black rose crate that actually had one of two masks. Some people got the mask of Katarina, that's what I got, and other people got the mask of the target that Katarina was after, aka the guy who was going to be the Darkin. You all watched the lore video on that, didn't you? Anyway, the point is this crate that we got at the very beginning of the year teased the story that's going to be happening throughout the rest of the year. In fact, it was linked to what would be in the cinematic, which takes me back to this because the first thing I can see on the front is the seal and the seal I'm pretty sure is the golden thing that was on Summoner's Rift that I was freaking about. I still don't remember where I saw it, but I but here it is again. And I'm freaking out again. I'm talking about the golden shield that's just there on the pedestal. I still think it is like an old icon of an old Demacian champion, but I went through all the icons and I couldn't find anything. But since I can see a lot of silver wings all around it, uh is it actually Cithria? Because yes, even on the pedestal on the new Summoner's Rift, there is Cithria's sword next to it. It is undeniable that that wooden sword is identical to the one from Legends of Runeterra. It even has the tiny feather on it. Now, inside the box, we do have this, which I'll dive into in a moment, but there is also this. Again, there is the same pattern that was on the Sona, so that's going to be like the Demacian pattern, I guess, for now. But also, is that is that a pin? Can I take it out? Oh, I can. That's not even 3D printed. That's like a cast mold. It's heavy. It's like it's metal. Anyway, what's cool is that this is this is the Demacian sigil, which I think they even wear on the cinematics. I think Garen has one of these on the cloak, even though I think technically on the in-game model it is completely different, but I he either has it in Legends of Runeterra or he had it in the cinematic where he fought Sylas, but they definitely Demacians have it on the cloaks. Although, specifically Crown Guards also have the the raptor on the other side, which is like the golden shield. That's a different sigil, but this specifically I think they have on their cloaks. Regardless, I wouldn't be surprised if we saw this in the cinematic. But now, let's actually have a look at this because this might confirm whoever is the next champion. Okay, so I will definitely show you some pictures because it's going to be awkward to show you guys. So, this is a letter from Prince Jarvan, so let's read it in a very royal way. Defender of Demacia, you have been chosen. In recognition of outstanding merit and valorous conduct, I have chosen you to join my personal retinue of Silverwing Riders. We who take to the skies and protect our capital from all that would assail it. Assail it? Assail it? I have never seen that word. Regardless, first thoughts the next champion is likely a Silverwing Rider, which is incredibly cool because I don't think we've seen anything like that in game before. I'm going to assume it's going to be some kind of a soldier that's like summoning silver wings, but if you're asking who could that be, well, theoretically, we don't know that many named characters [music] who would fall under this, except for one. It's got to be Cithria, isn't it? So, yes, Cithria now has a sword on the Summoner's Rift. There is the same sigil, which by the way, it's up here on the letter as well. And even though Cithria's lore is kind of all over the place because she has an entire storyline, at some point she will end up among the Silverwing Riders. On top of this, let's not forget that there was also a Cithria being developed for TFT, although that one was likely canceled because it couldn't fit into the set. And so, the traces for Cithria specifically are now all over the place. Someone should go check if {slash}r{slash}CithriaMains exists. Anyway, we continue. Enclosed in the crest of the great city, given to those who champion our virtue in times of peace and of war. From the Dauntless Vanguards to the Fearless Rangers, which that's a reference to Quinn. All who defend Demacia and deliver her justice bear this emblem. So, yes, that's going to be the emblem that I just showed you. Your oath, should you accept this honor and duty. Oh, I'm technically reading the oath now. Wait, hold on. I'm the Silverwing Rider now. I pledge to protect my people and my kingdom, for we are one. So long as I stand, our hope will not falter. So long as I breathe, our dream will not die. In a world of darkness, I defend the light. And by my will, our beacon shall burn ever bright. For Demacia, now and forever. It is no idle vow. In a few days, my father, the king, will call a special assembly of Silverwing Riders in the great city. I will accept you there to join our watch. May honor lift your wings and justice guide your lands. Prince Jarvan the Fourth. So, yes, while I did technically read the oath and I am a rider now, the truth is that this should be a canonical letter that was sent to someone. And now, we should be speculating who that is. But again, given everything that's happening in the current state, there is a really good chance it is going to be Cithria. Because let's think about it. There are some people who think that maybe Tianna Crownguard could become a new champion. The issue is that Tianna Crownguard was never pictured as a Demacian rider anywhere. There was also a reference to Quinn in this letter and the Rangers, which that's the whole part of her group that was shown in Legends of Runeterra. But again, none of those would be qualified as Silverwing Riders. We can also go all the way back to the Garen novella and even there even though that story was about Cithria and her entire group in the Dauntless Vanguard, there were some really cool characters, including a minotaur. That guy is my favorite in the entire group. There is also a part about Cithria in the old realms book. Then there are also the multiple short stories about her, but in none of those do we have a Silverwing Rider, if we're not talking about Cithria specifically. Which means yes, Riot just confirmed one of two things. Either they are coming up with a brand new character, which is someone we have not seen before, or they are doing what people really want Riot to do, focus on the ocean of side characters you already have in your universe. Because some of those characters are really interesting. And if that is the case, then yes, I do believe Cithria is pretty much confirmed. But there is one more thing that Riot could do. You see, Cithria's character is kind of all about the character development. And I mean, literally, this is not something that Riot is just doing with any character. With Cithria specifically, we know how she starts, we know what happens to her, we know how she develops, and we know where she ends up. In fact, going back to the canceled Cithria in TFT, there we had a child Cithria and an adult Cithria. She basically had multiple forms because again, the entire journey of Cithria is kind of her staple. So, what if Riot is now teasing Cithria, but we are not getting the Cithria as a Silverwing Rider until we get to the end of this year? What if Cithria is going to be the Zaunite of the new season? That would be a really cool payoff at the end of the year. But, the issue there would be that we would have a Demacian season without a very well-known Demacian. And it wouldn't be until the season 3 of this year that we would get something interesting. So, despite this being a cool idea, I don't think Riot is going to go for it. And if anything, for the fourth time in this video, I think we are going to get Cithria. And I am ready to eat my word because tomorrow the cinematic will arrive. And Garen is already on the cover. With Garen being very close to Cithria, League of Legends is totally getting a new poster girl.

titre: What Makes A Champion Popular? | League Of Legends
transcription: Being a competitive game, the popularity of a champion in League is closely correlated to their practical efficiency. The natural motivation when playing ranked is to try to win more games than you lose. Therefore, it's in your best interest to prioritize picks that statistically have a higher chance of achieving that outcome. I understand when race statistics are largely inconsequential since you have to know how to play the champion. But whenever someone ends up with like a 54% win rate, there's a pretty good chance you'll have an elevated success rate when playing them. Correspondingly, champions that overperform get played more often, while those who underperform tend to get played less often. In spite of that, there's a number of champions in League that, no matter what the meta or their win rate or whatever, consistently find a pick rate in the high single digits to even up to 14 to 15% all year round. I've covered a few of them individually in my why everyone plays series, but I wanted to talk about always popular champions collectively in today's video because there are a number of similarities among all of them, regardless of their class or role, that are worth documenting. So, let's get started. League is one of the few competitive games out there that extensively documents and records the performances of champions. So much so that there must be upwards of a dozen websites aggregating data on every possible metric in the game. You have OPG, UGG, Lolytics, etc., etc. They even go so far as to make third party apps like Pearl Offessor, Mobile, what have you, that give you endless amounts of information on how well a champion is doing. Gold GG also tracks proplay statistics. Essentially, every single aspect of this game gets tracked. League of Grass even measures down a team's chances of winning based on what combination of dragons they get. Thoroughness would be an understatement. With all this data given to us in the sheer sample size of games, any player worth their salt would be severely kneecapping themselves by not using it as a point of reference. And that in and of itself has influence on player decision-making. If people see a champion with a 55% win rate and a 10% pick rate, even if they themselves have never confronted that champion, they might just take the stats at face value and target ban them. Taking these two factors, a player's desire to win and advance in rank and the extensive and exhaustive access to champion data and statistics if we exclude personal enjoyment and mastery. Logically, why wouldn't people just pick the ones with the highest win rate? It's because there's one metric that people value more than any amount of data can measure up to. And it's also the one metric that can't really be physically quantified, agency. Agency denotes your ability to change or influence the outcome of something. For example, if someone's being a jerk to you in game, you have agency in the situation by muting them. In the context of the game itself, if the enemy Lulu is getting a little too comfortable harassing you from a distance, you as a Blitzcrank player can yanker into your team and punish her for doing so. On a broader scale, agency is often associated with pressure. Are you able to exert pressure on the map and influence the game to bring you and your team a victory? It's this metric that leads to some champions being consistently popular throughout the player base, even if all data points to them being a supposed bad champion in the current metag game. Quite a few of league's most popular picks ironically have poor win rates, too. One of the games most consistently popular marksmen, Ezreal, has almost never had a 50% win rate or higher. And yet, historically, he's almost always had a double-digit pick rate, or in many instances, a 20 to 30% pick rate. It all ties back to the agency he and the others have. An easy way to explain would be to actually go over champions who are consistently unpopular as there's a lot more of them than there are popular ones. For comparison's sake, let's talk about Kog'Ma. Kog'ma has perennially suffered from lackluster participation despite empirically being one of the highest win rate ADCs in the game. Why exactly is that? As I'm sure you're aware, Kog'ma is regarded by many to be the machine gun ADC, capable of outputting extreme amounts of damage by way of his pension for on effects. He can extend his attack range to an amount far exceeding most other marksmen. And with each attack applying on it effects, not the least of which being his own percent max health damage, he's able to shred through enemy teams at record time. The caveat is that he's also one of the most vulnerable marksman in the game, lacking any escape or defensive options of any kind. The only thing he can do in his own power is control space through voidus. But in the era of hypermobility, we're in that's kind of a moot point. As such, Kog'ma is extremely dependent on his teammates to compensate for his inability to move around very quickly or protect himself. So, even though he can potentially out DPS the majority of other marksmen in the game, experientially, Kog'ma has more agency on account of there being numerous things the player has little control over. For every one game where they get to myize the enemy team, there are nine games where they get run over by the jungler or the enemy assassin and explode. Relying on four random players to make up for something you're missing doesn't make for a tenable climbing strategy. Too much of Kog'ma's performance hinges on circumstances that are out of your control. The only reason he has a high win rate is because people only play him when it's an easy win or they're just so good at Kog'ma that they're the exception to the statistic, not the rule. How does Cesio compare in this regard? For one, he's one of the highest agency marksman in the game. And it's all thanks to the way in which he's designed. being the emblematic example of skill shots in this game. As's main way of attacking is dealing damage through mystic shot, doubling as a long-range empowered basic attack due to its ability to apply on it effects and on attack effects. The extremely long distance coverage of his skew and W afford him one of the widest threat ranges in the game even among his peers. Not to mention the consistent output he contributes. It's the generous safety margin Ezreal has that addresses the most important question governing agency in League. Can I do something? Esio's extensive tenure dating all the way back from his release to now is due in part to him always being able to do something no matter what the situation. Long-range physical attack and sure he's able to pose a constant threat to the enemy team since no one wants to just stand there and take a bunch of damage. Just his presence alone gives his team a strong neutral game. The scaling of mystic shot combined with items like Triforce make it so every Q will chunk you for a couple hundred damage. And if your team doesn't have a tank or a modicum of sustained, eventually he can push you guys back and give his team the freedom to advance. At 1,200 units, even champions with the dash will have trouble getting in range of him. With most combat mobility tools only offering 6 to 800 units of distance, you would need to commit most of your resources just to get within striking range of him as a melee. To an extent, even range champions have to find a way to get close to him. Ordinarily, those of the wide pressure range have only that to fall back on, meaning if the enemy team can close the distance, neutral focused champions can't do a whole lot to bail themselves out. Ezreal, however, can thanks to Arcane Shift, essentially being flashed on a basic ability. Long-distance combat in tandem with a way to escape immediate pressure offers him not only a strong safety margin but a way for him to always contribute something to the situation. People like having the ability to do something, especially ADC mates. Ezreal was popular for so long for that very purpose. When looking through any champion belonging to this category, you can find some semblance of persistent agency amongst all of them. Up in top lane, you had champions like Renekton, Atrox, and Darius. Common staple encounters every top laner should be prepared for. What makes them so popular? To start, they have a strong laning phase. Each of them gets hardc countered in one way or another, but generally speaking, they're not as prone to getting scooed over in lane compared to most others. Then, when transitioning into the mid game, that's when they can really start doing stuff. Each of them can serve as a raid boss, wherein you have to focus them down, or else they can and will eventually rip through your team. But by focusing them down, that makes it harder for you to go after the rest of their team. Renekton's ability to 100 to zero a target with the stun makes it so even if he explodes, he gets to take someone down with him. Darius having built-in percent armor penetration and getting a mountain of AD from his passive means you have to respect a full stack Darius no matter how far ahead or behind he is. He trucks a strong battle healing and area wide disruption make him a sizable threat through sheer base damage alone high enough to allow him to easily destroy squishy enemy backlines with a couple sweet spot cues. Much like how Estiel can avoid enemy pressure while comfortably exerting his own, the three affforementioned top laners have a consistent means of applying pressure in addition to responding to pressure, giving them a stable pick rate all year round. Jungle has a number of these as well. The two obvious examples in particular would no doubt be Leen and Viego. Leon's one of the most consistently popular champions in League of Legends history because he has everything a jungler would be expected to have. Being a strong early game pick is alone half the battle. What a jungler can accomplish in the early game has immense bearing on how the mid to late A game plays out. Applicably speaking, Leon has two free target dashes, giving him a multitude of ways to gank all three lanes without the enemy laner having to be overextended. He has a respectable clear thanks to Tempest doing strong AoE damage and iron will giving sustain. And he has enough base damage to reliably take on most other junglers in skirmishes. Though Leon does eventually fall off in the late game, his high tempo in concert with his trademark ultimate always gives him something to do. by extension, always giving the person playing him a feeling of agency in affecting how the game plays out. Vieo is actually popular for the opposite reason. He's not the most imposing early gamer, and his ganks are not game-changing, but what he does have is the ability to turn the enemy team's own power against them, a trait that can be exercised while ahead or behind. If he's ahead, sniping out enemies one by one adds more fuel to his already overwhelming advantage, letting him add the enemy team's power to his own. Meanwhile, if he's behind, he can compensate for what he lacks by stealing the enemy team's power instead, effectively implying he's always going to be a threat no matter what. Even his base kit makes him a fairly strong duelist. Passive current health damage on his queue and rapid on it effect application gives him fantastic dueling power. Further, the execution on heartbreaker necessitates that you be careful dying around him, even if he's the weakest member on the enemy team. Vieo can always find a way back into the game when he's doing poorly, and when he's doing well, he can basically mess around limit testing. Mid lane tends to fluctuate a lot more, but by this point you should be able to notice the trend here. Two examples we're talking about are Silas and Yon. In the case of Silus, the situation very much resembles that of Vieos. Being able to steal enemy ultimates and use them as his own opens up the door for countless different roles he can take. If they have a Nautilus or Malight on his team, Silus can be the engager. If there's a Terra Kindred, Silus can pull off a clutch team fight save and mid to late him. If there's a Pantheon Twisted Fate, Silus now has a global teleport to go anywhere he wants. If there's a Trinameir or Alistar, Silus can front line for his team. If even one champion on the enemy team has a worthwhile ultimate for him to steal, Silus will always have the agency to do something for his team. In some cases, he can even use the ultimate he steals better than the original owner can. For instance, any ultimate that has a high AP ratio, but its owner isn't known to build AP like Hecrim or Nessus. Similarly to Viego, his base kits also quite practical. Being a rush down champion with two gap closers and all of his attacks having strong AP scaling makes it so the enemy backline still has to anticipate the possibility of a silus blitzing them and taking them down with them. If that team happens to consist of many worthwhile ultimates, Silus will always be a threat. Turning the enemy strength against them is something few in this game can remotely do. Then we had the vein of my existence. Yona might be the prime example of an always popular champion. Man has like a 46% win rate at the moment which by most people's standards would make him complete garbage. And yet he still is one of the most played mid laners in the game. Granted, he's played top and mid, so that's kind of misleading, but overall, people enjoy playing Yona, and it's not hard to see why. Some of the highest DPS skilling in the game by virtue of getting full crit chance, and the power spike attached to it with a mere two items. He can also tap into on hit effects and the benefits that come with that, like Blade of the Ruin King. Having three dashes, two of which carry AoE knockups grant him both backline access and team fight pressure, affording him plenty of freedom to do stupid stuff in the mental game. People like playing him for the simple reason that he's always able to do something. Even if he runs it down early game through his combination of high late game scaling and playmaking potential, he can assuredly still pull something off. As you can see, all these champions can do stuff at a bare minimum level. Or they have access to an alternate win condition to pursue, even if it doesn't always translate to a path to victory and may even do more harm than good to the player by choosing to pick the champion in question despite statistically being subpar. What matters more to people than win rates or stats is agency or at the very least the feeling of agency. The feeling that you can do something or work towards something that people go after. For almost any champion with a year-long pick rate of like 7% or higher, you hear them being characterized by the same things. The four horsemen of top lane all have good playmaking potential, high adaptability, fantastic solo carry ability courtesy of their ability to make plays around the map, alternative win conditions to go for, and a decently strong laning face. Mid laners like Zen, Ari, Silus, Yasul, they get picked a lot for having the agency of taking matters into their own hands and a consistent baseline of strength. AD carries like Kaisa, Jin, Jinx, Ezreal. For the likes of Kai'Sa, she's got that 1v9 playmaking potential through her hybrid damage, percent missing health damage, long-range dash, and fast repositioning. Jin and Ezreal can play back and fire from people from long distance. Jinx can fall behind super early, but hypers snowball in the late game off a single kill. Supports like Na'Vi, Lulu, Thresh, and Nautilus carry utility and/or crowd control indispensable just by their very functionality. In their case, they have multiple of them. Some champions might be able to excel in certain aspects that lead to a higher win rate overall. For instance, up in top lane, Warwick is one of the most broken cheese picks of all time, but it never catches mainstream attention and usage because whenever the cheese doesn't work, you feel way more useless than if you just picked a conventional top laner. In essence, always popular champions are constantly played quite literally due to copium, like actually copium. The feeling that you can always do something whether or not it actually is reality. People instilled mid because the prospect of living out that fantasy is dialing all over people, dip duck dodging around, one-shotting everyone is so addictive, people go for that even though they can realistically achieve more success playing a simple mid laner like Annie or a Malahar and being a stunbot. Above all else though, the champions are fun to play. And I suppose ultimately no amount of wins can measure up to just enjoying the game. What stops the game from being fun is frustration, and what causes frustration is loss of agency. So even if it means losing more than you win, having agency is what matters most. What do you guys think about always popular champions? Do you agree or disagree with my points? I'd like to hear your thoughts in the comments down below. That'll be all for today. So if you enjoyed the video, I'd appreciate it if you left a like and subscribed. Don't forget to follow me on Twitter, Vars, join my Discord server, and check out my other champion discussion videos if you haven't yet. Until next time, thanks so much for watching, and I look forward to seeing you again soon. Take care. [Music] [Applause] [Music]

titre: Yone Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription:
Yone is an assassin returned from the dead
after a duel gone wrong. His killer? None other than his little bro, Yasuo. Now Yone's back in the land of the living
with an evil spirit-mask attached to his face, which he uses to hunt down demons and expel
them from their mortal hosts. So, let's meet the man behind the mask. Welcome to the Yone Champion Spotlight. For his passive, Yone wields two weapons for,
you guessed it, two types of damage. Every other auto attack deals split damage -
half physical, half magic, all Yone. [Yone] Walk both realms. Way of the Hunter also doubles Yone's crit
chance, but reduces his crit damage. Sort of like his brother half, Yasuo. Yone's Mortal Steel also works a lot like
Yasuo's Steel Tempest—But Yone's the older sibling here, so who's really steel-ing from
whom? Yone strikes forward, damaging enemies and gaining a stack of Gathering Storm on
hit. After two stacks the storm is unleashed, and
Yone dashes forward with a gust of wind, knocking enemies up in the air like he just don't care. Then, if he's ready to run like the wind,
he can activate Mortal Steel to dash over walls and slay his prey all day, Yone. Yone slashes forward with Spirit Cleave, dealing
a percentage of his target's max health as split damage and shielding himself on hit. Attacking minions or lone champions activates
Yone's base shield - but, if you're cleave-r enough, you can use Spirit Cleave on multiple champions for some added protection. With Soul Unbound, Yone enters spirit form
for a short time, leaving his untargetable body behind, and gaining ramping movement
speed to hunt down and eliminate foes. Once he returns, champions he damaged while
in spirit form take an additional burst. Use Soul Unbound to hop over small walls for
added mobility, but be careful where you leave your body lying around. Whether you wait the full duration or reactivate
early, eventually you'll have to snap back to reality—and sometimes, reality hurts. [Yone] Another... failure. Yone's ultimate damages all enemies along
a path. Then, he blinks behind the last champion struck
and knocks all enemies Airborne towards him— the perfect setup for one killer combo. While his ultimate has some serious range,
Yone will only blink as far as the last champion he actually hits. If you hit no champions, you'll go the distance
and travel the max range of his ult, which could put him in danger. Casting Soul Unbound before Fate Sealed lets
you blink through walls and close the gap between you and your fleeing prey without
putting yourself in too much danger if it goes wrong. [Yone] What does one do with an abundance
of masks? The laning phase is all about small advantages. Win early 1v1s by priming Mortal Steel's knock-up,
then using Soul Unbound to go in for the trade and pull back to safety. Or, activate Soul Unbound during larger fights
for a true out-of-body experience. Remember, Yone is undying to wreak havoc when
he's in spirit form, so you'll want to deal as much damage as possible for as long as possible, then snap back to your body to finish off foes. Both Spirit Cleave and Mortal Steel scale
with attack speed. For a shorter cast time and cooldown, build
Yone fast and furious and you'll be sending your foes six feet under with time to spare. Focus AD carries in mid to late game team fights, using Soul Unbound to dive the enemy back line. And finally, if you're willing to set sibling
rivalry aside, team up with Yasuo to pull off the ultimate Bro-ne wombo combo. Send enemies skyward with Fate Sealed and let your biological brother in arms follow up with Last Breath. You know what they say: the family that plays
together slays together. [Yone] Forgiveness is... complicated. Well, that's Yone unmasked. Nothing? Really? Man, who died? Get into the spirit and learn more about this
double-edged lord at the links in the description.

other skins but aatrox is just a car aatrox is so similar to the new victorious skin that it looks like a big act of laziness from riot finally when begging and begging for reverts kog'maw Mane's and rengar Mane's got there old abilities back LeBlanc Mane's and re Mane's got there old abilities back too and even some recent changes to Shaco have proven that eventually they will listen to you but he's had an incredibly cool kit after the rework too and I personally love the rework but that doesn't mean that other players shouldn't be heard just because I like the rework and he plays very titre: Sylas Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription:
A prisoner turned revolutionary, Sylas is
ready to seize the enemy’s power - then break them with it. He’s a mage that prefers to beat down opponents
up close, stringing together a series of powerful strikes and bringing foes to their knees with
their own ultimates. It’s time for a revolution - welcome to
the Sylas Champion Spotlight. Sylas uses his Q to lash out with his chains,
damaging and slowing enemies. After a brief delay, the intersection of the
Chain Lash detonates, applying an increased slow and damaging enemies again. The best way to overpower enemies is with
his W, Kingslayer. Sylas lunges at a target with a powerful strike,
damaging them and healing himself. If the enemy is at low health, the damage
is increased; if Sylas is at low health, the heal is increased. It’s an extremely powerful finishing tool
to end any champion’s reign. Sylas has a two-part E ability. With Abscond, Sylas dashes a short distance
and shields himself. For a short time after, he can recast the
ability as Abduct to whip out his chains, stunning, damaging, and pulling himself to
the first enemy hit. Sylas’ passive is a whirling chain effect
that happens whenever he attacks after using an ability, kinda like Sheen. To make the most of this Petricite Burst,
pummel enemies every chance you get - link together basic attacks between each spellcast
and whiplash to rack up the shack-kills. Sylas is here for revolution, and he will
use his enemies’ powers against them - literally. His ultimate Hijacks an enemy’s ult for
his own use, which Sylas can then hold onto and cast exactly as if he were that champion. While Sylas can take an enemy’s ultimate
whenever Hijack is ready (even if that ability is on cooldown), he has to wait to steal from
the same target again. How long before he can Hijack them again is roughly determined by their ultimate’s base cooldown. His talent for grand theft ult-o means Sylas
gets max charges on champs with ammo... and completely chain-ges into champions that have
a transform ability. No matter what their ultimate does, Sylas
will find a way to use and abuse it. By late game, his low cooldown lets him take
multiple ultimates in quick succession - as long as they’re from different enemies. Here he snags Rumble’s Equalizer to even
the odds at the Baron pit. Unfortunately, he whiffs on Syndra, but he
still gets the Sy-last hit to finish her off. He’s not done yet, though - with Alistar’s
ult, the Unshackled becomes the Unbreakable, leaping back into the fight to turn steer
into steak. Moo, chick, get out the way. Each situation calls for a different strategy. Sometimes it’s best to take an ult that
helps out your comrades or has a big impact on teamfights. Other times, you just need a single finishing
move like Last Caress to whip a single foe. Gnarly. Sylas’ ult makes him a Hi-jack of all trades,
so no matter what you find yourself up against, if you play smart, you can beat champs at
their own game and kick Xer-ath. With Sylas, no fight plays out the same way
twice. While he defaults to a bruiser play pattern,
his ult-stealing potential means every skirmish brings something new. Improvise, adapt, and overcome: make the most
of what’s available and turn your enemy’s power into their demise. If you want a magical bruiser that wins with
brain and brawn, join up with Sylas and start an insurrection. For more info on the Unshackled, break out
to the links below.

time expecting the enemy team will send all resources of Manpower they can muster to make your life as difficult as possible there's an extremely prevalent belief among toplan is to hope for the best but absolutely prepare for the worst this is their primary way of carrying games by drawing as much pressure as they can so that it doesn't go to the rest of their teammates what separates the Challenger top laners from the hard suuck silver twos is whether or not the player crumbles under that pressure or manages to break through in spite of it with this in mind the major category of picks and top Ling consist of Lane boys Champions known for a fairly dominant early game having an even or more often positive match up against most of the top laners even amongst themselves this is complemented by an equally strong if not stronger m game letting them take any advantage of they Garner in lane and pressing that lead even further elsewhere as you may know because top Lanes often left alone relative to the other two lanes occasionally the outcome of the role hinge is solely on matchups as evident by top laners constantly requesting to be last and champ select and the reason most top laners a playing on the blue side just as the Four Horseman Cil Fiora Riven and dellia dominate the RO through their ability to overpower the competition through skill and execution The Four Horsemen atro Theus Jack and Renekton exhibit comparable traits one could even go as far as to say they thoroughly enjoy fighting multiple opponents at once with each of them carrying something in their toolkit that becomes more efficient when battling more than just one person and while they don't exactly possess the same extreme Side Lane and 1 V1 pressure with the exception of one of them they make up for that by being easily among the most destructive forces in team fights and skirmishes forcing the enemy team to address them first and foremost L they risk getting annihilated by their extreme damage output at the same time taking them out demands a superhuman effort especially when behind as they have far more conventional resilience than the four horsemen who can withstand quite a bit of punishment to be fair but certainly nowhere close to their male counterparts such effort often results in the enemy team exhausting more resources than they can afford to give which makes finishing them off an elementary task for the rest of your team The Four Horsemen at top Lan also share the distinction of being among the most difficult if not outright impossible Champions to make a comeback against once you fall behind even in the off chance you're playing someone with the potential to out scale them if piloted by a capable player these guys will never let you have that chance bullying you for the next 10 to 15 minutes and then exerting their lead against the rest of your team I should clarify that this category is notably more ambiguous than the four horsemen for them Glen is about the only interchangeable member whereas for the four horsemen they sometimes have to share their SE with other bruers or bulky Toppers including gante morer Garen and so on however I chose these four specifically for their consistency as they tend to withand shifts in The Meta more effectively than others so having established all that let's go through each of them one at a time to elucidate why they're so good starting with arguably the most widely played of the four atro prevailing wisdom understands that there's no such thing as a truly save blind pick in Top Lane or any Lane for that matter everyone has at least a handful of poor matchups but atro's popularity stems from the fact that he's probably the closest you can get to a truly save blind pick even against losing matchups he has a right set of abilities to survive Lane with minimal losses atro's greatest strength has to be without a doubt his neutral game most melee Champions are known for having a lack luster neutral due to their short attack range with some of them being unable to exert pressure of any kind with that retaliation hrock stands out for being a melee Champion with actual range the first two casts of his dark AR and blade Out cast ranges of 625 and 475 to 500 respectively being far enough for him to attack his opponent without getting struck back whatsoever is especially when you account for the brief knockup that comes with it and his ability to reposition himself with Dum roll Dash during the cast executed correctly atrox can repeatedly strike you a cues to chip away you while minimizing his own exposure said neutral pressure is accentuated by the absence of any cost to his abilities but more importantly the substantial battle healing is inly equipped to it if you let him get a few too many pokes you'll find yourself gradually bleeding out in HP while he suddenly regenerates back to full being a manaless champion with range and sustain makes for a very effective combination that can allow him to deal with all manner of Champions comfortably at worst but safety isn't the only thing he's known for assuming to land a sweet spot on all three attacks dark and blad's total damage output reaches a staggering 420 base plus 5 40% total ad as of 14.10 the highest ratios in the game on a single ability I believe Granton easier said than done particularly with the mobility creep we've been experiencing as of late but he doesn't have to necessarily land all three to threaten your life atro can slowly Whittle you down then when he feels confident he can finish you off in one rotation he can do so however assuming the atro player mixes up their timing positioning and whatnot they can make it very difficult to tell whether they're committing to an Allin or just going for poke damage outside of Lane atro's team fire pressure is almost Second To None becoming a literal raid boss especially with his ultimate thanks to the massive boost in ad and increased healing his Q can dish out damage in the thousands while healing him equally as much and with worldender extending his duration upon takedown once he gets a Kill he can tear through everyone else people greatly underestimate how strong this guy becomes too at rank three world enter damage boost is 45% ad I've seen many games where if F A TRS had over 700 AD causing his SK to deal potentially over 4,000 damage in total per Target the challenge of course lies in whether you can actually play him correctly and of the Four Horsemen he is the steepest learning curve but there's a reason at TR has a consistent near double digit pick rate he's just a very solid champion who can account for many situations and is one of the few quote unquote melee top laners you can play extremely safe Next Top laner is Darius a Cornerstone a Top Lane owing to being the most straightforward of the for to play an historical Lane bully known for being the bane of many tanks' existence in no small part thanks to his any tank busting capabilities and high base damage the number one rule of thumb when fighting a daries is to never take him head on he possesses one of the best allins of all time that even if you manage to avoid a q or two he still has enough damage to kill you twice over upon reaching five stacks of hemorrhage it gains a massive boost in ad and the ability to instantly apply five bleed stacks on any Target like the subsequent ones which in another Dove does very significant damage the difference between a base deries and noite daries is like Super Saiyan Goku versus base Goku in addition to his basic abilities dealing increased damage his ultimate grows stronger the more bleed Stacks are on a Target even in the early game a full power noxian Guillotine can do about 600 true damage which is around 40 to 50% of most top lers Health pulls by that the challenge of fighting darus is that like I said most top laners are known for having a board neutral game some are outright unable to exert pressure without giv giving their Lane opponent the same opportunity even a simple trade combo of Auto W reset and Q can chunk you a fairly decent amount and with this passive armor penetration it's hard to really mitigate his damage assuming the dar's player is good at Landing EXC it's similar to atro where he can chip away you while recovering from any damage you deal to him against mainly champions of low mobility and no sustain of their own Darius can be an extremely hard counter but even against Champions that look like they could be counters he has some needs to force interactions and likely come out on top the real threat of this Champion though is towards the mid to aing once he acquires more base damage and durability the probability of him activating noxia M gets even higher and at level 16 his ultimate damage becomes so great that all it takes is a single five stack bleed application from W or q and then ultimate to literally one- shot squishy Champions so just like atrox if he scores a reset it's in your best interest to run the hell away from him as he becomes exponentially more dangerous to deal with if there's ever a champion who embodies the notion of kill him first or else he kills your entire team it would be Darius ironically us a front line to hold him down is a bad idea as it makes it easy for him to Prime his stacks and as I mentioned with how much armor penetration and true damage he has dar's ging quick work of even beefy Champions and then turn his rage on to you counterintuitive as it may sound the best way to stop him is with full damage taking him out before he reaches that legendary backload of pressure he's known for an equal combination of strength and sturdiness that few members of the roster are capable of dealing with directly third Horseman is Jax who ostensibly differs greatly from the other three as he's a skirmisher rather than a fighter but his strengths are comparable in many respects while his colleagues prefer to distribute their strength equally between abilities and auto attacks Jax is almost entirely Auto attack based consequently that gives them the highest sustained damage out of the four melee Auto attackers almost universally have bad neutral games in light of them needing to get really close to you to attack but what allows Jax to get away with murder is Counter-Strike rendering him impervious to basic attacks of any kind which many to liners are dependent on even his own fellow Horsemen Counterstrike also reduces AOE damage by 25% giving him a token bit of defense against most damaging abilities during those 2 seconds of evasion he can really attack as he pleases and then can decide whether he wants to use that 1 second stun to retreat to safety or to press the advantage not unlike Darius Jax is one of those Champions where long drawn out fights are heavily discouraged with his passive granting and bonus attack speed with every successive Auto attack along with Grandmaster at Arms empowering his autos and granting bonus armor and Magic resist he can rip through just about everyone in the game the best way to fight jaacks is ideally to never engage him at all and just pooke him down which is not an option for most top laners once you reach the mtil a game his incredible dueling power only continues to amplify giving him s lane pressure level with the four horse limit exemplifying his constant pressure Jax is one of those Champions who while obviously stronger with his ultimate doesn't really need it to fight the difference between Jax with and without his ultimate isn't as measurable as other Champions but that also means this guy will always be a threat Counter-Strike is one of the most overpowered basic abilities in the game a massive vied Champions will rely even remotely on auto attacks while at the same time being an auto attacker himself he can make excellent use of either on hit effect items or Bruiser ones whichever is more convenient for him once you make it to laking this guy can have 380 2.5 attack speed at least before they took out lethal Tempo and 300 armor and Magic resist making him the ultimate fighting machine admittedly he doesn't have nearly the same raid boss pressure as his Pierce but if he had that on top of everything else that would be excessive final Horseman is Renekton another staple on top laner for very apparent reasons like hro Reon a mano is Champion with the ability to sustain himself courtesy of his poent stun on ruthless Predator he also has a similar trade pattern as Jack where he can go in stun his Target deal damage and then get back out or continue attacking if he so chooses proper Fury management and situational awareness can make him one of the deadliest champions in the early game as he has a low coold down AOE damaging ability that if it strikes a champion can heal for a decent amount while having a 1.5 second point and click stun essentially guaranteeing he can land all his abilities on you while renekton's all in pressure is definitely the weakest of the four it's unwise to believe you can take him on in Long sustained battles without a fight his Fury generation increases below half HP while Dominus grants a huge boost of health and a magic damage aura that can actually deal an insane amount of damage if he stand in it long enough being a manaless champion with a strong burst rotation even in the early game Renekton can make your Landing phase a living Health if you so much as give him an inch he's one of those champions of the right set of abilities to handle many situations whether or not he's the most optimal at them for example C and dice gives him fairly good mobility in chasing downrange Champions or anyone far enough away while also being equipped with up to a 35% AOE armor strike he has a notably far burst range compared to even the likes of Jacks and having four damaging abilities makes him a terror in skirmishes and team fights Renekton loves nothing more than a cluster since if he applies an Empower q and dice onto all Targets not only does he deal a metric ton of damage but he also heals a ton and shreds everyone's armor making the enemy team squishier for him and other physical attackers on his team I think that's the main factor why so many people like to play him he has the Simplicity of a Bruiser but the skill expressiveness of a skirmisher now he does fall off harder than his fellow Horsemen but even towards a late game he's still a threat thanks to his inate strength and durability you still have to respect what he can do since worst case scenario he can always take someone down with them by stunning and killing them that's why he's so popular in Pro play you really can't put a price on a point and click 1.5 second stun there's a reason most point and click stuns are only 1 second at most if it were any higher it would be broken overall The Four Horsemen are almost identical to the four horsen in that they're very stable independent and reliable picks while Fior Camille aelia and Riven are much more dominant in the Apex Ste atro Renekton darus and Jacks dominate almost every ELO in the game and they'll continue doing so being a Renekton co-main though I do wish they would stop nerfing him just because he's popular in Pro play but uh that's a discussion for another time anyways let me know your thoughts on the four horsemen in Top Lane along with if you think someone else deserves to be part of this category more than one of them I know many are thinking about including gante but in his defense he's still a very new Champion relative to these for so I feel like at best he would be an honorable mention kind of like how Gwen is for the four horsemen in any case if you if you enjoyed the video a like And subscribe would be very much appreciated don't forget to follow me on Twitter at barsam join my Discord server and if you haven't be sure to check out my video on the four horse Women on Top Lane if you haven't yet but till next time thanks so much for watching and I look forward to seeing you again soon take care [Music] titre: Kayn Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription:
This is Kayn, the Shadow Reaper. Kayn's not alone, the scythe he wields is possessed by a darkin named Rhaast. Each time Kayn steps onto the Rift he has
to fight Rhaast for control. Inevitably, one will consume the other. You choose the victor. Welcome to the Kayn Champion Spotlight. Kayn's passive is The Darkin Scythe. At point during each game, Kayn transforms into either the Shadow Assassin or the Darkin. For Kayn to conquer and destroy Rhaast he must become the Shadow Assassin by damaging ranged champions. Meanwhile, fighting melee champions will empower Rhaast, which enables him to take over Kayn's body and transform into the Darkin. Once either form has won, return to base to attain your final form. This process will amplify most of your active abilities as well as replace The Darkin Scythe passive with an entirely new one based on the form you took. The Shadow Assassin's new passive deals significant bonus damage during the first few seconds of combat with champions while the Darkin heals for a chunk of all ability damage dealt to champions. No matter which form wins, you can simply wait a few minutes to choose the loser instead. Kayn's Q is Reaping Slash. Kayn dashes then swings his scythe in a circle. Both the dash and slash damage all enemies they hit. This ability deals bonus damage to monsters. If Rhaast wins the struggle, Reaping Slash deals bonus percent-health damage against all enemies except minions. Kayn's W is Blade's Reach. Kayn rears back and slashes in a line, damaging
and slowing all struck enemies. If Kayn becomes the Shadow Assassin, the range
is increased and a living shadow will strike for him, allowing Kayn to move freely after casting. If the Darkin is in control, Blade's Reach will knock up its foes. Kayn's E is Shadow Step, which grants a burst of movement speed and the ability to walk through any terrain for a few moments. When he first enters terrain, he'll receive
a heal and significantly extend the duration of Shadow Step. However, the extended duration won't be
granted if Kayn's in combat with enemy champions. As a Shadow Assassin, Kayn gains additional
movement speed and immunity to slows when activating Shadow Step. Kayn: I am everywhere. Nearby enemy champions will be alerted of Kayn's presence while he's inside the terrain. Because the base duration of Shadow
Step is rather short, make sure you're close enough to a wall to actually phase through and extend the duration. When you plan out your ganks as Kayn, think
about how you can use Shadow Step to invent creative jungle and ganking routes. You have completely unique pathing as Kayn
and learning that is extremely important. Even the Rift itself isn't big enough to hold
back the Shadow Reaper. Kayn's ultimate is Umbral Trespass. When activated, Kayn leaps inside an enemy
champion he's recently damaged. While Kayn's ghost-riding his whip, he's untargetable
and has the option to jump out early. Upon exiting his host, he deals massive damage
to the target. In Shadow Assassin form, the cast range is
increased and it refreshes the bonus damage from his passive. As a Darkin, Umbral Tresspass's base damage
is replaced with max health damage and heals Kayn based on his target's max health as well. Basically, use it to chomp on tanks. When you Trespass into an enemy, it's like
they're a train and you're the world's scariest freighthopper, you go wherever they go. And then you kill the train. Kayn: Even Zed would be impressed. Regardless of which form you gain access to
first, think about which of Kayn's transformations will benefit your team the most during the
mid to late game. Does the enemy team have a lot of squishy
carries you can burst? If so, go assassin. Or, if they have big bruisers and your team is lacking crowd control, consider choosing the darkin's slayer. As Kayn, you're the only champ in the game that can fully change roles in the middle of a game. Choose wisely. The Shadow Assassin excels at flanking and
picking off high priority targets when they least expect it. Use Blades Reach to engage, then dive in
with Reaping Slash and dodge their big abilities with your ultimate. Kayn: There was a time to run, but you missed it. The Darkin is, just like in his lore, real
strong against assassins. Between his built-in sustain and crowd control
abilities, the demonic force is extremely hard to kill. When the situation gets tight, you can heal
up with a carefully timed trespass, then knockup your pursuers and slip away into a wall. Thanks for tuning into the Kayn Champion Spotlight [DEMON PHREAK] Click the links below
 or I'll eat your soul

titre: Guide du Noob | Épisode 2 : Choisir son Champion | League of Legends
transcription: league of legends propose de nombreux champions et on peut parfois se sentir perdu mais que ce soit pour son apparence larmes ou encore l'élément qui l'utilisent vous pourrez trouver le champion qui correspond à vos enfants mais la raison principale dans le choix des champions est bien entendu le style de jeu de chacun d'eux il existe six catégories de champion les assassins les tanks les combattants les mages les tireurs et les supports [Musique] doté d'une grande mobilité d'importants dégâts les champions assassins comme kasic se sont capables de sauter sur une cible la supprimer et repartir aussitôt mais attention ce sont des champions très fragile et bien que les dégâts qu'ils infligent soit élevé en combat d'équipé ils doivent avant tout être capable d'attendre le meilleur moment pour attaquer les tanks comme maokai sont des champions dotés de compétences qui leur permettent de prendre des coups pour leurs alliés et d'engager un combat de plus il dispose toujours d'une capacité de soins ou d'absorption de dégâts les rendant capables de subir l'effet adverse sans vaciller les combattants comme garenne sont des champions qui se trouve entre les tanks et les assassins mais il ne remplace en aucun cas l'un ou l'autre chacun de ces champions est le résultat d'un mix unique entre la mobilité les dégâts et la survivabilité peu importe le champion le combattant veut profiter de cette force pour attaquer des cibles fragiles lors d'un combat d'equipe forçant ainsi les adversaires à se concentrer sur lui pendant que ses alliés infliger un maximum de dégâts sur l'équipe ennemie les mages comme luxe excellent infliger des dégâts magiques à distance sans prendre de risques mais le manque de mobilité et la fragilité de ce type de champion les oblige à rester derrière lorsqu'un combats commencent il faut donc rester le plus en retrait possible utiliser ses sorts de contrôle pour maintenir des champions ennemis éloignés et infliger un maximum de dégâts sur l'équipe et demi comme ghzaya les tireurs utilisent leur portée pour attaquer à distance bien que leurs dégâts en début de partie ne soit pas aussi élevé que la plupart des champions grâce aux objets qu'ils achètent les tireurs voient leurs dégâts augmentés de façon exponentielle au fur et à mesure que la partie avance c'est la raison pour laquelle les tireurs sont les cibles prioritaires lorsqu'une escarmouche éclate les supports ont pour rôle d'apporter du soutien et de la vision à leur équipe la diversité de champion permet de jouer de nombreux styles de jeu différents karma peut grâce à ses compétences infliger des dégâts à distance à ses adversaires tout en protégeant son ou ses alliés rom peut sauter sur ses compagnons pour bloquer les attaques à l'aide de son bouclier et avec ses autres compétences il peut ralentir et a sommé les adversaires pour qu'ils soient plus faciles à tuer d'autres supports comme diana sont beaucoup plus passifs doté principalement de capacités défensives leur rôle est de s'assurer que ses alliés ne puissent pas mourir aussi bien en les soignant ou en les protégeant avec son bouclier qu'en contrôlant les ennemis [Musique] chaque semaine une rotation vous permet d'essayer gratuitement un échantillon de 14 champions en essayer un maximum dans les différentes catégories vous permettra de trouver votre rôle de prédilection ainsi que vos champion favori dans chacun d'eux la méta game désigne tout un ensemble de stratégies et de méthodes qui ont été forgés par l'expérience des joueurs aujourd'hui nous trouvons généralement un tireur avec un support sur la voie du bas un champion au solo sur la voie du haut et du milieu et enfin un champion dans la jungle s'occupant des monstres neutre et arrivant par surprise sur les autres lignes pour donner l'avantagé à son équipe s'il en est ainsi c'est tout simplement parce que les joueurs ont décidé que c'était la chose la plus optimale ce n'est pas figé dans le temps et tout peut changer d'un match à l'autre dans de prochaines vidéos nous vous parlerons des différentes stratégies connu pour chaque voix de la faille de l'invocateur n'hésitez pas à nous dire en commentaire quel rôle vous intéressent le plus voilà qui conclut cette deuxième vidéo de la série dans la suivante nous nous intéresserons aux statistiques et objets afin que vous sachiez quoi acheter et pourquoi vous pouvez cliquer sur la vidéo de gauche pour regarder le premier épisode si vous l'avez raté ou sur celle de droite pour passer directement au troisième épisode

titre: Ekko: Seconds | New Champion Teaser - League of Legends
transcription:
People waste a lot of time. Then, they wish for more. They want more hours in their days.
More days in their years. More years in their lives.
Because if they had all that extra time... They could fix any mistake. I don't need hours, or days, or years... ...I only need seconds. Here's the thing about time: If you can't make the most out of any given moment, Then you don't deserve a single extra second.

titre: Milio: The Gentle Flame | Champion Trailer - League of Legends
transcription:
Yesterday, I helped fight a GIANT snake! Purple, with a THOUSAND eyes, and huUUUUuge, pointy, TEETH! GRRRRRR! But woosh! Fwwwwwwooom!  My fire looked so scary,  it rushed off into the jungle! NEVER to be seen again! Pretty amazing, right? "Yeah, you’re so brave, Milio! Tell us more!" Fiiiiiine... but only ‘cause you asked! I’ve done so many new things since leaving my village! You won't believe this but I battled a green monster too!  It had six– no, EIGHT legs, and massive claws. Taller than the trees! I learned to heal a lot of people at once, way more than I did at home. "Wow, that sounds tough! How do you do it, Milio?" After I get it once, it's easy! Wherever I go, I try to make new friends. And it always works... eventually! I know it's surprising,
but fire doesn't have to hurt. It's nice to help people
see it differently. "That's great training to get stronger too!" Yeah, exactly! Hey, we think a lot alike. My family is gonna love those stories. They’re probably waiting for my next letter… "I wish they were here right now." They are here right now. And remember,
we're going to make them so proud. Oooh, I'm sleepy! I bet they have comfy beds in Ixaocan.

titre: Lillia Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription:
Lillia is a magical fawn, collecting dreams
for her Mother Tree. Darting in and out of battle while wielding
her branch staff, she sends foes to their slumber, mumbling apologies along the way. It's a scary world out there, but you're the
real Fawn of the Fed. Welcome to the Lillia Champion Spotlight. Blooming Blows blasts nearby enemies with magic damage, plus true damage along the outer edge sweet spot. Blooming Blows also has a passive component:
All of Lilia's spells that hit enemies grant her added movement speed for a short time. These Prances stack up to five times, enabling
you to put the petal to the metal. Speaking of passives, she has another one! Spells on enemies also apply Dream Dust, which
deals maximum health damage over time. Lillia winds up a huge strike, dashing, and
damaging enemies in the area. Those at the center will pay deer-ly, taking
tons of damage. Enemies are not the only ones who need to
watch out, Lilia cannot jump over walls with the dash. Lillia lobs a Swirlseed that damages and slows
enemies where it lands. Swirlseed has infinite range, so it rolls
until it hits an enemy or collides with terrain. Because it can fly out from across the entire
map, you don't wanna be caught like a deer in the headlights, so pro-SEED with caution! All enemy champions afflicted by Dream Dust,
no matter how far away they are, become drowsy and slow for a few seconds, before falling
asleep. If they're awakened by damage, they take a
bunch more. Haha, Dream Dust printer go ZZZZZ! Lillia's forte is speedies and sleepies — run
in, put everyone to sleep and dot 'em, then tippy-tap out. Sorry, sorry, sorry! Start games by clearing jungle camps with Blooming Blows, darting away to kite monsters and prancing
faster and faster as you go. Sweet spots are always a fixed distance away from you. So if you want to clear the jungle well at all, you must kite the camps backwards for maximum damage. Your ability to kite is what separates your
team's deerest ally from just another Jane Doe. PVP combat is also difficult in the early
game. Avoid getting caught by enemy champions, using
your speed as an advantage while you're squishy. As you power up, Lilting Lullaby can be a
clutch escape tool or all the setup your team needs to deliver a rude awakening. During mid to late game, look to hit high-value foes hiding at the outer edges with Blooming Blows and in the center with Watch Out! Eep! When they snooze, they lose. For more things Lillia, click the links in
the description. And remember: Always believe in your dreams.

titre: The First Time Riot Killed Off a Champion (BEFORE Xin Zhao)
transcription: did you know that Riot actually killed off a champion in League of Legends I don't mean that they nerfed a champion to death or even that they reworked a champion into something unrecognizable sorry atrox in an in-game lore event a champion actually died players who logged in on July 29th 2015 were greeted with an in-game notification Gangplank is dead and has been disabled in all cues this is the story of the only time riak GES killed off a champion in this way and why they will never do it again back in the day Riot wasn't really sure how to handle League of Legends lore in the very early days we would really only get the occasional Journal of justce entry and not much else some Champions like Evelyn basically had no lore whatsoever and for the early days it's not surprising for a long period Riot was dropping a new Champion every 2 weeks if you're new to League you're probably shocked to hear that but it's true in 2010 Riot released 24 new Champions and they did the same thing in 2011 it's no surprise Uris that it was near impossible to have a coherent story in League when they were designing and releasing Champions so quickly over the years Riot would try new things to make the low more consistent and to bring the lore into the game itself the bwat event and the death of Gangplank is one of the most bold things Riot has ever done people who have played League a long time might be surprised to hear me talk about something that happened in 2015 as if it's a part of old League lore usually when people are talking about old League they're referring to somewhere between 200 9 and 2012 or 13 when I started making this video I almost scrapped it all together because I thought the event was too recent then reality hit 2015 was almost 10 years ago this event happened 6 years after League's launch and this video is being uploaded 9 years after the event the gap between now and the event is 50% larger than the gap between League's launch and the event life really comes at you fast so what was this event the event was the bugeater update which updated the lowering gameplay of buw waterer Champions there was also a new aad map called butcher bridge and many other things that will cover Misfortune received a visual update as well as a small gameplay update the changes to her kit were relatively minor overall gangplanks changes were huge and this would basically be the Gangplank still in the game today the one that does this let's watch this okay boom before looking more at the event let's look at old Gangplank first when people think of the old version they immediately think of deny plank his original e raise moral allowed him to kill an Allied minion giving Champions an attack speed and movement speed increase this also denied the enemy the gold and XP from that minion the ability to deny minion kills was ridiculously strong Gangplank would wait for minions to spawn in his own base and start killing minions right away by the time the minion wave even got to Lane Gangplank would already have a lead because there was less gold and experience for the enemy champ to get Once in Lane he would deny every single Cannon minion for the entire laning phase this meant that right from the start Gangplank was ahead of you in XP and gold and as long as he didn't run it down it would only get worse on top of that he commonly built gold generating items like philosopher stone creating even more of a gold Gap all Gangplank had to do was deny minions whenever possible and he would outscale everyone this ability was eventually changed in April 2011 to no longer have a minion denyed component later that same year he also received his first visual update oh and did you know that in the alpha Gangplank didn't have a beard what here's a cursed image for you you're welcome so back to 2015 when the event launched on July 22nd the Gangplank rework released including a visual update this was the splash art and this was his new in-game model note that they aren't the same ones used today keep that in mind this is also when the First Act of the bwat story had been released in this act Misfortune had hired Graves and Twisted Fate to steal from Gangplank she also hired a bilgewater local to leak this information to Gangplank so that he would capture them mid Theft Act two of this event released that same week included the capture of TF engraves by Gangplank he was depicted as a brutal pirate who tortured his own crew he would practice scrimshaw the act of carving on Ivory by carving on the bones of the people who we flayed alive a few days later act three of the event would be released in this act Gangplank staged a public execution of TF and G Graves it's revealed that this is all part of misfortune's plan to get revenge on Gangplank 15 years earlier Gangplank stormed her parents Workshop he killed them both right in front of her and shot her leaving her for dead for years Sarah Fortune waited for the moment she could exact her revenge and this was it while Gangplank was intervening on the heist as planned she had her people plant explosives within Gangplank ship when Gangplank returned he staged a public execution of TF and Graves he chained them both to a cannon and pushed them overboard with the intent of drowning them when this happened the explosives were detonated destroying Gangplank ship killing his crew and the captain himself the ACT ends with Misfortune giving the order to kill any of gangplank's crew who survived and burn down anything owned by or related to him it's also revealed that Twisted Fate and Graves had managed to survive on the day that this act was released Gangplank was also killed in game this is where this notification came from Riot also released a statement that champion death in League was unprecedented and that they would not address any refund requests for him or his skins at this time they also released the captain Fortune skin as Misfortune would be the new captain and queen of bilgewater many players felt this was a very cool lure event While others felt that it was annoying and frustrating to not be able to play their main Champion for lore reasons this also caused a debate in the competitive Community which we'll cover later you can find all sorts of posts asking why Gangplank was unavailable it was commonly believed that he was was actually disabled due to a bug but a riot employee commented saying that bugs weren't involved and this was solely for lore purposes a few days later the final part of the bilgewater story would be released this part revealed that Gangplank had survived the explosion he was seriously injured and his arm had been mangled he had made his way to a back alley physician who told him that there was no way to save his arm the story ends with Gangplank throwing a bone saw at the physician's feet demanding that he cut it off Gangplank states that he has work to do Riot revealed that Gangplank would be available again as of August 5th but with an updated look the new version of Gangplank would reflect his appearance after surviving the detonation of his ship and crew this would be his new default Splash art while the previous version was swapped to a skin Instead This commemorative skin would be available for $500 I couldn't resist this Captain Gangplank skin would be given to all players for free as long as they owned Gangplank and played him in any que by the end of the event this meant that for the lore update Riot went through the effort of making two new appearances for him solely for the sake of this event and they did it for free on top of that Riot released an entirely new game mode Black Market Brawlers was a Summoner Rift variant where you earned Krakens throughout the game you would use these to buy a brawler minion which you could only buy one of then these Brawlers would accompany each minion wave and you could upgrade your brawler by spending Krakens the event included 16 unique items most notably Dead Man's plate Dead Man's plate was permanently added to the game at the end of The Burning Tides event it's also featured in gangplank's new Splash art Riot went above and beyond for this event and it really shows something that has to be discussed is the controversy of disabling Gangplank and especially how it impacted professional play gangplank's rework launched on July 22nd 2015 he was disabled due to his death 1 week later on July 31st Pro Player Wicked made a post talking about how this impacted Pro play he had contacted officials of the Challenger Series to see if Gangplank would be disabled for competitive play and he wasn't in his post he asked how can they leave a champion open for competitive play if he's disabled everywhere else a champion that was reworked and available to play for only one week was allowed to be picked in competitive games this meant that players had basically no time to learn how to play as or against Gangplank many people felt that this was a competitive Integrity issue and just a bad decision in general funny enough only a few days later one day before gang plank would be re-enabled this would happen in the semi-finals and now we're seeing the gang plank all right pretty different uh we did see some gang Clank before we've actually seen it by kashad in the sumers split but that was old gang plank that was AP run glaive gang plank this is New Gang plank which we've never seen ever anywhere in the world no we have not had an opportunity because he wasn't really enabled until this patch correct um and then he was dead he back from the dead we'll see whether he uses the ghost skin that is kind of problematic um you know there was a lot of discussion from specifically Wicked this is kind of ironic that this is happening in this game uh about the fact that teams on live weren't able to to play with Gangplank but clearly wonder why it feels like he put enough games in before Gangplank was disabled to actually play it so I am looking forward to seeing how new Gangplank with the rework does um it's usually how they give up those advantages but this time again they get this advantage in the Top Lane wik will just pop the ultimate a response by using the ultimate from wonderwear but wik is going to walk into the bush has the overheat damage has the Min wave at the front line for team dignitas he's using the civer ultimate to go in the alist the Rex side wonder is just going to try and trade with people and completely blow them up S hooks comes after land lands that orb obvious as well looking for this fight what away from the back lines assassinates the Victor looking for carry as well he has the barrel set up he gets the double kill it's unfortunate that Wicked was the person who flagged this as a potential issue and also ended up being the person on the receiving end of it after the game wonderwear also tweeted that it was his first time playing reworked Gangplank clearly wonder why it feels like he put enough games in before Gangplank was disabled let me know what you think of this down below do you think it was a bad decision by Riot to disable Gangplank but leave him available for competitive play the bilgewater event in general was a ton of work from Riot and overall it was an incredible in-game event the lore was actually very interesting and Riot was willing to try bold new things like killing Gangplank off and having it actually affect the game itself league is first and foremost a game and I think events like this add a lot to it overall the bwat event in particular had so much in it that it's easily one of the best in-game events of all time that's why is the goat the goat back in 2016 in an Ask Riot Dev post they answered a question about if they would ever do another event like this again they said that they would not be doing another event on this scale it was simply a lot to produce and because so many teams are involved it made it very hard to achieve a specific release window this one event alone had an 11,000 word story a new a-an map the Gangplank rework with an extra skin for free a gameplay and visual update for Misfortune a new item a fully fleshed out 5v5 game mode a full bwat themed voiceover for the announcer that's how you spill blood new icons and in-game missions all completely free content along with this was a skin line inspired by the event but players got the main skin Captain gang plank for free I really wish Riot would commit to doing more content like this but it's much easier to Simply use the events as a tool for marketing new skins maybe even with an outrageous price tag attached before finishing up I wanted to take a moment to thank you all for supporting the channel the algorithm works in mysterious ways and every like or comment really helps let me know if you played League during the buw waterer event down below I'm curious what your thoughts are on Gangplank being disabled for lore purposes was it a fun detail for Riot to include or was it a bad decision overall let me know what you think down below and again thanks for watching
