titre: Ranged Top Laners: Why They Don't Work (And Why Top Laners Hate Them) | League of Legends
transcription: no matter who you are or what role you play everyone hates a champion the day you find someone who doesn't have a personal Vendetta against one or more members of the cast is the day the world explodes hating a group of Champions is also very common the assassin class is without question the most despised in the game for reasons that I'm sure need no explanation on occasion there's a local playground bully in a role that grinds people's gears for example a commonly disliked ad carry is driven due to his tendency to well bully Lane and snowball out of control whether he is or isn't strong in The Meta it's exceedingly rare for a role to have more than one of these especially if they're not picked ban op at the current moment yet up in Top Lane if you ask any Topman who they dislike fighting the most you're gonna receive one of two answers either they hate the four horse women of top lane or their answer will be ranged top Lanes despite roughly half or so of all champions in this game being ranged attackers top laners Foster a special contempt for the ones frequently or rather infrequently seen on the island and regardless of the strength or what the meta is almost no one enjoys getting matched against the Quinn vain Cassiopeia Canon Nar teemojson have you surprisingly however they seldom if ever dominate The Meta in Top Lane even though it's common for them to have very high win rates having made a video on AP bot laners last time I want to go to the opposite side of the rift and talk about ranged top laners more specifically I want to talk about why they're not as broken as top Mains tend to propagate full disclosure I myself am a top Main and have a vested grudge against ranged top laners just like everyone else but I'll try my best to avoid letting bias over exaggerate things also unlike AP botliners which are a designated group of Champions there really isn't a formerly established category for arranged top laners as the term implies it's any Champion with range pressure as Top Lane features primarily melee Champions a mobile melee Champions to be exact due to the world's isolated nature placing less emphasis on map rotations it's the ideal location for juggernauts or tanks to farm up in steamroll the mid to late game conversely it's also the role least likely to receive any external intervention giving whoever wins the 1v1 up top in natural Advantage range top laners are notorious for exploiting these circumstances with the majority of Top Lane is consisting of champions of either a short threat range or a predictable one ranged attackers with a strong neutral game and a way to peel themselves from enemy engage practically get a free Lane most of the time of course it takes a certain skill set to find employment up top not just any ranged Champion can make it for one you'll need to possess a form of disengage just because you have the range Advantage doesn't mean you're never at risk of getting attacked going through the list of conventional range top laners teemo's blinding guard in tandem with move quick and then later noxious trap can drastically mitigate the enemy laner's pressure especially if they're Auto attackers which most dark Nars Boomerang slow combined with this three hit passive movement speed buff and hop give him an easy way out at a moment's notice Canon's lightning Rush is an easy runaway tool provided he's not slowed beforehand Jace may not be able to dash away but thundering blow is a fantastic get off me tool sing those for Quinn vault is arguably one of the best self-peel tools you can ask for up in tablet on the off chance you see vane top she has condemned and tumbled to put distance quickly while there are a measurable number of top laners with gap closers they usually don't have enough Tempo or Mobility to continue their Pursuit after blowing engage for example if Jack sleep strikes onto you as Fame you can condemn and then run away he doesn't have a second leap strike he can use immediately after juggernauts have it even worse assuming competent positioning Canon Quinn aksha Nar Etc should theoretically never let juggernauts get close enough not without losing all of their health first you may have also noticed that range top laners typically have above average Tempo something in their kit that gives bonus movement speed Canon z teemos w Nars W akshan passive Quinn's W passive Chase's passive and Canon e vayne's passive cassid q and so on this is probably more important to make note of than the range advantage and is also the reason you don't see a zerat or luxoff and tablet The increased movement speed allows them to Kai targets with consistently higher Tempo more so when paired with the slow of some kind finally it helps to have good Lane endurance either in the form of health or Mana sustenance since top is a longer Lane than mid and your left to your own devices with no support to heal you or whatnot anyone who can either maintain their resources or require items to do so early can work well in Top Lane Jason cassia both have built-in Mana regeneration vein Quinn shun and by extension most 80 carry top laners Rush Vamps up your first item meanwhile Cannon is an energy user Nar is manilist and vladimirkin region team was the one champion who has neither easy access to health or meta sustain but toxic shot greatly increases his Auto attack damage allowing him to push waves decently fast without having to use abilities at all those are the three main things you need to have to be a successful range top laner disengage Tempo and endurance now that we've established the group the next two things I want to go over are one why they're so hated and two why they don't work let's address the elephant in the room why do so many top laners despise ranged Champions more so than any other role I'm gonna answer that with the question of my own why do Zed Talon Katarina Kiana King cossix rexai and so on enjoy range Champions After all their melee too right how can they be thrilled to face an enemy team of ranged attackers when top laners would ask to be put out of their misery at the site of even 2 or 3. the main reason is how top laners exert their pressure out of all the roles buying maybe 80 carry top laners have the highest persistent damage output being able to stay in a fight for very long time and continuously pump out damage the caveat is that they tend to need time to get going it is possible to get one-shotted by number for the most part you need time to get that pressure off assassins like the ones I mentioned earlier King Gap quilts on you within fractions of a second and nuke you hundred to zero equally as fast moreover they tend to be better at dodging whatever disengage tools you have or have enough mobility in their kit to press on even after you do so top liners on the other hand Place less emphasis on evasion and more on head-on confrontation but for many of them even a small peel tool is enough to negate their threat capacity as their engage is more linear and Telegraph range top Lane is excel at maintaining the line of scrimmage always staying just that side of most melee Top Lane or slap bubble while being close enough to still attack them and if their opponent is tilted and charges had on they have their appeal Tools in their back pocket now more often than not Jace's thundering blow or Nars hop is nowhere near sufficient to spare than the terror of Darius with ghosts and stride breaker and that's kind of how the dynamic boils down to in toppling the range Champion continuously harasses the melee champ until the melee champ loses patience and all wins in a full blow my V1 fight melee top laners always win against range because they have more base damage more base stats and so on that being said the only time they're allowed to do that is that they commit to an all-in an opportunity that doesn't always present itself unfavorable terms if the range top laner is playing correctly they're able to freeze the wave build Dominion lead deal enough damage to where you had a significant help disadvantage or ideally all of the above in that situation trying to haul in is tantamount to intent feeding so realistically the only time when a melee top laner gets to rush in is if the range top missed positions or overextends it responsibly range top laners bear the same negative reputation as zoners in fighting games dolphin from Street Fighter is notorious for having very long range attacks and projectiles that can keep opponents from Ever Getting within Striking Distance of them to offset this if Dawson fails to hold down the neutral game his poor durability and underwhelming Close Quarters options makes him an easy kill but the difference between a fighting game like Street Fighter and league is that for the former the battlefield takes place in a small arena with nowhere to hide and most owners don't have easy get off until is to reset neutral for League though we have turrets a proverbial safe place they can hide under letting them do whatever they please while you can't do anything about it additionally most if not all of them have ways to force you off of them and reset back to neutral where they can exert pressure with impunity all over again to repeat they do crumble to pieces if caught with a disengage on cooldown and in a best spot but that's influenced less by skillful play on behalf of the melee top laner and more by careless play on behalf of the range top laner if that makes sense ultimately ranged top laners are so despised because agency is effectively taken away from the melee top later if the range top Lane in place correctly they will never give the melee top laner a chance to fight resulting in a perpetually unfavorable neutral State for the entirety of laning piece Mordecai's most unbearable matchup is vain her percentile true damage can shred through any help or armor he builds and she can use tumble to sidestep that's grasp even if Mordecai gets in range to use Ulta on her she can use condemn and the invisibility from final hour to buy time and kite him into Oblivion there's literally nothing the mordekaiser player can do in this scenario one of her neckties was matchups is Quinn every time he wants to dash in she can use Vault to knock him back and run away until he gets prowler's claw he's at the mercy of Quinn and if she zones him away from the wave correctly it's going to be a very long time before he buys it Chase is a humongous key to Jax because every time Jax wants to lead strike onto him Chase switches to hammer form and knocks him right back before blasting him with damage these circumstances are always in favor of the ranged Champion the only time it's not is when they get caught with their pants done but once again that comes down to the range toppling in short the outcome of laning phase is decided solely on how well or how poorly the ranged Top Lane in place as the melee player has very little if any equity in the matchup now if that's the case wouldn't it make practical sense for everyone to play ranged top laner with all the Privileges I just talked about it sounds like a done deal right yes and no while it's true that ranged top laners have the advantage in lane and will almost always come out ahead of a melee top laner in the early game that doesn't take into account what the goal of Top Lane is for those who didn't see my video on the five rolls topdiff isn't decided on who gets the first Tower or who gets better KVA or has a higher CS not exclusively anyway Top Lane is all about pressure how much pressure you can insert and how much pressure you can draw in juggernauts tanks divers and skirmishers are very popular Top laners by virtue of their ability to soak up pressure like a sponge they possess a tremendous threat to the enemy team if left unchecked requiring them to focus them down in a fight and constantly respect them less to get hacked to Pieces even though prevailing wisdom has Top Lane of spending for themselves everyone has said that one game where the enemy mordekaiser aatrox or Darius got a few ganks and snowballed into mid game becoming this destructive force of destiny that 1v9's entire team fights or on the other hand the enemy Tryndamere or Fiora went 0-5 who then committed to slow pushing all game back during your Inhibitors and then eventually Nexus you may have done a good job putting down the malphite or Oren but they still pull off a clean wobble combo with their team winning the game very rarely does the same thing happen for range top laners and that's because their advantage is in Lane don't transfer over to the rest of the game as easily as melee top laners do don't get me wrong I don't think anyone wants to deal with the FED Cannon or kill but by mid to late game your range Advantage diminishes as every everyone gets faster Tempo shorter cooldowns more burst damage Etc even the melee Champions by mid game Darius Karen and Olaf likely have stride breaker and maybe deadmass played to chase down the chase or Roxanne to hell and back Jackson tryndamere's Dash cooldowns have been drastically shortened and they now also have enough damage to kill you in three or four Autos also that's assuming you have a lead in the first place if you're a range top laner and you fall behind you're never getting back into the game under normal circumstances if you pick Jace Cannon or Vladimir and you lose link to a melee not only did you make the enemy Jacks Darius Renekton Fiora or whoever a massive problem for your entire team to deal with but you don't have an insurance policy to fall back on your side lane pressure is non-existent especially compared to a Tryndamere York or nessus you don't really have a lot of Team fight pressure compared to a malphite horn or mordekaiser and what's more by picking a ranged top laner you placed the onus on your team to handle the pressure that you were supposed to take remember Top Lane is all about exerting and absorbing pressure that's what his Champions excel at everyone who played back in seasons 3 4 and 5 may remember all the memes about a divers getting camped all game back when he was the top laner for TSM you want the opportunity to do a little bit more damage I'd like the opportunity to play League of Legends but that's partly what made him such an effective top laner he was so good at being the team's punching bag that it got Bjergsen wild turtle Etc free time to farm up and get their mid game Power spikes by absorbing pressure you take it away from your team melee top laners are fantastic at doing so even when behind range top laners are not therefore more pressure is put on the rest of your team pressure they may not be able to afford to take it's hard to quantifiably explain but I guess in layman's terms by picking a ranged top laner you're gambling on winning the early game hard enough to win the game through sheer oppressive offense because if you fail to or Worse lose early game not only are you behind but your team has one less person who is willing to take the L so your team can get the W if nothing else the Darius Renekton or whoever can still be a meat shield for your team even if they fall behind they can walk up and face tank ass shows Ari charms or just Zone away assassins and divers from your team even when you're 0-5 the enemy Wukong can't just walk right past you and go for your backline but the same can be said when you're a Canon or Vladimir because then your team has no Frontline and it devolves into an every man for himself situation it's inherently a very selfish pick that while viable is actually more risky or tenuous than just playing a melee champion of course there are moments where the enemy range top laner gets super fed and hybrid carries the game but there are just as many if not more outcomes where you get team comp gift because you have nothing but damage while the enemy top laner has a mordekai so who raid bosses your team or a set who one shots everyone with W or a malphite with five men also for the win I would say the only time range top laners are a good choice at least in Pro play is if someone else on the team can afford to be the sponge for example if you have a tank jungler or maybe a galio mid then in that case you can get away with the Jace top or you can pick one of the few range top laners like Nar or Kennen who have Team fight pressure stuff like Teemo Quinn and vayne are too inconsistent but that's about all I have to say for today let me know your thoughts on range top laners in the comments down below whether you agree or disagree with my points as always if you enjoyed the video be sure to leave a like And subscribe really appreciate it don't forget to follow me on Twitter join my Discord server and check out my other Champion group discussions if you haven't yet but for now thanks so much for watching and I'll see you again soon in the next one take care [Music] foreign [Music]

titre: What League of Legends Champion Has The Most Lore?
transcription: What champion has the most lore? Throughout the history of League of Legends, when it comes to story, some champions were treated well and some weren't. With the gap between the two being so big, it became obvious which champions are the favorites. But it's not really similar to the skin situation. Because when it comes to skins, the most popular champions get the most skins because they can sell the most of them. But when it comes to the lore, overall, an average lore enjoyer doesn't really care about their favorite champion getting a story that much. Usually, people were excited to learn about everyone, which meant that for the most part, every champion got at least a little bit of lore. For the most part, however, we soon realized that all the champions who got the most interesting lore also got a lot more of it. And that's usually because with an interesting story, there is a lot more creative freedom for the writers around them, which resulted in some champions having a bit more generic story with others getting entire books worth of stories, literally. And so today, that's what we're diving into. We will not go through every single champion in League of Legends, but we'll go through all the champions with the longest stories. But first, slight disclaimer. The stats we'll have a look at today are not 100% accurate. And that's because there is a massive difference between all the media. I mean, how do you compare a game to a short story? Also, if there is a story that contains multiple champions, does the total length of the story count towards every champion? And what if one of the champions is the main character of the story? Well, because all of that, let's take everything with a pinch of salt. Anyway, end of disclaimer. Let's get on it. Going back into the past for a little bit. Back in the day, every champion got the story the exact same way. At the absolute start, we got a bit of the tribunal story. I'm not going to be explaining that one in this video, but on top of that, every champion got their own lore. Later, this transitioned into every champion getting their own bio, after which there was a whole new formula. While I can't really point out when this started, I am pretty sure Kindred were the first ones to do this back in 2015, but around the time Aurelian Soul was released, the modern lore of League of Legends became a thing, and it became a standard for every new champion to get a bio. And a little bit after that, they would also get a short story with the bio essentially summarizing the champion's lore and the short story giving us a piece of their story from their own perspective. And so around 2016, every champion was getting this treatment. However, even though this is when the lore of League of Legends started getting serious, that's not when some of the longest stories came out because a year before that, back in 2015, we got the first ever League of Legends lore event called the Burning Tides. This is the one infamous event where a Gangplank died. People will also remember this because this is when we got that cool buildwater ARM. But to me, most importantly, this is where we also got a lot of lore. I mean, The Burning Tides was a massive story that included four main characters. Misfortune, Gangplank, Graves, and Twisted Fate. And out of all of these, Misfortune is the one who got the most lore. How much lore specifically? We'll talk about that in a sec because surprisingly, she has far more than you think. And so, we can't start with her. Instead, we need to talk about one champion that quickly became the new king of long stories, or rather the queen. And so, the first champion who actually got massive lore was Civer. Almost exactly a year after the Burning Tides, Riot released the Bloodline story. This story became the foundation of the now canonical Shereima we know today. It was a story that was originally supposed to tie Talia into the story of League of Legends, but because it was explaining Azir and his entire bloodline, of course, his descendant Civer got included. And so, let's actually dig into some numbers. If we have a look at Civer's bio, we can count 838 words. Her short story that was included also has 1,250 words. Bloodline, which is the massive story that included multiple champions, including stuff like Nasus or Civer, got 9,899 words. But that's not technically where Civer's story ends because we also got a story called the Twilight of the Gods. Now while that entire story is focusing on the story of the darkin and how the aspect of twilight came down and taught mortals how to banish them the aspect used the chalikar which is the celestial weapon that civer holds which means that entire story gave us one big context for civer's weapon and so if we include this here too with twilight of the gods giving us 7,074 words in total that is 19,061 words. Now, if we have a look at the average reading speed, we can see that people read 238 words per minute. So, slapping a time value on all of these words, Civer's entire lore equals 80 minutes of pure reading. That sounds like a lot, but you would be surprised to see that a lot of champions are in this category. For example, the one that is somewhat connected to Civer is Talia. Kalia got a bio with 1677 words. She got a short story with 1,040 words. Once again, the bloodline story got 9,899 words because yes indeed this story was also about Talia arriving in Shereima. But then there is also the bird and the branch story which is the story where Talia was in Ionia where she met Yasuo which is also where Yasuo became her master. And that story got 5,235 words. But that's not all because there is also the hollow spun story which is the story where later on Talia met Kaisa in Shereima. It is the story that was directly tied to that one cinematic. And so that story has 5,790 words. If you combine this with the 5 minutes of Talia cinematics, we have 23,641 words, which equals 99 minutes of pure Talia lore, which is not too far away from Civer. And yes, as I mentioned, there is a lot of champions with similar length in story. While most champions have about uh 30 minutes of lore if they are lucky, those who got a special treatment got over an hour. This is where we can find someone like Jyn or Zed or Udier or Jarvin or even Morichiser. But to break out of this over an hourong mold, you need special special treatment, which is something Riven got. Riven got a bio with 729 words with a short story that has 1,36 words. But then for no big reason at all outside of being connected to Yasuo, Riven just started getting massive stories left and right, which included the entire massive Confessions of a Broken Blade series, which was a threepart story that has in total 9,371 words. But that's not it, because on top of that, there is also the Sisterhood of War series. This is a gigantic story about Noxian hunters who are looking for Riven in Ionia after the Noxian invasion was over. It is also a massive origin story for Riven in the Ionian invasion. And somehow this entire series has three massive parts. Part one had 9,800 words. Part two had 10,245 words. And part three had 8,315 words. But that's not where Riven's story ends because remember there was also the ruination event. We all remember the sentinels of light. But while that entire event was quite massive about 30 minutes of it all belong to Riven. And so in total Riven has 197 minutes which equals 3.2 hours. So yes that is 3 hours of your life dedicated to Riven. But things only go crazier from here because next let's have a look at Garin. Garin's bio has 739 words. His short story, which is about killing a hag somewhere in Damasia, has in total 1,958 words. Quite a long one for just normal short story. But then you also got the entire for Deamasia story, which is where he teamed up with Lux and they fought Nocturn. That story has 7,842 words. Then there is the entire Lux comic which has five issues and about 30 minutes of reading in total. But on top of all of that, Garin also got his own nolla called Garin. This one had 136 pages with about 260 words per page. This would be about 33,800 words. And so the novela alone is about 142 minutes of reading. So, in total, with all the other stories Garin has, we're talking about 214 minutes or three and a half hours of Garin lore, what's crazy is that Garin got his entire novella. And yet, he is quite close to Riven. But here's the thing, reading is not the only kind of media that gives us lore, especially since outside of reading, Riot also did one more thing that was purely focused on stories. Riot Forge. I mean, why else would every game have a League of Legends story in the title? And so, Riot Forge gave us some games which might have tackled some outdated lore or it might have not had lore at all or we got a purely story focused game such as Song of Noo-Noo with Song of Noo-Noo having about 6 hours of pure story playthrough. And so technically above all the champions that have a lot of reading, Noo-Noo and Willimp still have more lore with about 6 hours of entertainment. But speaking of entertainment, there is this one more thing kind of small thing uh called Arcane. Yes, by now Arcane is canon. And so Arcane is giving us a lot of lore for the champions we know. But when it comes to Arcane, we can tackle it in multiple ways because Arcane includes a lot of fractured stories. We can assign the value for each champion depending on their screen time. And thankfully, someone was actually crazy enough to write down the screen time. And so, for example, Ambessa got a screen time of 56 minutes. Then there is Mel with 1 hour and 22 minutes. Victor with 1 hour and 51 minutes, Jayce with 2 hours and 19 minutes, Caitlyn with 2 hours and 29 minutes, Jinx with 3 hours and 12 minutes, and finally Vi with 3 hours and 31 minutes. However, I don't think this is a fair way to do this because when it comes to the big stories, we also didn't chop them into pieces and give each champion only their value. No, instead those stories counted for every champion because it is giving everyone a context after all. And so let's do the crazy thing and let's assign every Arcane champion the full length of Arcane with all the episodes together being about 13 and a half hours long. And so now since none of these champions outside of Ambessa have their bios or short stories that are fully canon now because of again Arcane, they all have 13 and 1/2 hours of lore. But there is still not the most lore you can find in League of Legends because next there is also one champion that was not in Arcane at all and that's Misfortune. You see Misfortune has a bio that has 840 words, a short story that has 866 words. Then there is the Burning Tides event which we mentioned at the very beginning of this video. That event had 9,758 words after which she also got the shadow and fortune story which is where she defended the buildwater from the undead. That story had 3,698 words. But then there is also another story she got called dead in the water which is the story where she faced an old enemy and she also kind of happened to meet Nautilus. That story has 10,065 words. But then she also got her own comic that would be about 15 minutes of reading. Then once again the entire ruination event happened and she was there which would be about 30 minutes of story for her alone. After which there is this one tiny thing uh called the ruined king game. Now here's the thing in total the ruined king game is about 40 hours long. But because it is so massive, sure, let's not give 40 hours to Misfortune. There is a lot of context for all the other champions and that is a massive game. But since Misfortune is the main protagonist here, I would easily say about 15 hours of that game are her. And so if you want to get the complete story of Misfortune, that would be about 10,050 minutes of story to sit through. or in other words, 17 hours of your life dedicated to misfortune. But even that is not the most lore we have in League of Legends because next, and I can't believe it, there is Ambassa. You see, Ambassa has a bio that has 606 words and that's it. She did not even get a short story. I mean, why would she? In Arcane, she did have 56 minutes of screen time. And here I would like to make an exception for what I said before with Arcane. Sure, we can treat all the champions equally with their three and a half hours of Arcane lore, but let's be honest, Ambessa wasn't even there for half of the entire series. On top of that, when Embassa was there, she was a really cool villain. But when it comes to her lore and her background and where she came from and what she was doing, we did not learn anything. You knew she was from Noxus. You knew that she did something with Mel and that was it. She was just a cool big bad guy. But still, let's be generous and let's give Embassa half of the entire Arcane time. You'll see later. It doesn't really matter, but let's be fair. And so, 7 hours of Arcane belongs to her. Well, it so happens to be that Ambassa also got her own book. Now, this is a book that I still have to cover on this channel. It is quite big and I have no idea if I can even format it. But that book has 425 words with about 270 words per page. Meaning that is about 114,210 words, which would be 480 minutes, which would be 8 hours. Now, here's the thing. Those 8 hours is just you reading under a perfect conditions, I guess, because interestingly enough, there is also an official audio book. And that official audio book has 15 and 12 hours. And so again, let's be fair, let's be accurate, and let's give her the full value. This would mean that with the audio book, her bio, and arcane, Ambessa's full story comes to around 23 and a half hours. For a character that came out of nowhere, that's incredible. But that is still not the most lore we have in League of Legends. No, that crown goes to the one true king. The one that is linked to the entire story of Buildwater. The one that's been at the center of multiple events. The one that is still being linked with new champions years after his release, which is also the same champion that has a massive potential for the MMO. I mean, I'm going to be very sad if he doesn't make it in. And that, of course, is VGO, the ruined king. You see, Viego's story is so complex and so massive that Riot had to release their own infographic to show you just how tangled it is. And even this shows you like 10% of the truth. And so, let's break down the numbers. His bio has 694 words. His short story, which finally introduced his queen, had 1,174 words. But then he also got the second short story which introduced Kalista as his general. This one had 3,191 words. However, Viego is only one half of his own story. I mean, there is a reason why he's always looking for his queen. And so, although I herself isn't a champion in League of Legends, she is heavily tied to Gwen, where Gwen's existence is even responsible for the banishment of Viego at the very end of his story. So, if you want to have the full context of Viego's story, you also have to include Gwen. And so, Gwen's bio also has 685 words. She also has a short story which has 1,361 words, which again, it's not like it really matters, but let's be accurate. After which, Viego also got a story where Santa picked up a piece of his old soul. That's what triggered Viego's hunt. And that story had 4,956 words. Now, if we go back to the character tree, you can see that there is a lot of other characters, but most of those were already described in his book. And so, yes, of course, in case you didn't know, Viego also has his entire book with the ruination book also having an audio version. And that one is 12 hours and 50 minutes. So, I don't think we also have to go through the bios of all the other characters. But there is also another story that was released specifically for VGO, which is The Will of the Dead. a story that has 10,830 words. It is a story that was released by Riot Forge and it was a story that was supposed to give Yoro some justice because he wasn't included in the hunting of Viego. Speaking of which, let's not forget that there is the entire ruination event with the Sentinels of Light not only having Viego as their main villain, but it is also an event that had a separate story in League PC and Wild Rift. And since Viego was the centerpiece of both of these, that is about an hour and a half of lore. But of course, we're still not done yet because Viego also had his own game, The Ruined King. Again, in total, this is a 40hour long game with about 20 hours of the main story with the main story being nothing but VGO lore. Now, sure, again, there is a lot of side quests and a lot of more context that can be found there. And so, realistically, let's give VGO about 25 hours of the entire game. Now, of course, he was also the main character of three separate cinematics. That would be about 15 minutes in total, but he also has a lot more lore in Legends of Runa, and that one is really hard to put some value on. I mean, there we learned a lot about the Gamavore and dragons and their armor and their soldiers. And let's not talk about the entire lore of Smolder, so let's give VGO about 20 minutes there. But in total, that means that VGO currently sits on the throne with the most lore out of any League of Legends champion with the summed up total of 2,395 minutes of lore, which equals 39.9 hours. It's almost like he could have had his own series or something, but that would only inflate his numbers more. I mean, it's still crazy to see that Ambassa is on the second place, though VGO still almost doubled her numbers, and we were still generous with not giving him the entire value of the game. But yes, that's the numbers we got at the very end. Now, I'll be honest, I did not go through every single possible champion. I kind of went by memory of when I was working on the stories of those champions and I just remembered how some champions stuck way up above the rest. I mean, some are still waiting for their judgment day. And so, I would love to see if someone could go through every single champion and actually note down their values because you still have some other champions such as Ari who was also in the ruined king game and she also got a lot of other stories, but that would be way too much of a time sync for me. And so at the end of the day, let's just be happy we got a classic Nekrit video. I know it's 2025. Who would have thought that?

titre: Finality: Aurelion Sol | Champion Update Trailer - League of Legends
transcription:
Avant moi, l'existence était vide.
Stérile. Je l'ai donc emplie de merveilles. Les mortels s'essaient à leurs propres
pathétiques créations... ... mais la vraie puissance vient du cosmos. Vous pensiez réussir.
Vous pensiez pouvoir me dompter. Mais qui peut dompter la grandeur ? Certainement pas vous ! À chaque fêlure, votre emprise faiblit. À chaque brèche, ma force me revient. Et avant de retrouver mes précieuses étoiles... je me déchaînerai. Qu'y a-t-il, Targon ?
On perd le contrôle ? [Rire menaçant]

titre: Gnar: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription:
Welcome to the League of Legends Champion
Spotlight, featuring Gnar, the Missing Link. Gnar is a prehistoric yordle with a soft,
cuddly face and a terrifying temper.  After being trapped in true ice, Gnar became frozen in time until his prison finally weakened enough for him to break free. In game, Gnar is a volatile top lane champion who cycles between speedy ranged Mini Gnar
and tanky monstrous Mega Gnar. Mini Gnar's a spritely little guy thanks
to his passive, which gives him bonus movement speed, attack speed and range. However, he's also
got a temper: Rage Gene. With it, Mini Gnar generates rage whenever he's in combat, gaining bonus rage whenever he deals damage.  Once Gnar fills his bar,  he becomes aggravated--transforming after
a few seconds,  or on his next ability cast, into
a terrifying monster: MEGA GNAR! Mega Gnar gains bonus health, armor, magic
resist and attack damage but loses his mini form's
bonus movement speed, attack speed, and range. After transforming, the Rage bar slowly drains, and he transforms back into Mini Gnar when it's empty. Finally, once he's back in mini form, Gnar
can't gain rage for a while. He's tired! Mini Gnar's Q is Boomerang Throw,  which serves as his primary damage and harass ability. Gnar throws out his boomerang in a straight-line
skillshot, dealing damage to the first enemy struck before slowing and returning toward Gnar.  Boomerang Throw deals reduced damage to all enemies struck after the first, and it can't hit the same target twice.  Once the boomerang turns, it travels back
towards wherever Gnar is heading. If he catches it, Gnar significantly reduces the ability's cooldown,  which is absurdly long otherwise. Boomerang Throw is a great tool for last hitting
and harassing your lane opponent from range. Up next is Mini Gnar's W: Hyper. Gnar passively applies stacks of Hyper with his basic attacks and abilities. Stacking Hyper three times on the same target deals bonus magic damage based on the target's max health and grants Gnar a huge
but temporary movement speed boost.  Gnar can apply stacks of Hyper to multiple targets simultaneously, and trigger the stacks to refresh his speed boost. Additionally, Hyper's speed boost automatically
triggers when transforming back to Mini Gnar. Trigger Hyper as often as you can during laning to win trades and keep away from your opponent. Gnar's E is Hop.  When activated, Gnar skips
a short distance to a target location,  gaining an attack speed boost for a few seconds.  If Gnar hops on a unit, he bounces, continuing in the same direction-- applying a slow and dealing damage based on his max
health if he bounces on an enemy. Note that when he hops into a cluster of enemies,
Gnar only damages the target closest to his  landing spot, and that if he's stunned, snared or rooted during his initial hop, he won't bounce at all. Hop is really useful for juking enemies through
the jungle by bouncing through neutral camps. Here are a few examples of hoppable spots! Save Hop to escape from jungle ganks. It's
awesome. Gnar's ultimate is GNAR!  As Mini Gnar, it only functions as a passive, giving him even more movement speed whenever he triggers Hyper. In Mega form, Gnar's Q swaps out his boomerang
for a big phreakin' boulder... or in some cases a house. Boulder Toss is a straight-line skillshot
that stops at the first target struck,  slowing and damaging all nearby enemies. Once the
boulder's landed, Gnar can lumber over to it and pick
it up to drastically lower its cooldown. Use Boulder Toss when chasing an enemy. You'll
pick it up as you make your pursuit, giving you multiple opportunities to throw it again for
extra slows and damage. Unlike Mini Gnar, Mega Gnar has an active
W: Wallop. After a brief wind-up, Gnar smashes his massive paws into the ground, damaging and stunning
all enemies in front of him.  Mega Gnar Wallops so hard that he can't be interrupted by stuns during the windup. Since Mini Gnar has no active W, Wallop is always available when Gnar needs to transform. Use it for a surprise transformation when your other
abilities are down!  Mega Gnar isn't quite as bouncy as Mini
Gnar, so while the latter hops,  Mega Gnar just crunches. Crunch is another short leap to a target location,
only this time, Mega Gnar lands with a bang, slowing and literally dealing tons of damage - to
everybody near his landing site.  Note that if you fill your rage bar as Mini Gnar before Hopping onto a target, you'll bounce off them, transform during
the second leap, then Crunch as you land. Use Crunch to dive into teamfights.  Mega Gnar has limited mobility, making Crunch the most effective way to get into the thick of a fight. Finally, Mega Gnar's ultimate is... GNAR!, a huge ability that throws all nearby enemies in
a targeted direction, damaging and slowing them. If Gnar throws an enemy into a wall including turrets and jungle walls they'll take increased damage and
be stunned. Use GNAR! to disable as many enemies as you
can during big teamfights.  It's especially effective
in closed off areas like the jungle and baron pit. Gnar's the only champion in League of Legends whose
transform you can't directly control. When facing a teamfight, you'll play differently depending on which form you're in, or about to be in. Here are some quick combos to help you crunch into and hop out of fights. If you're about to become Mega Gnar, here's
your typical game plan for initiating on a teamfight. As Mini Gnar, first keep your distance --  as you're about to transform, get in position to hop in. Find a bouncy target and use E to catapult
yourself into the enemy team.  Once there, use GNAR! to shove enemy champions towards the nearest wall or obstacle. Follow up with Wallop on any stunned targets
before throwing a house...party. While Mega Gnar's got the health and resistances
to take a pummeling, Mini Gnar's just a fragile little Teemo in comparison and needs range to survive. If you're already fighting as Mega Gnar,
use Wallop just before turning mini, taking advantage of Hyper's speed boost to gain some distance  as you throw out a boomerang towards your pursuing enemies.  Save hop for emergencies, focusing on attacking and Q'ing to whittle your opponents down from range. When laning with Mini Gnar, use your range
to your advantage.  Trade with your boomerang and basic
attacks to trigger hyper and bully your opponent so you can farm safely. Mega Gnar lanes very differently.  His AoE abilities give him great wave clear for shoving the lane--just keep an eye out for ganks,  'cause he's way less mobile in this form. Past level 6, Mega Gnar can also chain his abilities together to keep his enemies stunned and slowed. Leading in with Wallop, he uses the stun to
land his ultimate  and combos Boulder Toss with Ignite for surprising burst damage. Gnar has the potential to turn around fights
by using his transformations effectively.  Though he's outnumbered and low on health,  Gnar uses Skarner as a springboard to burst down Syndra with his MEGA combo.  Note that Gnar has great burst, but lacks the sustained damage to continue fighting Skarner. He instead uses Boulder Toss and
Wallop to stall for time, knowing that once his rage bar depletes he'll get Hyper's speed boost as he transforms back into Mini Gnar, at which point he's
pretty much uncatchable. Keep in mind that Gnar can hope over small walls to engage in fights. Note that by catching his boomerang with every throw, Mini Gnar's able to deal better sustained damage and, since his abilities share the same cooldown between forms, have his boulder toss ready for when he goes Mega. Gnar has excellent positioning here, able to shove Lucian into the turret with his ultimate,  locking him up with Wallop for the quadra kill. Thanks for tuning in to the Gnar Champion Spotlight. Please subscribe to the Riot Games YouTube channel and leave us your comments just below the video. Hugeify!

titre: Viktor, the Herald of the Arcane | Champion Update Trailer - League of Legends
transcription: It is peculiar... how humankind is determined... to struggle. How they resist change. Transformation. Evolution. They cling to suffering... because it is familiar. They flee from perfection... because they cannot comprehend it. It falls to me, then. To lead them. To free them. To show them. I alone see what must be done. To save them... from themselves.

there's a spike in his band rate chart right here what is that spike all about in march of 2018 guinsoo's rageblade saw a rework the cost was changed from 3600 gold down to 3 000 but the changes to the passive are the more interesting part the passive that granted stats went from a flat 18 attack damage and 24 ability power to 24 bonus aed and 24 bonus ap right now you're probably thinking well okay jin's passive gives him a crazy amount of bonus a.d in order to compensate for his reloading and slow attacks so 24 bonus is pretty nuts but the item in general could not have been very good for him it still gives ap which you don't really want and attack speed is a fine stat for gin but it's not a priority and of course it has a terrible build path except for the fact that it had a bug according to riot the math of the bonus stats were being applied recursively not multiplicatively this meant that he was gaining absurd amounts of attack damage it was far and away one of the most broken things we've ever seen in league of legends and i mean that literally too because within the game it just wasn't working correctly it was straight up breaking it became quite easy to get well over 1000 ad with a normal build and not in the practice tool just for fun testing you could expect to have around 1400 to 1600 ad at max rageblade stacks this did not fly under the radar for pro play either as the lck over in korea decided on a ruling that would disable the use of rageblade gin in the game by any means should mention it's our first gin on patch 8.6 it's worth noting that right now we have been told that rageblade is disabled for gin what that means is if the gin player buys rageblade on patch 8.6 he will be forced to go back to base and sell it and take the gold loss now they have the full information but they cannot build rageblade but there's always those tendencies the item is still clickable it is not allowed this came to an end a couple of weeks later when riot would give a little explanation as to what happened they made sure that no more attack damage well into the several thousands would keep happening and they explained how the math was working out back to jyn without rageblade his standard crit build that we most commonly see would not work his way back into the meta until around season 10. considering all of the buffs that he received and a buff to stormraiser at the end of last season it meant that he started to get some momentum and became pretty dang strong he could snowball hard out of the laning phase and for a while there it was popular to build multiple infinity edges three or four ies turned this marksman into an assassin once he got in range with huge ad numbers and massive crits at the 2020 world championship there was an 80 carry from the lpl who would make a name for himself not just because of how good he was but also specifically how good his gin really was and that is of course wong fung he would show some things on this champion that we've almost never seen before like flanking the enemy from their own base playing hyper aggressive and getting into the backline to set up a perfect ultimate his skill on this awesome champion would become a huge reason why sunning had success going into pre-season 11 he was a solid a or even s-tier pick not quite as good as some of the new champions like samira but he more than got the job done however with the item changes that came with this new season there may not have been a single other champion aside from amumu that benefited more from these new items you all know what's coming next just a couple of weeks ago with the pre-season patch he became a monster so i knew the nerfs were coming and that's pretty much the only thing i was thinking about at the time i was like you know what they'll probably nerf him and he'll probably still be okay which fast forward he is still okay and other than that i would say i was just happy that i was finally relevant as a player so for like a long time i was very used to picking jin in high hilo and people just saying like i'm tired of people trolling and picking jin on my team like why do you do this and then you know people would just flame me all game if we lost because they would blame it on the pick you know me picking gin or people would just dodge me all the time so after him finally being relevant um in season 10 and also starting to be you know quite relevant in season 11 it feels very nice being able to pick my champion feel useful feel impactful and you know just knowing that i have some impact in my game and it makes him way more enjoyable to play because back then he was always enjoyable but it was those one good game every 10 bad games that would make him enjoyable and keep me going but now it's like every game that i play of gin win or lose is always enjoyable and i'm hoping he stays that way i know you'll probably get nerfed again or you know the meta will shift and as long as he's like average or above average that's the best that i can hope for preseason 11 introduced the strongest version of gin that we have ever seen for him on patch 10.24 his solo queue stats are pretty crazy particularly because of his extremely high play rate in platinum plus he had a 20 pick rate a 30 band rate and a 52.60 win rate on that patch he was the most picked champion in the game had one of the highest band rates while also holding the highest win rate for all adc's the absurd power of this champion was thanks to two new items eclipse and collector these two lethality items gave him all of the tools to one shot you and turn this marksman into a proper assassin and because of how overtuned eclipse was at the time he could kite you with a shield and movement speed boost and survive your damage now of course jyn is not the only champion to abuse these two items miss fortune was doing that in the preseason as well but as it stands right now jyn is still very strong with collector as a second item the collector is a straight up bread and butter item for gin it gives him his two favorite stats in one lethality and crit the execute portion of the item also synergizes very well with jyn's kit considering that he already has missing health damage in his abilities another change that i haven't seen too many gin players talk about is the ravenous hunter change last season the rune page of choice for gin was typically fleet footwork with sorcery secondary but now it's almost always dark harvest this is because ravenous hunter has been changed to omni-vamp which is a big buff for jyn being able to life steal and spell vamp with one rune even with riot trying to directly tune him down with nerfs the prominence of gale force into collector has meant that he stayed a top tier 80 carry in season 11 jyn reminds me in a way of champions like dr mundo or shyvana or udyr some of these simple champions can become strong when their items let them stat check you because their abilities are so tragically basic and have very little interaction the synergies with items are what is able to bring them into the meta it's the fact that they become able to kill you and you are unable to kill them jin's kit is very well designed and completely the opposite of those simple champions he has tons of outplay potential for himself and the enemy can also easily outplay gin if they're good enough but right now he isn't required to do some of the same things that he used to do because of these marksman items his passive loves raw stats and with a build of gale force collector infinity edge bloodthirster and lord dominic's all five of those items give crit when you tack on lethality and lifesteal to this build and the op gale force active all of a sudden you can see why this champion has become extremely powerful it has been an up and down roller coaster for this champion over the last couple of years he's fell to his lowest lows and recently hit his highest highs we're going to continue seeing him in competitive play this season so if that's your thing you can be excited to watch your favorite pros pilot him for solo q if you're thinking about picking up gin now is probably the best time ever to do so of all of the champions in league of legends that players could point to as being well designed i believe jyn is right there at the top of the list and in the conversation every single time it would not be unheard of for me to call him the coolest champion in the game what i love most about him is the consistency of his thematic and his gameplay he strikes a perfect balance of being easy to play and then hard to master it really isn't all that tricky to play him for the first time and get the hang of him he feels very clean and fluid to play but when you're practiced on him when you reach the level of guys like i keep it taco you can hit those crucial shots with your ultimate you can snipe people with your w you can kite and position better in team fights and you can move in more aggressively for the fourth shot i'm also not somebody that ever reads league of legends lore and does not interest me all that much yet i still believe that jin's lore is excellent it's creative it's incredibly thought-provoking this marksman deadeye assassin type killer who wants to build up to a kill who wants to execute you perfectly i also love the fact that during his ultimate it plays the music you can say whatever you want about riot's team and any criticism you might have of riot is perfectly understandable any criticism you might have of riot august or other people who have worked on this game i still feel like is valid but one thing you cannot say at least in my eyes is that they did not absolutely kill it with jin this champion should be the framework almost the foundation for how champions should be designed in this game and i also need to give one more shout out to taco who helped out with this video please check out his twitch stream in the description down below follow him on all of his socials he was incredibly helpful and gave some insights on certain things about jyn in this video so go show him some love this has been a look at the complete history of gin i really hope that you guys enjoyed this video and if you did it would seriously mean a lot to me to give it a like and leave a comment for the youtube algorithm i can't understate how much it really helps these videos get to people and recommend it to them but either way if you really did make it this far that's awesome and i thank you all for the support if you want to see video editing tutorials you can check out my patreon like i said in the beginning using the link in the description down below if you want to see more stuff from me that's also the best place to support me and i'll keep you guys updated and posted on my youtube community about the next video thank you so much for being patient i understand that these videos do take a really long time to make but everything that you guys do for me whether it's a like a subscribe or just leaving a comment saying that the video made your day that really does make my day too anyway thank you guys so much and i will see you next time [Music] so [Music] you
