titre: Elise: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Bienvenue dans le Focus sur Elise, la Reine araignée. Elise est un hybride de mage à distance et d'assassin de mêlée ou de combattant.  En utilisant ses formes d'humaine et d'araignée, elle tente d'isoler sa proie pour la tuer avec ses compétences et ses araignées. Commençons par l'ultime d'Elise, automatiquement appris au niveau 1, qui lui permet d'alterner entre Forme humaine et Forme arachnéenne.  En prenant sa Forme arachnéenne, elle libère des araignées, inflige des dégâts bonus avec ses attaques de base et augmente sa vitesse de déplacement, son armure et sa résistance magique. Sa compétence passive est Horde d'araignées.  En Forme humaine, quand elle touche un ennemi avec une compétence, elle prépare une araignée, jusqu'à un maximum.  En activant la Forme arachnéenne, elle libère ses araignées, lesquelles combattent à ses côtés jusqu'à leur mort ou jusqu'à ce qu'Elise reprenne sa Forme humaine. Voyons ses compétences en Forme humaine. Neurotoxine blesse un ennemi et inflige des dégâts bonus en fonction de ses PV actuels.  Plus il est en forme, plus il subit de dégâts. Araignée explosive va dans la zone ciblée et explose sur le premier ennemi touché. Si elle ne touche rien, elle cherche un ennemi proche ou explose au bout de quelques secondes. Quand Araignée explosive explose, elle blesse tous les ennemis proches. Cocon étourdit un court instant le premier ennemi touché. Passons aux compétences en Forme arachnéenne. Morsure venimeuse fonce sur un ennemi et le blesse, infligeant des dégâts bonus en fonction de ses PV manquants.  Plus il est blessé, plus il subit de dégâts.  Je conseille de mettre un point dans Neurotoxine/Morsure venimeuse au niveau 2 et de les maximiser au niveau 13 pour plus de dégâts contre les cibles isolées. Frénésie symbiotique augmente grandement la vitesse d'attaque d'Elise et de ses araignées pendant quelques secondes.  Quand Frénésie symbiotique est active, les attaques d'Elise et de ses araignées soignent Elise. Mettre un point dans Araignée explosive/Frénésie symbiotique au niveau 1 et les maximiser en premier accroît votre présence dans la jungle et facilite le farm sur votre voie. Avec Suspension, Elise et ses araignées grimpent en l'air, devenant impossibles à cibler et révélant les ennemis proches. Si Suspension est lancée sur un ennemi, elle lui tombe immédiatement dessus. Si Suspension n'a pas de cible, Elise reste en l'air un court instant et la compétence peut être réutilisée sur un ennemi proche pour lui tomber dessus.  Mieux vaut vite mettre un point dans Cocon/Suspension, mais vous pouvez les maximiser en dernier. Pour jungler, débutez en Forme humaine et lancez Araignée explosive.  Passez immédiatement en Forme arachnéenne et activez Frénésie symbiotique pour vider les camps.  Pour aller d'un camp à un autre, restez en Forme arachnéenne, car son bonus en vitesse de déplacement vous aide à être plus rapide dans la jungle. Pour ganker, mieux vaut être en Forme humaine et engager avec Cocon et les autres compétences. Activez ensuite la Forme arachnéenne pour tuer votre proie avec Morsure venimeuse et Frénésie symbiotique.  Gardez Suspension pour rattraper les ennemis en fuite. Si vous ne pouvez pas rattraper votre proie, engagez le combat avec Suspension.  Après avoir pourchassé la cible avec Morsure venimeuse et Frénésie symbiotique, passer en Forme humaine permet de placer Cocon facilement.  Ensuite, vous pouvez reprendre la Forme arachnéenne pour achever l'ennemi avec Morsure venimeuse. Parfois, les ennemis peuvent anticiper un gank si vous êtes trop agressif.  Avec Elise, vous n'avez pas à faire ce genre d'actions.  Jayce vient ganker Gragas. Quand Gragas tente de fuir, Elise passe en Forme arachnéenne, place Suspension et l'achève avec Morsure venimeuse. Comme vous gankerez souvent, vous vous retrouverez parfois derrière la tourelle ennemie.  Après avoir infligé des dégâts initiaux, Elise s'éloigne de la tourelle ennemie pour éviter ses attaques.  Pendant ses récupérations, elle revient avec Jarvan,  elle lance Cocon et Araignée explosive en restant hors de portée de la tourelle pour assurer le kill sans risque. Suspension révélant les ennemis dans le brouillard de guerre, Elise est parfaite pour contre-jungler ou réagir au contre-jungling adverse.  Elise peut se laisser tomber pour voler son propre buff du golem et immobiliser Lee Sin avec Cocon.  Bien qu'il parvienne à fuir, Ziggs peut lui régler son compte grâce à Elise. Dans la jungle, essayez de rester près des murs des camps. Cela peut vous sauver. Evelynn attaque Elise. Sachant qu'elle ne peut pas fuir normalement, Elise lance Suspension,  révèle le camp de spectres et atterrit de l'autre côté du mur, ce qui lui permet de survivre. En combat d'équipes, Elise doit vite anéantir une cible puis rester prudente en attendant de trouver une autre cible.  Ralentissons l'action pour voir ce qui se passe.  Quand elle repère Leona, Elise fonce avec Morsure venimeuse et inflige de gros dégâts avec ses araignées. Son équipe réussit l'élimination.  Affaiblie, Elise se replie et reprend sa Forme humaine pour attaquer à distance.  Depuis les herbes hautes, elle tire Cocon et enchaîne avec Neurotoxine pour tuer Lee Sin.  Elise voit Evelynn et Graves, tous deux affaiblis,  alors elle active sa Forme arachnéenne, lance Suspension et retombe pour tuer Graves avec Morsure venimeuse.  Tandis qu'elle fuit, elle tire Cocon vers le nord pour que la Reine araignée ennemie ne la pourchasse pas. Même chose lors d'un autre combat d'équipes.  En Forme humaine, Cocon, Araignée explosive et Neurotoxine infligent des dégâts initiaux à Lee Sin. Elise s'éloigne ensuite des adversaires avant de se tourner avec Tristana vers Leona.  Après avoir placé Cocon, elle prend sa Forme arachnéenne et fonce avec Morsure venimeuse pour la tuer.  Bien que Lee Sin l'ait repoussée au loin, Elise reste à la recherche d'une proie.  Elle va vers le mur, utilise Suspension, trouve Lee Sin et lui tombe dessus pour le tuer. Pour le build d'Elise, je conseille de mélanger dégâts, durabilité et réduction des délais.   Voici un exemple de build qui fonctionne bien, même si vous jouez avec elle pour la première fois. Prenez des marques de pénétration magique, des sceaux d'armure, des glyphes de résistance magique et des quintessences de puissance. Pour les maîtrises : 9/12/9, avec la pénétration magique en Attaque, la durabilité en Défense et la régénération du mana et la durée des buffs neutres en Utilitaire. Prenez Embrasement pour jouer sur une voie ou Châtiment pour jungler, plus Saut éclair. Débutez avec Bottes de vitesse, des Potions de soin et quelques Anneaux de Doran ou un Anneau du prospecteur. Achetez vite Sceptre de Rylai, puis prenez Chaussures de sorcier et Sceptre abyssal pour infliger plus de dégâts.  Ensuite, améliorez votre durabilité et la réduction de vos délais avec des objets tels que Cœur gelé, Sablier de Zhonya et Ange gardien. Merci d'avoir suivi ce Focus sur Elise. Abonnez-vous ci-dessus à la chaîne YouTube de Riot Games et laissez-nous vos commentaires sous la vidéo !

titre: BEST Champions for EVERY RANK in EVERY Role | League of Legends
transcription: Today, we're going to be breaking down the best champions to main at every rank in every role to help you guys climb before the final split of season 2025 comes to a close. We'll break it down into master and above, diamond, emerald, platinum, gold, and then silver, and bronze together. And no, we won't be including iron because if you're still stuck there after 2,000 games, you don't really need this video. You need professional help. 2026 is just around the corner and before you know it, you'll be smashing down some pigs in blankets and Christmas turkey and opening riot points gift cards and maybe a few packs of Riftbound if you're lucky. But you've still got time to climb to a new peak and brag about it to your deaf grandmother while she drinks her Christmas brandy. So, let's start off with the top lane first then and we'll start from the bottom and work our way up. First up, we have Garen with a 52.7% win rate in silver and 51.6% in bronze. Now, I probably don't have to do too much explaining as to why Garen is a solid top lane pick to main in low elo because to be honest, I think if you handed a three-year-old a mouse and keyboard and said, "Go spin, press R," they might actually do a decent job. That being said, Garen has a lot to offer. Easy all-in potential, crazy lane sustain, as overstaying in this elo is a given, and of course, carry potential, too. Because if you do get ahead on Garand when you inevitably get ganked from over pushing, you're probably going to come out on top anyway. If you're a gold top lane main, then you need to start playing set. This muscly mahusive mammoth is a bit of a beast right now. And his ability to 1v2 ganks, dive, counter dives, and just bulldoze his way through team fights make him a real problem for so many different matchups and comps. And the best part is when you outplay your enemy, you can then do a bunch of situps because if you're playing League, you're probably not doing them IRL. In platinum, it's the rock with a 53.5% win rate. And yes, that is very high. But when you think about it, it just makes sense. Everybody in platinum thinks they are the bees needs the next big thing with so many Riven and Yasawo one-trip wannabes with huge egos flying around all over the place. So, pick Malight and remove their ability to play the game. Simple. Our emerald pick is Kale with a 52.9% win rate. And here is when those egos go up a notch even further and truly is the land of the throws. Emerald is probably one of the most dense ranks in the game with a huge variety of skill discrepancies and throws happening in almost every single game, which is part of the reason why Kale is such a good pick here. Because if games get to a certain point, she turns into a machine gunning pentacill farming ascending queen of LP. So lock her in. Don't in early game. Relax, chill, get some farm, focus on XP, and wait for your inevitable moment to bring home the win. Into diamond now. And to be honest, there is a lot of choice here, but we've gone for Darius, as diamond players are fairly easy to tilt, and who better to tilt other top laners than this delicious dunking daddy. Darius has the ability to completely dominate in some matchups and can feel unstoppable once he gets a lead. Plus, he's one of those picks that autofilled top laners absolutely hate to play against and often end up 05 by the 10-minute mark simply because they don't actually understand how to exist in the same lane as him. Then finally, we have master tier plus, and it just has to be Fiora with a 55.2% win rate. Fiora is the ultimate split pushing sidelaning dueling pick that can completely take over a game once she has a lead. and higher ranking players know how to push her limits to constantly defy the odds and take multiple enemies down at once. Along with every single tower in the game whilst wasting the entire enemy team's time. So yeah, if you know how to play her and understand those side lane miniames, she can be pretty hard to beat. Into the jungle next then and starting off with bronze and silver, we've got Brier, the champion whose whole personality is get fed or die trying. With a 54% win rate in silver and a 53.2% 2% win rate in bronze. She's proving to be one of the best picks to go for here. So, aside from her natural ability to int, she's actually fairly easy to play and it's pretty much all or nothing. And with her ability to just send it and stat check most other junglers, this plays into her favor most of the time at these lower ranks. She clears the jungle fast and easily. She does well in chaotic and extended fights, and low players just haven't really got a clue how to play against her. Then for gold, it's going to have to be Amumu with a 53.4% 4% win rate here. Now, gold is the land of accidental AAMS and constant team fighting as soon as one tower goes down. That's just how it is there. So, who better to play than one of the easiest champions you can go for with a game-winning ultimate. It really is as simple as that, guys. His clear is a piece of cake. And despite Amumu not having any friends, you'll be getting plenty of requests after carrying with seven fiveman ultimates in a row. Our platinum pick in the jungle is going to be Jax with a 52.8% win rate. And similar to Mauight we mentioned earlier, there's a ton of wannabe one-trick picks in and around this elo, so Jax is the perfect pick to shut them down. He doesn't really lose many early 1v ones and he's always useful with his AOE stun and DPS. He takes down camps without too much trouble, has easy ganks, kills objectives, and scales really hard later on, so basically just ticks every box you need. For Emerald, Warrick is the one to go for here with a 53.8% win rate. Emerald is a place where respect just doesn't exist. And if you don't respect Warrick, you're instantly going to regret it. He'll zoom around the map and hunt you down, win fights you wouldn't think he's going to be able to win. He melts through objectives, and he's just a bit of a problem once he gets fed because before you know it, he's dashing across your entire screen with a huge ranged ultimate, locking you down and making you regret queuing up. So, if you're an Emerald jungler and you haven't tried him recently, here's a reminder to give him a go. Now for Diamond, we've got a bit of a wild card pick because his popularity might not be that high, but his stats are undeniable with a 53.9% win rate across all regions. It's Ivvern. So many players don't give Ivvern a chance because he's not a traditional jungler. And a lot of people straight up refuse to play him because they probably don't actually understand how he works. But trust me, you need to give him a try. He's an amazing addition to any team comp and his utility and unique play style brings so much to the table. So walts around the map humming and singing as you pop by a lane, get some free kills and cruise around doing your thing and farm in easy LP. Finally, for Master and Beyond, Nidley is just the best jungler to pick right now with a 56% win rate in high elo. Nidi has always had a reputation for being a bit of a problem in the right hands. And this is even more true than ever with her stats matching her stature. From max range spears making you let out a suspicious gasp IRL to big clutch pounces and skirmishing and snowballing and 25 stacked magi shining through in the reflection of your bloodshot eyeballs. Little is a lot of fun and if you've got what it takes there's not many better. Into the mid lane next then. And first up we have Maar with a 52% win rate in bronze and 52.4% in silver. Mahar is the perfect champion to play here as it feels like the game just plays itself for you. move around a bit, use some spells, watch the minions evaporate in front of you, and just permanently have lane priority, so you can wander if you need to, and lock someone up with your ultimate. He definitely does fall off the higher rank you go, but there's no argument that he's not a top choice at lower ranks. Into gold next then, and here we have Vigar with a 52.6% win rate, and his cage alone is probably enough to have him here, as every enemy player will walk into it every single time. It can easily win you games on its own by locking up teams in choke points, setting up ganks, or outplaying dives. And then you factor in how hard this dude scales and his 200 years point-and-click one-shot bye-bye button in his ultimate. For platinum, we've got Diana with a 52.8% win rate. And all it takes for you here is to learn your damage limits really because she can just dash in and deal a ton of damage a few times before doing it again and taking the enemy mid laner out. Then you have her ability to carry harder outside of the lane phase as she dashes into grouped up teams, pops her ultimate, and probably wins most fights in just a few clicks. For Emerald, it's time to get that frozen chicken out of the freezer like your mom told you to 5 hours ago, but you've been playing League Day and you forgot your mom existed. >> I'm in danger. >> Ania is a fantastic midlane pick to play in Emerald with a 52.5% win rate. She's got lane control, wave clear, utility, CC, scaling, and some underrated burst damage, too. And she can be one of the most frustrating enemies to play against if you do get good enough with her. For Diamond, it's going to be Vex, who has a whopping 54.4% win rate across all regions. Now, with a ton of highly mobile menaces running around the mid lane in Diamond, why not play someone who can just abuse them like Vex can? Not only can she survive or shut these mobility matchups down, but she can snowball game super hard by roaming around with her ultimate and resetting her way through teams. Finally, for master tier and above, we have Syndra. Arguably the most consistent mage should play in the mid lane into all sorts of comps and matchups. And with a 53.5% win rate in master plus, she's up there with the best. Synra has basically everything a good player needs to carry. wave clear, poke, burst around her level six and nine power spikes, massive AoE CC to set up ganks and carry fights, and she scales well, too. She truly shines in the mid game, and with there being so many constant team fights these days around the many objectives you need to fight over, she's just such a great option to main in the mid lane. Into the bot lane next then, and let's take a look at the best AD carries you can play at every rank. However, the first pick in bronze and silver is actually AP. One that doesn't really require much attack moving and just a ton of bombs instead. It's Ziggs with a 52.1% win rate in bronze and 52.3% in silver. Ziggs is a super safe scaling wave clear bursty farming beast really. And you can't really go wrong with him. He has the highest win rate for any pick in the bot lane at these ranks and a decent amount of games played too. to push, poke, farm up, spam your bombs, and make your mark into gold then. And here we have Misfortune with a 51.8% win rate. Misfortune has always been a popular pick for the average player, and it's again fairly simple because even if you don't really achieve that much, all you need is one good moment with a big ultimate in a big team fight to really have an impact. Now, I'm not saying that Misfortune isn't more than just an ultimate as she can snowball super hard in lanes and carry hard too, especially in duo with the gold fiesta gameplay. Just play around your ultimate's cool down and you'll see great results. In plat, it's actually Vain who comes out on top with a 53% win rate here. Now, Vain is typically associated with higher mechanically gifted players, but actually, it's likely the fact that Vain is overrespected and underpunished in this rank to the point where once she gets those items, she can just roll over lobbies. The hardest part about maning Vain is the early game and the amount of difficult matchups she has. But if every game you're getting through the lane phase without any trouble, you'll be carrying team fights before you know it. In Emerald, we have one of the strongest AD carries in the game as of late, Ash, who has a 53% win rate across all regions recently. To be honest, Ash is just in a really good spot right now and offers more than enough to carry. She's a bit of a bully in lane, works with a bunch of different support picks, scales really well, kites like a beast, and even if she gets a bit behind, still has value to bring to comps. Emerald is where players tend to really start learning how to kite and attack move properly, and Ash is the perfect pick to use this to your advantage. In Diamond, it's Jinx who proves on top here with a 52.8% win rate. Jinx has been one of the most consistent AD carries all year really, and it doesn't look like this is going to slow down soon. She's a bit vulnerable early game, but all it takes is one fight with a few resets for her to show why she's always worth thinking about. A good jinx player who gets fed is never fun to play against. And in Diamond, there's plenty of good jinxes. Then finally for Master Plus, we have Kaiser with a 53% win rate. Kai'Sa is one of the most versatile AD carries in the game and one of the most consistent picks you can play into any draft. That's one of the main reasons why she's so popular in high elo because a champion's ability to be blind picked is one of the most desirable traits they can possess. Matchups are a huge thing in modern League of Legends and have been for quite a few years now. So, a flexible AD carry like Kaiser who works into so many lanes and with and against so many supports and carries is just so ideal. Plus, when you factor in her itemization options too, depending on the game and what you and your team needs, getting good at Kaiser will give you a solid pick to play every game. Finally, let's have a look at supports then. And up first, we have Brand with a 53.2% win rate in silver and 52.5% in bronze. This just goes to show how strong a poke mage can be in the bot lane when your enemies don't actually know how to dodge. Bran support is just not really played very much at higher ranks, but in bronze and silver, he's one of the best picks you can play. He's a lot of fun to play, and you can carry super hard, too, as you basically just turn into another AP carry if you get ahead during the lane phase. So, spam those spells and enjoy watching your enemies burn into crispy little piles of LP. For gold, we have Sona with a 52.6% win rate. Now, Sona is just undeniably impactful just by existing. So many games you can see a bad pike or bad Thresh just whiff everything and do nothing. But with Sona, you don't really have anything to miss. And all you have to do is not int and you'll be super valuable to your AD carry and your team. She brings sustain, scaling, utility, and AoE crowd control. And in goal, with so many A rams and extended team fights happening from the 15minute mark until the end of the game, she'll bring so much to your team come. For plats, we've got Milio with a 53.3% win rate. Milio really has been shining recently, and he's pretty easy to play, but his utility is so strong. He makes all the popular AD carries and plat just better and much harder to punish. and enemy players here just don't respect what he brings in his kit. Medio is one of those picks that can turn any doomed fight into a win. So when your enemies overcommit, this is when he's at his best. In Emerald, we have the solo Q queen in Janna with a 52.4% win rate. Janna has been one of the most consistent picks to play in ranked for so many years now, and around Emerald seems to be where she's at her best. She can chill in lane in games where she needs to, like when she's partnered up with an unstable racist straven, or she can roam like a psycho and actually carry the map pretty hard instead. She can turn any team fight with a few clutch tornadoes. And her ultimate can outplay fights and dives more often than not. In Diamond, we have Nami with a 52.1% win rate, and she's been one of the best support picks in the game all season. Patches come and go, but Nami still stays on top, and that's been the theme of this year. And we could have put Nami for many of the ranks in this video. But in Diamond, she is very popular and super successful. From big bubbles, welltimed waves, or just boosting and buffing up her carries, she's fun, easy to play, and with good positioning and good timings can go from decent to delicious. Finally, for master tier and above, you know it, it's going to have to be Bard. With a 53.3% win rate, a good bard is just so much fun to watch with big clutch stuns in the lane phase, roaming, and creative plays with big ults and magical tunnels full of LP, and so many ways to impact games rather than just AFKing in the lane phase and hoping for the best. High elo bar players have been abusing his kit for many years now. And when he's as strong as he has been recently, there's just simply no better option to carry with. But also, no other champion can compare to how fun this chime chap can be. Well, there you have it, guys. That's some of the best champions you can play in every rank, and every role to end the season 2025 on a high. Don't forget to head to mobileitics.gg to see builds and runes and guides for every single champion, and we'll see you all in the next one. Thanks for watching, and as always, guys, good luck and take care.

titre: Rell: The Iron Maiden | Champion Trailer - League of Legends
transcription: The helpless fight. The hardened live. That's what they taught me. What they forced down my throat. They wanted me to be strong. Ruthless. Cold as iron. Well... They got their wish.

titre: Ahri: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Bienvenue dans le Focus consacré à Ahri, le Renard à neuf queues. Ahri est un assassin magique très mobile. En utilisant des compétences de tir, des charges et en se plaçant correctement, Ahri excelle à éliminer des cibles isolées et à semer le chaos dans l'équipe ennemie. Le passif d'Ahri est Dévoreuse d'âme. Lorsqu'un de ses sorts touche un ennemi, Ahri gagne une charge de Dévoreuse d'âme. Et une fois le nombre de charges au maximum, le sort suivant bénéficie d'un effet Sort vampirique important. Orbe d'illusion permet de projeter l'orbe d'Ahri, puis de le faire revenir, infligeant des dégâts magiques à l'aller et des dégâts bruts au retour. Je prends Orbe d'illusion au niveau 1 et je le maximise au niveau 9. Lucioles permet de créer trois flammes qui orbitent autour d'Ahri. Ces flammes partent ensuite à l'attaque des ennemis qui s'approchent trop près. Une même cible peut être frappée plusieurs fois, mais chaque coup après le premier inflige des dégâts réduits. Je prends Lucioles au niveau 4 et je maximise cette compétence en dernier. Charme envoie un sort qui inflige des dégâts au premier ennemi touché et le charme. Les ennemis charmés sont ralentis et contraints d'avancer en direction d'Ahri pendant quelques instants. Je prends Charme au niveau 2 et je le maximise au niveau 13. L'ultime d'Ahri est Assaut spirituel. À l'activation, Ahri fonce en direction du curseur et blesse jusqu'à trois ennemis proches. Ahri peut effectuer jusqu'à deux charges supplémentaires dans les quelques secondes qui suivent la première charge d'Assaut spirituel avant que la compétence ne passe en phase de récupération. En début de partie, je pense à compter le nombre de charges de Dévoreuse d'âme. Lorsque les charges sont au maximum, j'utilise Orbe d'illusion pour frapper la vague de sbires la plus importante possible et récupérer un maximum de PV. Une fois que j'ai appris Assaut spirituel, je vais ganker sur les autres voies. Je fais l'erreur d'engager avec Charme, au lieu d'attendre pour attaquer sans danger. J'utilise Assaut spirituel pour franchir le mur et je m'éloigne rapidement de la tourelle, pour ne pas me retrouver bloqué à portée. Yorick et moi abattons l'adversaire avant que Skarner n'arrive. Je le touche avec Orbe d'illusion et j'active Lucioles avant d'aller droit sur lui pour l'abattre avant de battre en retraite avec Yorick. Pendant que mon équipe fait le siège de la tourelle du milieu, j'essaie d'aller provoquer les adversaires avec mes compétences. En la touchant d'abord avec Charme, je peux envoyer Orbe d'illusion sur Annie sans qu'elle l'esquive. Là encore, je surveille mes charges de Dévoreuse d'âme et j'utilise Lucioles pour déclencher l'effet de Sort vampirique avec Orbe d'illusion. Assaut spirituel est à nouveau disponible et Annie n'est pas en position. J'active Lucioles, je charge avec Assaut spirituel, je lance Charme et je suis avec Orbe d'illusion. J'utilise mes deux charges restantes d'Assaut spirituel pour échapper à Singed. Ahri est parfaite pour surprendre les adversaires. Là, Malzahar tente de défendre sa base, mais un Assaut spirituel suivi d'un Charme permet de le bloquer. J'enchaîne avec Lucioles et une charge d'Assaut spirituel pour envoyer un Orbe d'illusion à bout portant et le tuer. J'utilise ensuite ma dernière charge d'Assaut spirituel pour fuir la tourelle. Lorsque tout le monde se regroupe pour le combat, j'essaie de garder mes distances et je n'utilise que mon attaque standard jusqu'à ce qu'une ouverture se présente. C'est le cas quand les PV de Lux commencent à être suffisamment bas : je charge avec Assaut spirituel deux fois de suite et je la tue avec Lucioles avant d'utiliser la troisième charge d'Assaut spirituel pour me remettre en position. Nos adversaires nous attaquent près de Nashor. Je commence à attaquer Skarner, car je ne peux assassiner personne avec tous ces contrôles de foule dans l'équipe ennemie. Nous parvenons à le tuer assez rapidement, car j'utilise Assaut spirituel non pas pour charger les champions ennemis, mais pour blesser les ennemis proches, Skarner et Singed. Quand Skarner à terre, je commence à chercher des cibles plus rapides à tuer, comme Annie, et j'envoie Charme puis Lucioles pour la tuer. La plus grosse partie de l'équipe adverse à terre, je peux jouer plus agressivement en allant jusqu'à Singed pour utiliser Orbe d'illusion et Lucioles afin de le tuer. Le combat est un 4 contre 1 désormais et je laisse mon équipe s'occuper de Lux pour retourner vers nos sbires et soutenir l'assaut sur la base adverse. Dans Dominion, le côté très offensif d'Ahri permet vraiment d'aider votre équipe dans les combats d'envergure. Alors que nous attaquons le moulin, je tire sur Tristana et Dr. Mundo, et j'active Lucioles pour aider à éliminer Fizz. En utilisant Embrasement et Orbe d'illusion, j'affaiblis encore plus mes adversaires. Ensuite, j'estime que je peux aller m'attaquer à Tristana. Fantôme, Charme et les attaques de base me permettent de la tuer et je file ensuite vers une relique de soin. Après que nous avons capturé le moulin, Fizz fait son retour. Je sais qu'avec si peu de PV, je ne peux qu'essayer de l'interrompre pendant la capture, mais lorsqu'Alistar vient à la rescousse, j'essaie de l'appâter. Il se jette vers nous avec Joueur/Filou et dès qu'Alistar le touche avec Atomisation, j'enchaîne avec Charme, ce qui permet à Alistar de lui donner un Coup de tête qui l'envoie sous notre tourelle. Toucher les cibles avec les compétences de tir est essentiel quand vous jouez Ahri. Je repère Heimerdinger qui se dirige vers le sanctuaire de vitesse. J'essaie de l'attirer avec Charme, mais il esquive en s'arrêtant sur place. Il esquive ensuite Orbe d'illusion en faisant demi-tour, mais en utilisant à nouveau Assaut spirituel, je le touche quand même. J'ai trop de compétences en récupération, et je fais la bêtise de poursuivre Heimerdinger dans ses tourelles, où je tombe sous les coups combinés des tourelles et des roquettes. En revanche, quand les compétences touchent, les choses se passent différemment. Heimerdinger vient du moulin et je le touche avec Charme. Je peux ensuite le toucher avec Orbe d'illusion sans risque de le voir m'esquiver. En ajoutant Lucioles et Assaut spirituel à l'attaque, j'en finis rapidement avec Heimerdinger et je pars capturer le point. En runes, je prends des marques de pénétration magique, des sceaux de puissance par niveau, des glyphes de puissance par niveau et des quintessences de puissance fixe. Ce build est conçu pour me permettre d'infliger un maximum de dégâts magiques tout au long de la partie. Mes maîtrises sont de type 21/0/9, avec tous les bonus en dégâts magiques et en puissance dans l'arbre offensif, et en profitant du bonus en mana, en régénération de mana et en durée des enchantements. Je prends Embrasement et Saut éclair comme sorts en mode classique, mais je préfère Fantôme à Saut éclair dans Dominion. Dans les deux cas, j'améliore mes deux sorts d'invocateur dans leur arbre respectif. En mode classique, je commence avec un Cristal de saphir et deux Potions de soin. Je passe rapidement au Catalyste protecteur et je prends les Bottes de vitesse avant de passer à Bâton séculaire et à Chaussures de sorcier. J'achète de gros objets de puissance : Coiffe de Rabadon, Volonté des anciens et, si nécessaire, Bâton du vide. Je m'offre ensuite Fléau de liche et je réfléchis à changer mon Bâton séculaire pour un Sceptre de Rylai. Dans Dominion, je choisis pratiquement la même chose, mais je commence par l'Anneau du prospecteur et les Bottes de vitesse, en faisant l'impasse sur le Bâton séculaire. Je continue comme précédemment, en prenant Coiffe de Rabadon, Volonté des anciens et, si nécessaire, Bâton du vide pour contrer la résistance magique de mes adversaires. Comme en mode classique, je prends également Fléau de liche et Sceptre de Rylai. Merci d'avoir suivi ce Focus sur Ahri. Pensez à vous abonner à notre chaîne Riot Games sur YouTube et n'oubliez pas de cliquer sur le pouce sous la vidéo !

titre: Urgot Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription:
Welcome to the League of Legends Champion
Spotlight featuring Urgot, the Dreadnought. Urgot's passive is Echoing Flames. Attacking an enemy near one of Urgot's six
legs will fire out a blast like a shotgun, damaging all enemies in the area. Each leg has its own internal cooldown. Additionally, multiple Echoing Flames to the
same target deal reduced damage. In lane, Urgot's passive - combined with his
ranged auto-attacks lets him weaken his opponent before he goes for the kill. But be careful; the cooldown on each leg is
quite long at lower levels, so if your legs are down, you're the one who's weak. Urgot's Q is Corrosive Charge, which explodes at a location, damaging and slowing everything around it. Enemy champions struck are locked onto. Locked onto for what? That's up next. Urgot's W is Purge. When activated, he gains a shield and slow
resist, but lowers his base movement speed for several seconds. While the effect holds, he automatically fires
at the nearest enemy, or preferentially, the nearest locked onto enemy champion. Like you'd expect, these attacks will trigger
your passive at the correct angles, which means you'll want to reposition around your opponent to get all the damagey goodness. Urgot's E is Disdain. Urgot charges, damaging and pushing side all
enemies he tramples. If he runs into an enemy champion, he'll stop,
stun them, throw them behind him and lock onto them. If Urgot runs into a wall with Disdain, he'll stop at the wall, but won't stun or throw it over his head. Getting on the other side of your opponents
 can be incredibly difficult, so it's hard to shoot all six Echoing Flames. Thankfully, Disdain puts your target right
behind you if it connects, allowing you to get more bang for your buckshot. Urgot's ultimate is Fear Beyond Death. Urgot fires out and impales
 the first enemy champion struck, damaging, slowing based on their missing health, and locking onto them for the duration of the tether. While impaled, things get scary for Urgot's foe. If they fall low enough, Urgot may re-cast
Fear Beyond Death to seal their doom. He suppresses his prey and pulls his chains back in. When his target reaches him, he brutally executes them. If Urgot successfully grinds an enemy, he
terrifies the living who still remain. Purge churns out a ton of damage but it's
up to you to make sure it hits the right targets. Locking onto the right one is paramount to
avoid wasting all of your damage and getting off on the wrong foot...er, knee. Urgot may be beefy, but he's not a tank. His ranges are low so he'll need good positioning
not only to maximize his damage but also to simply stay alive. Stay on the fringes of a fight, looking for
the perfect moment to harvest your first victim with Fear Beyond Death. Try to draw an opponent out
 and unload your rotation on them. Once they're low, you can go for the execute,
even if they try to run away. From there, it's cleanup time. Focus the targets who can't escape you, and
take them down one at a time. The most important skill to learn with Urgot
is choosing the right moment to go all in. Done right, you'll go from Urgot to Urgod. Thanks for tuning into the Urgot Champion Spotlight. Click these links to learn more about League's
newest Star Guardian. I'm kidding, but you will Dread Nought reading
up on everything else.

for something a little bit more neutral like jigs now I just said a key word you may not have really paid much attention to that was that I called Jinx a neutral champion because she can be paired into talking about pick order blind picks and Champion Select in itself now something to remember when you blind pick in Solo Q is that the champion you pick can be counterpicked not only by your so if they go and pick garen's hop and Katarina mid this is the team comp we have now we don't have any Champion synergies our bot lanes are different archetypes our jungler can't gank for literally when you're drafting in Solo Q you want to make sure you have at least one thing in your champion pool like this that can kind of be a multi-tool that fits into any comp and works with any jungle or Lan pairing the idea of neutral Champion picks is a huge reason for why we have a somewhat standardized pick order in Pro play for the last 10 years drafts usually look a little bit like this first we open with whatever OP champion there is on the patch or whatever Flex pick your team might play outside of this though things mainly look the same an opening pick will most likely be an ADC and or a jungler jungle counter picks don't matter too much because you don't directly Lan in against your counterpart and most of what decides a bot Lan's matchup is the support matchup not the edc's what's important though is that you can easily pick neutral champions in these two roles especially with ADC with almost every mid to longrange ADC being a neutral Champion pick then you simply copy your opponent counterpicking the roles that they pick the entire way down the draft so you get good matchups everywhere what's great about doing this is that you don't get locked into having to play a notably a couple of which we've already covered like Rumble carus and Samira committing to a specific style of team composition too early into the draft is generally a pretty bad thing to do because you leave yourself open to being stylistically counted which is what we'll talk about next but real quick before I do I want to take just 20 seconds to share out my patreon you see YouTube ad Revenue quite frankly just sucks and it's only because of my current patreons I have right now that I can justify the time it takes to make a video a week on YouTube for you guys over on my patreon I upload almost daily recordings of my best coaching sessions for you to watch and learn from all sorted by Roland rank so if it's within your means and you want to help support me and maybe even get some counters sometimes a champion pick is just so good you have to pick it one of the most famous examples of this is from this famous clip now we have to go corki no all all of we're going by the way are you serious it's TR should we just go are you serious is it going to win us the game of course we could see the Cory again again that's a mid lane caps was right here when versing four melee tank Champions vain is just situationally amazing this is most easily seen with a lot of Top Lane Bruiser Champion such as Darius Darius is completely immobile and Incredibly susceptible to being kited which is why he's almost never seen in Pro play if you open up a draft with a blind picked Darius you might end up versing an entire team comp of long-range Champions with a ton of crowd control and you would just never get in range of your opponent it's completely unplayable but picking darus against short-range enemy compositions is just heaven on Earth you don't need to worry about getting Ked if the enemy team is also made up of melee Champions completely ating darius's biggest weakness maybe this is something you already knew to a certain extent but what you probably didn't think about is stylistic synergies take a look at this game from World 2024 famously this is where the top laner for BDS Adam picked Darius on the world stage the enemy has a ton of crowd control on their comp this game they don't have the longest range composition in the world but they've still got three range Champions so what gives here how did Adam get away with this Darius pick into such a cc heavy and long-range enemy team composition well that has a ton to do with the champion picks of his own teammates bds's carries are playing syndra and ezil cnra outranges her counterpart Nico and ezil massively outranges his counterpart Kaiser so in a poke battle bds's team comp will just simply win also to note is that BDS has two super tanks with ra and Alistar against the much squishier vgo and raan meaning in a front to back team fight BDS would simply kill the enemy front line first this puts the enemy team in a difficult position they're forced to engage as their carries are being out ranged and out pooped and when they do engage they have to get onto the back line and get on the carries since they won't win a back to front team fight this is where the Darius pick comes in if we put the Darius right on top of our carries and then we may have to dive onto our carries well now the Darius is in range on one of my former pro teams this was the exact drafting style we used to do with Gwen instead of Darius if you try to dive on top of aw you just get destroyed while your range Champions can't follow up because they're met with this annoying Gwen's immune message so what we with the mobile adcs Diego needs a kill to get a reset so he's often paired with the Sandro Nautilus who can easily secure him one and nocturn works super well with Oriana because there's no way to avoid the double ulti combo and this is what we really need to look out for in draft so next time you go into Champion Select don't worry so much about picking the perfect team fight comp you're titre: Akshan: the Rogue Sentinel | Champion Trailer - League of Legends
transcription: I hope I haven't kept you waiting my friend. Yes, I know, I said I wouldn't use your Absolver. But look at it... ...it's magnificent! Aim... Here we go, get in, avenge fallen ally, get out. I don't know how you live with yourself, but you won't have to for long. Fixing the world one scoundrel at a time. Stealth time! I mean...stealth time... Now for my grand escape, grappling hook! The bad part about having friends? Always having to avenge them. There you are, we live to fight another day! But not you, not yet. I know you had other plans for me, Shadya. But I choose vengeance. Sorry! I taunt with my grappling hook. See how it taunts you? Taunt, taunt, taunt, taunt, taunt, taunt, taunt, taunt, taunt, tau-
