titre: The Most HATED League of Legends Champions In History
transcription: every game has characters that players hate for one reason or another but League of Legends players can oftentimes be a little more passionate than other Gamers have you ever seen a fictional character received death threats over the years many champions have taken turns being the center of hate from players within the game whether that's from being overpowered unpleasant to play with or the players who play said Champion having a bad reputation don't worry we'll talk about the cat later today we discuss the most hated Champions throughout the history of the game and speaking of things Gamers hate there's nothing worse than slow computers and a browser sucking up all of your resources so to help with that today's video is sponsored by Opera GX Opera GX is a reliable browser that allows you to customize almost every aspect of your internet browsing this is Made Simple with their built-in GX profiles for example if you're doing work or in classes you can have a profile exactly for that that that keeps your browser focused on Essentials and stops any distracting notifications or perhaps you're a streamer or thinking of streaming you can set up a streaming profile so that none of your personal information pops up while streaming also if you need your browser to use even less of your computer's resources you can set up a profile for that too and then for one reason or another maybe you need a profile that automatically clears all of your browsing data upon closing the session you know for reasons alongside all this Opera GX offers a ton more customization options and features animated backgrounds the hot tabs killer feature which allows you to quickly see which tab is using up your resources and stop it from doing so oh and it's also available for mobile so if any of this sounds good to you download Opera GX today with the first link in the description Teemo armed and ready if you go a few years back that was the answer for the most hated Champion but today players mostly collectively hate newer Champions champions with overloaded kits that include multiple dashes and invulnerabilities today a teemos room is nothing compared to whatever the hell this is an April Fool's joke posted by riot games in 2017 which features a 360 Yasuo wind wall three years later that is samira's W ability move over Yasuo the Tilt Queen is here now before going through specific hated Champions what about using real data over the years to actually determine the most hated Champions technically there is existing data for the most band Champions during each patch so over the years These Are the Champions players mostly don't want to see in their games it's said people don't want to see Zed in their games notably from patch 10.14 made on July 2020 to literally as of making this video the champion Zed has been in the top 5 most band champions for every single patch this is almost three years of being the most banned Champion specifically from August 2021 to today Zed has actually been the number one most band champion on every single patch except for three or four patches where he was overtaken by belveth Master Yi and Samira and although Zed Takes the Cake overall for a while Yasuo was definitely up there in ban raids I remember back in 2017 when Wright announced that they would change the band phase from three Champions to five Champions per team there was real concern that a champion like Yasuo would generally be banned in every game specifically in the lower ranks where someone like Yasuo can really take advantage in the end it was fine mostly because Riot gave us more Champions to worry about also it's kind of difficult to shape up exact percentages for this but at least for the past couple of years the other Champion that's also consistently in the top 5 most band Champions is Morgana which makes sense because no matter what getting hit by this and having to wait a couple minutes before you can play the game again is pretty annoying aside from Zed and Morgana Darius and Master Yi are also frequently in the top five oh and on the flip side as of making this video These are currently the least banned champions in the game now let's get back to the general most hated champions Lux is kind of a unique situation as an in-game character she can definitely be annoying but so can everyone else there's more than just her gameplay with Lux at some point Lux became the poster child for e-girl Champions or in general there was an assumption that lux was mostly played by women so she became pretty unpopular with people who behaved strangely around women but that's a convoluted topic that's not as serious as it seems just a few bad apples and silly stereotypes however among these sentiments existing luck started to receive a lot more hate due to it appearing like she was the developer's favorite Champion always being kept somewhat strong in game and receiving an overwhelming amount of skins compared to other Champions it became a meme that while some Champions hadn't received the skin in years Here Comes Lux with her fourth skin in the past couple of months this obviously has something to do with the fact that very few players are buying skarner skins for example and a popular champ Champion like Lux sells really well however the truth is Lux isn't at the top of the most skins list by herself she does technically have 18 skins but three are Prestige skins and two of them are pretty much the same skin after that she is followed by Misfortune with 17 skins and Ezreal Ari akali and sivir who all have 16. who is by far one of the most hated Champions but he falls into a specific bundle of Champions and players I'd say Yasuo yone Master Yi and maybe vane all share a lot in common though mainly the first two now all of these guys are very useful carry type Champions to have on your team but they also fall into the phenomenon that if they're on the enemy team they have 10 kills I haven't died yet and they're a few seconds away from killing you next but the moment they're on your team they can't get anything to work out they're giving up out of frustration and taking you down with them from my personal experience yone player specifically are consistently some of the worst possible teammates to have they almost always carry in All or Nothing mentality and they're generally not the nicest people in chat was that my first hater seraphine is a champion who was uniquely hated entirely outside of her gameplay obviously this is one of the most talked about subjects already so we won't spend too much time here from her real-life social media presence to her inconsistencies and errors in her lore she quickly became pretty disliked by many players however a valid reason that seraphin received hate for was her similarities to the already long time existing Champion Sona not only does she take her whole music theme but her abilities are very similar as well and despite stealing her whole vibe she still has the audacity to be nice to her in game wow Sona your sound is so beautiful could you produce my next song although there are a handful of Champions that receive a ton of diverse opinions no one has split the player base as much as Yumi from players playing at home like you and I to Esports players being paid a lot of money to play the game all the way to the actual developers everyone has had negative opinions on Yumi on April 26 2019 the gameplay for Riot's latest champion was revealed a support character that could attach to a teammate and use their abilities while being completely invulnerable on that day player's reactions were split some thought it'd be a bad Champion While others believed that the concept could actually be overpowered and in the end both were kind of right due to the way Yumi is in general she's not overpowered but when she's in a good spot she can be very annoying to deal with and the numbers show it in the most recent worlds event Yumi was in a strong State and she was picked or banned in 95 percent of all games she was banned 68 times and only slipped into eight games and in all eight games the team that had Yumi were the winners overall her biggest flaw is that when she is in a good State she's not fun to play against or even fun to play with throughout the years a handful of pro players supported the player's sentiment of outright deleting Yumi from the game this is longtime veteran and World finalist perks speaking his thoughts on Yumi on a broadcast okay if anybody is listening to me if anybody in the whole world and I was listening to me yeah and I'm pretty sure someone is somewhere you guys like you don't have to respect what I say right now but you have to listen to it please delete Yumi from the game please please that's that's all let's all put a petition let's put a petition to ride like let's ride it let's end it like let's I just put a petition to rallies to delete the game from the game it's Auntie fun to play it's only fun to play against you don't want to have her like watch you all the time be on you you know judge every move you don't want your support to like I know eat a burger and a milkshake like during Landing phase as well like just delete the cats nobody likes her um yeah and that's about it yeah personally I've never been a hater of Yumi but I do understand why people don't like her however people's wish may be getting granted she's not getting deleted outright but we're currently receiving details on updates being made that will significantly change how Yumi operates the goal of these changes is to eliminate her from being relevant in Pro play and more welcoming in solo queue and to beginner players we'll see but these changes are coming in the next patch so unless they fully turn around on this decision this could be the end of yumi's occasional domination in professional League of Legends if you're a band yumi's available ST1 just gonna slam it oh boy in the end this video could go on for hours because regardless of what others think we've all likely had unique experiences that have made us hate a champion for one reason or another for example I personally hate having nases on my team because the moment he's locked in I know that for the next 40 minutes this player is never going to group up with the team instead he will periodically tell us how many stacks he has and ever since in this moment this guy probably hates having Shen on his team hit the Nexus not the Vlad what the actual oh my God I just broke my glass

titre: Hwei: The Visionary | Champion Trailer - League of Legends
transcription: Something dark clings to me. Has always lived in me.  I see... ...destruction... ...and despair.  You brought it to light, Jhin.   Good. Evil. The line blurs.  I keep spiraling down paths I never thought I'd walk.  There is light in me.  I can't lose sight of it!  And the colors come flooding back.  Open your eyes!  Every question, every burden, every vision.  I'll lay them out here.   Art... Life... Death... All I've seen!  All made clear!

titre: Vi: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Bienvenue dans le Focus sur Vi, Cogne de Piltover. On commence par taper, et on pose des questions en même temps ! Vi est une combattante en mêlée, qui compte sur la puissance dévastatrice de ses poings pour écraser ses adversaires. Très mobile, infligeant de lourds dégâts et capable d'initier le combat, Vi excelle quand il s'agit de mener la charge ou de nettoyer le champ de bataille. La compétence passive de Vi est Bouclier antichoc. Lorsqu'une de ses compétences actives touche un ennemi, Vi profite pendant quelques secondes d'un bouclier dont la valeur dépend de ses PV max. Bouclier antichoc a un délai de récupération modéré entre deux activations. Brise-coffre est une compétence chargée. La première utilisation ralentit Vi, et augmente progressivement la portée et les dégâts. Lors de la deuxième utilisation, Vi charge devant elle, renversant et blessant tous les ennemis touchés. Si Vi touche un champion ennemi, elle s'arrête. Je conseille de placer un point dans Brise-coffre assez tôt et de la monter au max au niveau 13, en raison des dégâts et de la mobilité qu'elle confère. Avec Coups fracassants, toutes les 3 attaques sur une même cible, des dégâts supplémentaires sont infligés, fonction des PV max de la cible, en plus de réduire l'armure de la cible et de conférer à Vi pendant quelques secondes un bonus à la vitesse de déplacement. Les attaques standard ainsi que Brise-coffre comptent pour Coups fracassants. Je conseille de prendre Coups fracassants au niveau 1 quand vous jouez jungler, mais de la monter au max en dernier. Force excessive permet à la prochaine attaque standard de Vi d'infliger des dégâts bonus à sa cible, ainsi qu'aux ennemis dans un cône derrière la cible. Vi peut charger deux effets de Force excessive à utiliser l'un après l'autre. Je recommande de prendre Force excessive au niveau quand vous jouez sur les voies, et de la monter au max au niveau neuf, pour charger les effets plus rapidement. Marteau-pilon est l'ultime de Vi.  Quand vous l'activez, Vi charge le champion ennemi visé et est insensible aux effets de contrôle de foule,  tandis qu'elle repousse et blesse tous les ennemis sur son chemin. Quand Vi atteint la cible, elle la blesse et la projette dans les airs avant de l'écraser au sol. S'il peut être difficile de l'utiliser correctement contre les champions à distance, n'oubliez pas que Force excessive peut souvent atteindre vos adversaires. Trouvez un angle qui met un sbire entre vous et votre adversaire et utilisez Force excessive pour le harceler. Il est également possible d'affaiblir vos adversaires en leur tendant une embuscade depuis les hautes herbes et en utilisant Brise-coffre quand ils approchent Nidalee approche pour placer Guérilla, mais en combinant Brise-coffre, une attaque standard et Force excessive, Vi lui fait regretter cette audace Cette combo est encore plus terrifiante quand vous apprenez Coups fracassants. Si Nidalee parvient à esquiver Brise-coffre, Vi se place sur sa voie de sortie. N'oubliez pas que Force excessive réinitialise le délai d'attaque, et vous pouvez ainsi l'utiliser juste après une attaque standard pour frapper encore et déclencher Coups fracassants. En combinant tout ça, regardez Vi charger avec Brise-coffre, suivre avec une attaque standard, puis avec Force excessive... Et Nidalee est une proie facile quand Maître Yi arrive pour le gank. Dès que vous avez votre ultime, Marteau-pilon, vous avez encore d'autres options. Quand vous combattez un champion disposant de compétences de contrôle de foule, l'ultime de Vi la rend insensible à ces effets. Swain lance Capture, mais avec Marteau-pilon, Vi charge tout de même et obtient un kill en 1 contre 1 sans même ralentir. Envisagez souvent de nettoyer rapidement des vagues de sbires avant de partir ailleurs sur la carte ou de rentrer à la base. Il suffit pour cela de charger Brise-coffre et d'attendre que les sbires s'entassent. Tapez ensuite dans le tas et utilisez Force excessive. Une fois les sbires éliminés, vous pouvez aller où bon vous semble. Si vous faites face à un adversaire en mauvaise posture, n'hésitez pas à charger. Vi découvre Caitlyn en situation délicate et lui saute dessus avec Marteau-pilon. Avec l'aide de son équipe, elle s'en débarrasse rapidement avant de continuer l'assaut. L'ennemi ayant perdu son principal DPS, l'équipe de Vi élimine rapidement Nunu et Elise pour l'emporter 3 à 0. Si Vi doit normalement utiliser toutes ses compétences pour éliminer des adversaires par elle-même,  elle peut compter sur ses coéquipiers pour l'aider et utiliser ses compétences surtout pour gagner en mobilité. Maître Yi pourchasse Nidalee, Vi charge avec Brise-coffre pour se rapprocher, puis utilise Marteau-pilon pour l'aider à rattraper l'ennemi. Là, Vi absorbe les dégâts de la tour en poursuivant Nidalee, et parvient à obtenir le kill grâce aux efforts combinés de l'équipe. L'objectif de Vi, dans un combat d'équipe, doit être de s'attaquer aux cibles les plus importantes en premier. Elle charge Swain avec Brise-coffre, l'affaiblit avec Force excessive et Coups fracassants, et obtient ainsi le premier kill du combat. Tout de suite après, elle charge Caitlyn avec Marteau-pilon et l'élimine, malgré le Saut éclair. Ensuite, une attaque standard, Force excessive et Brise-coffre permettent à son équipe de réaliser un Ace en éliminant Elise. Je conseille de développer Vi en mettant l'accent sur les dégâts physiques et en ajoutant une touche de résistance quand c'est nécessaire. Essayez des marques de pénétration d'armure, des sceaux d'armure, des glyphes de résistance magique et des quintessences de dégâts d'attaque. Préférez des maîtrises 21-9-0, en privilégiant les dégâts physiques et l'armure et les PV en défense. Je conseille de choisir Embrasement et Fantôme ou Saut éclair pour les sorts d'invocateur, afin de pouvoir plonger au combat et d'infliger un max de dégâts. Merci d'avoir suivi de Focus sur Vi. Pensez à vous abonner à la chaîne YouTube de Riot Games et laissez-nous vos commentaires sous la vidéo.

titre: Viktor: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Bienvenue dans le Focus sur Viktor, le Héraut des machines. Viktor est un mage, mais son style dépend du joueur. Vous pouvez être un mage tank à moyenne portée, un DPS à burst ou un soutien à longue portée. Viktor a différents outils pour faire face aux adversaires. Sa compétence passive est Technologie évolutive. Viktor débute avec un Coeur magique dans son inventaire. Ce Coeur magique peut être amélioré dans la boutique du jeu en un des trois modules qui correspondent à ces compétences de base. Chaque module améliore différentes caractéristiques et la compétence concernée. Nous allons voir chacun des modules. Transfert de force blesse une cible et Viktor récupère une partie des dégâts sous forme de bouclier temporaire. Si Viktor achète "Module : Force", utiliser Transfert de force augmente également la vitesse de déplacement de Viktor. C'est son principal instrument de dégâts, alors je maximise Transfert de force en premier. Champ gravitationnel crée une zone qui ralentit tous les ennemis s'y trouvant. S'ils y restent trop longtemps, ils sont étourdis. Si Viktor achète "Module : Gravitons", la portée d'incantation de Champ gravitationnel augmente. Ce sort a une grande utilité, j'y mets un point très tôt et n'y touche plus avant de le maximiser au niveau 18. Avec Rayon de la mort, vous décidez du point de départ et de la trajectoire, et vous blessez les ennemis sur sa route. Si Viktor achète "Module : Mort", Rayon de la mort inflige des dégâts bonus sur la durée à chaque cible touchée. Étant à effet de zone, ce sort est plus utile en milieu de partie qu'en début de partie, alors je le maximise au niveau 13. Tempête du chaos blesse et réduit au silence les ennemis dans une zone. Pendant quelques secondes, la tempête créée blesse continuellement les ennemis proches. Vous pouvez rediriger Tempête du chaos vers votre curseur en réactivant la compétence. C'est mon ultime, j'y mets des points aux niveaux 6, 11 et 16. En prenant Transfert de force tôt, vous pouvez facilement prendre le dessus sur des champions ennemis. Ici, je veux aller vers Morgana pour la toucher avec Transfert de force. Je reste à proximité pour placer quelques attaques car mon bouclier bloque une grande partie de sa contre-attaque. Ayez toujours Champ gravitationnel disponible en cas de gank. Quand Shyvana vient me ganker, je place le champ pour être sur un bord et Shyvana sur l'autre. Je le traverse dans toute sa largeur. Ainsi, si elle me poursuit, je suis sûr de m'en tirer car elle se fera étourdir. Malheureusement, à cause de ses sorts Fatigue et Saut éclair, je dois esquiver Sombre dessein de Morgana avec mon Saut éclair. La combo offensive de Viktor est très puissante. Shyvana tente de me foncer dessus, alors je lance Transfert de force, je l'éloigne de la tourelle et je cours jusqu'à la fin de la récupération du sort. Ensuite, je balance tout. Champ gravitationnel la garde à portée de Tempête du chaos, tandis que Rayon de la mort et Transfert de force la blessent gravement. Elle survit grâce au Bouclier noir de Morgana, mais Rammus surgit pour la tuer. Champ gravitationnel est idéal pour initier un combat. Quand Nunu et Shyvana arrivent, je crée le champ pour les séparer : Nunu ne peut pas se replier et Shyvana ne peut pas approcher. Ils se font étourdir, puis Rammus vient éliminer Nunu. Après l'avoir écrasé, on continue de pousser. Comme il leur manque des alliés importants, je peux être très agressif. Je repousse Swain avec Transfert de force et Rayon de la mort tout en coupant la retraite de Shyvana avec Champ gravitationnel. En fin de combat, je trouve Swain à l'aide de Saut éclair et je lance Transfert de force. Grâce à "Module : Force", le bonus de vitesse et le bouclier me permettent de fuir Ashe tandis que mon équipe m'épaule. Champ gravitationnel est phénoménal combiné à un autre contrôle de foule allié. Lux et moi affrontons Yorick à la carrière ; je lance Champ gravitationnel quand Lux place Entrave de lumière. Yorick est alors coincé et subit tous nos dégâts. Il tombe avant d'avoir pu aller bien loin. Rayon de la mort n'interrompt pas vos déplacements et Tempête du chaos inflige des dégâts continus, ce qui vous permet de suivre vos adversaires. J'attaque Ahri avec Transfert de force et Rayon de la mort. Quand elle fuit, je la touche avec Tempête du chaos et redirige la tempête vers Galio. Bien que je ne sois plus à portée pour lancer mes compétences, on réussit à les tuer facilement. Cette combinaison fonctionne également en attaque. Alors que Rumble et moi allons vers l'ossuaire, on superpose Champ gravitationnel et Éradication. Twisted Fate se fait prendre et meurt avant d'avoir pu infliger des dégâts. Tristana repousse Rumble, mais je peux la contrer pendant que mes sbires neutralisent le point. Dernière remarque stratégique : lancer Rayon de la mort à la limite de sa portée vous permet de frapper n'importe qui impunément. Lorsque Tristana tente de défendre la foreuse, Rayon de la mort lui inflige environ 500 pts de dégâts et elle ne peut pas riposter. En runes, j'ai des marques de pénétration magique, des sceaux de puissance par niveau, des glyphes de puissance par niveau et des quintessences de puissance fixe. Je peux ainsi infliger de gros dégâts avec mes compétences. Si vous préférez un build tanky avec Transfert de force, prenez plutôt des sceaux d'armure et des glyphes de résistance magique. Mes maîtrises sont 21/0/9. J'augmente ma puissance, ma pénétration magique, ma régénération de mana et la durée des buffs neutres. Comme pour les runes, ce build me permet d'infliger toujours plus de dégâts. Pour un build tanky, préférez des maîtrises 10/11/9 : troquez la puissance contre de l'armure, de la résistance magique et des PV. En mode classique, je débute avec un Cristal de saphir et deux Potions de soin. Je prends vite Catalyste protecteur et Bottes de vitesse, puis Bâton séculaire. Après Bâton séculaire, je prends mes Chaussures de sorcier. Le choix du module dépend de vous ; j'ai choisi "Force" dans ce Focus. Certains objets sont très utiles, quel que soit votre build, tels que Sceptre de Rylai, Coiffe de Rabadon, Volonté des anciens et Bâton du vide. Dans Dominion, votre build final est similaire, mais il débute différemment. Je commence avec Anneau du prospecteur, Bottes de vitesse et deux Potions de soin. Je prends vite Chaussures de sorcier, Sceptre de Rylai et "Module : Force" pour tourner autour des combats et prendre l'avantage en vitesse de déplacement. Là aussi, prenez des objets très puissants comme Coiffe de Rabadon, Volonté des anciens et Bâton du vide. Merci d'avoir suivi ce Focus sur Viktor. Abonnez-vous ci-dessus à la chaîne YouTube de Riot Games et cliquez sur J'aime sous la vidéo !

titre: Aatrox Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription:
This is Aatrox, the Darkin Blade. A fallen god-warrior bound to an ancient weapon,
Aatrox is hellbent on bringing total obliteration upon everyone who crosses his warpath. He's an unstoppable juggernaut that dishes
out massive damage with mid range attacks, fighting til death… and then some. Welcome to the Aatrox Champion Spotlight. Aatrox's passive is Deathbringer Stance. Every once in a while, Aatrox's next basic
attack has increased range and damages the target for a percentage of their maximum health. This attack also reduces all healing and shielding
on the target for a few seconds. Deathbringer Stance's cooldown is slightly
reduced whenever Aatrox activates an ability, and additionally when he lands the edge of
his Q, The Darkin Blade, on a champion. Speaking of which, let's talk about his Q:
The Darkin Blade. Aatrox swings his greatsword up to three times. Each cast increases in damage and impacts
a different area of effect. His Q has a sweet spot. Precise hits with The Darkin Blade further
increase the damage and knock up targets. On the first cast, the sweet spot is at the
very tip; On the second, it's along the outer edge;
And on the final cast, it's the very center, immediately in front of Aatrox. Each cast of The Darkin Blade brings the hitbox closer to Aatrox - so keep advancing to go three for three. Each Q also increases in damage - regardless if you landed the previous one for about twice as much on the final cast. That's what I call "Tons of damage". Aatrox can be a real bastard sword in lane. The range on his Q lets him poke, punish,
and push lane against melee and even midrange foes. You know what they say - two-handers are better
than one. Aatrox's W is Infernal Chains. Aatrox smashes the ground, damaging and slowing
the first enemy hit. If it's a champion or large monster, they're
chained to the impact area. If they don't leave the area to break the
tether, they're dragged to the center and take damage again. If they break free, they'll be insubordinate... and churlish. Infernal Chains pulls enemies to the same
spot every time. Use it to tee up a Q for the perfect smackdown. Aatrox's E is Umbral Dash, which can store
up to 2 charges. Passively, Aatrox heals for a portion of the
physical damage he deals. When activated, he dashes a short distance
and gains attack damage for a few seconds. You can Umbral Dash in combination with his other abilities to reposition, helping you land abilities like the sweet spot of The Darkin Blade. Umbral Dash is usually too short, much like
the real Dash, to be used for chasing or escaping, but it's just enough to get you over some
walls. Ta da! Use Umbral Dash mid-swing to match your opponent's
movements and claim more Q hits. Aatrox's ultimate is World Ender. Aatrox unleashes his full might, becoming
monstrous in size and taking flight on massive wings. As he transforms, Aatrox fears nearby minions
and briefly increases his movement speed. This speed bonus is reactivated while out
of combat. While transformed, Aatrox's attack damage
is increased and he gains a Blood Well that revives him if he takes lethal damage. The Blood Well fills up over World Ender's
duration, meaning the longer you're transformed, the more health you'll have upon reviving
- as long as you die before you return to your puny humanoid form. With World Ender you can really wing it - the
extra damage and revive means you can tower dive with impunity. When you're far away from the fight, you can
ult early to give doom some zoom and arrive while the party is still going strong. When you're ready to bring ruin, go big with
World Ender. Once you're in the warzone, lock down lesser
warriors with Infernal Chains and make them cry with a QQQ. As chaos unfolds, use Umbral Dash and Flash
to stay on top of your fodder and treat em to the sweet spot of your Blade. Don't bother running from a darkin. None can escape Xer-wrath. Thanks for tuning into the Aatrox Champion Spotlight. Crush these links below or be annihilated.

titre: Draven: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Bienvenue dans le Focus sur Draven, le Glorieux exécuteur. Draven est un carry AD à distance qui peut lancer et rattraper ses haches. Grâce à son gameplay, Draven peut infliger des tonnes de dégâts et dominer la partie. Sa compétence passive est Lames ravageuses. Les coups critiques de Draven font saigner sa cible, qui subit des dégâts supplémentaires en quelques secondes. Avec Hache tournoyante, la prochaine attaque de base de Draven inflige des dégâts bonus, applique Lames ravageuses et ricoche sur la cible. Si Draven rattrape cette hache, sa prochaine attaque de base lance automatiquement une autre Hache tournoyante. Si Draven lance et rattrape des haches assez longtemps, il peut avoir jusqu'à deux Haches tournoyantes en même temps. La Hache tournoyante précède Draven : s'il est immobile, la hache tombe près de lui ; s'il court, elle tombe sur son chemin. Hache tournoyante est sa principale source de dégâts, je la prends au niveau 1 et la maximise immédiatement. Pulsion sanguinaire augmente ses vitesses de déplacement et d'attaque pendant quelques secondes. Si Draven attrape Hache tournoyante, le délai de Pulsion sanguinaire prend fin. C'est un stéroïde majeur pour les dégâts, j'y mets donc vite 1 point et maximise la compétence au niveau 13. Avec Division, Draven lance ses haches en ligne droite, blessant, ralentissant et repoussant les ennemis touchés. Division est surtout utilitaire, j'y mets vite 1 point mais je ne la maximise qu'au niveau 18. L'ultime de Draven est Volée mortelle : Draven lance deux énormes haches à travers toute la carte, blessant tous les ennemis touchés. Si Volée mortelle touche le bord de la carte, un champion ennemi ou si Draven réactive la compétence, les haches font demi-tour et blessent tous les ennemis touchés en revenant vers Draven. Comme pour tous les ultimes, je prends Volée mortelle aux niveaux 6, 11 et 16. Au début du laning, harcelez vos ennemis avec autant de Haches tournoyantes que possible. Utilisez Pulsion sanguinaire pour les rattraper ou pour les envoyer dans une direction sûre avant qu'elles ne touchent un ennemi. Notez que Lames ravageuses force la tourelle à vous attaquer. Restez hors de portée et harcelez un maximum en dirigeant Hache tournoyante vers des zones sûres. Sur votre lane, gardez une Hache tournoyante en tuant des sbires et rattrapez-la jusqu'à être prêt à engager le combat. Draven fonce sur Ezreal mais ne s'implique pas totalement, à cause d'Alistar. Quand Alistar utilise son étourdissement, Draven active sa deuxième Hache tournoyante. Gardez Pulsion sanguinaire activée après chaque hache rattrapée et lancez des Haches tournoyantes. Lames ravageuses tue Alistar et Purge empêche Embrasement d'achever Draven. Si vous êtes face à Draven sur votre lane, le contrôle de foule est votre ami. Alors que Draven lance Hache tournoyante sur Ezreal, Alistar utilise Coup de tête et Atomisation, ce qui stoppe le push. Draven entre en combat avec Malphite. Il le ralentit avec Division, lance Hache tournoyante et réactive constamment Pulsion sanguinaire, et les PV de Malphite tombent extrêmement bas. Quand Malphite fuit, Draven lui lance Volée mortelle. Malphite part vers le haut pour esquiver le retour des haches, mais Draven fait de même et Volée mortelle parvient donc à tuer sa cible. Draven est exceptionnel pour finir les combats. En rejoignant un combat, il voit Renekton affaibli : il le tue avec Volée mortelle, Saut éclair et une attaque de base. Draven traque ensuite Alistar en activant Pulsion sanguinaire et prépare une Hache tournoyante. En lançant et rattrapant constamment des Haches tournoyantes, Draven maintient Pulsion sanguinaire et réussit à tuer deux ennemis. En combat d'équipes, la priorité de Draven est de bien se placer. Même s'il aurait pu frapper Shyvana, il retourne derrière son équipe jusqu'à pouvoir attaquer sans danger. Il peut ainsi poursuivre Shyvana en rattrapant des Haches tournoyantes, puis il se tourne vers Rumble. Hache tournoyante tue un ennemi, mais au lieu d'aller la rattraper, Draven fonce pour participer au combat. Son équipe élimine Lulu et Draven part à la poursuite d'Ahri. Udyr mène la charge et étourdit Ahri sous sa tourelle tandis que Draven aide à tuer le quatrième ennemi du combat. Pour continuer d'infliger des dégâts, Draven frappe les champions avec des Haches tournoyantes, puis il se tourne pour garder ses distances tout en activant Pulsion sanguinaire pour accélérer la manœuvre. Shyvana est repoussée et Draven s'attaque ensuite à Rumble. Une fois Rumble mort, Draven suit son équipe jusqu'à trouver Ahri un peu trop avancée. Il lui lance Division, Hache tournoyante et une attaque de base. Lames ravageuses tue Ahri et Draven réussit un doublé. Draven est censé rattraper ses Haches tournoyantes, mais ne le faites pas devant l'équipe ennemie. Draven tue quelques sbires avec Hache tournoyante. Malheureusement, en voulant rattraper une hache, il se met à portée d'Urgot, lequel permute avec lui et le tue. Je conseille de choisir un build augmentant les dégâts physiques et un peu de résistances, pour les escarmouches qui vous feront perdre des PV. En runes, je conseille des marques de dégâts d'attaque fixes, des sceaux d'armure fixe, des glyphes de résistance magique par niveau et des quintessences de dégâts d'attaque fixes. En maîtrises, je conseille une page 21/9/0, en privilégiant les dégâts physiques dans Attaque, et l'armure et les PV dans Défense. En sorts d'invocateur, je conseille Purge et Saut éclair, qui vous aideront à tuer des ennemis et à fuir le danger. En mode classique, je débute avec Bottes de vitesse et des Potions de soin. Prenez deux Lames de Doran puis Jambières du berzerker. Achetez Lame d'infini et Sceptre vampirique tout de suite après. Ensuite, prenez Danseur fantôme, Dernier souffle, Soif-de-sang et Ange gardien pour finir votre build. Dans Dominion, je débute avec Bottes de vitesse, Lame du prospecteur et des Potions de soin. Prenez Jambières du berzerker, Lame d'infini et Sceptre vampirique avant de finir votre build avec Danseur fantôme, Épée sanglante, Dernier souffle et Maillet gelé. Merci d'avoir suivi le Focus sur Draven. Abonnez-vous ci-dessus à la chaîne YouTube de Riot Games et cliquez sur J'aime sous la vidéo !

titre: Swain Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription:
We are, all of us, born into a lie... That there are those who are good, and those who are evil. But these words mean nothing. In truth, the only measure of a soul is the strength of their will. Welcome... to the Swain Champion Spotlight. Swain's passive, Ravenous Flock has two
components. First, enemy champions leave behind Soul Fragments
when they die. Shadowy ravens scavenge nearby Soul Fragments,
healing Swain. He can also store a bunch of Soul Fragments,
and we'll go over what that means a little bit later. Now let's talk about Ravenous Flock's second
effect. Right-clicking any nearby immobilized enemy
champion damages them, drags them toward Swain, and rips out a Soul Fragment. The drag has a moderate cooldown, so you'll have to choose the best victim when following up on area-of-effect lockdowns. Swain's Q, Death's Hand, unleashes several bolts of eldritch power in front of him, each damaging the first target struck. Multiple hits on the same target deal reduced
damage beyond the first. Bolts pierce through targets they kill, restoring a portion of Swain's mana and hitting the next unit in their path. Take advantage of this in lane to shock your
opponent through their low-health minions, or refresh your mana pool wave-clearing. Swain's W, Vision of Empire, opens a demonic
eye at a target location, damaging and slowing all enemies in the area after a moderate delay. Champions hit by Vision of Empire take extra 
damage, grant Swain a Soul Fragment, and are temporarily revealed. Vision of Empire's range is phreaking massive. It gives tons of... versatility as a zoning, reconnaissance, and follow-up tool. It's up to you as a master tactician to figure out the best way to use it. It's typically not waveclear. Swain's E, Nevermove, projects his demonic
hand forward, then calls it back, damaging everything it touches. Nevermove passes through enemies on the way
out but explodes if it hits something as it's returning. Targets caught in the explosion are rooted
in addition to damaged. Nevermove explodes if it hits any enemy on
the way back, not just enemy champions. Keep an eye on what's behind your target,
or you could end up rooting a minion or monster by mistake. Feels bad, bird dad. Watch for opportunities to follow up on Nevermove
with your other abilities. Pull your opponent in with Ravenous Flock's right-click, then drop a five-finger Death's Hand for tons of damage. Once you've got a grasp on Swain's kit, eyeball
an opportunity to add Vision of Empire into the mix. Swain's ultimate, Demonic Ascension, invokes
his full power, granting a big chunk of health on-cast and draining life from the nearest
enemies for an extended duration, prioritizing champions. When Demonic Ascension ends, either by running
down the duration or by reactivating after draining enough health, Swain casts Demon Flare, the payoff for collecting all those Soul Fragments. Swain crushes every fragment in his possession
to unleash a nova of demonic energy, damaging the closest enemies around him. The nova's damage increases for each fragment
consumed. Demon Flare is Swain's make-or-break moment
in teamfights. Flash-bombing the opposing backline is a play
for the highlight reels when Swain does quick maths, but if you don't know two plus two
is four, miscalculations will leave him stranded in the middle of a very angry enemy team with
no access to his survival tools. Let's sum it up with a late-game teamfight
to strike fear into the foes of Noxus. Swain catches Ezreal and Braum with a well-aimed
Nevermove, then pulls Ez in with Ravenous Flock. Ezreal tries to Flash to safety but is winged
by the far edge of Demon Flare. A few more pecks murder Braum, bringing the
teamfight to a tidy conclusion. Thanks for watching the Swain Champion Spotlight. Click the links below for more intel on the
Noxian Grand General.

titre: UPDATED Season 2026 TIER LIST for League of Legends Patch 26.01!
transcription: Gangplanks popping off Lucian's obliterating everything. Noo-Noo zooming around the map with home guards. Akali's dashing from two screens array and evaporating you and Bum's face tanking entire teams. Welcome to season 2026 and today we'll be taking a closer look at the meta so far and going over our updated tier list for patch 26.1. Last week we did our predictions and this week we're back to see who's on top. But today we are going to be doing something a little bit different to our usual tier list videos. Now, if you didn't know by now, our website has two separate tier lists. One for all ranks and one for high elo specifically. The main tier list is for the majority of players from around silver and gold all the way through to diamond. Then our high elo tier list is for everything above that essentially diamond 2 and above all the way through into challenger. So, let's kick it off with the top lane then. And in the S tier, we have Darius, Fiora, Gangplank, Gwen, Aurelia, Jax, Kale, Malight, Renekton, Riven, and Zahen. In the A and B tier, we have tons of other choices with honestly many options being viable across the board so far with some looking much stronger than last season. Then in our high elo tier list, you can see a bit of a difference here. A lot of the champions in the S tier are fairly similar, but we also have Ambessa here too. And Malight and Gwen are more A tier picks in high elo. Here we also have a lot less choice in the lower tiers because as you get higher up, less champions tend to be picked as many players don't see them as anywhere near as viable. mainly due to how matchup dependent higher elo lobbies are. So, let's talk about some of the strongest picks and why they're so strong. And of course, we have to talk about Gangplank. Now, the crit changes clearly helped him out, but in reality, it's Essence Reaver that's really making him shine. This item has already been nerfed since the patch with the hot fix increasing its gold cost by 150 and reducing its AD by five. But that hasn't had too much of an impact on the meta massively revolving around this item on some specific champions and more so in the bot lane. But more on that later. As expected, Kale is one champion that's seen a really big improvement in the season so far with mainly her ability to ramp up quicker with the increased experience gains allowing her to reach level 16 and start taking over games. Typically, when Kale was in a good spot, she's still only played up to a certain rank or by Kale one tricks. But now she's seeing a lot of popularity at every rank in every region. Riven is still probably the best top laner in the game overall though if you can play her well enough. Now I'm sure you've all seen plenty of clips of Rivens coming out on top in impossible situations or maybe you've seen this happen in your games or maybe you play her yourself. And to be honest, we kind of do expect some nerfs to come her way soon. She's just got insane amounts of damage to match her crazy mobility, too. And one minute you think she's going down and the next minute she's full health and your whole team is dead. Well, let's have a look at the jungle next then. And in the S tier, we have a lot of choice with Diana, Ekko, Gwen, Jarvin, Jace, Kazix, Lein, Malight, Noo-Noo, Ramis, Rexai, Silus, and Zed. There's also plenty of super strong champions in the A tier 2 like Dr. Mundo, Nocturn, Hecarim, Talon, and even Lilia. In the high elo tier list, it looks fairly similar again in the S tier. We just have a few less picks here, but you'll see the A tier is a lot less packed and many more picks are down in the B tier. if you can play them well enough. Nidi, Rengar, and Kiana all still have their place in the right games. And other strong junglers right now include Zed, Malight, Lilia, and Jace. All of which have already got their names lined up with some nerfs next week. So, one pick we just have to talk about this patch is Noo-Noo, who is running riot with the changes so far with home guards being super OP and allowing Noo-Noo to basically just snowball around the map 24/7. Games are just feeling so much more fast-paced now, and it's always a surprise when you've just been ganked, and then 20 seconds later, they're back again with a brand new item. Noo-Noo also has solid clears and can take down dragons and objectives quickly, too. So, overall, an all round great pick to play. Lein is doing much better than we expected to, and a lot of players are having great results with him so far. He's still got the same old builds as always that are tried and tested, but the profane hydra setup is seeing a lot more action this time around, too. Diana is without a doubt one of the best junglers you can play this season 2 as she was already in a great spot. But with the changes to the meta with farming jungler seeing improvements, plus the addition of the new Dusk and Dawn item, which was basically made for her, she's now up there with some of the best champions in the game. Into the mid lane next then, and in the S tier, we have Ahri, Akali, Aurelia, Cassadin, Katarina, Silus, Syndra, Twisted Fate, Vigar, and Zed. Now, a pretty big range of champions here with very mixed and unique different play styles. And it's nice to see some picks like Vigar come back into the meta again. In Hyo, we have Ahri, Akali, Katarina, Mel, Oriana, Kiana, Silus, Syndra, Twisted Fate, Victor, and Zed. Again, a few different changes here as you go towards high elo with these super consistent control mages like Oriana and Victor proving to be some of the most timeless and everlasting popular picks in high elo. So, let's start off by mentioning one of the biggest winners of season 2026 so far in the mid lane, Akali. And it was relatively expected as with the addition of Hexte gunlade, Akali veterans were salivating at the chance to play her with this item again. Now, Akali has been in a good spot for a while in high elo, but now she is back to her best again. Another mid-lane pick that's shot back up to the top tiers is Cassadin, and I generally think that people have just forgotten about how strong he can be. He's seeing a lot more play this season and his stats across the board are looking so much better again. So, if you haven't tried him again recently, give him another go. Twisted Fate is back up there again, too, and winning tons of games, especially towards higher ranks. Now, he's just so simple yet so effective in what he does best, setting up ganks with gold cards, wave clearing, roaming, and of course, ganking with his ultimate. AD mids are also doing better than expected so far too with picks like Zed, Kiana, and Talon all still showing great stats and proving they aren't to be forgotten about, especially in the hands of one-trick ponies. Now, these champions might not be the best picks at lower ranks, but the better your mechanics are, the more you can abuse them. Well, let's move down to the AD carries next then. And here in the S tier, we have Aidios, Caitlyn, Jinx, Lucian, Misfortune, Nila, Cir, and Smolder. A bit of a shakeup in the meta here this season so far. Mainly due to how strong Essence Reaver has been in the high elo tier list here. It's much more stripped back so far with just four champions in the S tier in Lucian, Seir, Smoulder, and Nila. These are by far the most popular and most dominant AD carries in the game so far at higher ranks. And although Nila is more of a counter pick compared to the other three, in the right matchups, she's free LP. Lucian has definitely been one of the biggest winners of the season so far with essence reaver being an item that feels like it was made for him and with waves spawning faster, aggressive gameplay being rewarded with the quest completion and then owning boatloads more gold. Lucian is the perfect pick to capitalize on all things AD carry this season. Other champions that are also performing much better with essence Reaver are those other S tier picks like Civ Moulder and Ela. Now, so far Vain is having a real mixed opinion across players with many people thinking she's absolutely broken and others stating that her lack of wave clear prevents her from keeping up with those other top AD carries. Now, build-wise and statwise, Vain is performing pretty well and often only really drops off towards higher ranks where they can punish her lack of wave clear more often. We're also seeing more AD carries alongside Vain in the top lane so far this season too. So watch out for those if you do main top. Aphilia started the season really strong too and already received a hot fix nerf just a few days after the patch. But despite this is still showing solid results so far. He does need a bit more help from his teammates to function as well as other AD carries do, but sometimes you can really see what he's capable of with the sheer amount of damage he can dish out with the right weapon combos. Mages in the bot lane are also performing very well too with wave clear just being a big deal so far. We're seeing Vigar, Ziggs, Syndra, Mel, and Swain all statistically doing well so far despite their standard lower pick rates compared to AD carries. But if you enjoy playing them here, it's not a bad shout. Finally, full support. Then in the S tier, we have Bum, Janna, Karma, Leona, Milio, Nami, Sona, and Thresh. Now, the meta here is relatively similar to the end of last season with a few changes like Bum coming back up to the S tier. For high elo, we have Bard, Lulu, and Nautilus up to S and Janna and Millio down in A. And aside from that, it's just a fair few more limited choices in the A and B tip as a running trend similar to other roles in this video. So, who's on top then? Well, it's hard to argue with Sona being one of the best supports in the game at every single rank. Her stats are insane, and if you've played a single game with a Sona so far this season, you simply won't need me to convince you. She packs ridiculous utility that's undeniably effective into literally any draft, and she's also very easy to pilot, aside from making sure your positioning is on point. The Bandal Pipes item has been a very strong addition to the game in this season so far. And for only 2,000 gold, you could get it up and running quickly and instantly feel its power. and Bum has really enjoyed this item so far as well as just generally starting the season off super strong. Now, he's one of the best supports in the game at the moment and not only brings his crowd control, but also has a big role to play in countering many of the strong AD carries and even AP carries in the meta with his big fancy shield. Speaking of bandal pipes though, they are getting a nerf next patch with the gold cost increasing from 2,000 to 2,300. Now, finally, Nami keeps her place as one of the best supports and probably one of the best champions in the entire game overall. She had an exceptional 2025 and is still keeping up the pace in 2026 with her versatile kit and ability to slot into any team comply. It also helps she's even better with the wave clear meta and of course works brilliantly well with that classic combo with Lucian alongside her. And that brings us to the end of our updated season 2026 tier list video. We hope you guys are enjoying the season so far and don't forget to check out our website for all the best builds for your favorite champions. Thanks for watching guys and as always, take care.
