titre: Karthus: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Bienvenue dans le Focus de League of Legends sur Karthus, la Liche. Karthus a volontairement abandonné sa condition de mortel dans les Îles obscures et est ainsi devenu une puissante liche. Aujourd'hui, il parcourt Runeterra pour offrir la non-vie éternelle à qui la désire. Dans le jeu, Karthus est un mage solitaire ou un jungler. Il peut infliger d'énormes dégâts de zone  avant d'abattre son célèbre ultime sur tous les ennemis de la carte, où qu'ils soient.  Sa compétence passive est Déni de mort.  Quand il est tué, Karthus devient invulnérable, impossible à cibler et il peut lancer ses sorts gratuitement pendant quelques secondes avant de mourir.  Quand Déni de mort est actif, Karthus ne peut pas se déplacer, utiliser son attaque de base ou lancer des sorts d'invocateur.  Dévastation est sa principale source de dégâts et son délai de récupération est extrêmement court.  Karthus concentre sa puissance sous votre curseur, offrant de la vision avant de provoquer une explosion qui inflige des dégâts magiques aux ennemis proches. Notez que les dégâts sont doublés si le sort frappe une cible unique, ce qui fait de Karthus un redoutable duelliste. Utilisez Dévastation pour achever les sbires et harceler votre adversaire.  Mur de douleur est son Z.  Il crée un mur spectral qui ralentit les ennemis qui le touchent tout en réduisant leur résistance magique.  Plusieurs choses sont à noter : le ralentissement et la taille du mur augmentent avec le niveau du sort  et les deux extrémités offrent de la vision, ce qui permet de vérifier l'état d'un objectif sans trop de risques.  Utilisez Mur de douleur pour fuir les ganks.  Placez-le sur votre agresseur pour l'empêcher de vous poursuivre.  Le E de Karthus est Souillure.  Désactivée, Souillure restaure passivement votre mana quand vous tuez une unité. En l'activant, Karthus s'entoure des âmes de ses victimes, drainant son mana pour blesser les ennemis proches en continu. L'effet actif suit Karthus, ce qui est idéal pour les combats rapprochés. Toutefois, la restauration du mana ne fonctionne pas quand Souillure est active et ce sort s'active automatiquement quand Karthus meurt. Ou ne meurt pas. Peu importe.  Utilisez Souillure lors des combats d'équipes pour blesser massivement plusieurs ennemis.  Karthus est souvent une cible prioritaire ; activez donc Souillure quand l'ennemi charge pour infliger des dégâts même si vous mourez.  Ou pas. Peu importe.  Son ultime est Requiem. Karthus canalise le sort quelques instants avant d'infliger des dégâts magiques à tous les champions ennemis, qu'ils soient visibles ou non. Cette longue canalisation peut être interrompue par le contrôle de foule et la MORT, même la fausse, le timing est donc crucial. Gardez un œil sur les autres voies et lancez Requiem pour achever les ennemis qui réussissent à fuir. Demandez à vos équipiers de vous prévenir quand ils ont besoin d'aide.  Profiter de la courte invulnérabilité de Déni de mort est essentiel pour maîtriser Karthus  alors voici quelques conseils pour bien gérer ces sept secondes de fausse mort. Tout d'abord, la canalisation de Requiem nécessite presque la moitié de la durée du passif ;   vous ne pourrez pas lancer le sort s'il ne reste pas assez de temps pour la canalisation.  Il faut donc bien gérer le nombre de sorts lancés pour optimiser vos dégâts.  Ensuite, quand vous mourez, ne paniquez pas.  Il faut agir vite mais vous avez du temps.  Commencez par lancer Mur de douleur de façon à ce que plusieurs ennemis le traversent.  Vous avez alors le temps de lancer trois fois votre A, ou quatre si vous avez maximisé la réduction des délais, avant de canaliser Requiem.  Vous pouvez ainsi utiliser toutes vos compétences et infliger un maximum de dégâts.  Dévastation remplace votre attaque de base et vous sera utile dans presque toutes les situations. Mémorisez le rayon d'effet du sort pour doubler les dégâts et achever les sbires, puis harcelez l'ennemi dès que possible.  Le passif de Souillure doit vous permettre d'achever chaque sbire avec Dévastation.  Avec Requiem, Karthus peut menacer chaque voie, alors surveillez la carte pour achever des champions sur d'autres voies ou pour aider vos équipiers à les tuer.  Ici, Udyr va ganker sur la voie du bas ; Karthus quitte donc le milieu pour l'assister.  Sachant que Xerath peut interrompre Requiem, Karthus se cache avant de canaliser son ultime.   Il tue Renekton sur la voie du haut et obtient une assistance pour la mort de Caitlyn, en bas.  Après cela, Karthus affronte Xerath en duel.  Karthus agit vraiment bien : en restant au contact, il inflige des dégâts continus avec Souillure,   esquive les tirs de Xerath et frappe avec son A jusqu'à remporter le combat.  La longue canalisation de Requiem est l'une des pires faiblesses de Karthus.  Comme c'est un mage fragile, il doit réfléchir avant de lancer son ultime, afin qu'on n'interrompe pas sa canalisation. Karthus perd ce combat car il faisait une cible facile à un moment crucial.  Cela dit, Requiem peut aussi être contré par des objets et des sorts d'invocateur, alors pensez-y avant d'utiliser votre ultime.  Karthus recule pour pouvoir canaliser Requiem et achever Cassiopeia, mais celle-ci survit grâce   à son Sablier de Zhonya et elle offre ainsi un peu de répit bien mérité à ses équipiers.  N'oubliez pas que Mur de douleur ralentit pleinement les ennemis même s'il ne les touche qu'avec le rebord. Ici, l'utilisation du sort par-dessus un obstacle ralentit assez Lucian pour que Quinn le rattrape et le tue.  Contrairement à la plupart des mages, Karthus est plus efficace au cœur de la mêlée ;  et contrairement aux autres champions, il doit parfois mourir pour aider son équipe.  Ici, Karthus saute dans l'antre de Nashor avant de déchaîner ses sorts et d'empêcher les ennemis de tuer le monstre. Bien qu'il meure, il force l'ennemi à s'éloigner de Nashor et il aide son équipe à remporter le combat. Pour le build de Karthus,  concentrez-vous sur le mana, la puissance et la pénétration magique. Avec son important coût en mana, Souillure nécessite des objets de mana pour être efficace. Prenez ensuite des objets de puissance et privilégiez la pénétration magique en fin de partie pour accroître encore vos dégâts.  En fin de partie, le Sablier de Zhonya est idéal, car son invulnérabilité se combine très bien avec l'actif de Souillure et le style de jeu de Karthus.  Merci d'avoir suivi ce Focus sur Karthus. Abonnez-vous à la chaîne YouTube de Riot Games et laissez vos commentaires sous la vidéo.

titre: Darius: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Bienvenue dans le Focus sur Darius, la Main de Noxus. Darius est un combattant de mêlée qui s'accroche à ses cibles, les saigne en leur tranchant les veines et leur inflige le coup de grâce. Si vous aimez plonger dans le combat et faire mal, intéressez-vous à Darius. La compétence passive de Darius est Plaie béante. Ses attaques de base et ses compétences infligeant des dégâts permettent de cumuler les Plaies béantes. Les ennemis touchés par Plaie béante saignent et subissent des dégâts sur la durée à chaque cumul. De plus, Darius accélère à chaque champion ennemi qui saigne. Avec Décimation, Darius fait un cercle avec sa hache, blessant tous les ennemis proches. Les champions ennemis au périmètre sont frappés par la lame et subissent des dégâts supplémentaires. Je prends Décimation au niveau 1 et la maximise tout de suite. Estropiaison permet à la prochaine attaque de base de Darius d'infliger des dégâts supplémentaires et de ralentir sa cible. Estropiaison se recharge plus vite en fonction des Plaies béantes accumulées sur la cible. Pour plus de dégâts et du contrôle de foule, je mets un point dans Estropiaison rapidement et je la maximise au niveau 13. Crampon donne passivement à Darius un bonus de pénétration d'armure. À l'activation, il attire tous les ennemis devant lui. C'est une compétence utilitaire, je mets un point dans Crampon rapidement mais je ne le maximise qu'au niveau 18. L'ultime de Darius est Guillotine noxienne. À l'activation, Darius saute sur le champion ennemi, infligeant des dégâts extrêmes. Guillotine noxienne inflige des dégâts bonus en fonction des cumuls de Plaie béante sur la cible. De plus, si Guillotine noxienne inflige le coup de grâce, son délai de récupération prend fin immédiatement. Agissez sur votre lane, au début, selon les adversaires. Contre les champions de mêlée comme Singed, harcelez. Utilisez Décimation pour faire des dégâts bonus et ajoutez Plaie béante à chaque fois que possible. Essayez de rester entre les sbires et le champion ennemi, pour l'empêcher de récupérer de l'or. Si les sbires ennemis se dirigent vers votre tourelle, les maîtrises réduisant les dégâts des sbires vous permettront de les tanker et d'empêcher la tourelle de vous voler l'or. Une fois vos sbires arrivés, laissez-les tanker et placez le dernier coup comme d'habitude. Gardez-vous aussi contre les ganks en plaçant rapidement un Jalon de vision, sur la rivière ou ce buisson, ici. Si vous voulez harceler l'ennemi sur la lane, attirez-le avec Crampon, placez une attaque de base, enchaînez aussitôt avec Estropiaison et enfin Décimation. Vous pouvez aussi commencer avec Décimation, puis enchaînez avec une attaque de base et Estropiaison pour infliger beaucoup de dégâts en très peu de temps. Quand un coéquipier vient ganker, gardez Crampon en réserve jusqu'à la tentative de fuite. Je commence sur Fiddlesticks avec Décimation, puis attaque de base et Estropiaison. Quand Fiddlesticks commence à filer, je le récupère avec Crampon pour une élimination rapide. Darius est particulièrement puissant quand Décimation atteint le niveau 5. Cherchez tous les combats possibles quand vous atteignez le niveau 9. Je vois que Twisted Fate essaie de fuir, je lance Crampon puis Décimation, une attaque et Estropiaison. Je garde le rythme et j'élimine l'ennemi, évitant Rafale de corbeaux jusqu'à la fin. Puis je m'en prends à Fiddlesticks. Ma combo standard fait chuter ses PV et je l'achève avec Guillotine noxienne. Je passe à Singed, j'utilise Décimation et Estropiaison, pour plus de dégâts et un ralentissement. J'utilise Fantôme et j'enchaîne autant d'attaques que possible, et je le poursuis jusqu'à ce qu'il meure avec Plaie béante. Darius est excellent pour isoler et abattre les cibles solitaires faibles. Dès que je peux, j'attire Twisted Fate avec Crampon. J'utilise Décimation puis Estropiaison dès que possible. Je me déplace entre les attaques et, après une Décimation supplémentaire, je l'abats avec Guillotine noxienne. Il faut comprendre que chaque bloc de PV dans la barre de santé d'un champion représente 100 PV. Cela vous permet d'estimer si un champion a suffisamment peu de PV pour pouvoir être achevé sans avoir besoin de cliquer dessus pour lire ses PV. Darius n'est pas un vrai tank et ne devrait pas être le premier à foncer au combat. Quand vos ennemis sont distraits, vous pouvez facilement tuer tout le monde. Je vise d'abord Fiddlesticks. Je peux cumuler cinq fois Plaie béante rapidement, enchaîner avec Décimation et terminer avec Guillotine noxienne. Je me dirige ensuite vers la voie du milieu, pour tomber sur Blitzcrank et Twisted Fate. J'utilise Décimation sur les deux champions pour obtenir un buff de vitesse de déplacement, exécuter Twisted Fate et revenir aussitôt sur Blitzcrank. Je le ralentis avec Estropiaison et je le tue avec Guillotine noxienne quand ses PV sont assez bas. Crampon peut aussi servir de soutien aux alliés. Ici, Singed pourchasse Ashe. Je me glisse derrière, je l'attire avec Crampon et j'utilise Estropiaison dès que possible, ce qui nous permet d'achever Singed et de sauver Ashe. Je recommande pour Darius un build mélangeant dégâts physiques et durabilité et de rester au contact via les bonus de vitesse et les ralentissements. Pour les runes, prenez des marques de dégâts d'attaque fixes, des sceaux d'armure fixe, des glyphes de résistance magique par niveau et des quintessences de vitesse de déplacement. Pour les maîtrises : 9/21/0. De la pénétration d'armure en Attaque et de la durabilité et la réduction des dégâts des sbires en Défense. Pour les sorts d'invocateur, choisissez Embrasement et Fantôme pour coller aux cibles et les détruire. Commencez avec Bottes de vitesse ou Armure d'étoffe et des Potions de soin. Prenez Pierre philosophale, ajoutez Lame de Doran puis Sandales de Mercure ou Tabi ninja selon vos besoins. Ajoutez Phage, Égide de la légion et Hexdrinker en milieu de partie. Construisez Songe de Shurelya, Maillet gelé, Gueule de Malmortius et Présage de Randuin. Dans Dominion, commencez avec Lame du prospecteur, Bottes de vitesse et deux Potions de soin. Prenez ensuite Tabi ninja ou Sandales de Mercure selon vos besoins, et construisez Phage et Hexdrinker pour le milieu de partie. Ajoutez en fin de partie de la durabilité et des dégâts avec Songe de Shurelya, Présage de Randuin, Maillet gelé et Gueule de Malmortius. Merci d'avoir regardé le Focus sur Darius. Abonnez-vous ci-dessus à la chaîne YouTube de Riot Games et cliquez sur J'aime sous la vidéo !

titre: Renata Glasc Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: You work for me now! Renata Glasc climbed her way from rags to Chemtech riches, becoming the most powerful chem-baron in Zaun. Now her eyes are set on Piltover and as a master of persuasion, she knows exactly how to get what she wants. It's just business. She’s a proactive enchanter who weaves both sides of the battlefield to her command. But whose side is she really on? Her own, of course. "I'll make you kill your loved ones" is a more effective threat than any gun. Welcome to the Renata Glasc Champion Spotlight. We don't have to be enemies. Let me change your mind. This boss is used to having the upper hand thanks to her passive, Leverage. Her basics apply a mark that deals bonus damage, which her allies can detonate, dealing even more damage. I'll make a killing off of this! Like any business deal, be sure to choose your targets wisely. Only one can be marked at a time. Her Q, Handshake, sends out a missile that damages and roots the first enemy hit. She can then recast the ability to nudge her competitor in the right direction, which is of course whatever direction she chooses. The hapless fool damages all enemies hit, and if she throws a champion, those champions are also stunned. I said, "Jump!" Put her there! Renata Glasc's always willing to lend a hand, so long as there’s interest. Bailout buffs an ally with increased attack speed and movement speed toward enemies. You're welcome! Allies who manage to kill an enemy during the buff will reset its duration. High-class chemtech. If her... investment... would die while buffed, Bailout grants her ally a fresh, full health bar that quickly decays before finally dying. However, allies that get kills or assists in this decaying state heal and stop the decay, saving their life. This health bar reset won’t interrupt channels and abilities as they don’t die just yet, so teammates at death’s door can take full advantage of Renata Glasc’s incentives. Remember who helped you in your hour of need. With her E, Loyalty Program, Renata Glasc fires a missile that detonates at the end of its path, damaging and slowing all enemies encountered. Your value's dropped. Similarly, allies affected by the missile receive a shield. Better sign up! This deal won't last forever. I'm a giver. With her ultimate, Hostile Takeover, Renata Glasc sends out a cloud of toxic chemicals. Enemies caught in the fumes succumb to her influence and go berserk. While berserk, their attack speed increases and they'll attack the closest target, starting with their own teammates. Don't think, just do. If other enemies aren’t around, they’ll go up the chain of command, Starting with Renata Glasc’s allies, and ending with herself. Everybody wants to be my enemy. In the early game, Renata Glasc should rely on Leverage to punish poor positioning and help her business- I mean, lane partner... win trades. Make them pay! Create opportunities for kills with slows, roots, and pulls that dominate the market. If you chain your CC, you’ll win the fight before it’s even begun. Ready to take control. Teammates need a bailout? No problem. Rescue your assets at the right time, and you can always turn a fight around. A worthy investment. But, stay away from risky investments. For what Renata Glasc boasts in engage, she lacks in mobility and escapes. So wait for the right moment to secure the deal, and you can watch Renata Glasc’s enemies destroy themselves. Time to make some new business partners. Set up plays for your team with opportune buffs, shields, and roots. And when they’ve succeeded, make sure you take all the credit. I'll bill you later. Don’t underestimate the pull of Renata Glasc. One way or another, you’ll be working for her eventually. Ready to invest in the Chem-Baroness? Check out the links below. Ambition isn't greed.

titre: The EASIEST Champion Of Each Role | League Of Legends
transcription: Hey guys, welcome back to another League video. And for today, I'm going to be making the follow-up episode to the hardest champion of each role. This time being a focus on the easiest champion of each role. While it's debatable as to what most people consider difficult on the basis of there just being a wide range of attributes you can assign to the skill floor and/or ceiling of a champion. Players generally look to be in agreement on which cast members are the easiest to play. The ones with the most straightforward win condition or the simplest mechanics would come to mind. That being said, even then you can argue as to which ones are truly the easiest. Like for example, I for one do not consider Dr. Mundo or Sona to even be in the top three of their respective lanes, despite them commonly being viewed as in contention for such a title. But regardless, same basic flow applies to this episode, too. I'll be going through each of the five roles, beginning in top lane and then working my way down, picking the definitive easiest champion in my personal opinion, and then including two honorable mentions. After you're done watching, feel free to share your own list if it's different from mine. But without further ado, let's get into it. Opening up in top lane, by far the easiest champion to play up there is going to be Malight. This should not even remotely be a competition. Malight's considered by many to be the go-to pick when you're autofilled top lane. First of all, he's got one of the most straightforward kits in the game. Granite Shield offers a bit of lane endurance by simply walking away from your opponent and waiting for a few seconds. Excuse a point and click ranged attack that deals a fairly solid amount of damage and applies a slow and equal movement speed buff which can obviously be used to harass opponents in lane, chase after fleeing enemies or run away from pursuers. The relatively short cooldown of his Q coupled by the fact that most top laners can't really punish you for doing it makes it very easy for Malight to Q spam over and over again to out neutral his opponent. All the while, Grand Shield offers the modum sustain against any attempts that opponent might make to attack you back. By default, Maline has an advantageous or damn near unloosable matchup against physical damage or auto attack based champions of any kind, which just so happens to comprise the overwhelming majority of top laners. Trindir, Kale, Atrox, Pantheon, Renekton, Riven, Jax, Jace, Fiora, what have you. Being able to harass them while face tanking anything they throw at them. Anytime they try to attack you, all you need to do is press W and E, and you can chunk them for good damage while preventing them from getting much against you. The temple seal afforded by seismic shroud gives Malight the ability to trade with you, then run away to regain his shield before repeating the process all over again. For all intents and purposes, despite being a melee himself, Malight plays like a range top laner. If he doesn't want to engage you all in, he doesn't have to. And if he wants to, against most AD champions, he can and often does stat check you with only a single item. Very few tanks get the same increased offensive capability by building defense the way Malight does. Finally, of course, Malight will always be relevant for your team. Not only because typically there's at least one auto attackbased champion on the enemy team, the ADC, but because this trademark ultimate is widely regarded by many to be the single best engage tool to ever exist. In a team fight, all you have to do is tag two or three enemy champions with an ultimate and you basically won the fight for your team. The only thing keeping Malight from being more oppressive than he is is because he ironically loses to most other tanks who primarily excel in magic and percent health damage. Other than that, the reason Malight's so effective is because of how easy and forgiving but rewarding he is to play. People just don't like to play him because he's boring. Honorable mentions go to Morichiser and Garen. Both champions are stat checkers with a lot of base power and endurance built into their kit. They each have a straightforward and consistent combo pattern or win condition, and Zen win condition must be constantly respected by the enemy team for being able to effectively nullify a single member of the enemy team. Death ROM belongs to the class of abilities that at best completely prevent an opponent from playing the game and at worst makes that opponent as useless as Mornaser is. On the other hand, Damasian Justice makes Garen the most threatening opponent on the enemy team against anyone below half HP. For the most part, neither ability really has any counterplay to them. I want to start out by saying the jungle is a role with an inherently high barrier to entry. Even for the champions I'll be mentioning today, there are still aspects of skill required to play them correctly. As all junglers, no matter what, necessitate a strong sense of macro mechanics and fundamentals. In this specific case, the champions I pick will be based on how easy they are to pilot for someone who already has those affforementioned prerequisites. That is, how good they are at a base level for all things related to the jungle. The most intuitive choice for that would be Amumu. In a similar fashion to Malight, you can't really mess up with this champion. The most difficult part of him is being good at aiming cues, but even then, you get to store up to two bandage tosses at once. So, even if you miss the first one, you can still throw the second one. Amumu skew might very well be the best ganking ability in the game. It is an absurdly long range, good damage output, a clean 1second stunning gap closer built into it, and again, you can hold two of them at once. When you do get in, the rest is simple. Turn on W and mash the E button. That's it. You really can't mess up on this champion, even if you try. Some might claim his susceptibility to getting caught out by predator junglers makes him deceptively hard to play in higher ranks compared to a more independent jungler. Even so, his contribution to the team makes up for that. He's got respectable clear speed, courtesy of Despair and Tantrum being AoE. He's insanely powerful if your team has two or even one other AP champion by way of his passive. And Curse of the Sad Mummy can be an even stronger team fight ultimate than Malights by way of a larger cast radius, although without the dash. Obviously, he won't be able to have the same kind of impact as a hyper carry jungler like Vieo or Bellev. But that being said, Amumu's baseline and ease of use is what earns him a spot as the easiest jungler to play. His ganking is straightforward, clearing is straightforward, win condition during a team fight is straightforward, and not a single team doesn't want to be vun line with a long range stun and a massive AoE stun. Once more, people just don't find Amumu all that entertaining to play, but you cannot disregard how practical he is. If you have like an 80% Q accuracy or higher, you basically master the champion. Honorable mentions go to Warick and Ramis. Both are slightly more nuanced than how you play them, but they're still fundamentally very cut and dry. Warwick's whole gimmick is to endlessly attack a target until they die or he does. The main mechanical requirement of him is to get good at manipulating the blood trails for optimal movement speed and then of course being good at landing his ultimate. That's about it. Personally, I think Warick top is harder to play than Warick jungle since in top lane you have to be even more proficient at manipulating his W for pressure. Jungle Warick is just easier to play. Ramis is a bit more situational, but he has some of the best ganking in the game. And once you get in, you just turn on your W and taunt someone and that's about it. Ramis is a literal demon against high physical damage enemy teams, notably those without any way to cancel his Q. After receiving his new ultimate, he's become considerably more effective in the mid to late game, essentially being a discount malfun ultimate. Very easy champion to play, but very effective. Mid lane places noticeably higher emphasis on either macro or skill shots than the other lanes on account of most of his champions being skillshot oriented or capable of roaming. But the overall premise is still the same. anyone with a straightforward win condition who can contribute a consistent amount of pressure by following that win con. As a result, I consider the easiest mid laner to be Annie. Mechanically, Annie is much in the same vein as Moriciser, Garen, Amumu, and stuff where outside of maybe one specific thing in her case, making sure the right ability applies a stun. She's not that difficult to play. You can't mess up on her rotation. Whichever one applies a stun, you want to cast that one first and then afterwards, just press the other two abilities and there you go. You get a guaranteed rotation. It's even more guaranteed in Annie's case because unlike Garin or Morichiser, she's a range champion with one of her abilities being point and click and the other two being near instant activation. You can even use molten shield to give you a speed boost to get closer to your target of interest. I get that for inexperienced players when caught in a bad situation, they might panic and use their ability rotation incorrectly or carelessly. And even in that scenario, if Annie gets engaged on, it doesn't really matter what ability gets a stun because Annie can press all of her attacks one after another in quick succession, accomplishing the same end result nonetheless. Her W and ultimate having a generous AoE make them, in my opinion, better candidates for Pyromaniac than Disintegrate for the sheer fact that they can stun multiple targets. Honorable mentions go to Mahar and Lux. You might be surprised that I didn't call Malahar the easiest mid laner to play. A lot of that has to do with them not being as consistent as Annie. Knowing how to utilize your E in lane to not only last correctly, but be able to apply triple space aids on your opponent and rack up serious chip damage actually gives a fair bit of skill expression to his kit. Sure, in the mid to late game, he eventually devolves into being a suppression bot, but Malahar can be deceptively tricky to play effectively. Now, regarding Lux, again, you might find it strange that an all skill shot champion is in the list of easiest champions to play, but I think in Lux's case, she's a bit less demanding in that respect than a true skill shot mage like Zerith or Velkos. If you can land a light binding on a target, it effectively guarantees the rest of her rotation. And on top of that, she's got passively strong harass and wave clear via Lucen Singularity. Long-range pick potential and artillery fire seldom encounter a situation where they're not appreciated. All righty. Now to discard all care for macro and talk about the ADC role because we all know ADC mains have non-existent macro knowledge. For this particular section, I'll be focusing specifically on which ADCs are the most mechanically easy to play. And probably to no one's surprise, the gold medal goes to Ash. Ironically, actually, Ash requires more macro awareness than most other ADCs in the game, owing to Hogot being a global vision scouting tool and enchanted crystal arrow explicitly mandating you to pay attention to the map. That aside, Ash is by far the easiest ADC in the game to play. Her main damaging tool, her W, has the most generous AoE in the game. You basically throw it in the general direction of the enemy team, and it will be guaranteed to tag and subsequently slow someone. Ranger's focus gives you an attack speed boost and bonus damage on your autos after autoing a few times beforehand. Again, no way to really mess up this ability. And finally, to ease the mechanical skill floor of playing a marksman, brash makes all of Ash's autos passively slow enemies, making it easy for you to either kite or chase after people. No complicated skill shots or even intensive attack moving required to play this champ. To clarify, intensive attack moving very much does enhance Ash's effectiveness, potentially even more so than on the marksman. But my point is, it isn't as imperative to perform on her as opposed to say a Kog'aw. Worst case scenario, you can play Ash as a W spammer and auto attack whoever's in front of you. And there you go, you're in the clear. If you can consistently land her ultimate, you're probably better than 90% of other ADC players in the game. She contributes a steady baseline of pressure and utility for the lowest mechanical demand. Honorable mentions go to Cra and Misfortune. Cra does require noticeably more mechanics to play than Ash in the sense that you actually do have to learn to kite and stuff with her. But thanks to her Q being a humongous penetrating skill shot and her W auto bouncing to nearby enemies, server is one of the best frontto back marksmen in the game. Namely because she can damage the backline while working on the front line. Spell shield offers a quick and easy way to deal with any potentially threatening attacks like Morana Q or Blitz hook. And her ultimate offers some value to the team. So at worst, she can be a walking human Shurelius battle slung practically. MF is also easy to play. Her literal hardest combo is auto and Q. That's it. You just auto a target then Q and do it again on another target. Granted, lining up her Q, so then it always hits an important target behind her is also necessary, but she doesn't really take much effort to play. A good team fight ultimate can easily be achieved if someone on your team has a hard engage like what do you know Malight Amumu or Annie. Now do you see why I picked those champs? They each offer consistent and readily exploitable utility to make it easy for the rest of their team to do their job. Oh and also MF is pretty fast for a marksman. It's hard to catch her when a W's up. Okay, we arrived at support. Once again, support can be noticeably more macro inensive on account of having to know when to engage and when to peel, who to peel for and what not, along with extensive knowledge of vision control. But because that applies to every support, we're going to focus purely on mechanics. And because of that, the easiest support is 1,000% Saraka. The amount of times I see an autofill support first time Pike or some other champion instead of picking Saraka. By far the easiest enchanter in the game, but still insanely effective is just tilting. Towards the middle late game, Saraka basically doubles the effective health of her entire team. Astral Fusion and Wish offer so much extra survival and endurance that if you can't kill her first, you basically can't win the fight. She is a decently effective harass and regen tool in the form of her Q and Equinox is a huge middle finger to any assassin champion who tries to go in. Katarina blinks on your team and uses ultimate. Press the honor. Set tries to death mark you. Put Equinox down on yourself. She's the ultimate front to back support and extremely easy to play. All you have to do is avoid getting engaged on the same way every enchanter has to. play to spam cast W at no cost and have enough map awareness to press ultimate at the right time for your top laner and you instantly contribute more to your team's chances of winning than if you were the first time Pike or Leblanc support. Please, for the love of God, stop picking carry supports in your autofill. Just play Saraka. It's so free. Like, it's so free. Why does no one take one for the team in this elo? Anyway, honorable mentions go to Janna and Leona. Once again, you have a very stable enchanter who excels at defensive utility. She can keep her team alive without much mechanical requirement outside of landing her tornado. And she covers multiple bases, defense, crowd control, disengage, and tempo. Very solid support, and consistently one of the best performing ones at that. She also happens to be a hard counter to the vast majority of hard engages like Nautilus, Alistar, and Leona. Speaking of Leona, I didn't want to do all enchanters, so I'll include her. Among the engage supports, Leona is the simplest to play. She's got a point andclick stun, a gap closer doubling as a route, which can open up for that point andclick stun. And then after you get that point and click stun off, you can press ultimate to stun them for even longer. The only nuance after that is what order you want to do it. You can open up with ultimate first and then engage or whatever combination after. I find her more easy to play than Allistar or Nautilus mainly because of her queue, but opinions can vary. All righty, that concludes my list of the easiest champions of each roll. Top lane Mali, jungle Amumu, Midlane Annie, ADC Ash, and support Soraka with their respective honorable mentions. Let me know in the comments whether you agree or disagree with my choices. And if you disagree with them, tell me what your top three of each troll would be. For now though, if you enjoy the video, I would appreciate it if you love to like and subscribe. Don't forget to follow me on Twitter, Farster, join my Discord server, and check out my episode on the three hardest champions of each troll if you haven't yet. Until next time, thanks so much for watching, and I look forward to seeing you again soon. Take care. Ooh.

titre: The UPDATED Lore of every LoL Champion Dumbed Down to a Single Sentence
transcription: Some of you may remember about a bit over a year ago, I made a video dumbing down the lore of every single champion from League of Legends into a single sentence. Well, since that time, Riot has actually remembered the lore was even a thing. So, there's been a whole host of new champions, dead champions, visual updates, and a show that retconned the entire universe for the third time. Before Riot had even finished writing the second one. Worry not though, my friend. I have seen your many requests for a modern follow-up to that old video. So, let me be your guide once again, explaining the lore of every League of Legends champion in only a few seconds each. Corrupted god warrior believes the only way that he can die is if everyone else does first. Soul-sucking kitsune accidentally kills her boyfriend because she forgot she had soul-sucking powers. Rebel ninja seeking peace disowns her mentor for being too peaceful. Charming rogue destroys the narrative stakes of death with his undo button McGuffin. Minotaur man is forced to fight as entertainment for the equivalent of modern-day sports fans. Grizzled warmonger goes out of her way to protect her family by kickstarting the apocalypse. Tiny yordle confirmed now is forever cursed to be stuck in the no friend zone. Marvel's what if the Phoenix was locked in a deep freezer. Trouble child accidentally systematically murders her entire bloodline to save her stuffed animal. Cult sister astral projects impractical firearms to her brother so they can kill bigots together. Frost archer attempts to unite a country of people that would kill each other over a turkey leg. God of the universe is tricked into enslavement by wearing a fancy hat. Funny girl wizard befriends assorted ungulates and is autistic. Emperor pigeon forcefully subjugates an entire continent to prove he's not the bad guy. Animate mattress from space goes treasure hunting. Mutant manta ray names herself after her first meal. Is no longer canon. Thanks, Arcane. Overeager apprentice obtains obscene power through third-degree burns. Jolly folk here slams people's face into his portable door. Buy a weapon, hope she has enough time to befriend people before she accidentally eats them. Daughter of a corporate wealthy aristocrat realizes, wait, are we the bad guys? I'll get back to you on this one once Riot decides what the hell hextech even is anymore. High-ranking elite gets turned into monster girl. Still nothing. Still nothing unless you count ads for the mobile game. Hardened soldier makes the difficult decision whether to protect his family or fit in with a bunch of communists. Moon-worshipping cultist falls in love with a sun-worshipping bigot. Purple man think he good doctor, but purple man just kill people. Egomaniac runs a Roman Colosseum like the WWE. Street punk accidentally invents time travel while trying to invent dimensional travel. High-ranking elite gets turned into monster girl. Spider edition. Demon discovers how to weaponize gooning. Self-centered loser pretends at heroism after stealing a magic glove. Scarecrow role-plays as a kenku. French fencer duels father to the death for trying to give narcotics to her original opponent. Fish freak suppresses his trauma by annoying the ever-living kind of everybody. Stone statue gets Pinocchioed by the power of hypocrisy. Spelly pirate learns that actions have consequences sometimes. Soldier makes the difficult decision whether to protect his family or fit in with a bunch of racists. Approximately seven IQ caveman rat is able to hulk out occasionally. Please laugh at the obese alcoholic. Petty criminal does crime genuinely just for the love of the game. Plush doll is granted sentience by splitting her creator's soul in half. Psychopath gets ghost magic DNA spliced with his horse. Scientist discovers the definition of apathy. Witnessing a mass murder of innocents by a literal serial killer convinces femboy painter to become a centrist. Priestess beats you to death to show you how great life is. Dancer with telekinetic razor blades defeats invasion force by cutting off some fellow's arm. Bloodthirsty barbarian gets turned into a tree after chopping down a tree. Desert goddess willingly demotes herself to sentient air purifier. Tarnished prince tries not to be racist anymore because his girlfriend left him. Wandering nomad uses the last remnant of his civilization as a bonking stick. Good-intentioned inventor gets corrupted by politics and almost destroys the entire world. The name is very fitting. Monster hunter lets go of his ego so that he can hunt more monsters more better. Genderbent with even more fan service and a symbiote they forgot to give a personality. Vengeance. Host body wielding the godly power of an entire continent meditates all day. Incel loser loves death so much he kills himself. Professional Darth Vader cosplayer jumps into a hole to search for his lost daughter. High-ranking assassin for an expansionist dictatorship constantly tries to take the moral high ground. Self-appointed judge, jury, and executioner. Blood god literally lives rent-free in the head of a teenage schizophrenic edgelord. Lightning rat older than the land he lives on talks like he was born five years [music] ago. Small-scale Australian insect picks a fight with a cat. Death, straight up. Local Texan riding an immortal reptile believes everywhere is his private property. Bug eats things. Master deceiver actively with and up the entire planet in the name of protecting it. Bruce Lee knockoff blinds himself with his own powers. Sun-worshipping bigot falls in love with a moon-worshipping cultist. Dear lady is spawned to guard a magic tree. Ice switch works tirelessly to save the world from a cataclysm that she herself caused. Gunslinger loves his wife. Like like really, really, loves his wife. Pixie gremlin ruins people's lives for her own entertainment. Mage is gaslit her whole life into loving a city that hates her. Chunk of a floating fortress grows into a mountain-sized foster father. Sorcerer prophet gets brainwashed by a giant purple stingray. Tree spirit gets pissy when a simp king destroys his garden. Blade master able to defeat entire invasion force single-handedly chooses not to. Aristocratic politician becomes a superhero. First grader discovers the healing potential of the campfire song song. Traumatized pirate struggles to choose a career path. Emperor Karen wills himself back from the dead because the afterlife just didn't meet his quality standards. Ascended being attempts to aid the righteous while reforming sinners through torture. Decorated warrior learns the value of teamwork after being turned into a dog. Mermaid traverses the world in search of a magic rock. Anthro jackal creates a library to sulk in after his entire civilization collapses. Ocean sludge turns deep sea diver into 20-ft tall toll booth worker. Shape-shifting chameleon creature wants to be Shape-shifting cat creature already has her own History buff becomes cursed with god-like power and eternal happiness. Bootleg Freddy Krueger. Grandma gets lost while on vacation. Studio Ghibli copyright infringement gives child the powers of Frosty the Snowman. Viking seeking glorious death in battle becomes infuriated after learning he cannot die while in battle. Child is cured [music] of terminal illness by turning her into a snow globe decoration. Introverted blacksmith gets annoyed people are actually interested in his work. Buff man detests the gods despite technically being one. Would-be delivery driver is too humble to realize the package is for herself. Superpowered Jack the Ripper. Whiny brat thinks she's better than everyone because she can throw magic grass at them. Army scout unleashes her secret weapon of tossing her bird at people. Charismatic dancer joins the freedom fighters to impress his girlfriend. Oh good. Sad shark is a shark. Metal bender gets entire narrative undone to serve as the story of another champion who's dead now anyways. Why did you do this? The average billionaire just a tiny bit more transparent about how evil they are. Crocodile man holds fabricated grudge against his brother for way too long. Cat watches way too many predator movies. Sword master decides it might be time to switch sides after witnessing war crimes first hand. Garbage rat acts like garbage so he can collect more garbage to prove it's not garbage. Spindly sorcerer spends centuries collecting infinity stones. Bounty hunter sabotages her own missions them more challenging. Chieftain purposely makes life way harder on herself and the people around her because it's more natural that way or something. Ghostbuster felled by lamp comes back as a ghostly ghostbuster. I'll get back to you on this one once Riot decides what [music] the hell hextech even is anymore. Hybrid of a hybrid becomes kingpin of a fight club to stick it to his dad. The damn T hex has more lore than this guy. Sacred protector of his homeland skips out on almost every single [music] major story moment that has ever happened in his homeland. Magical monster girl somehow falls for a man very racist against magical monster people. Scientist does war crimes, okay for him to do that cuz his daughter's sick. Decorated war veteran is never allowed to die because he was just too damn good at his job. Royal descendant dodges morals in exchange for more money. Scorpion kaiju puts his entire country into permanent lockdown. That's a baby dragon, all right? Yeah. Mute mage musician lives in a city that's tolerant of only a single one of those character traits. Celestial god heals morals by slowly killing herself. War strategist tricks a demon into becoming his prosthetic arm. Prison rat sentence is to be determined once the retcon storyline actually catches up with him again. Sorceress with potentially enough power to level the entire planet takes multiple extended naps instead. Catfish demon goes gambling for souls. Earth bender embarks on a cross-country journey to kill a racist pigeon. Crybaby assassin gives up if he doesn't one-shot his opponent. Former soldier turned cosmic imbued protector crashes the jewelry industry. Goody two-shoes boy scout knowingly irritates everybody around him. Basement dweller enacts his life goal of subjecting the entire world to endless suffering for the heinous crime of making him work a 9-5. Fun-loving gunner forms brigade of rat people to shoot at trespassers. Troll claims dominion over his people because he has a giant block of ice now. Barbarian refuses to die because yeah. Gambit knockoff teams up with boyfriend frog to do crimes and be gay. As in gold. Shaman harnesses the power of the gods to become man bear pig goat bird. Revolutionary contradicts his own might makes right philosophy by augmenting himself to be mightier than everybody else by default. Gay couple and an angry furry who do a Dragon Ball Fusion dance. Racist Van Helsing, former prisoner of war, tries to be evil but is really bad at it. Space squid learns about the physical realm by disintegrating it. Immortal hamster goes full emo upon learning she's immortal. Street rat told how good she is at fighting growing up barely wins any fights as an adult. Depressed king genocides the planet after becoming maidenless. Crippled scientist becomes god for about a half hour. Hannibal Lecter if he was a blood bender and had dementia. God bear gets outraged over the mere idea of relaxation and agriculture. Honest man turned into slightly less honest parent turned into golden garbage turned into cyborg furry. Obligatory Sun Wukong and you made him boring. Xenophobic freedom fighter gets consistently distracted by her boyfriend's corny romance. Slave absorbs the power of the sun to become a giant electrostatic generator. Seneschal sacrifices body to become the corporeal form of his new friend's boyfriend. Disgraced swordsman fights a never-ending battle with his inner demons. Undead swordsman fights a never-ending battle with actual demons. Half-corpse monk's entire story arc is upended by a charming rogue's undo button McGuffin. Shrine maiden halts the aging process by banishing herself and her boyfriend to the shadow realm. Magical parasite and sentient book team up to search for a lost senior citizen. God warrior sentences himself to eternal torture on the pretense that he might commit crime one day. Rubber Batman. Shadow ninja brings balance to his homeland by constantly destroying the balance of his homeland. I'll get back to you on this one once Riot decides what to do with a Zuanite mage post-Arcane season two. Bombs. Time wizard cannot perform the one kind of time magic that would easily solve the problem he's been trying to fix for centuries. Child obtains phenomenal cosmic powers only to use them to with people. Destroyed forest is given new life as a Raid Shadow Legends OC. End video.

titre: 3 BEST CHAMPIONS To ONE TRICK For Every Role - End of Season 15!
transcription: If you're looking to achieve that next rank for end of season and want to just spam one champion to get there, then we've got you guys covered in this one as we'll be providing you with three of the best champs to one trick for every single role. The two main pieces of criteria we always follow for these videos revolve around no champion who's featured will have a ban rate above 10%, which makes them easily accessible for you to one trick. And then secondly, of course, they have to be who we think are strong picks for the current meta. We're going to add in one more piece of criteria though for this specific video. Since it's close to end of season, you're not going to have a whole lot of time to really master whatever pick you choose to one trick. So, we're going to feature picks who have lower skill floors, who you can pick up and not have to spend a ton of games on to really get the hang of them. So, for instance, even though Zahen and Malphite are two of the best top laners right now, choosing them as your one trick is pretty much impossible due to Zahen's ban rate being 50% and Malphite's elevated up at 27%. So, because of that, they aren't included in this video. And with season 15 coming to a close, if you want to rank up the fastest, there's no better place to be than Skillcapped. First, start with any of our fundamental courses like macro, wave control, and trading to boost your general game knowledge. Then, dive into our brand new season 15 courses from Coach Rogue and Coach Chippy, along with our newly updated jungle clear course [music] that covers the fastest clear for all the meta junglers in the game. And for whatever champion you're looking to one [music] trick, we've got full game commentaries on them as well, where our challenger players walk you through how to climb [music] out of the exact rank you're stuck in. We're so confident in the quality of content we have to offer at Skillcapped that we don't hide away from anything and offer you a five division rank up guarantee. Climb at least five divisions while actively using Skillcapped or get your money back, no questions asked. So, what are you waiting for? Click the link in the description below and get the rank you deserve for season 15. So, one of the best top lane champions to one trick for end of season who fits every piece of our criteria perfectly is Urgot. Urgot is a bit of a unicorn in the sense that he has a top 10 win rate across every single Elo bracket, which is really rare to see. And his ban rate is less than 1%. There's definitely ways to express your skill on Urgot the more games you put in, but it doesn't take very many games to be able to play him at an impactful level. With E start, you have an incredible level one, so you really want to get up to top lane and look to control the far lane brush. Sprint straight from base when you spawn into the game up into that top lane brush and look to fight your opponent if they face check into you. If you take ignite, have bone plating in runes, and start your E at level one, there are few champions, if any, who can actually beat you level one. This is what makes Urgot such a strong solo queue champ as you have the ability to take control of the lane right from the jump and generate a lead without needing any help from your jungler. For your build on Urgot, you always want to rush Black Cleaver and go into Sterak's Gage second, while Overlord's or Jak'Sho are good [music] third item options depending on want a more offensive or defensive power. And then, for the rune setup, it's going to be Press the Attack with Triumph, Bloodline, and Last Stand, while Bone Plating and Overgrowth are for secondaries. Our second top lane selection is going to be a bit more low Elo skewed, but one of the best low Elo champs you can play being Garen. So, if you're someone who's diamond or above, then just sticking to Urgot is what we'd recommend. However, anywhere emerald or below, Garen is a no-brainer pick up. Garen is actually one of the few champs in meta right now who has a positive win rate into Zahen. So, if you don't want to have to use your ban on Zahen, it gives Garen more value. Garen's ban rate does differ depending on rank quite a bit, but even in bronze, where he's usually banned the most, his ban rate is only 6%. So, the fact that he's not even being banned out a ton in low Elo right now is great. Garen is an amazing top lane champion to one trick for end of season because his skill floor is really low. You can't miss his damage and is just innately tanky without having to build any defenses. Because of this, you have a really good chance of being consistently impactful in every single game. And there's a lot less volatility compared to if you're playing someone like Irelia or Fiora. In order to rank up the fastest for end of season, you really need to limit the amount of stinker games that you have, and Garen's a great pick to ensure that. The core build for Garen is going to consist of a Stridebreaker rush into Phantom Dancer second and Spear of Shojin third, while Conqueror is the keystone rune. And for our third one trick top lane selection, we've got another pick who's excelling across all Elo brackets being Singed. So, in the past, Singed was a much more high Elo skewed pick, but since he got those changes this season to where his Q poison will execute minions, his viability as a low Elo top laner skyrocketed. Singed is literally the highest win rate top lane champion in silver and bronze right now and it's been that way for most of the season. Ban rate is literally non-existent as well, sitting under 1%, so you'll have no issues spamming Singed. Singed's kit is super simple to play at a competitive level because nothing requires any sort of mechanical skill. You just build AP bruiser and then transition into tank so that you're really difficult to take down while still being able to output a good amount of damage at the same time. Singed is one of the most oppressive champions in the game when he gets ahead because of the fact [music] that he can just run right up to his opponents with a million movement speed due to ghost and his R popped, and he'll just stat check everyone around him. Your goal in the mid game and how to be most effective on Singed is to shove out side lane and then look for flanks towards mid when the enemy top laner is forced to retrieve the wave you just shoved. This is a pretty foolproof way of winning with Singed because it guarantees your team will have the numbers advantage around mid lane, and Singed on a flank is where he's at his best. A jungler who has remained a really powerful pick down the stretch and kicking off the top three for the role is Diana. So, Diana jungle is a really good pick to one trick for end of season because she's a top tier jungler with a ban rate under 10% and her skill floor isn't all that high. Diana is a really easy jungler to be impactful on because of the fact she wants to full clear and she wants to hit her level six as soon as possible. Being consistent around this and hitting level six before the enemy jungler is how you're going to open up windows of opportunity where you have the upper hand. If you are always getting six before the enemy jungler and you force plays as soon as you hit level six, then you're going to be able to snowball games really well with Diana. The champion thrives around grub fights because of her AoE impact from R. So, if your mid and top laners have priority, you want to be forcing grub. The meta build for Diana makes her even easier to succeed with because you go AP bruiser and have more margin for error as opposed to going all out burst. Our second jungle selection is going to be one who's similar in theme to Diana and a really underrated pick being Zyra. So, Zyra has the same kind of game plan as Diana early on as she full clears really fast and can use that to her advantage. Zyra's full clear clocks in at under three minutes, which means you're going to have completed your clear before most meta junglers, which opens up a window where you can gank before them or look to invade the enemy jungle while they are trying to finish their last camp. To rank up the fastest for end of season, you just have to play champs who can be reliably impactful in all games. And because of Zyra's super fast full clear, it gives you a big edge up in your ability to impact the map before the enemy jungler can. And then, as you scale into the mid game as Zyra, she's always going to provide a great team fight. So, with heavy emphasis around objectives in season 15, fighting for grubs, dragons, and Autocon, Zyra provides so much value for you. And the cherry on top with Zyra jungle is the fact that players just aren't used to seeing her at all, so that unfamiliarity and enemies not knowing what to expect is always something that gives you an edge up in solo queue. What's really nice about playing Zyra in the jungle is that you don't need a mana item. You get enough mana sustain as is, so you get to rush Liandry's for a super powerful one item spike. For the rune page, you want to grab Electrocute with Cheap Shot, Sudden Impact, and Treasure Hunter, while Haste and Cut Down are for secondaries. One jungler who's just excelling a ton in the lower ranks right now and rounding out the top three for the role is Briar. So, Briar just barely makes the cut for this video with her average ban rate across all Elo brackets clocking in at 10.1%. It does jump slightly above 10% in silver and bronze up to 11, but we're going to make an exception here because of how insane Briar has been performing in those low tiers. Briar is quite literally the number one win rate jungler in bronze, silver, gold, and plat right now. So, there's just nobody else you really need to prioritize if you want to climb out of low Elo for end of season. Briar is what you could refer to as a stat check champion, meaning if you have an item lead on your opponent, then there's really no way for you to lose a one v one. The majority of Briar's damage is point click, so her skill floor is pretty low, which is exactly what we want in a champion that you're going to pick up and spam for a short amount of time. For the build, you have two main options right now as you can either rush Blade of the Ruined King or rush Titanic Hydra. Blade does provide that percent health damage, so if you are into a team who's going to be stacking more health, it's a good play, but if you're into a more squishy bursty enemy comp where durability is more valuable than added damage, going Titanic Hydra is the better option. And then, for the rune page, you want to be taking Press the Attack with Triumph, Alacrity, and Coup de Grace, while Sudden Impact and Treasure Hunter are for secondaries. So, moving on to our mid lane picks now, the best mid lane champ that you can spam for your your end of season climb is Swain. Riot just buffed Swain two patches in a row and have turned him into the perfect mid lane champ to climb fast. Swain's ban rate is just 2% right now, while he holds a top five win rate for mid lane across all Elo brackets. So, what's so great about Swain is that his kit is a lot more forgiving than many traditional mages. You build Swain as an AP bruiser, so he can get really tanky, and this allows for more margin for error and a lower skill floor. The only downside to Swain is that he can be kited easily in fights by longer range, but there are a few ways you can alleviate this weakness. First is that you should take the summoner spell combination of Flash and Ghost. Ghost is going to be really high value for Swain in team fights to allow him to stick on his targets easier. And then, for the build, you will look to pick up Rylai's, which is going to provide even better sticking power so that enemies cannot escape you. Key to note for the rune page is that Aery has been outperforming Conqueror lately. With Swain, you really just need to get out of laning phase and scale into your mid game to be effective, and Aery is going to enable you to achieve this easier. Second on our list for mid lane and the best assassin that you can play for end of season is Naafiri. So, even though Naafiri is an assassin, she's one of the easiest assassins in the game, which makes her a great champ to pick up and find success with right away. Ban rate is sitting at around 5%, so in a really great spot to one trick. At level six, you have the lock on gap close with R, so it makes deleting the priority target on the enemy team way more foolproof than most other assassins. With Naafiri's meta build, it's even more insane because you rush Voltaic Cyclosword, and the passive on the item gives you a 99% slow on auto attack, so you dive in, slow the enemy, and if they try to flash away, you just follow with the gap close from your E. This Voltaic rush in combination with Hail of Blades for the keystone is really nasty right now because as you slow the enemy with the Voltaic passive, it makes landing those three quick autos from Hail of Blades guaranteed. For a melee assassin, Naafiri's laning power is actually pretty strong, too, especially when playing up against other melee champs because of the range poke she has from Q. If you can get good at landing both Q1 and Q2 with Naafiri, then the champion just becomes extremely oppressive in lane. A more underrated strategy on Naafiri that finds a lot of success in solo queue is going symbiotic souls for your boots. With symbiotic you get a ton of movement speed and movement speed is such a key stat on the Fury so that she can get in range of enemies to use her R. As long as you're in range of R then you're going to assassinate squishies. So symbiotic souls are super strong on the pick. And then to round out the top three for mid lane we're going off meta a bit with this one but the champ is incredibly powerful right now being Brand. So Brand mid is a champ that our challenger mid laner Shory really advocates for and he's got a bunch of commentaries on our website which can help you out if you want to pick him up. With a ban rate of just 1% what makes Brand mid so strong is the fact that he can play from such a safe range while being able to land poke and farm really easily. Brand's W into E combo is one of the most disgusting combos in the game because of the range of your E. All you have to do is throw your W down in the range of minions and then if the enemy mid laner is standing anywhere close to the wave and you use your E on the minions your E damage will splash onto the enemy champion and provide uncounterable poke. Here's an example of this from one of Shory's recent Brand commentaries as he doesn't even care about hitting Akali with W. He just W's the wave then uses E on the wave and the E damage hits Akali too. And then out of lane Brand is incredibly strong as well as his team fight is one of the best with all the AoE damage potential he's got. You don't even need a ton of items to be impactful on Brand either. As long as you've got your Liandry's you're going to deal a good amount of damage in fights so falling behind on Brand never puts you completely out of the game. So taking a look at a few ADC picks now one champ who was buffed recently and is back in a great spot for solo queue is Tristana. Trist is a really good ADC to play if you want to try and climb the fastest because she has the ability to make plays happen on her own better than most other ADCs. Your burst threat at level six is incredible and with the gap close from W you have the ability to jump in and burst out the enemy ADC if the enemy support leaves them alone. Trist doesn't rely on any specific archetype of support to succeed either so she's really versatile as you can pair her with melee supports or enchanters and find success. Overall one of the best blind pick ADCs that you can play right now which makes her a great champ to one trick for end of season. Ban rate is really low at only 2% so you rarely have to deviate from playing Trist. For the build you'll either want Youmuu's Ghostblade or the Collector as your rush item. Youmuu's is ideal if you can base with 1300 gold however if you have to take an early base with less than 1300 then going towards the Collector is going to give you more power for lane. Rune page will consist of lethal tempo for the keystone rune and then dip into inspiration for secondaries taking the recently buffed cashback and triple tonic. Next up on our list for ADC with a ban rate of just 6% who has been a top tier pick for quite a while now is Ashe. So Ashe did get nerfed a bit recently but the change didn't lower her power level a significant amount and she's one of the best ADCs to pick up and be impactful on pretty quickly. You've got a really good laning phase with Ashe due to the long range from her W and auto attacks so getting the push at level one and hitting level two before your opponents is something you should try to achieve in every single lane. Hitting two before the enemy can be extremely deadly with Ashe because of the slows from your W and autos as the enemy will have to flash away if they want to escape. Such an underrated part about Ashe is that she can impact games in ways that don't involve dealing any damage at all because of the vision from her E and power from R. Most junglers in season 15 are going to full clear and finish clearing at the very earliest around three minutes so this is what you should look to do. You're usually going to hit level three between three minutes and 15 seconds and three minutes and 30 seconds into the game. So as soon as you hit three leveling your E and using it to scout the enemy jungler should make it so that none of your laners die to an early gank. So Ashe is an ADC who really helps you and your team get off to a great start early on in the game which is such a huge perk to playing her. For the build on Ashe you will be looking to rush Kraken Slayer into Phantom Dancer second and Infinity Edge third while lethal tempo is the optimal keystone rune. And then to round out the top three for ADC is going to be an amazing hyper carry as we've got Jinx. Jinx ban rate is super low right now at around 2% but she's one of the best ADCs you can play so that just bodes super well for you being able to one trick her. When it comes down to straight up team fight impact and being able to solo carry games Jinx is hard to beat. Her passive just provides so much carry potential in fights and the ability for you to completely take over a game on your own. The two item core of Youmuu's Ghostblade and Infinity Edge offers up a ton of raw AD so if you get your lethal tempo stacked along with a passive proc in a fight then your autos just hurt for a massive amount of damage. It's even better for Jinx right now because enchanters like Milio Nami and Karma are being played a bunch in solo queue and if you get any one of them on your team then the takeover potential in fights is insane. Your follow up and chain CC potential when paired with engage supports is just as good though. So very similar to Tristana it doesn't matter at all who you play Jinx with as she can be impactful regardless of her support duo. Kicking off the top three for support is going to be a melee support who has been severely underrated for most of the season being Maokai. Maokai support is an amazing champion to one trick if you want to climb fast as you can heavily influence games without any sort of mechanical skill required and his ban rate is literally 0.2%. The point and click root from W is the best gank assist tool in the game if coupled with flash as it's impossible for enemies to dodge it. This just makes it so that messing up a gank from your jungler or messing up an engage is very unlikely which brings a lot of consistency to the pick. If you're playing someone like Pyke Thresh or Blitz you always have the variance of potentially missing a hook and completely missing out on a kill. But with Maokai you never have to worry about that. The value that Maokai's kit provides when fighting around key objectives is incredible as well due to the zone control from his R and vision he can place with saplings. You know when you're fighting around Aticon and the enemies are just sitting in the tri brush and it's really annoying cuz they have a control ward so you can't see them. Well with Maokai you just toss your saplings in there and it gives your team so much value in being able to see exactly where the enemies are. The standard Maokai build is going to consist of a trailblazer rush into locket second while solstice sleigh is the item upgrade. And then for the rune page you are looking to grab aftershock for the keystone rune while inspiration tree are for secondaries. A support that you simply cannot go wrong with spamming for your end of season climb is Milio. Milio takes very little skill to succeed with while possessing one of the best kits in the game at keeping his carries alive. Ban rate for Milio is pretty close to 10% sitting just below at nine so he just barely makes the cut for the video. Milio really excels at keeping dive champions off his carries because of the peel power from his Q and R. So many champions in this day and age of League have gap closers. So Milio's Q being able to stop those champions in their tracks is such immense value. And even if you end up missing Q then you have the insurance from your R which is point and click and will give your carry the cleanse and heal so they can kite away. As long as you're focused on playing around the most fed member of your team then your ability to impact games on Milio is going to be great. The build that you'll want to run consists of an Echoes of Helia rush into moonstone second while dream maker is the item upgrade. As for the rune page you should be taking Aery for the keystone rune and then resolve for secondaries. And for our final support selection we have a more lane dominant enchanter being Karma. So even though you have the Q skill shot with Karma the hit box is large enough to where really doesn't take a whole lot of skill to hit consistently. What you want to focus on in lane to really increase your hit rate of Q is only use it when the enemy is looking to last hit a minion. Especially when there's a cannon minion that the enemy wants to secure that's when trying to land an R Q is going to be really good value because the enemy ADC is not going to want to give up their cannon minion. By poking away at the enemy ADC whenever they try to last hit minions you're bound to generate a health advantage in lane and that's when you can start threatening for kills. As you transition into the mid game with Karma you become more of a protector with the shield and speed boosts you'll be dishing out with E. What's really nice about Karma is that she's an enchanter who can win lane really hard and then with that advantage she buys defensive items that allows for her carries to completely run away with games. The optimal build for Karma is going to revolve around a redemption rush into moonstone second. The setup is all about providing as much shielding and healing strength as possible so that you can keep your carries alive in fights. As for the rune page you want to take Aery for the keystone followed by resolve for secondaries. guys one last thing our ranked guarantee is insane. It's like signing up for the gym and getting a refund if you don't get ripped. That's how confident we are in Skill-Capped. We obsess over making the best guides with top players rigorous testing and top tier video editing to make your climb easy. If you're ready to level up visit skillcapped.com and see the difference. So that's going to be all for our end of season edition of the best one tricks for every single role. Thank you all so much for watching good luck with your ranked climbs and we'll see you in the next one.

titre: HOW TO PICK YOUR MAIN CHAMPION - MID LANE EDITION
transcription: Picking a main champion to play in League of Legends is one of the most important things you can do. But finding the exact champion that fits your play style, can be pretty difficult with so much choice, particularly in midlife, which has the greatest champion and class variety of any role. In this video, we're going to go through all of the different midlaners, each categorized by difficulty and class. For each one, I'll talk about strengths, weaknesses, play style, and any additional info you might need to know. I'll also give suggestions for what kind of players they may appeal to in order to help inform your decision. As always, all the different sections and champions will be timestamped below. So, without further ado, let's get into it. Assassins are the solo Q stompers of League of Legends. If you want to make montages, execute flashy combos, and outplay your opponents 1 v one, then the assassin class will probably appeal to you. Assassins are often thought of as being hard to execute and somewhat one-dimensional, but you'll find that this take is a bit outdated. The class itself has a lot of variety from champ to champ, but the one thing that remains consistent is having some of the highest 1v9 potential of any class in the game. Let's start off with Diana. Diana is one of the easiest assassins to pick up, and she offers a really easy gateway into the rest of the assassin class, especially if you want to work your way up to something more difficult. She has relatively straightforward combos and trading patterns, but also a lot of room for skill expression, specifically when it comes to playing the side lane and choosing the right moment to engage. Whilst Diana is primarily picked against either a mobile mages or burst assassins, she has a ton of flexibility in both build and runes to enable her to play almost any matchup or situation. You can play her as a full AP assassin, as a fairly tanky bruiser, or even as a hybrid of the two. Additionally, like several other assassins on this list, Diana is a very powerful jungler, which makes her a great option if you want a champion to cover multiple roles. All of this versatility makes Diana one of the best one tricks in this video. And a final bonus to sweeten the deal is that if you secure a pendic with the winter bless Diana skin, the entire map gets covered with this really cool visual effect. Similar to Diana, Nefiri is a champion I highly recommend to newer players looking for an assassin to start with. Despite her straightforward nature, she's a very satisfying champion to play with the potential to pull off some really disgusting outplays. Nefir's kit comes with plenty of in-built forgiveness. Your Q will allow you to farm from range and more difficult matchups, and a combination of both your W and a naturally tanky build will ensure that you don't instantly explode after one mistake. This makes Nefar an excellent champion for learning the game itself without having to invest a ton of time into Nefury before you start to find some success. If you want to play an assassin bruiser hybrid that scales extremely well into late game, I'd really recommend trying the fury. Now, moving on to our medium difficulty assassins. First up, we have Ekko. Ekko is in that wonderful sweet spot of being both approachable to play whilst also having a very high skill cap, meaning this is a champion you could stick with for a long time and still have room to grow. Ekko is known for his crazy snowball and skirmishing potential, as well as being one of the most difficult champions to deal with once he gets ahead. One thing worth noting with Ekko is that his difficulty heavily draws from fundamental game knowledge and timing as opposed to some of the more mechanically intensive in the- moment assassins, which could be a positive or a negative depending on what kind of assassin you're looking for. Overall, Ekko is a pretty unique take on the assassin play style and is one worth trying if the others aren't clicking for you. Talon is the quintessential assassin, featuring the highest first blood of any mid laner by a significant margin, as well as some of the highest total kills and solo kills per game. Talon is the champion you want to play if you just want to kill people. He has excellent roaming, skirmishing potential and is one of the most destructive 1v9 champions in the hands of a smurf. His high kill pressure and strong wave clear, granting him the ability to solo kill your opponent and easily translate that lead across the rest of the map. Talon does pay for this agency over the game by being rather unforgiving and especially weak if you fall behind or make any major mistakes and therefore will require some time investment to really see results. That being said, if you like pushing your limits and you really enjoy the knife's edge play style, then I think you'll love playing Talon. Fizz is perhaps one of the most annoying little rats in this entire game and might be the champion most likely to make your opposing mid laner break their keyboard. If you want to make your opponents regret ever playing this game, then mastering Fizz is for you. With a super powerful early game featuring high 1 v one kill pressure, amazing gank setup, and the ability to easily pick up kills in the river or off other lanes, Fizz can easily take over a game completely. The difficulty and downsides of Fizz come in the mid to late game. While he doesn't exactly fall off, he becomes a lot harder to play as people tend to group up and there are far fewer opportunities to make picks. A good Fizz can still make it work, but the best Fizz players are going to make sure that the game never gets to this point to begin with. Cassadin offers a very different play style to most champions on this list, being extremely weak in the early stages of the game and most of the difficulty coming from managing that very weak early game. Not just to ensure that you survive, but so that your teammates don't suffer too much as well. If you can manage to neutralize, Kassin really comes online in the mid game and scales exceptionally well into the late game. If you can manage to pick up a couple random kills here and there, Cassadin's snowballing potential is second to none. And it's no surprise that he boasts one of the highest pentacill rates of any mid laner. While Cassadin can be one tricked, I'd highly suggest pairing him with at least one other champion, as his weak early game makes him best used as a counter pick into matchups where either you can scale for free or find some easy kills. Now, for our most difficult assassins, despite having an absurdly high skill ceiling, I almost put Zed in the medium difficulty tier because his fairly low skill floor and ability to neutralize bad matchups make him less punishing to learn than some of the other assassins. That being said, after playing against Z99 and on Zed many times in Korea, I was convinced that the sky really is the limit with this champion. Zed has always had insane outplay potential, but with the rise of haste focused builds giving access to more sustained damage and lower cooldowns, his playmaking potential has really gone through the roof. Zed's play style overall is quite unique with him being considered the mage of the assassins or sometimes jokingly being referred to as a range champion due to his spammable longrange combo. If you don't enjoy the single target assassin style and instead prefer killing the entire team instead, or if you're just looking for a champion to invest thousands of hours into, it's absolutely worth giving Zet a shot. Kiana is another champion we considered top of the line when it comes to 1v9 potential, primarily due to her insane snowballing and lack of effective counterplay once she does get fed. The big downside to Kiana is it can be pretty difficult to get the ball rolling in the 1v one. Like most assassins, she's an incredible skirmisher and can easily get fed.
