play with a lot of them being mechanically demanding which makes for a really fun skill expressive gameplay and that can scare a lot of newcomers away from trying to learn the role but it really shouldn't mid can definitely be difficult to master but becoming decent enough to perform consistently really isn't all that difficult learn just a few Concepts and you can start doing well almost immediately and that's what we're going to be covering in this guide we're going to go over some beginner-friendly Champions how they trade a key skill known as break points some super simple warding tips to keep you safe how to roam and win the map properly and then how to win the game in the later stages so whether you're a completely new player or someone just looking to pick up a new role this guide should really help get you started at skillcapped we pride ourselves on giving players the best information from the top players in the game and simplifying it so the average everyday player can actually understand it and use it properly everything we're going to discuss in this video is just the tip of the iceberg when it comes to improving at being a midlaner so if by the end of this video you found everything helpful and you want to learn even more then you can find way more information on our site over at skillcap there you're going to find our midlane Essentials courses that cover every single detail you could possibly need to become a great midlaner and that's on top of everything else you can learn ranging from mechanics settings farming guides and so much more so if you're interested after this video use the discount link below to sign up anyway first things first anyone either new to a specific role or league in general typically wants to know some beginner-friendly Champions to get started and have some success the best answer is to of course just play whatever you find fun or really interest you now we're a firm believer that you're going to improve faster if you actually enjoy the learning process now that being said the most common recommendation for beginner midlaners always tends to be Annie this is because of her extremely forgiving and really straightforward kit you just build up some Stacks to get your stun ready and then you drop tippers on someone for some easy kills but if you want to get better faster we'd actually advise you to stay away from Annie this is because midlane is a fairly skill expressive role centered around spacing and Landing skill shots Annie is so basic that she can teach you some really bad habits you won't learn how to play around your abilities properly or space out opponents which can really hurt your long-term growth in the game so after a few games we'd suggest getting off anny and into something at least slightly more skill expressive one of our top recommendations here is really going to be Lux although her entire kit is based around skill shots she's easy and she's straightforward if you manage to land your que which is a route it guarantees that the rest of your spells will land letting you score kills pretty dang easily so so with Lux you're going to learn good habits for landing skill shots but also be able to have immediate success as well besides Lux we'd recommend a couple more Champions that are fairly straightforward you could go for vex a burst Mage with a simple play pattern this bar near your health represents your passive when it's full this makes it so your next basic ability will fear any one hit your kill combo is really easy you press your ultimate and hopefully landed on a squishy Target if you tag someone you can press your R again to Dash right on top of them then you immediately press W dealing damage around you and this is going to trigger your passive and fear and that'll let you land your e and Q which are skill shots effectively guaranteed since your opponent is feared this is usually enough to burst squishy targets if you're looking for an assassin option though keep in mind that most melee champions in mid are very skill expressive and really hard to have success with as a beginner the simplest one that we'd recommend you have would be Diana Diana isn't really an assassin but she can function as one if you build her that way her kit is pretty dang simple as well if you land your Q which is a pretty easy to hit skill shot you're going to Mark a Target your e is a dash that deals damage but the cool down immediately resets if your target was marked by Q so the way you kill other players is by pressing q eing and dashing on top of them and then just Auto attacking them to death while also using your W's shield and if they get away you have your second e to chase him down the best part is that Diana can easily duel a ton of Champions but she's also a very easy team Fighting Champion as well her ultimate pulls everyone around her dealing massive damage this makes Diana a very versatile Champion that can let you play in a variety of play Styles where you either go the Lone Wolf route or you play more grouped up with your whole team all right so let's move on and discuss trading of course we can't cover every little detail in one single beginner video but we're going to be focusing on the skills that are most relevant to Mid lanane so for example we'd probably discuss how to maximize your auto attacks in bot lanane filled with Marksman Champions but in mid we're trying to get the most value out of our abilities so let's focus on that the number one trading concept that you should really practice is tracking your own minions HP and getting ready from when your opponent is about to last hit one this is because most midlaners are based around skill shots you cannot pass this opportunity up as your enemy is about to CS a minion they have to stand still so it's way more likely that you're going to be able to land your spell making your harass extremely consistent now we cannot overemphasize this part as simple as that sounds it's probably the single most important mid-lane fundamental to really learn and master it's just that valuable to do and in the future when you learn some more advanced concepts along the way you're going to see that many of them are really based on improving this one simple trading tactic the second thing you want to look out for is know known as a double value spell for every lane including mid one of the simplest trading tactics is having a minion Advantage if you've got more minions you generally win almost any trade regardless of matchup in mid lane because most trades are based on abilities you want to look for key moments where your opponent is standing next to their own minions using a spell to trade and push the wave at the same time is going to accomplish two goals the enemy mid had no choice but to run away because her wave died during that trade so you win the trade now but also get Lane control by having the minion Advantage afterwards too you may also want to consider avoiding this yourself standing outside of your own minions would force your opponent into choosing whether to use a spell on you or the wave of course do be mindful of standing outside your minions if you're against a champion like Ari who can punish you with her charm obviously there is some Nuance based on what you're up against another key concept to consider is playing around your own Rune cooldowns Mages and assassins mid have trading patterns that are generally based on the runes that they take for example Lux tends to run Arcane Comet because of her e slows so in Lane her main trading pattern is just eing to deal damage and also proc her Comet or leblanc's main trading pattern is going for a qw auto tray to proc her electrocute and Sudden Impact from her runes basically you usually want to track your own runes cooldowns in your bar and coordinate your trades based on when those runes are up this is especially important because many champions have Mana issues early on going for damage efficient trades is going to be really important if you want to win Lane before going out of Mana our final simple tip you want to look out for is the specific level UPS where you become much stronger in mid even if you're completely new to The game everyone already knows that level six is a big moment for your champion because you get access to your ultimate but in mid lane because almost every Champion has an area of effect spell that they're maxing as their first ability there's some specific level UPS you really want to keep in mind that are going to help you trade and control the pace of the lane and that brings us to our next concept break points now this is going to sound really complicated at first but soon we really hope you're going to be able to see that it really isn't a breakpoint is simply a very specific way in which your Champion can kill a Minion from 100% HP let's use Lux for an example here at level six Lux has one of the best break points in the entire game she can place her e as a wave is coming in and use her ultimate on it your e plus RS damage is enough to kill the melee minions and the casters behind it it's a bit abnormal to use your ultimate that way but Lux is unique since her old cooldown is so low the reason this is so good on Lux is because she's clearing the wave so quickly and so early into the game that no other Champion can match how fast she's pushing at this time the faster you can push in a wave the more time you're going to have to do whatever it is that you want to do in League you can recall roam around the map Ward whatever you want the key key is clearing that wave faster which brings us back to break points every Champion has very unique ways of clearing waves but 90% of mid laners tend to follow a very specific pattern for killing specifically Caster minions for a beginner focusing on the Caster minions is really all you need to do at level five casting the spell that you're maxing first will deal half of the Caster's HP so casting that spell twice will kill all three casters easily at level seven your main ability will leave casters really low on health you can typically finish them off with an auto attack or another AOE ability if your Champion has one then at level 9 your main spell should be killing the casters in one single cast trust us these three break points is extremely useful for planning ahead you can use this information in so many different ways for example at the most basic level it can let you know whether you can push a wave or not this Vex is running low on mana and only has enough left for two more Q's which is the main spell she Maxes because she's level five and knows her break points she decides to push she cast one Q putting the casters to half HP and then another to finish off the wave simply knowing this information beforehand let her make a good decision at a critical moment where she was low on resources or at level seven she knows that her Q Plus one other spell will kill all the Caster minions this gives her the confidence to instantly push that wave and then go roam to somewhere else on the map again a decision you can only make with knowledge of these basic break points so keep these breakpoint timers in mind level five level s and level 9 are big moments for your wave clear on most Champions as a midlaner roaming is a topic that we're going to cover soon this is because mid is in the center of the map so you have access to moving and making a play anywhere this is a huge upside but one that comes with a massive downside too being in the center of the map also means that everyone has access to you top and Bot Lane's only options are to roam towards you not only that but almost every single jungle camp on the map is relatively close to Mid a jungler is never really that far away from your lane so you're really going to get ganked a lot to make sure you stay safe we're going to teach you what we like to call the autopilot Ward that can help you avoid almost any gank here's what you do pick a side of the map that you think is dangerous and move into the river then place a ward along the bottom of either of these ramps depending on what side you've chosen the reason you ward this is because it covers gangs from this area of the Jungle but also the river if you warded this brush for example you'd be susceptible to a gank from here problem is you've only got one Ward you can't Ward both sides of the river so the next step is to play on the side of the map that you've warded during the lane this makes it so there's only two outcomes that can happen first if an enemy ganks you from one side of the map that you've warded for example we see VX has placed her autopilot W down afterwards you're going to notice how she's playing on the right side of the lane close to the the vision that she's put down as she keeps pushing she notices the two Champions coming thanks to reward and can now simply walk to the other side the second outcome is if someone comes from the side where you haven't warded during this fight Diana was on the side of the lane with her vision as the Fight Continues the enemy jungler comes in but because the lane is so big and takes a while to cross Diana can react to the enemy jungler and simply just walk away basically you're going to be safe either way this autopilot Ward it really isn't perfect but it's pretty effective and one of the best strategies for avoiding most ganks especially at a beginner level all right so let's talk about roaming as a midlaner because this is definitely one of your biggest strengths but doing it right oh man is it important the one thing you want to avoid when roaming is leaving your Tower vulnerable to damage as you leave mid-tier one is super valuable going into the mid game but if you roam at a bad time it allows your enemy laner to get chip damage on it as you leave Lane this can result in your mid-tier one going down very easily later on which is definitely not good this is your number one priority if you can roam without your Tower taking damage you're going to be good the way to do that is simple though by clearing the wave in mid you take away your opponent's ability to push your Tower so now you can roam freely well kind of there's an important detail to me that a lot of players really don't think about waves always spawn at the same time in every every lane however mid is unique where the lane is shorter so the waves end up crashing in mid way faster than in other lanes what this means is that you have less time than other rolls to move from Lane before another wave gets there to allow your opponent to push this reduces the amount of time you have to roam so what you'd want to avoid are situations like this you walk up to a wave that's already crashed in mid you slowly push it and crash it into the enemy Tower and start to roam but the next wave is already here so this would be a horrible time to roam you can see we haven't even reached bottom yet and there's a wave your opponent could use to push into your Tower this is likely going to be a really bad Ram where your Tower would take a ton of damage midlaners make up for this smaller Ram window by having the best wave clear in the game the best thing to look out for is when you can clear a wave as it's walking into the lane if you clear a wave quickly as it's coming into your lane you're going to have way more time to roam you can see we reached bot Lane this time before the next wave had even arrived in mid this will give you way more time to get kills during your roam but also make it back in time to defend your Tower this is exactly why we spent so much time discussing wave clear break points level seven and nine are really key moments in the game where your wave clear amps up significantly now you can clear waves really quickly and roam around the map to score kills and because you killed the wave so dang fast the next wave won't be a arriving for a while leaving your opponent with nothing to do but wait for the next wave those are the two key details to roaming think about how vulnerable your Tower will be if you leave Lane and how you can avoid that by clearing away faster so if you want to learn more about roaming properly then you can check out our much more detailed course covering everything you need to know on our website along with everything else you could ever need to master mid lane you can access them right now with the special midlane beginners discount Link in the description below although we can't dive super deep into macro and a quick starter guide here we can offer you some decent strategies based on the champion that you're playing that you may want to look out for for starters if you're playing a mage then you need to understand a really simple concept that's going to make your life so much easier typically in almost every single game there is some sort of Defenders advantage to holding an area first so even if you're equal in power with your opponent if you're the one defending you're probably going to win the fight in League this happens in the form of skillshot Advantage when someone is walking towards you and you you fire a skill shot it is very easy to hit them because their hitbox and your skill shot are moving towards each other so they're going to collide very quickly so anytime you play a mage you're going to quickly notice that it's super easy to land spells and combo players when they're walking towards you now on the other hand it'll often feel really difficult to try and land a combo if you're the one walking into your opponents one of the best things you can do as a mage is track when objectives are spawning if you think you and your team are going to to fight over an objective you want to be in control over that area first Mages bring insanely strong Zone control spells that make it really difficult for enemy players to walk into an area because of what we just discussed now as a result being at the objective first over your opponents will make their lives very miserable going into you and you're going to be much more likely to succeed in fights like these now of course we're not saying to always go to every objective no matter what to achieve this but just make sure you press Tab and check when objectives are spawning if you think your team's going to contest then do try to follow this advice but don't force it just because we told you in this one YouTube video next let's talk about assassins or melee carries in mid these types of Champions tend to excel in 1 V1 or 2v2 small type skirmishes basically you're looking to assassinate players or go for smaller scale fights rather than always going for that 5v5 team fight this is because melee midlaners tend to have good mobility and dueling capabilities so you're extremely self-sufficient and you're going to typically have a lot of ways to outplay a fight in order to find these smaller scale fights more consistently you want to use the fact that you're so self-sufficient to always try and push Sid Lane waves aggressively the reason why you need to do this is because if no one is pushing in waves in the side Lanes then all 10 players in the game are just going to naturally gravitate to the mid lane with everyone so clumped up your strength as an assassin or skirmisher is completely gone by pushing In Waves aggressively you're forcing someone on the enemy team to split up and respond to the push this naturally is going to give you opportunities throughout the game to pick off people as they split off from their team to catch waves or as they're trying to rejoin them this is your main goal as a melee midlaner try to create Chaos on the map by utilizing the fact that you're self-sufficient enough to be alone in the sidelanes and then you wait for opportunities that are going to naturally present themselves as enemy players split up to try and defend the waves you pushing to their side of the map again this isn't the only thing you can do in a game but it is one of your main strengths and you should be trying to do this as often as possible all right guys so we really hope that gives you a much better understanding of how to begin your midlane journey again if you want to learn the role even more in depth check out our courses over at skillcap using the link below thanks for watching everyone we'll catch you next time

titre: Lucian: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Tout public. Bienvenue dans ce focus de League of Legends sur Lucian, le Purificateur. Lucian est un attaquant à distance très mobile équipé d'armes antiques, qui cherche à libérer l'esprit de sa femme de Thresh. Lucian : Tu as pris ce qu'il y avait de meilleur en moi, Thresh.  Ce qu'il reste, c'est ton pire ennemi. Phreak : Rapide et agressif, Lucian est si flexible que cela fait de lui un adversaire redoutable en voie solo ou duo.  Son passif est Pistolero. Elément essentiel de son style de jeu, Pistolero permet à Lucian de faire un double-tir après chaque compétence. Cela dit, le deuxième tir inflige des dégâts réduits aux champions et bâtiments. Si vous utilisez au mieux votre passif lors de vos attaques, vous pourrez améliorer grandement vos dégâts. De plus, comme le double-tir de Lucian compte comme deux attaques, cela lui permet de doubler son vol de vie et ses effets de sort, comme Lame du roi déchu.  Lumière perforante, son A, tire un rayon sur un ennemi unique. Même s'il n'y a qu'une seule cible, le rayon inflige des dégâts à tous les ennemis sur son chemin. La portée de lancer est assez limitée, mais notez que le rayon traverse tout de même sa cible, et peut donc atteindre des ennemis derrière votre victime. La trajectoire de Lumière perforante est fixée dès le début de l'animation, les cibles réactives peuvent donc éviter les dégâts. Grâce à sa portée et à sa faible récupération, cette compétence est idéale pour harceler les cibles à bonne distance sur la voie. Flamboiement tire un projectile en forme d'étoile en ligne. Quand il touche un ennemi ou arrive au bout de sa portée, il explose en quatre directions et marque les ennemis pendant quelques secondes. Attaquer une cible marquée octroie à Lucian une courte accélération qui se renouvelle à chaque attaque successive. Flamboiement révèle brièvement la zone d'impact, permettant de traquer les ennemis dans le brouillard de guerre. Pour dominer sur sa voie, Lucian peut utiliser l'accélération de Flamboiement pour chasser les ennemis en les attaquant un peu plus. Poursuite inlassable est un court bond en avant qui annule les ralentissements. Si Lucian est coincé, Poursuite inlassable permet de sauter par-dessus le terrain. Voilà quelques exemples d'issues de secours utiles. L'ultime de Lucian est Déluge de balles. Il désactive les attaques de base de Lucian et déchaîne un barrage d'énergie lumineuse dans la direction ciblée, infligeant des dégâts physiques et magiques. Chaque balle blesse le premier ennemi qu'elle touche, les tanks peuvent donc protéger leurs alliés fragiles. Lucian peut bouger, faire son bond et utiliser les sorts d'invocateur pendant son ultime. Ses autres compétences sont bloquées pendant la canalisation. S'il tue un champion pendant Déluge de balles, la récupération de Poursuite inlassable est réinitialisée. Il n'y a aucune limite à la réinitialisation de son bond pendant son ultime. Déluge de balles inflige des dégâts bonus aux sbires, permettant de dégager le terrain vers les cibles prioritaires. Si votre ennemi sort de votre ligne de tir, appuyez sur R pour annuler votre ultime et reprendre l'attaque. Au début, Lumière perforante est mieux adaptée pour harceler les ennemis sur la voie que pour farmer à cause des dégâts réduits sur les sbires. Grâce à Pistolero et à son double-tir après compétence, Lucian réussit à infliger plus de dégâts qu'il ne peut en encaisser. Grâce à la portée de Lumière perforante, vérifiez toujours si vous pouvez percer plus d'un ennemi avec le rayon, pour infliger plus de dégâts. La portée de Déluge de balles permet d'éliminer des cibles tout en restant mobile. Après un échange de coups avec Leona, Lucian bondit et lance son ultime vers sa fuite afin qu'elle ne puisse pas éviter le barrage. La mobilité de Lucian combinée à la courte récupération de Lumière perforante permet de dominer là où un attaquant à distance devrait normalement mourir. Bien que piégé par Maokai, Lucian est agressif et déchaîne Lumière perforante et Pistolero sur Caitlyn, puis marque Maokai avec Flamboiement afin de s'accélérer. Cela lui permet de tenir le rythme pour réussir à tuer sa cible avec Lumière perforante. Comme Pistolero s'applique à toutes les compétences, Lucian peut faire de très gros dégâts en attaquant après chacune d'elles. Ici, il élimine Zyra avec simplement ses compétences de base et le bonus de son passif. De plus, même si son ultime n'inflige pas beaucoup de dégâts, Lucian réinitialise Poursuite inlassable en l'activant avant qu'Embrasement ne tue Zyra, lui permettant de bondir à l'écart de Caitlyn. Comme chaque balle de l'ultime peut déclencher le bonus de déplacement de Flamboiement, Lucian peut s'échapper magistralement en combinant les deux. En plus du bonus de déplacement, Déluge de balles inflige assez de dégâts pour décourager l'ennemi de poursuivre Lucian. Même Leona ne peut pas dépasser la tourelle en traversant son ultime. Lucian peut contribuer aux dégâts en combat d'équipe grâce à Lumière perforante et Déluge de balles. Mais en l'absence de vrai contrôle de foule, il est difficile de lancer toutes ses compétences dans l'aide de son équipe. Ici, Lucian brille car ses alliés bloquent l'équipe ennemie, ce qui lui permet de concentrer son ultime sur Elise et Zyra. Ayant affaibli ses cibles avec Déluge de balles, Lucian bondit jusqu'à sa deuxième victime. Côté config, concentrez-vous sur les dégâts d'attaque et la réduction des récupérations pour renforcer son usage des compétences. Côté runes, marques de dégâts d'attaque, sceaux d'armure, glyphes de résistance magique, quintessences de vol de vie. Côté maîtrises, tentez 21/5/4, avec réduction des récupérations et dégâts d'attaque en Attaque, PV en Défense et régénération de mana et Saut éclair amélioré en Utilitaire. Côté sorts d'invocateur, Saut éclair avec Embrasement et Barrière pour vite vous replacer et sécuriser les morts. Merci d'avoir regardé ce focus sur Lucian. Pensez à vous abonner à la chaîne YouTube de Riot Games et laissez-nous vos commentaires sous la vidéo !

titre: Jhin: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Welcome to the League of Legends Champion Spotlight, featuring Jhin, the Virtuoso. A twisted murderer behind a smiling mask, Jhin  isn't interested in simply killing his victims, but obsesses over killing them perfectly. He sees death as the single most important moment in a person's life, and strives to make each spectacular. In-game, Jhin takes up a marksman role, and terrorizes his enemies with powerful pistol-based attacks and some of the longest ranged abilities in the game. Jhin's passive is named after his gun - Whisper - and has two components. First, Whisper can only chamber four bullets, the last of which always crits and deals bonus damage based on his target's missing health. After he's fired his fourth bullet, or if a few seconds pass without him firing at all, Jhin will take a moment to reload. Second, Whisper has a fixed attack speed, and any attack speed and crit chance he gets during a game converts into attack damage. His crits deal less damage than usual but give him a burst of movement speed based on his would-be attack speed. The TL;DR is that Jhin's going to attack a lot slower but deal a lot more damage per shot, while any crit and AS buys will help him kite or chase. Next up is Jhin's Q: Dancing Grenade. Jhin throws a cannister at an enemy, which deals damage before bouncing to the next nearest baddie. Dancing Grenade can strike up to four total targets, and deals increased damage on successive bounces whenever it lands a kill. The damage amp stacks, meaning Dancing Grenade will deal tons of damage on its fourth bounce if the previous three all scored a kill. Use Dancing Grenade to keep your damage flowing while you're reloading, and to inflict damage on your target whenever they're near low-health minions! You'll also get easy minion gold, which is nice. Now to Jhin's W. Deadly Flourish passively marks enemy champions who've been struck by Jhin's basic attacks, his E, or allied champion damage. When activated, Jhin fires a shot in a target direction that stops at the first enemy champion struck, damaging them along with all minions and monsters along the way. If Jhin hits a marked target, Deadly Flourish will root them and gift Jhin a burst of movement speed as well. Use Deadly Flourish to help out your allies from backstage. As mentioned in the Jhintroduction, you have massive range - especially with your W - and you can help your allies flee from danger or stick to their targets. Jhin's E, Captive Audience, plants a lotus trap at a target location that turns invisible and arms after a short delay. Enemy champions who walk over these traps trigger them, revealing nearby enemies, and creating a sizeable slowing zone for a couple of seconds. That's basically the countdown timer to gtfo, because when the slow zone collapses, it deals damage to everyone still inside. Jhin can store up to two traps at a time, and once they're placed, they last a couple of minutes before disarming. Captive Audience also has a passive - Beauty in Death - which causes enemy champions killed by Jhin to instantly create the same slowing zone and subsequent explosion as a triggered lotus trap. Place your Captive Audience traps along typical jungler paths to buy you a few seconds during enemy gank attempts. Remember to keep an eye on your map though - traps save lives, but only with solid map awareness! Finally, Jhin's ultimate is Curtain Call. On first activation, Jhin takes aim in a broad target area. He can then reactivate Curtain Call to fire up to four shots. Each bullet reveals the area around them as they travel, damaging minions and monsters in their path, and stopping at the first enemy champion struck. Each round slows the champion they hit and deals increasing damage based on the snipee's missing health. In case you haven't picked up on it yet, four is totally Jhin's favourite number, so the deadliest bullet - which is Garenteed to crit - comes last. Finally, if his targets all manage to duck hunt their way out of Jhin's target area, he can cancel Curtain Call early and refund a portion of its cooldown based on the number of unspent bullets. Curtain Call is clearly a super powerful execute move, but sometimes you're better off using it to slow distant enemies for your allies that are chasing them down. Or you can just steal all their kills. I mean, you're a marksman - you do need the gold more than they do. Jhin is nasty in lane when he teams up with a cc-heavy support. To get the biggest bang out of your buck, you'll want to chain Deadly Flourish so it roots right before your lane pal's cc wears off. Getting this right takes practice, but you can ima-Jhin all the implications. Jhin's only got four bullets in most encounters, meaning you have to be super selective when choosing your targets during fights. While his target selection in this fight makes sense for any other marksman, Jhin doesn't have enough bullets left to finish off Kalista, leaving her alive with just a sliver of health. Jhin can start fights from well outside counterattack range, using Deadly Flourish and Curtain Call to whittle down or kill off your enemies. Here, he sets up to go foraging for kills from the opposite side of the dragon pit, landing his fourth shot on Jinx to speed into the fight. From here, Jhin waltzes in, hitting whatever's convenient before Flashing towards Jinx and killing her off with that lethal fourth bullet. If you're trying to run from an enemy, try laying down a Captive Audience trap along your escape path, then once your wannabe killer triggers it, nail them with Deadly Flourish. You'll slow, root, and hurt them, and probably enable your great escape. Sometimes you're better off tagging beefy frontliners, slowing them from reaching your team's squishies like you. Other times you'll spot low health baddies clustered with their team, giving you the opportunity to pick up the target and deal AoE damage from the subsequent beauty in death explosion. Jhin has a ton of tools to help his team take down stranded enemies, even when the Virtuoso himself is miles away. Alistar tries going in, but ends up pulverizing a whole lotta nothing after a pretty Gnar-ly Flash. Now stranded, Jhin's allies start hungering for beef, applying the passive from Deadly Flourish and setting up a long-range root. Repeated slows from Curtain Call then send Alistar to the slaughterhouse, meaning Jhin tipped the cow's fate while remaining farther than a screen away. Thanks for tuning into the Jhin Champion Spotlight. Please Subscribe to the Riot Games YouTube channel and leave us your comments just below the video.

titre: Poppy: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Welcome to the League of Legends Champion spotlight, featuring Poppy, Keeper of the Hammer. League's lightweight brawler, Poppy packs a mallet-sized wallop into a mini-sized frame. In her quest to find the rightful wielder of the legendary hammer she carries, Poppy wanders Runeterra, never suspecting she might have already completed her mission. Unaware, but unafraid, Poppy brings her baddie-battering to the battlefield along with a slew of potent crowd control abilities to unlock her tanky, disruptive potential. Poppy's passive is Iron Ambassador. Every several seconds, Poppy throws her buckler as her basic attack, gaining range, bonus magic damage, and applying on-hit effects. Once thrown, Poppy can pick up her buckler to gain a shield, but if an enemy walks over it, they'll destroy the Buckler. If Iron Ambassador kills the target, the  buckler returns to Poppy automatically,  but still goes on cooldown as normal. Poppy's Q is Hammer Shock. She smashes the ground, dealing percent max health damage and slowing enemies in a small area. After a moment, the crushed ground erupts, dealing the same damage again. When laning, use Hammer Shock as your opponent tries to last hit. If you manage to land the aftershock as well, you'll hammer away a huge chunk of your opponent's health and easily come out ahead in the trade. Poppy's W has passive and active components. Passively, Poppy gains bonus Armor and Magic Resist.  This bonus is increased while Poppy is low on health. When she activates Steadfast Presence, the wee warrior gains Movement Speed and creates a magic barrier around her, stopping enemy dashes and damaging any enemies struck.  It's important to note she only stops the first dash from any enemy - so the second  and third casts of Ahri's Spirit Rush get through - and Steadfast Presence can't stop blinks like Flash or Ezreal's Arcane Shift. Poppy can use Steadfast Presence to get out of harm's way, instead of staying in trouble.  The movement speed boost keeps you ahead of your enemies, while the zone will prevent them from dashing on top of you. When using her E, Heroic Charge, Poppy tackles an enemy, damaging and carrying them forward. Poppy deals additional damage and stuns the target if they collide with terrain. Always keep an eye out for terrain to slam your enemies into. Getting the stun on Heroic Charge will often lead to a kill if your team is around to follow up. Poppy's ultimate is Keeper's Verdict, for when you really need to bring the hammer down. Poppy rears back for a mighty blow, slowing herself. On the second cast, Poppy unleashes a massive hammer smash, battering enemies hit towards their own nexus. She can hit more than one enemy in a small area around the first champion struck. Channelling longer increases the knockback, or you can just mash R instantly to knock them straight up in the air instead. Oh, and enemies in the air are untargetable until the very tail end of the knockback. Keeper's Verdict is the most extreme displacement effect in the game, and using it well is the difference between a 5v5 slugfest into a 5v4 slaughter. Poppy excels in tight areas of the map. Steadfast Presence's movesment speed into Heroic Charge's wall-slam Wrecks'sai. Thanks to the stun, it ensures both hits of Hammer Shock, picking up an easy kill. When Poppy's caught out, it only means one thing: Hammer time. Poppy perfectly times Steadfast Presence to block Lee Sin and LeBlanc's dashes, then uses Keeper's Verdict to bash them back toward their base and keep calm carrying on towards her side of the map. In teamfights, Poppy acts a yordle-shield for her team, keeping enemies from dashing past or away from her. Lucian can't E out and Shen can't Taunt forward until after the ability ends, giving lots of free time for Annie and Caitlyn to deal tons of damage. Poppy falls back and helps her team take down the beefy ninja before pursuing their remaining foes. Poppy's ultimate, Keeper's Verdict, is game-changing but not always for the better.  Here Poppy carefully, methodically... smacks Kassadin and Shen out of her base just in time for Annie to drop Tibbers on nothing at all.  Sometimes not even /allchat can make it worth. Putting it all together, let's check out this early tower dive. While searching for the hero of legend in the jungle, Poppy runs into Lee Sin, angling her Heroic Charge to just barely pin him a gainst the edge of the wall. Like our hearts when Worlds isn't on, Steadfast Presence is a dash-less zone,  and Lee can't Safeguard to his buddy Bard. Then, Iron Ambassador's buckler toss into the Wandering Caretaker applies red buff, making Heroic Charge easy to land. As Bard waddles away, Poppy sits back, not wanting to tower dive recklessly. After he falls, Poppy turns to Tristana, making the smart choice to Charge a minion instead of the enemy yordle, which forces Trist's Flash as Poppy is blocking the escape. Thanks for tuning into to the Poppy Champion Spotlight. Please Subscribe to the Riot Games YouTube channel and leave us your comments just below the video. Poppy: "Didn't I just knock you away?" Ekko: (laughs)

ever argue over two things first is that Top Lane is without a doubt the most unforgiving role in the game if you make a single mistake in this role it feels like you're unable to play for the rest of us to the second thing every High ELO player knows that Wave Control knowledge is by far the most important for top laners out of every role and that it is probably the most critical skill you can learn to get better at it like we said you're likely in a melee matchup where you don't have access to range wave clear to try and farm safely other worlds can often push waves from really far away to avoid danger but in top you have to get up close and personal to farm so if your wave is in a bad spot it is unlikely you get out of it unscathed not only that but a matchup can completely swing based on where the wave is crashed as you can see this Darius is struggling to trade versus the enemy atrox while the wave is here the lane feels unwinable at the moment so you think that both players are gets destroyed which eventually lets Darius hit level two first and Zone him from renekton's perspective he probably thinks Darius is just overpowered I mean he literally just ran at Renekton to out attack the minions to get the push lead and here's why notice here how Renekton is going to land a ton more Auto attacks on the minions compared to Wukong then when renekton's ability is off coold down he can look to land it but the goal is to get what's called a double value spell you're looking to damage the opponent and the minions at the same time now the ability is on coold down so we're back to spamming Auto attacks on the wave to secure the push lead then once the ability comes back up we can look for another double value spell you repeat this pattern on the first wave Auto minions and look for a double value spell when your ability is up as once the second wave arrives the first melee minion will Spike you level two this sets up level two allins on the opponent which can outright win you your lane in the first 3 minutes of the game basically the matchup won't matter if you get the push lead since the level two spike is so powerful and the majority of top laners simply aren't aware of this concept as we saw in the Platinum replay where neither darus or reneka knew they needed two Auto minions and both just sat AFK at the start of the lane so step one in Top Lane is literally just making sure you get the push lead on the first wave by making sure you're autoing the minions whenever you can great this sounds simple enough you'll probably head into your next game put this into action get that push lead early land some double value spells hit level two and then the opponent sits a mile back not falling for the level two Allin guess what this is still winning when the opponent doesn't fall for your level two Allin you then transition into what's called a cheater recall Step 11 in our Wave Control course on our website perfectly summarizes how to do a cheater recall so let's watch it a cheater recall is a really famous early wave strategy and one of the deadliest tactics you can pull off for a big lead if it's done properly here's how it works on the first wave get the minion Advantage by damaging the wave more than the enemy as the second wave arrives look to maintain your minion Advantage as much as possible don't make the mistake of over pushing though as you don't want to crash just yet that's because you want to crash precisely on the third wave so once it arrives push as quickly as possible to crash it into the tower now you can you so you can now freeze and force trades with your item advantage and play the lane normally from here you can use a cheater recall in all three lanes to get a guaranteed Advantage right at the start of the game great so what you're going to see is Renekton follows that advice gets the level two Spike transitions to only last hitting to not over push the wave third wave arrives so we hard push it to set up your recall timing and then you head back to Lane with an item advantage while the wave pushes back to you letting you set up a freeze you'll see how this one tactic wins Reon his Lane so Now Zed is being zoned from Minions as he can only last it with his Q due to renekton's item lead at the same time whenever Zed gets to close Renekton can go in for a trade this pattern of denying minions and engaging for winning trades continues until eventually the enemy jungler tries to gank to relieve pressure but it's a waste of time since Z is so low and speaking of which once an enemy gets low enough you just push to set up a tower dive we'll talk more on this later for now though Z has been zoned a ton of Cs ended up dying and losing the wave to the tower all from this one cheater recall tactic so this is your very simple game plan get the push level one and either get a level two all in to win your lane or you transition into a cheater recall if you don't get the enemy low enough in health at which point you come back to Lane and start forcing winning trades so this will work against the majority of players you face but what about when you don't get the push lead at level one whether it's due to the enemy knowing to fight for it or the matchup just sucks for you or you have to give a leash like in this game well here's what you do your goal is to take minimal damage level one you don't try to fight against the push you're actually hoping the enemy over pushes and crashes a wave level two so they can't cheat or recall this does mean at some point you're going to be giving up CS especially once the enemy is going to spike level two on you once the wave crashes into your Tower you're just focusing on last hitting until you're level three as once you're level three you're equal in levels with the opponent and can begin to start looking for potential trades this is because when an enemy crashes a big wave into your Tower like this it creates what's known as a bounceback or rebound the third step in our brand new Top Lane Essentials course explains this concept way better than I can when you crash a wave at Tower it will often stall like so this causes the wave to eventually slow push back the other way the major reason it's so important to understand bounce backs is because slow pushes have far more trading value in Lanes where both Champions are melee but another key factor that bounce backs provide is safety Top Lane is also the most vulnerable role to multiple factors for starters it's the counterpick role if you're in a bad matchup you will absolutely feel it way more than any other lane you all know how awful it feels to be counterpicked here not only that but it's a very snowbally role if you fall behind the game is unplayable so you need to be able to avoid ganks Dives and allins as much as you possibly can bounce backs are not only your best friend for getting leads but also to keep you safe from Bad matchups and ganks let's break down all the aggressive and defensive plays you need to know how to play around bounce backs properly let's start at the beginning after your opponent crashes a wave it will eventually bounce as a slow push in your favor your goal is to build up as big of a wave as possible slowly pushing it to your opponent's side of the lane during this time you can be very aggressive looking for trades especially early on into the game like we said before more minions gives you an easier time to harass that's the simple part the push is both the most aggressive part of a bounceback but also the most vulnerable the problem is that although you have a slow pish going you're pushing towards your opponent's side of the lane this means that you can get ganked or if you take a bad trade the wave can be frozen against you and so on so if you're in a bad spot you're low on HP or you're in a bad matchup then when you've got a bounceback slow push going only focus on crashing the wave don't focus on trading or anything like that if you play on your bigger wave during the early levels your opponent probably doesn't have the wave clear to fight you with so many minions around as long as you crash the wave you will be able to neutralize the lane this is because of what happens after you crash the wave your opponent is now stuck in Lane unable to move not only that but you now know for certain that the wave will bounce back to your side of the lane eventually you canuse use this information to plan around building a lead or stabilizing a bad Lane so for example cion knew that this was a bad lande state so he focused entirely on crashing the wave one of the best things you can do off of any crash is simply to base and heal up while buying items this is how you can neutralize a lot of bad Lane matchups the reason this is so effective is because you won't lose almost any Farm while you're gone because the wave is pushing back into you you're going to come back to a wave either close to or at your Tower which you can Farm up easily then if your opponent based you'll be alone like cion is here and you can crash again and if you crash again you can play safe and even repeat this process or as a hypothetical if the garant had stayed Canan would have had a massive item lead either option was good for him in this rough early Lane okay so that was a lot of info and we're not even halfway through the video so let's stop it there basically when this wave crashes the first step is hitting level three to equalize with the opponent you'll then have that bounce back so if you're stronger at this point you can look for aggressive trades as the enemy is so overextended that you have a ton of room to run them down in a trade if instead you felt weaker due to the matchup you simply focus on maintaining a minion lead and building up a big wave off your bounce back instead regardless both scenarios will usually result in you being in this exact spot you'll hit level four before the opponent due to your minion advantage and have a big wave built up it's worth noting a lot of the times the enemy will actually just die to you overextending during this time we'll cover this later however if instead you get an enemy really low but didn't kill them like you saw in the Z clip then this is a great time to Tower dive like we taught you earlier if instead the enemy is too healthy well you just push this wave into setting up a recall that probably sounds familiar and yeah it's pretty much the same game plan as the cheater recall which is why I like to call this a delayed cheater recall so we now have the item advantage and the enemy is pushing back into us and so whenever they overextend we can look to trade with our item advantage and you can see how this once again sets up a solo kill for us so let's quickly recap our game plan early on level one we look to get the push lead if we do we either get a level two Allin on the opponent or they play safe and so we cheat a recall get back to Lane with the wave pushing into us with an item advantage and look to trade on on the opponent and Zone them from CS if an enemy gets low enough we always break the freeze to push and set up a tower dive if we don't get the push lead level one we look to play safe and take as little damage as possible even if that means giving up CS once the wave crashes we'll hit level three that's our signal that we can start to look to play aggressive into good matchups that means looking to trade while the enemy is overextended up the lane and into bad matchups that means focusing on maintaining your minion lead as you build up a big wave if the enemy doesn't get themselves killed during this time you'll end up in this spot level four while the enemy is level three with a big wave built up if an enemy is low enough in health we of course always Tower dive but if they're healthy enough to survive we turn it into a delayed cheater recall head back to Lane with an item advantage and look to trade aggressively as the enemy overextends with the wave pushing into US often setting up solo kills for us great so you now know how to play the early part of the laning phase but what about the rest of it well lucky for you there's really only one concept you need to understand and it's that Top Lane is effectively just taking turn slow pushing into each other for example here Wukong is building up a slow push off a rebound with such a big minion Advantage it can be quite hard to win any trades into him unless you have some sort of big Advantage so often you'll just sit back let the wave crash into your Tower and then you start setting up your own slow push back into the enemy off that rebound when the wave crashes the enemy will often use it as a recall timing to get an item Advantage this is incredibly important we'll explain why shortly your response to this is to slow push so you build up a big wave to counteract that item Advantage then once the wave is big enough and far enough up the lane you crash it to set up your own recall timing to get your own item advantage the enemy will respond by once again building up a slow push off the rebound crashing that wave to set up their own recall timing you then respond by building up another slow push off the rebound and crashing a wave to set up your recall timing well hold on you might be thinking didn't you just teach me that after you cheat a recall you get an item advantage and when it bounces back you can go aggressive so why is it that you're letting Wukong push the wave into your Tower and not going aggressive off the rebound well let's look at the previous examples to learn why yoro made two mistakes he over pushed and crashed on the second wave so he didn't have enough gold to cheat a recall and since he didn't recall it meant he had no item Advantage when the wave rebounded at the same time he overextended instead of playing safe off of the rebound resulting in a trade during renekton's slow push or renekton's turn this put him low in health so once Renekton crashed the wave and recalled he came back with a huge item lead and a health lead this is why Renekton could go aggressive and kill him despite yoro having a rebound Zed on the other hand made one simple mistake Renekton crashes the wave and recalls to get the item lead notice how once Renekton gets back in Lane Zed is level four while Renekton is level three Zed also has a big minion lead what Zed needs to do is use the small timing window to push the wave to try and crash it and set up his own recall timing instead Zed plays way too scared and so Renekton is able to set up a freeze and start thinning out minions this results in Zed missing his timing window and now being in a horrible position compare that to Wukong in the exact same position he's going to fight in this spot to make sure the wave crashes so we can get out of this and actually recall basically if everyone is playing perfectly the laning phase can result in just taking turns slow pushing crashing the wave and recalling into each other on repeat however the vast vast majority of your opponents will not be playing perfectly the most common mistake is they'll trade into your slow push timing basically when it's not their turn setting up a Checkmate position for you if they recall you push the wave and they lose all the Minions to the tower if they stay you Tower dive them like we taught you getting a kill and denying all the Minions to the tower anyway the second most common mistake they'll make is they won't recall after they crash their big wave this means they didn't get to recall to spend their gold on it it so once you finish your slow push into a crash into recalling you're back with an even bigger item lead letting you break the rules fighting back on their turn to slow push and the third most common mistake they'll make is they don't use their slow push to then crash the wave if they get too scared you can end up thinning out the wave and setting up a freeze with your item advantage and suddenly with you having an item advantage and the wave in front of your Tower they can no longer crash the wave to set up a recall to buy items of their own great so you're now pretty much ahead of 99% of the top laners you'll face and have a game plan for the entire laning phase well almost the last thing you need to understand is that everything we just taught you will no longer apply from around levels 7 to 9 this is for several reasons First Once A Champion is level seven they'll have four points in an ability which is usually their best wave clear ability at level 9 they'll have five points in it so these are the levels where Champions unlock very strong wave clear second as the game gets later freezing is simply no longer as strong this is because the whole idea of freezing is that you're denying minions from the opponent as their minions kill yours forcing them to move up to last hit or give up the CS the problem is later on giving up CS like this isn't as punishing minions don't scale they give the same amount of XP at level 1 or level 18 at the same time it requires more experience to level up the higher in level you are what this means is that level one giving up two waves could be the difference between being level one or level three at level seven though giving up two waves could have no impact on your level at all players also have access to more items their ultimates and boot upgrades letting them roam faster while also hitting roams harder so while you're freezing and they're losing a wave or two you're getting kills and objectives elsewhere long story short hard pushing will pin the opponent down preventing that scenario and third by pushing it gives you a roam timing which is often the best way to snowball your lead and top this is because the majority of the roster and Top Lane are melee Champions so often when you get a lead the enemy top laner will just turtle at their Tower if you try to damage the tower they just get to trade on you from safety from under their Tower and they're usually bruisers or tanky Champions so it's not like you can just Tower dive them from 100 to zero an important trick when doing this though when you implement this push into Rome you need to place a control ward in the river brush this is because even if you end up not getting anything done on the ram it often sets you up to get a winning trade if the enemy face checks the brush or they just assume you recold or are roaming and so we'll move up the lane to push so when you get back to Lane lets you get behind them without them knowing and forcing a winning trade with your lead which again can get them low enough to set up a tower dive on them on the next wave and if they just play Passive avoiding this well that means you'll get to any fights in the river first combine that with your lead and it's an Easy Snowball from the winning fights and of course you can always look for a midlink gank off your push into Roam if you see a good opportunity to do so and now you have a simple but effective game plan to win and snowball your lane next is what to do once the laning phase ends and to prove just how good our new Top Lane Essentials course is on our website let's give you a sneak peek into the macro section if you've watched any macro content before you'll know that macro basically boils down to pushing minions and playing around the pressure of those waves so what it simply if no one pushed in any Sid Lane waves everyone would just group in mid and 5v5 team fight all the time if that was the case the only skill needed to climb would be learning how to team fight which is obviously not the case as a top laner you are likely the most self-sufficient champion on your team you're probably a durable tank a great dualist or a mobile carry you're also likely running the Summoner spell teleport this means that you are usually the most suited player on your team to push inside Lane waves and by pushing those waves you give you or your teammates the ability to play around the pressure being created which brings us to the most important macro concept you can learn winning the landing phase you have to remember that both top laners are usually going to be the ones Sid laning in the game typically against one another who stronger is going to be able to push waves into the other player's Tower so one player will have a lot of pressure while the other will have very little impact on the map this is why winning early is so important as a top laner you will have infinitely less options than your opponent if they're stronger than you here's what you can expect if you're losing the enemy top will likely push waves onto your side of the map your job will be to catch the waves they push and to prevent them from just pushing your Towers because they're stronger they will be able to move first on the map so if they push a wave and then rotate towards your team you will always be late that's why you need to try to save teleport to react to their plays so you can join the fight as well we're not saying that's all you can do when you're losing but that's what you should expect your job to be from behind you may be thinking what if I don't have teleport well that sucks that's like asking what if my opponents have Baron Elder Dragon and are 20,000 gold ahead we're exaggerating but sometimes there's nothing you can do when you're behind especially as a top laner you just have to pray your opponent make a mistake and throw just do the best you can which is why you want to have ideally won the laning phase or at the very least be capable of pushing waves after you push a side lane there's two options you can either group with your team or remain in the side lane we'll discuss split pushing in a later video but let's talk about what grouping with your team should look like here pushing a wave to your opponent's Tower forces a response if the enemy does literally nothing then they are losing farm and taking Tower damage they must do something as a response or they fall behind let's take a look at an example jayon and his team are all calling that they'll be contesting dragon in 50 seconds time jaay even pings that he's on his way while pathing to the complete opposite side of the map this will be one of your bread and butter macro plays you want to push Sly waves before the objective has spawned it's important you do it 20 or 30 seconds in advance at the very least now that Jace has crashed this wave the enemy team is feeling the pressure they either have to force a dragon fight immediately or they respond to J Jace's push and send someone to defend in this case the enemy top laner chooses to defend and teleports up here the second that he does jaay counter teleports to join the dragon fight and helps turn it around and just like that he helps secure his team a dragon despite them playing from behind this will be your basic macro rhythm in most games push a wave Force someone to respond and join up with your team let's be clear this won't always yield a positive result but the point is that you keep trying it over over and over again so for example Sani pushes the wave and forces the enemy Gwen to respond then she titre: Cassiopeia: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Bienvenue au Focus sur Cassiopeia, l'Étreinte du serpent. Cassiopeia est un mage très actif, qui doit bien se positionner, attirer ses cibles et avoir des réflexes pour exprimer tout son potentiel. Sa compétence passive est Cadence mortelle. Après avoir lancé un sort, les sorts suivants coûtent 10% de moins pendant 5 sec. Cumulable 5 fois, pour une réduction de 50%. Sa compétence A est Bombe nocive. Similaire à Dévastation de Karthus, elle empoisonne les cibles proches du curseur après un délai. Si elle touche un champion, sa vitesse de déplacement augmente temporairement. L'inconvénient de Bombe nocive, c'est son délai de récupération de 3 sec. Quand on a l'habitude de jouer avec Karthus, c'est assez difficile de s'y faire. Ensuite, on a Miasmes : un nuage de poison apparaît à un endroit, blessant et ralentissant les ennemis qui le traversent. Morsure fatale : une attaque à cible unique dont le délai de récupération est pratiquement annulé si elle touche une cible empoisonnée. Regard de la Méduse, son ultime, blesse les ennemis dans un cône devant elle. S'ils lui font face, ils sont pétrifiés (étourdis). Sinon, ils sont ralentis. J'ai essayé d'être jungler avec Cassiopeia. Vous le voyez, au niveau 1, elle peut mourir contre la plupart des monstres de la jungle. Privilégiez le jeu en solo ou duo sur une voie. Lancer Bombe nocive à répétition permet de garder votre compétence passive au max. Avec mes runes et maîtrises, on peut la lancer quasiment indéfiniment et tuer les sbires magiques ennemis en 3 sorts, tout en harcelant vos ennemis. Mes runes sont des marques de pénétration magique, des sceaux de régén. du mana par niveau, des glyphes de puissance par niveau et des quintessences de puissance. Ma configuration de maîtrises est 9/0/21, avec pénétration magique en Attaque et les maîtrises standard d'un mage en Utilitaire. Je débute avec Anneau de Doran et 1 Potion de soin, puis je prends Chaussures de sorcier, Sceptre de Rylai (dès que possible) et des objets génériques augmentant la puissance. Je préfère Anneau de Zhonya, Bâton du vide, Bâton de l'archange et Bracelet de feu mortel, mais vous pouvez opter pour la résistance avec Ange gardien, Sceptre abyssal et Voile de la banshee. Je prends Embrasement et Saut éclair. Je débute avec Bombe nocive et la garde au niveau 1. Je maximise Morsure fatale en premier, car c'est ma principale source de dégâts. Je maximise ensuite Miasmes, en augmentant l'ultime quand c'est possible. En solo, lancez Morsure fatale après avoir empoisonné votre cible. Sans cela, vous réduisez à néant l'utilité de vos combos contre votre ennemi. L'avantage avec Cassiopeia, c'est qu'une seule de ses compétences a besoin d'une cible. Ici, je cherche Twitch en aveugle avec Bombe nocive. Vous voyez que je l'ai touché car Bombe nocive m'a octroyé un bonus en vitesse. Mais si vous n'arrivez pas à deviner la position de l'ennemi, vous risquez d'être tué avant de pouvoir réagir. Twitch a pris le temps de contourner les zones visées pour pouvoir m'attaquer sans subir de dégâts préalables. Si vous jouez en duo, le poison des champions tels que Teemo et Singed fonctionne aussi pour Morsure fatale. Ici, on tue facilement Kog'Maw le renne avec uniquement ma Morsure fatale et le Tir toxique de Teemo. Contre Cassiopeia, n'oubliez pas qu'elle repose beaucoup sur sa combo complète et, dans le pire des cas, sur son ultime. Si vous évitez Bombe nocive ou si vous vous mettez hors de portée de Regard de la Méduse, vous avez de grandes chances de gagner. Et maintenant, une annonce de Sona nocturne et de Kog'Maw le renne. Malheureusement, Sona nocturne n'était pas terminée, mais vous avez les grandes lignes. Esquiver Bombe nocive et Miasmes vous donne de grandes chances de victoire contre Cassiopeia. Parfois, vous devrez lancer Regard de la Méduse pour pouvoir placer votre combo. Dans ce cas-là, vos ennemis essaieront de vous tourner le dos pour ne pas être étourdis. Si vous empoisonnez une cible, je recommande de lancer Morsure fatale en Sort facilité (Maj-E par défaut). Sona n'est bien sûr pas un grand duelliste, mais la méthode est efficace. Merci d'avoir regardé le Focus sur Cassiopeia. Alors ? Vous avez envie de l'invoquer dès maintenant ? "Bien sûr..."

titre: Lulu: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription:
Bienvenue dans le Focus sur Lulu, la Sorcière féérique. Lulu est un champion de soutien à distance. Ses buffs et sorts de contrôle de foule la rendent inestimable dans toute composition. Bien jouée, Lulu peut entraver les ennemis tout en renforçant ses alliés pour dominer les combats. Sa compétence passive est Pix, le Compagnon féérique. À chaque attaque de base de Lulu, Pix tire des projectiles sur la cible de Lulu. Ces projectiles sont guidés et touchent le premier ennemi touché. Avec Duo éclatant, Lulu et Pix tirent tous les deux en ligne droite, chaque projectile blessant et ralentissant tous les ennemis touchés. Les ennemis ne peuvent être blessés que par un seul projectile. Le gros atout de Duo éclatant est sa capacité à ralentir et interrompre ; mettez-y vite un point. Cette compétence est assez puissante avec un seul point, alors ne la maximisez qu'au niveau 13 ou 18. Fantaisie peut être lancée sur les alliés ou les ennemis. Sur un allié, la cible obtient un bonus temporaire en vitesse de déplacement et en puissance. Sur un ennemi, la cible est transformée, ce qui la ralentit et l'empêche brièvement d'attaquer ou de lancer des sorts. Le buff et la transformation font de Fantaisie un sort indispensable à bas niveau. Maximisez Fantaisie immédiatement, au niveau 9. Pix à la rescousse ! peut aussi être lancé sur les alliés ou les ennemis. Vous envoyez Pix suivre temporairement la cible au lieu de Lulu. Sur un allié, Pix offre un bouclier bloquant les dégâts pendant quelques secondes. Quand cet allié lance une attaque de base, Pix attaque aussi la même cible au lieu d'aider Lulu. Sur un ennemi, Pix blesse la cible et la révèle pendant un court instant. Pix continue d'attaquer les cibles de Lulu à partir de sa nouvelle position. Le bouclier, les dégâts et la capacité de déplacer Pix requièrent que vous mettiez vite un point dans ce sort. Si vous voulez buffer le bouclier et les dégâts, maximisez-le au niveau 9. Si vous préférez seulement déplacer Pix, attendez le niveau 18. Croissance prodigieuse fait grandir le champion allié, repoussant immédiatement les ennemis proches. En outre, votre cible gagne temporairement des PV bonus et un halo ralentissant les ennemis proches. Vu la puissance de Croissance prodigieuse, mettez-y des points dès que possible, aux niveaux 6, 11 et 16. Lulu se joue plutôt agressivement. Dès le début de la partie, je prends l'ascendant sur Janna et Kog'Maw en plaçant Duo éclatant et en attaquant aussi souvent que possible pour maximiser les dégâts de Pix. Une fois que vous avez l'avantage, campez dans les herbes pour empêcher les adversaires de tuer des sbires. Coordonnez-vous avec votre allié et, quand il avance, poussez pour infliger plus de dégâts. Encore une fois, Duo éclatant et des attaques de base suffisent pour dominer le combat. Quand vous lancez Duo éclatant, placer votre curseur près de votre champion permet de tirer dans différentes directions. Je peux ainsi tenter de ralentir Janna et Kog'Maw, même si Janna va dans tous les sens. Le ralentissement nous permet de repousser Kog'Maw et de continuer à dominer la voie. Si vous êtes agressif, pensez à poser une balise pour éviter les ganks. Je place une balise à la sortie du camp de l'ancien golem pour surveiller le champion du milieu et le jungler. En combat rapproché, lancez Pix à la rescousse ! sur vos carrys AD. Leur vitesse d'attaque permet de bien profiter des dégâts de Pix et vous pouvez ainsi prendre le temps de lancer vos compétences. Les compétences de Lulu facilitent aussi les ganks. Ici, je lance Fantaisie sur Maokai, ce qui augmente sa vitesse de déplacement pour ganker Kog'Maw. Dès qu'il est au contact, je lance Croissance prodigieuse pour coincer les ennemis en les repoussant et en les ralentissant. On tire sur Janna et Kog'Maw, ce qui les tue tandis que Maokai encaisse une tonne de dégâts sans mourir, grâce à Croissance prodigieuse et Pix à la rescousse !
00:03:58.000,00:03:56.900
N'oubliez pas que Fantaisie peut aussi contrôler les champions ennemis importants. Quand votre équipe tente de tuer Nashor, gardez Fantaisie au cas où un ennemi tenterait de le voler. Lee Sin vient voler Nashor, alors je lui lance Fantaisie et notre équipe lui saute dessus. On le tue, puis on élimine Nashor. Avec un bon timing, vous pouvez longuement contrôler les ennemis. On fonce dans la base ennemie et Maokai attaque Janna. Je lui lance Croissance prodigieuse pour coincer Janna. À la fin de l'étourdissement, je lance Fantaisie sur Janna. Elle active ensuite Mousson, mais il est trop tard. Janna et Cho'gath sont déjà suffisamment affaiblis pour qu'on les tue tous les deux. Lulu est exceptionnelle pour sauver des champions dans le pétrin. Ici, je me lance Croissance prodigieuse pour survivre à Blitzcrank. J'utilise Fantaisie pour m'éloigner de Blitzcrank et de Garen, et on réussit à tuer Garen. Bien que Lulu soit un soutien, je la joue comme un jungler. Pour ganker, engagez avec Fantaisie et Pix à la rescousse !, puis lancez Duo éclatant. Cette combo inflige de gros dégâts et permet à votre équipe de tuer des ennemis. Restez devant votre cible et déplacez-vous entre deux attaques pour continuer à infliger des dégâts. En runes, je favorise le harcèlement sur les voies et la progression en fin de partie. Je prends des marques de vitesse d'attaque, des sceaux d'armure fixe, des glyphes de régénération du mana par niveau et des quintessences de PO/sec. Mes maîtrises me servent à être puissant en début de partie et à bien progresser en fin de partie. Je choisis 0/9/21. Dans Défense, je prends de l'armure et des PV. Dans Utilitaire, je prends la régénération de mana, la vitesse de déplacement, les PO et l'expérience bonus et la réduction des délais. Avec les objets, je veux devenir puissant en fin de partie et soutenir toute mon équipe. Pour cela, je débute avec Charme féérique, des Jalons de vision et des Potions de soin. J'achète rapidement Pierre philosophale et Cœur d'or pour gagner des PO, et je prends des Bottes de lucidité. Vos premiers gros achats sont Calice d'harmonie, Égide de la légion et Songe de Shurelya. Pour la fin de partie, prenez Médaillon de l'Iron Solari et Coiffe de Rabadon. Dans Dominion, votre build est similaire. Augmentez la puissance, la réduction des délais et votre capacité à soutenir votre équipe. Débutez avec Bottes de lucidité et Armure d'étoffe. Achetez un maximum d'objets à halo, comme Égide de la légion, Songe de Shurelya, Blason de Zeke et Volonté des anciens. Terminez avec Coiffe de Rabadon pour avoir beaucoup de puissance. Merci d'avoir suivi ce Focus sur Lulu. Abonnez-vous ci-dessus à la chaîne YouTube de Riot Games et cliquez sur J'aime sous la vidéo !

titre: The 3 Step Jungle Program to Hit Diamond INSTANTLY! (Seriously)
transcription: There's a massive problem with educational content for junglers on YouTube. The problem started when most channels, including ours, would simply analyze other pro junglers to try and teach you how to carry. But this content had an obvious issue. Challenger players, including myself, these days we go smurf in low elo to show you how to carry. Typically, you'll hear stuff like just full clear, play for yourself, take every kill, and so on. But this, of course, runs into yet another problem. A challenger player prioritizing gold on themselves will win every game because they just have insane mechanics. If you go try the advice, it's often going to feel like you're just farming camps, getting outganked, and losing every objective. The advice basically boils down to just 1v9, bro. So, here's what I'm going to give you in this video. Actual practical advice that genuinely works. I've developed a super simple three-step process for improving faster as a jungler. And to prove it, we took one of our World of Warcraft players, someone who hadn't played a single game of rank before this year, and we brought him into the League of Legends team. We basically said, "Hey, we're going to coach you until you hit diamond." His name is Zot, and most importantly, he is like most of you watching this video. He has a full-time job at Skillcap, working on the backend type of things. He has a wife and on top of that, a Runescape addiction. He's always telling me about some AFK farm he's doing or whatever. The point is, League isn't his entire life, and it doesn't have to be yours either. And we didn't have him take any shortcuts either. When coaching students, challengers always tell them just play Diana, Amumu, Nocturn, or Fiddlesticks. I will say though, he was a bit of a meta abuser picking Zed towards the end of the climb. But as you can see, he hit diamond playing a pretty decent variety of junglers. The average gold elo jungler is effectively doing nothing for around 20 to 25% of the game. That is so many ganks, objectives, recall timer, scuttle crabs, and more, just wasted. Now, you may think this doesn't apply to you. There's no way you're that slow or time inefficient, right? But a harsh truth is that if you weren't, you wouldn't be in that ELO in the first place. But that's actually good news for you, though. And that's how I got Zot to power through the early ranks so quickly. Being more efficient with your time is such a simple thing to fix that will give you far better results than almost anything else you can work on. To be fair to low elo junglers, I have noticed that there's one way that they almost always try to be more efficient in, and that's in their initial clear. Time efficiency is so important. I want you to watch this clip from a rank one jungler. He doesn't know what to buy and kind of messes around in the shop for a bit. And he physically cringed at wasting like 3 seconds. >> So slow. Now, before I lose you, don't think you need to sweat out of your mind every game to win. Remember how I said you're likely wasting four to five minutes every game? That's by challenger standards. All you need to do is save maybe around 2 minutes compared to other junglers in your elo, and you would improve dramatically. Just by doing that alone, you would likely increase your ELO by an entire rank. This is what I drilled into Zot constantly. in every single VOD review and live coaching session. I was always calling him out when he was slow and telling him ways to fix it. Being faster is by far the most important thing you can work on, and it's why it was our very first lesson, and it was relevant all the way until he got diamond. Now, where the biggest time sync is for most players is simply in how they kill the jungle, not just on your initial clear. For example, I don't think I almost ever see junglers play with a camp's patience in low elo, even though that is a huge timesaver. To be fair, I do understand that it can be very stressful to kill a camp at its max patience. When you drag a camp out of its leash range, that bar looks like it starts dropping really fast. And there is nothing worse than having a camp reset, especially in a ranked game. But if you didn't know, a camp's patient bar lasts a total of 4 seconds. And 4 seconds is a really, really long time. In fact, a really simple tip that will immediately speed up your clear is the following. If a camp is low enough to where an AoE ability would kill it and it has a 4-se secondond cool down remaining, you can just move on to the next camp. Your spell is guaranteed to be up before its patience runs out, letting you speed up your clear significantly. To put into perspective how useful that is, think about how often you cycle your jungle every game. That tip alone might save you around 15 seconds or so per clear. So, in just four cycles of clearing your camps, you've saved an entire minute of game time from that single tip. That's one or two extra ganks, maybe an objective you had time to solo. You could play the exact same way, change nothing about your decision-making, and you'd already be a much better player. That's why I personally believe our ultimate jungle clear course is the best source of information on the internet to help you immediately improve at jungling. It teaches you start to finish all the little concepts that challengers use to speed up their clears. Not only that, but it even shows you how to combine all those concepts, making your clear so much faster than everyone else in your ELO. That's on top of our world's record course that we have for every meta jungler I mentioned earlier. And of course, the main dish here, the entire journey of how I coach Zaw to Diamond. There you'll be able to explore just how easily Zah achieved his goal by following super simple and practical advice that anyone can use to succeed in solo Q. You'll see he really is just winning games by doing the simplest things possible that I'm going to teach in the rest of this video. And while that's already a ton of content to help you improve, you'll also get access to another five chapter course that teaches you every little other fundamental I may have missed when coaching Zod.
