If you don't rank up while actively using Skill Cap, you just get your money back. That's how confident we are that we have the best jungling courses on the internet. So, make sure to use the discount link below after this video to sign up. All right, now that we've sped up our clear, I'm going to give you a few rules that I foresaw to follow every single game. A couple of things first though, these rules are mostly here to make sure you don't ever dillydally around in the jungle. I just spent a while ranting about how wasting time is bad. The biggest problem junglers face is that it's a role where there's so much to do that it is very easy to get baited around by your team spam pinging you or you see a juicy gank that you want to go for and so on. Basically, these rules are here to keep you grounded. You don't know what to do, just follow the rules. Secondly, these are things you've probably heard of before if you watch educational jungle videos, but these rules will not win you a game of League of Legends. We'll cover the rules first, but I will make sure to perfectly guide you so that you know how to break them and take over games consistently. Rule number one, the thing every jungle coach will repeat a thousand times. Just full clear before you do anything. It's straightforward. You can divide your time into two sections: clear time and free time. While your camps are up, it's clear time. Just kill the camps and get gold and experience. Then you'll have free time to do whatever you want. Gank, do an objective, base, doesn't really matter. Just depends on the game, of course. But keep in mind that free time ends when your camps respawn. So you go back to clearing. A quick note is that if you've tried full clearing before, and it feels bad to do, keep in mind that you will have way more free time if you follow our first lesson to clear faster. This is why full clearing looks so OP when challengers do it in low elo because they breeze through the jungle and they have so much time to impact the map. Also, it is super important that you do not gank during your clear time unless the gank has an 80% chance or higher of working. So, if you see bot lane pinging assistance, but it doesn't really seem like you're going to get a kill, you do not go. Keep clearing your jungle. It is very simple and straightforward. Clear time is clear time unless there is something absolutely crazy going on. Rule number two, get your camps as they respawn. I'm sure you've all heard of the 420 timer by now, which is when your first camp respawns after your initial clear. Ideally, what you want to do is end your free time right as your first camp is about to respawn. This way, you clear the camps as they're spawning in a line, which has a couple of benefits. Doing so almost guarantees you're efficiently farming. The more often you cycle your jungle, the more gold and experience you get. Secondly, it keeps your jungle safe. A huge problem with letting all of your camps respawn before clearing them is that it leaves your jungle vulnerable. For example, let's say I base and my entire jungle is up. If I go clear my top side, that entire time my bot side is exposed. So, by the time I get there, all of my camps might be gone. The benefit of clearing your camps as they're respawning is that it makes it almost impossible for your jungle to be stolen. There's almost no time for you to be invaded, which means it's impossible to fall behind. Now, of course, you don't have to be this precise in a real game. As long as you're at your camps within 10 to 20 seconds of them spawning, you're probably doing well. And rule number three, quadrant clearing. Sometimes, for whatever reason, you're going to need to deviate from your full clear. Maybe you see that the enemy jungler is about to tower dive your bot lane, and you need to be there. That's fine, but always kill every single camp in a quadrant of the jungle before leaving that quadrant. In my lessons with Zot, I ran through a lot of issues that ignoring this rule can cause. But the main thing is that it just screws up your clear in the long run. Let's say you were on Kugs and you decided to gank bottom. The problem is that you now have to walk all the way back to your raptors. A lot of players don't think about how terrible it is to spend time doing nothing where you're just walking to another area of the map. You want to minimize your time wasted. But that isn't the main issue. The problem is that when I come back to my respawning kugs later, my raptor camp is now desynced from them. So now I'm in a cycle where all of my camps are spawning at different times, which feels bad because it lowers your farm experience and so on. So again, rule number three, if you enter a quadrant of the jungle, kill every camp in it and then go do something else. Of course, following this rule might cause you to miss a kill sometimes or get flamed by your teammates, but League is a percentages game. 80% of the time clearing your quadrant is good and 20% of the time it might feel a little bad. Okay, those rules are simple and easy to follow and they're tips you've likely heard before. But those rules are not what Gaza Diamond knowing when to break them is. And to understand our next concept a little easier, I do want to compare climbing in league to something like fitness, which I think is something most people know about these days. And if you're like me, your algorithm won't stop showing you these three guys over and over again. If you want to get fit, all you have to do is go to the gym two to three times a week, eat a good amount of protein, do your 10,000 steps every day or whatever. If you do that, you would be in better shape than 99% of people just by doing that. League, in a funny way, is fairly similar. If you want to be emerald, you simply have to be in the top 10% of players. Diamond is the top 2.5%. Obviously, it takes time and effort to climb to that ELO. But if you compare it to something else like fitness where the top 1% is easily achievable, it starts to seem a lot more manageable. Right? By the way, just know that as I'm high horsing about fitness right now, if you took that South Park guy, made him Latino, had a sombrero for fun, and like 20 cans of pineapple fa all around the desk, that's me. That's who's talking about fitness right now. My problem is that after watching both types of fitness and league content is that they share the same problem, that they make things way more complicated than they actually are. In fitness, you got to spread out your meals four to five times a day. Creatine, eccentric, pyramid set, reverse pyramid set, blah blah blah. In League, I feel like jungle has it the worst of any other role. Priority, weak side, strong side, objective timings, cross mapping, and so on. My personal opinion is that these concepts do not matter until you're actually in diamond. For example, what good does it do to even think about your teammates's priority? Why are you even thinking about what they can do? Trying to predict what your gold elo teammate is going to do with their priority is the biggest waste of time because they don't know what they're going to do with it. The problem I believe most players have and why it takes so much time to climb is because everyone is focusing on learning a ton of different concepts at once and overloading themselves with how much they think they have to do. In my opinion, climbing should be about focusing on a few concepts and just becoming really good at them before trying to learn more. So with Zot, I minimized how many things I taught him and I forced him to focus on the equivalent of eating protein and going to the gym in League. And it is the simplest concept of all. You just need to learn how to reliably kill enemy champions. I swore the player base has gone so macro crazy, and that's partly my fault, that they've forgotten the best wink condition in the game is to just score kills. Because if you kill the enemy bot lane, you're probably going to secure dragon for your team. You don't need to force it from priority or anything like that. You just kill bot, get dragon. If you kill mid lane, you're probably going to have priority for grubs. If you kill the enemy jungler, you can take all of their camps. And if you kill top lane, well, you've probably made that guy rage quit and reconsider all of their life choices. This is why I foresaw it to follow our earlier rules so aggressively because farming your camps makes you stronger, and if you're stronger, you can fight. Now, jungle camps make you stronger in two different ways. By giving gold and experience and with those, you obviously level up and buy items. So, here's a quick quiz, and no, I'm not trying to trick you. If you are 600 gold away from a core item, and your entire jungle is up. From a scale of 1 to 10, how much value would you place on your jungle camps at the moment? I would have taken any answer eight and up. Your camps give you guaranteed resources, and by doing them, you would level up, get enough gold for your item, and power spike really hard. Okay, how about this? Let's say I'm walking out of base, and all of my camps are up. I just bought my core item, though. I'm basically at zero gold left and I'm 0% into my next level. Again, 1 to 10. How valuable are my camps right now? Any low number would have been correct here. Even if I were to kill all of my camps, I wouldn't have gotten any stronger. So, they have very little value to me right now. And that brings me to the final piece of the puzzle, the most important concept to climbing as a jungler. Your role can do whatever you want to be doing at all times. Laners are stuck with the responsibility of having to catch waves and protect their towers. They can't just do whatever they want. They have very strict recall timers and they have very small windows to leave their lane and roam. On the other hand, as a jungler, you don't have any responsibilities that affect you personally. So, while you basing at a bad time maybe loses you an objective or maybe your top laner gets, it doesn't put you behind in any way. What this means is that you can always always do what you want on the map. And this is how I got Zot to destroy Loilo. Basically, I taught him the eb and flow of how your matches should go. While your camps have high value, like we discussed earlier, you should follow our rules. Then, while your camps have close to no value and you're on a power spike, you go look for fights. And that is it. All you need to do is track your power spikes diligently. Like I said, you don't have any responsibilities. Right now, Saut sees he has a good base, so he just recalls whenever he wants. Then, after basing on a good spike, you just build the habit of pressing tab. Then compare how strong you are to everyone else. Zot is kind of even with the enemy Darius, but he hasn't base yet to spend his gold. So all Zot has to do is follow my basic rule. If you power spiked and your camp's at no value, just go fight. And he just one-shots the guy. You might think that playing on a power spike is such a simple thing that there's no way it could get you to diamond. But the reason it's so effective is because most players attention is split between so many different concepts. They're thinking, "Oh, dragon is spawning soon. I need to be here to establish vision or my bot side is being pushed in and I need to go counter or whatever. So, while most players think that they play around power spikes, they're really not doing it as well as I taught Zot to. Loyal low junglers are so distracted by so many things going on in the map that they're not doing the single most important thing in League. Meanwhile, I've taught Zot that this is basically the only thing that matters. Here's another example from one of his games, but with me live coaching him this time. Before starting though, just look at the enemy Viego. He is having a better game than Zach. Let's just play the clip though. Viego hasn't based. You could actually just go to his raptors right now. Go. Ignore wolves. Ignore wolves. Ignore. Oh yeah, there's action on the map and VGO hasn't based. So you want to fight right now. Good. Well played. >> I killed him. >> Yeah. Just contest everything here. I'm going to let you do your thing, but you want to fight aggressively here. Nice. Think about his red. Got it. You're stronger. I tried to kick him into that guy. Now >> would have been stylish though. >> Mhm. So that's all of that rule we talked about. It happened. It happens early in the game, too. You just based, you just bought items. You just leveled up. Skip your camps. Go just go brawl to the death. Like I said, most Ly low junglers aren't playing around this concept. That VGO is probably sitting on like 2,000 gold during this play, but he willingly took a fight down in items and a level. Zot basically 1v2 him and Yunar here, even with that questionable kick. And that is the main thing I failed to neglect in this video. Zot's mechanics are so bad, and I still managed to get him to diamond easily by following a few simple concepts. And the best part about both of those plays we saw is what I like to tell junglers. You don't need to worry about objectives. If you just kill the enemy players, you're probably going to get the objectives for free anyway. You don't need teamwork to do it. I can't emphasize how simple and effective this is to do. Everyone else is focusing on way too many things. Meanwhile, Saw is just eating his protein, going to the gym, and having way better results than everyone else. And that is my three-step process for quickly climbing as a jungler. One, play fast and don't waste any time. Two, follow our clearing rules to make sure you're always strong. And three, then you fight when you're strong, and only when you're strong. Of course, there is a little more nuance to these concepts that can make them even more effective. But as I mentioned, every other lesson I've taught Zot that I didn't have time to cover here is all in our bronze to diamond weekly courses. There you'll get to see Zot's progress week by week following the simple advice I've taught here and how effective it is. I also taught him some other critical concepts that are much more relevant than low elo, such as how to go for natural plays, then how to actually play around low elo priority with teammates who don't look at the map, and so much more. And on top of that, remember we've got the world record clear for every meta jungler on top of the jungle clearing course for the rest of the game. So you know how to outpace everyone in your elo bracket. And it's all backed by our rankup guarantee. If you don't climb a full rank following our advice, you get your money back, no questions asked. titre: Why Hypercarry Champions Are Impossible To Balance | League of Legends
transcription: hyper carry champions in League are among the most difficult to find a healthy win-win situation for in general the very goal of the class is to unironically literally 1 v9 games further distinguishing them from more traditional carries one of my favorite videos I ever made on this channel was on hyper carries and how I believed they're actually one of the worst Champions to carry the game with following up a few months later with a video on anti carries since it's been a while I wanted to revisit this topic one more time and go more in detail on why balancing them feels almost impossible demonstrating this via jungle hyper carries in particular but I will include everyone else in that discussion I'm sure we're all privy to the dominant narrative that jungle has the highest immediate influence out of any role in the game not necessarily that it's the easiest to carry I feel the need to make that disclaimer as I often get comments poing how difficult the role is to learn in any case through recent years the jungle has also become the most class diverse role in The Game featuring members of all seven classes Fighters Mages Marksman Slayers controllers tanks and Specialists after much effort to increase accessibility for players there's been a significant increase in carry jungles whose lack of early survivability has been made up for by the less hazardous environment compared to the early days of seasons 1 through 5 an unfortunate consequence of this is that it has led to a proportional increase in 1v9 Champions new and old for today we're going to be analyzing the hyper carry archetype once more specifically jungle hyper carries and talk about why there will never be a reality where they're effective and balanced at the same time first and foremost the biggest problem with Hyper cares is that they need to get fed and rather than waste their time getting kills they should get real food with hellofresh hellofresh is a meal prep catering service that delivers fresh handmade meals right to your door with over 40 easy recipes to choose foam that take anywhere between 15 to 40 minutes to prepare time to time I use hellofresh whenever I feel too lazy to cook and I know that you guys experience the same feeling too Don't Lie by going on their website you can look at their menus from week to week and choose which ones you're expensive than your average takeout so if you're looking for a variety of good quality meals at affordable price then use my code pogar o 16 or use my link on screen for 16 free meals plus free shipping great to be working with hellofresh again but for now let's go back to talking about hypercases some time ago I think early 2020 Exel made a fantastic documentary on hyper car junglers going in detail on the circumstances that prevent them from being buffed I highly advise you check that video out after this one if you want some context on how this situation came to be but for a basic tldr there have been multiple attempts to give hyper car champions the means and wherewith all to achieve their win condition that have all been discarded at this point due to their simply being no healthy solution for them and their opponents they're still listent to recap what separates hyper carries from traditional ones is usually self- sustainability Marksman of the textbook definition of carries with the added condition that they're being supported and protected by their team meaning they can only carry if their team enables them to and while that certainly extends the hyper car as well they're a bit more Soul oriented hybrid carries are known for extreme persistent Andor wide covering damage having virtually Limitless uptime to where the only time they can ex their pressure is if they're being hardc seed or they're dead to bounce that out they tend to lack a modum of utility whatsoever what little they do have is me to Aid them not the team finally even though hyper carries have a reputation for 1 v90 games it plays out more like a king of the hill type situation where you take on one person at a time not really all five members of the enemy team at once these Champions are among the most item dependent in the game often being significantly more vulnerable than most of champions of the first 10 to 20 minutes of the game mandating that the person playing the hyper carry do their best to hold the line and not bleed out too much to where they're simply too far behind to make up that difference in the mid L game likewise their teammates have to be aware that they're at a serious disadvantage and pressure in the early game and should play more conservatively with that in mind such conditions are why top and jungle have the highest density of hyper carry Champions up and top L the remote nature of the role lowers the probability of external intervention for and against them if you get lucky you might be able to pick a hyper carry into a favorable match match up giving you a more comfortable early game experience for example if the enemy team blind picks Mundo Mor Kaiser Garen Cante or anyone who has a hard time getting up close and personal really fast it's basically a free Lane fore kale copia is sometimes play top for the same idea on rare occasions you might encounter a vein top as well trendir is constantly abused top whenever he's even the slightest bit strong another reason Top Lane is a hot bed for hybrid carries is because of the disproportionately higher experience game up there dless you've had games where bot Lane's level 11 while your top laner is 14 even though you have the same CS numbers faster experience gain means faster power Spike regarding the jungle it is by far the highest number of 1 v9 Champions and the biggest contributing factor to that would have to be the absence of a direct opponent in practice you are against an opponent the enemy jungler and will most definitely come in contact with them in the early game as you navigate your way around the map but technically speaking there's no Champion who's in constant presence of you even Predator junglers which means to some extent you are guaranteed to scale and it just so happens many hyper carry junglers are power Farmers you can be up against an unfavorable matchup like master vers Rex ey but through good jungle tracking and your own movement you can spend most of the early game avoiding her entirely it just might come at the expense of your laners flaming you for quote unquote doing nothing while they're being ganged all over the place junglers in particular can also easily create lowrisk high reward situations to score kills and leads with the exception of being counter ganked you're always at a number's Advantage when you gank and depending on how good your jungle tracking is having the element of surprise can make all the difference between killing or being killed allowing hypercarries to way to punish enemy junglers who are too careless of their Health Management or map Bess and the last thing you want is for Master E Kindred carus belv or cane to get a free kill in the early game cuz then you're really screwed now comes the part where we actually talk about the balce issue of hyper carries why exactly are they impossible to balce to start let me bust out my handy dandy decimal graphing calculator to show you two charts chart number one is the scaling of a regular Champion as they purchase items get levels and progress through a match chart number two is the scaling of a hyper carry now look at both lines put together for the sake of argument we can assume the numbers on the x-axis represent champion level conceptually a regular Champion outpaces a hyper carry for the early parts of the game getting more value from their first and second item than the latter's corresponding first and second item but at a certain point the hyper car scaling measurably ramps up to where the third and fourth item would provide as much value as the normal Champions fifth and sixth putting them infinitely above the normal Champion for the remainder of the game that's not to say a six item normal Champion like KX can't beat a six item ye but assuming we're building for taking each other on it will take Immaculate play from the kixs and or terrible play from the e for the kha'zix to win that here's another example cidan versus LeBlanc assuming equal player skill the cidan player will out pressure LeBlanc every single time whether in 1 v1s or full-blown team fights there is no Universe where a six item LeBlanc out carries a six item CID I don't care who you are but what allows a LeBlanc player a chance to overcome that disparity is the time frame she has before that equilibrium point as demonstrated by the graph I previously brought up by roughly level 11 is when cidan starts St SC of block but prior to that she can put up a reasonable fight against him in lane and can also have a bigger impact on the map in terms of skirmishes Team fights and rs the question of bouncing a hyper carry then becomes where should that equilibrium point be and more importantly how much should be required to reach that point that brings us to the first stumbling block with balancing hyper cares for years nay over a decade the balance team has tried and failed to find where that inflection point should be when is it appropriate for a hyper car to speed past everyone else empirically speaking I don't think there is one because it fundamentally clashes with the ideal that every Champion should have a fair and equal chance to be viable in other words you have to choose between two mutually exclusive of options if you try to give a hyper carry Champion any amount of leg room to scale up you're actively taking away from their opponent's time frame to shut them down and vice versa some of you might try to say okay then why not just put that point right in the middle 50/50 that way Nei side gets an advantage it's not that simple if it were merely a contest of numbers versus numbers like one Pokemon versus another Pokemon and it's just them versus you then it would be much easier to gauge which one is superior to another at what time and how can they bounce each other out the league is of course more complicated than that items runes team compositions dragons Lanes rolls so many things that can throw a wrench into all of that going back into jungle For a Moment Like I said before the reason there are so many hyper carries in Jungle is due to the absence of a constant opponent yes Rex side might be a huge threat to Mastery in the early game but unlike top mid and Bot Lane you're not always going to be staring her right in the face placing theonas on Rex side to go out of her way to hunt down ye and put him in the dirt but he has his own jungle you have yours he can go from Camp to Camp without ever coming in contact with you and can actively scale no matter what you try to do even if you do a great job ganking Lanes or taking Your Dragon or Herald it doesn't slow down the master ye in bing for instance V is a beast Lake game the original hyper carry she will easily outdamage most other Marksman but this is offset by a poor wave clear and subpar neutral game making it easier for Draven lucians zyus and zeras to bullly at a farm thus slowing down a rate of scaling which buys the more time it's much more difficult to bully the enemy jungler if the E players Resolute on power farming there's nothing you can really do about it unless you're invading him 24/7 but that in in of itself is a huge risk for you since it leaves you vulnerable to being being collapsed on not to mention you're neglecting your own camps so unless you successfully kill him every time you see him it's a net loss for you and remember unlike other matchups where 3 to 4,000 gold lead for your team is adequate enough of a lead to maintain for the rest of the game provided you don't throw or get out smited at Baron against tibre carries you need a 6 to 7,000 gold Le with the same Effect 3 to four isn't a safe enough margin for error against the hyper carry by the way this applies to every hyper carry jungler I'm just using ye for this example cus KRA belth cane Graves maybe Lilia all of them can do the same thing this is similar Dynamic for this in top and by extension mid lane as well take Renekton versus trendir in typical early game Lane bully fashion Renekton sh on trendir but to ensure trir stays behind Renekton has to devote the entire rest of the game matching him in s Side Lane which is a huge waste of time so it's in the Renekton player's best interest to ro and get stuff done elsewhere but in doing so he gives trendir a free Lane to power farm and catch up that's the second issue with Hyper case they're always a problem there's kind of a misconception of hyper carries being feast or famine Champions if you put them behind early they're useless which is true to an extent however because of their parabolic scaling they're guaranteed to turn into a problem it just depends on how long it takes against the colie if you beat her in Lane you can rest assured she's not going to magically turn into Jesus and whoop your entire team's ass sure the squishy Champions might still have to keep an eye out for her but she's not going to 1 v9 pentac kill your team but even if you manage to push down a kale early on if you take two long 10 the game or she scores a shutdown in two she can and will destroy you against hyper cares you don't have to push them down once you have to keep pushing them down since all it takes is one mistake in the mid game for them to blow 180 in a sense they're like time bombs only no matter how many times you disarm them they can still explode circling back around to the idea of fair opportunity to be viable and fair opportunity to shut them down the reason hyper car champions especially junglers will never find themselves in a state of fairness is because the goal poost keeps moving in EXO's video he mostly talked about I believe seasons 4 through 6 back when we had ferof flare and sated devour League was a lot slower in Tempo back then giving hyper carries plenty of time and subsequently a generous March of fror to scale into the m late game when snowballing wasn't as crazy which meant they had to be relatively weaker than the rest of the competition to make up for that in the present day games are much faster early mistakes are more consequential and so the gap between hyper cares and normal Champs was narrowed a lot but that raises concerns over the possibility of them beginning their 1v9 Conquest off even the slightest concession these days it hardly feels like these hyper cares are weak at all belvet is one of the most notorious Predator junglers with amazing dueling potential early game granted she's one of the newer Champions but even Kindred they used to be ass until items now you see them three buffing every game Mastery with Hal of blades is a very terrifying sight and it feels like all he needs is blade of the ruin King and boots to start penic killing everyone having talked about Kane just recently in his why everyone plays episode they made him go from non-existent at base to surprisingly formidable Lane hyper carries also no longer feel like they have an actual weak early game Trend Amir consistently averages a gold advantage of over like 95% of top laners by 10 minutes whereas 8 years ago he couldn't do anything until two items cidan used to be irrelevant until Level 16 but after successive Buffs it almost feels like his 1 v9 mode activates a rank two ultimate instead of rank three some might argue Vain's more of a midgame champion now not a late game with how explosive and volatile of the game is there essentially is no equilibrium point anymore hyper carries are just as strong as normal carries with the added privilege of scaling like a to be clear I'm not seeing a k can One V one a Darius 3 six in laning Phase or the copia has no losing matchups but there isn't as big of a disparity anymore which is why these Champions are repeatedly buffed nerfed buffed again and then nerfed again with The Gap and strength being the closest it's ever been tiny changes whether internal or external can make all the difference between hyper carries being at 46% win rate or 54 forcing the balance team to never stop making small adjustments you guys remember a few weeks back in version 13.17 when they bummed up tren's attack range to 175 from 125 and lowered his space ad0 from 72 to 68 this was originally intended to be an adjustment to make him feel more intuitive to play while at the same time not trying to push his win rate lo and behold Tren andir went from like a 49% win rate to almost 53 on the flip side in 13.16 Kindra was chilling at a 52.5% win rate in Diamond Plus very high for a supposed to hyper K jungler so they tried to push their power back a tiny bit by cutting mounting Treads efficiency only for that to knock them down a full 3% in win rate not saying these Buffs and Nerfs were not significant increasing the attack range of a melee champion whose entire ammo is auto attacking is a big deal conversely decreasing the advantage state of a jungler hurts them in many ways ganking and invading to name a few but it's the hypers snowballing nature of the game that causes such drastic swings and performance especially on hyper cares it is a never-ending Battle of Correction no matter how hard they try hypercar will always be in a position where they're too forgiving to play or too unforgiving to play there's no middle grounds and there never will be since one actively plays against the other I'm not implying that hyper cares are the most problematic champions in the game either but it's a good illustration of what happens when you give a champion too much carry potential it's a lot easier to balance a tank than a carry let's just put it that way anyways that's going to be it for today let me know your thoughts on balancing hyper cares in the comments down below particularly if you agree or disagree with with my opinion that hyper carries can never be bounced as always though if you enjoyed the video it would be great if you love to like And subscribe don't forget to follow me on Twitter at farsam join my Discord server and be sure to check out EXO's video on farming junglers as well as my other video on hyper cares if you haven't yet but till next time thanks so much for watching and I look forward to seeing you again soon take [Music] care [Music]

titre: Kha'Zix: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Bienvenue dans le Focus sur Kha'Zix, le Faucheur du Néant. Le changement est nécessaire. Kha'Zix est un assassin en mêlée qui profite de sa vitesse et de son invisibilité pour tuer ses ennemis. Kha'Zix évolue pendant la partie, améliorant ses compétences déjà puissantes en y ajoutant de nouvelles caractéristiques. Quand Kha'Zix augmente le niveau de son ultime, il peut évoluer et ajouter un effet unique à l'une de ses quatre compétences actives. Comme votre ultime ne va qu'au niveau 3, vous ne pouvez faire évoluer que trois de ces quatre compétences pendant une partie. La compétence passive de Kha'Zix est Menace invisible. Quand les ennemis ne voient pas Kha'Zix, celui-ci gagne le buff Menace invisible. Ce buff dure jusqu'à ce qu'il attaque un champion ennemi. Si Kha'Zix attaque un champion ennemi en ayant ce buff, il inflige des dégâts supplémentaires, ralentit la cible et consomme le buff. Activer Goût de la peur blesse l'ennemi ciblé. Si la cible n'a pas d'allié proche, elle est marquée et Goût de la peur lui inflige des dégâts supplémentaires. Kha'Zix peut apprendre Griffes évolutives, ce qui augmente la portée de ses attaques de base et de Goût de la peur. Griffes évolutives permet aussi à Goût de la peur d'infliger encore plus de dégâts aux cibles isolées en fonction de leurs PV manquants. Je conseille de maximiser et de faire évoluer Goût de la peur dès que possible pour vite assassiner les champions ennemis. Pique du Néant explose contre le premier ennemi touché, blessant tous les ennemis proches. Si Kha'Zix est dans l'explosion, il est soigné. Pique évolutive permet à Kha'Zix de tirer trois volées de piques dans un cône. Cette évolution permet aux piques d'appliquer le passif, Menace invisible, d'infliger plus de dégâts et de ralentir les ennemis touchés. Prenez Pique du Néant au niveau 1 et maximisez-la en premier si vous avez besoin de soins pour survivre sur votre voie ou dans la jungle. Faites-la évoluer si vous voulez mieux farmer et pourchasser vos cibles avec Menace invisible. Avec Bond, Kha'Zix saute vers une zone proche, blessant tous les ennemis proches à l'atterrissage. Avec Ailes évolutives, Bond voit sa portée grandement augmentée et réinitialise son délai de récupération quand Kha'Zix tue un champion ou réussit une assistance. Mettez-y vite un point, mais je conseille de le maximiser en dernier. Le faire évoluer vous aide toutefois à aller au combat et à pourchasser vos ennemis. Assaut du Néant est son ultime. À l'activation, Kha'Zix se camoufle brièvement et augmente sa vitesse de déplacement. Assaut du Néant peut être activé deux fois dans un court laps de temps avant d'entrer en récupération. Si Kha'Zix apprend Camouflage évolutif, il peut lancer Assaut du Néant trois fois et il subit moins de dégâts en étant camouflé. Mettez des points aussi souvent que possible dans Assaut du Néant pour pouvoir évoluer. Choisissez Camouflage évolutif si vous voulez avoir plus d'impact en combat d'équipes en étant plus résistant et insaisissable. Avec Kha'Zix jungler, n'oubliez pas que les monstres peuvent être isolés des autres monstres. Pour nettoyer les camps de monstres puissants, tuez d'abord les plus faibles et gardez Goût de la peur pour blesser davantage le gros monstre. Ganker depuis la jungle est facile, car Bond permet d'aller au contact pour lancer une attaque de base appliquant Menace invisible. Ensuite, attendez que l'adversaire soit isolé des sbires pour utiliser Goût de la peur. Après avoir forcé Malzahar à utiliser Saut éclair, Kha'Zix attend dans les herbes puis effectue la même combo : il bondit pour appliquer Menace invisible, il active Pique du Néant et Goût de la peur tue la cible. Il n'y a pas de monstre près du dragon, c'est pourquoi ce dernier est toujours considéré comme isolé par Goût de la peur. Ainsi, Kha'Zix excelle dans l'élimination du dragon, même seul. Placez une Balise de vision pour être sûr qu'aucun ennemi ne vous voit, et Kha'Zix tuera le dragon facilement. Si vous jouez seul sur une voie, frappez à chaque ouverture. Goût de la peur suivi d'une attaque de base permettent de harceler facilement. Kha'Zix bondit dans les herbes pour réactiver Menace invisible et repartir au combat. Il place encore une attaque de base et enchaîne avec Goût de la peur et Pique du Néant. Au niveau 3, Bond facilite encore plus le harcèlement et repousse Rumble.  Une fois au niveau 6 ou 7, Kha'Zix peut facilement tenter une élimination. Une attaque de base avec Menace invisible et Pique du Néant permettent d'engager le combat. Kha'Zix attend une seconde puis active Assaut du Néant, ce qui lui permet d'infliger beaucoup de dégâts, car Menace invisible est réactivée, et d'éloigner Rumble de ses sbires. Quand Rumble est isolé, Goût de la peur inflige de gros dégâts. Kha'Zix garde Bond pour esquiver l'ultime de Rumble ; il le tue et survit. Camouflage évolutif est parfait pour attaquer l'ennemi sous ses tourelles. Ici, Kha'Zix active Assaut du Néant juste avant d'être touché par la tourelle, ce qui bloque une bonne partie des dégâts et lui permet de fuir après avoir tué Miss Fortune. Notez bien que la survie de Kha'Zix en combat dépend de son camouflage et de sa mobilité. Pique évolutive facilite le harcèlement, car Pique du Néant applique alors Menace invisible à chaque poke. Mais Kha'Zix est gourmand : il tente d'attaquer Miss Fortune avec Assaut du Néant et Bond. Bien que Kha'Zix inflige de gros dégâts initiaux avec Goût de la peur et des attaques de base, la tourelle empêche Assaut du Néant de le sauver et il tombe rapidement. Utilisez plutôt votre mobilité pour trouver des ennemis fragiles à découvert. Avec Assaut du Néant, Kha'Zix surprend Miss Fortune, la tue avec Bond et Goût de la peur, puis il s'attaque à Morgana et lui ôte aussi la vie. Pour le build de Kha'Zix, je conseille de privilégier les dégâts physiques et de ne compter que sur le camouflage et la vitesse pour survivre. Voici un exemple de build : Prenez des marques de dégâts d'attaque, des sceaux d'armure, des glyphes de résistance magique par niveau et des quintessences de dégâts d'attaque. Prenez des maîtrises 21/9/0, avec les dégâts physiques dans Attaque ainsi que l'armure et les PV dans Défense. Pour jungler, prenez toujours Châtiment. Sinon, prenez Embrasement pour tuer des champions, Fatigue pour pourchasser vos proies et Saut éclair pour être plus mobile. Débutez avec Bottes de vitesse et des Potions de soin, plus quelques Lames de Doran. Vos premiers achats majeurs sont Brutaliseur et Soif-de-sang, ou Épée sanglante, pour accroître grandement vos dégâts et assassiner vos ennemis. Ensuite, améliorez vos bottes : Tabi ninja ou Sandales de Mercure vous aideront à survivre, et les Bottes de mobilité vous aideront à assassiner. En fin de partie, prenez Dernier souffle, Ange gardien, Gueule de Malmortius et Lame spectre de Youmuu. Merci d'avoir regardé ce Focus sur Kha'Zix. Abonnez-vous ci-dessus à la chaîne YouTube de Riot Games et laissez vos commentaires sous la vidéo !

titre: RAID: Shadow Legends | Champion Spotlight | Skeletor
transcription: A truly nefarious villain has returned to threaten Teleria once more.  Now though, his powers have grown,  and not even that musclebound moron He-Man can stand in his way - it’s Skeletor! That’s right - the Universe’s most dreadful sorcerer returns to RAID  with a newly-enhanced set of skills,  ready for everyone to claim him - even if you already got him the first time around. The Skeletor Chase will return on May 19th, and last until June 22nd.  Same as before, it’ll be available to all players,  whether you participated in the first Skeletor Chase or not.  You can Empower your Skeletor with this duplicate,  so don’t miss out on getting another one for free! Once you’ve logged in for 14 days to get Skeletor and his Perfect Soul,  there’s the usual second phase  where you can earn extra goodies to unlock Skeletor’s devious power.  Just make sure to start before June 15th or you’ll miss out on the whole thing. Now, let’s dive into Skeletor’s rebalanced skills! Determined not to be outdone by any do-gooders,  Skeletor’s Master of Evil passive skill now grants him an additional 20 Accuracy  whenever he’s attempting to either place a debuff, steal a buff,  decrease an enemy’s Turn Meter,  or the duration of any of their buffs, or increase the duration of any debuff on that enemy. That’s an additional 20 Accuracy per buff on that enemy by the way - talk about punishing! That’s not all, though - Skeletor now has a 50% chance  to place a Decrease Resistance debuff whenever he takes a hit.  It’s increases against targets not from the Telerian League,  with those pesky do-gooders having a 100% chance to receive the debuff. There’s also a whole new part to Skeletor’s passive -  he now has a 25% chance to place a Petrification debuff on an enemy that hits him,  if Skeletor loses 30% of his HP from that hit.  Once again, if the enemy is from the Telerian League, that chance is doubled to 50%.  Heroes beware! Skeletor’s first active skill, Havoc Scythe, is also getting an upgrade.  While attacking an enemy two times,  each hit now has a 75% chance of increasing the duration of two random debuffs,  and it’ll also decrease the duration of two random buffs on the target.  With just a few Skill Tomes, that chance goes up to 100% - talk about nefarious manipulation!  That’ll teach any buffoons to not stand in the way of the mighty master of Snake Mountain. His second skill is Skull Comet,  an attack so terrifying it has a 75% chance to place a Petrification debuff on a single enemy.  As if that weren’t bad enough,  it also now has an upgraded 75% chance to place a 50% Decrease Attack debuff  and a 30% Decrease Speed debuff for 2 turns on that enemy as well,  without any ‘ifs’ from the old version of this Skill.  You’ll never escape Skeletor’s evil clutches, mwahaha! As powerful as Skeletor is, even an evil boss needs minions.  With his third skill, Begone, Fools!,  Skeletor places a 50% Increase Accuracy buff on his whole team for 2 turns,  and has a 75% chance to steal all Block Debuffs buffs  and two more random buffs from each enemy, up from the one buff he could steal before. As if that weren’t devious enough, Skeletor then decreases each enemy’s Turn Meter by 15% - 30% if they’re from the Telerian League.  How very eeeevil! Lastly, Skeletor’s Aura skill increases his whole team’s Accuracy by 60 points in all battles -  a foolproof way to ensure your debuffs land! Where can Skeletor show off his dreadful brilliance?  Absolutely everywhere, of course - the master of evil knows no equal!  In the Arena, Skeletor can bamboozle buff-heavy teams  and take out key Champions with Petrification debuffs. In PvE content like the Doom Tower, Cursed City or Grim Forest,  being able to decrease the Attack and Speed,  coupled with enhanced crowd control through Turn Meter manipulation,  makes Skeletor a welcome addition to any team. The Master of Evil will benefit most from anything that gives him more Accuracy to land those all-important debuffs.  Speed will help Skeletor out-pace his enemies - and come up with quick-witted retorts! And that’s everything for this updated Champion Spotlight! With these changes, Skeletor becomes more viable across the board  and will present a greater challenge for He-Man and his Telerian League allies.  Don’t miss out on getting this pair of intergalactic masters when they make their return! Until we meet again!

titre: Yorick: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription:
Welcome to the League of Legends Champion Spotlight, featuring Yorick, the Shepherd of Lost Souls. By Shadow Isles standards, Yorick's a pretty good guy. The former monk just wants to free the souls who've spent a millennium trapped in the Black Mists of The Isles, and... he's willing to do some bad things to carry out his holy mission. Yorick carries the burden of a thousand condemned souls swirling infinitely in the ghoulish cape on his back. The Shepherd uses these lost spirits to fight anyone who gets in his way. They may not like it, but Yorick doesn't give a damn. He'll do whatever it takes to win, even if it means he's got to bring a little bit of hell along with him. Yorick's passive is Shepherd of Souls. Whenever an enemy minion or neutral monster dies near Yorick, they'll occasionally leave behind a grave. Enemy champs that die near Yorick always leave one. Graves last until Yorick moves away. Now, what do graves do, you ask? Well, heads up, it's about to get spoooky. Yorick's Q gives him access to two separate abilities, Last Rites and Awakening. Last Rites empowers the Shepherd's next basic attack, causing it to deal bonus damage and restore a little health. If Yorick is below half health, the healing effect is doubled. If Last Rites kills any target, a grave is created. If there are at least three graves nearby and Last Rites is on cooldown, Yorick can cast Awakening to raise Mist Walkers from the nearby graves. Mist Walkers basically act like nasty little minions; although you can only have up to four of them at once in your undead entourage, and they die if they roam too far from Yorick. None of the graves can have a cigar. If Yorick wants to win early trades in-lane, he's gotta wait until he's planted a proper cemetery before going in. Here, Irelia screws up by hanging too close to Yorick's graves. When the Mist Walkers rise, Yorick follows them in to deal additional damage and recover some lost health, earning an easy advantage. Yorick's W is Dark Procession. The Shepherd yanks a bundle of corpses straight from the earth, forming a wall of rotting flesh that encircles a target area. The wall decomposes on its own after a few seconds, but it can also be destroyed by enemy basic attacks, whether they're trapped or not. Yorick and his allies can freely pass through the 
Dark Procession. When you need to lock down a key target or get away from the living, a big ring of coagulated corpses is just the thing you need. Here, Yorick aims Dark Procession
right on top of himself to create a cage that only he can escape from. Unlike Kled, Yorick doesn't need a rope to
make a beartrap. Yorick's E is Mourning Mist. When cast, Yorick hurls a glob of mist that damages and slows enemies. Yorick gets a movement speed bonus when moving toward enemies marked by the mist, but the ability's true power is that it's the one way you can direct the actions of the otherwise-dumb 
Mist Walkers. Your Walkers gain movement speed and leap toward mist-soaked targets, harassing them until their bloody end. When you need to stick to a target, a little dab of mist can help clog up your lane opponent's wheels. The blood mage almost made it out of this trade but the mist helped the Shepherd take Vlad's health bar down to the red, where it belongs. Yorick's ultimate is Eulogy of the Isles. Yorick summons the Maiden of the Mist and, at higher ranks, she'll bring some Mist Walkers with her. The Maiden moves and attacks on her own, pushing down the lane and immediately raising any enemies that die near her as Mist Walkers. The Maiden disappears whenever Yorick dies, but, unlike Mist Walkers she doesn't have to stay near him to stay alive. When Yorick attacks the Maiden's target, he'll deal bonus magic damage based on  the enemy's maximum health. If the Shepherd gets into a fight within earshot of the Maiden, though, she will be smart enough to follow and lend him a helping hand. The bonus damage granted by the Maiden makes her scary strong in one-on-one duels. Here, Yorick doesn't need to wait for more graves before starting the fight, instead he busts out his pretty little lady friend and goes straight for the kill. The spectral duo stood united, giving Shen no time to do the same. When Yorick goes in for a trade, it's best if he brings a swarm with him. Here, Yorick waits until he has
a maximum four graves up, then unleashes a dark procession, and awakens his ghoul army the moment gangplank steps up for some minions. With the Saltwater Scourge trapped in the fight, Yorick siccs his undead buddies on him using a well-aimed spray of Mourning Mist. When laning, Yorick has very specific windows of opportunity to make strong trades, and misusing that opportunity can leave him very vulnerable. The mean old monk is in position for a strong play here, but he missed his E, causing his walkers to ignore the Grand Duelist. Fiora wisely takes the opportunity 
to pick off the Mist Walkers, leaving Yorick's schedule wide open 
for a fencing lesson. You know what they say:
good fencing makes good neighbors. Yorick is better than just about anyone when you need to press an advantage after a teamfight. Here, Yorick saves his ultimate until after his team's cleared out the bad guys, then summons the Maiden so her army can tank the first four turret shots. Thanks to the Maiden's passive, the defending wave of enemy minions winds up transforming into Yorick's minion wave. With a whole new wave of tower-tanking ghoulies at their side, the Shepherd's team blows through the inhibitor. In teamfights, Yorick is too slow to be a diver, so he's better off hanging back to peel for his carries. Here, Yorick wisely retreats to deal with the cold-hearted ladies who've flanked in behind him. He turns to help out his backline, quickly delivering Fiora her Last Rights. As soon as Dark Procession comes off cooldown, Yorick uses it to lock down the overfed Ashe, who quickly eats a faceful of Boomerang Blades. Should've ducked! Yorick doesn't actually have to stay in a
lane in order to splitpush it. Here, Yorick sees folks moving toward Baron, so he drops big momma Maiden in bot lane and sends her on her merry way. She refreshes her army of Mist Walkin' amigos every time the squad slays an enemy minion, so soon, she has a nasty pile of friends heading straight for the bot lane inhibitor. But, meanwhile, Yorick is present and ready for a pool party in the topside river. this forces the other team to either give up Baron entirely or hope they can win the teamfight before the Maiden takes everything in the bot lane. In the end, Yorick's team wins on both fronts, and the Shepherd gets to return to his flock and win the game. Thanks for tuning in to the Yorick Champion
Spotlight. Click the links below to unleash an unfathomable
darkness that destroys the entire world.

titre: Nami: Champion Spotlight | Gameplay - League of Legends
transcription: Bienvenue dans le Focus sur Nami, l'Aquamancienne. Nami est un champion de soutien offensif, qui augmente les dégâts infligés par son équipe et dispose de compétences de contrôle. Que ce soit en augmentant les dégâts d'un coéquipier ou en le soutenant lors de la charge, Nami peut aider votre équipe à surfer vers la victoire. Le passif de Nami est Déferlantes. Lorsqu'une de ses compétences touche un champion allié, y compris elle-même, sa cible profite temporairement d'une vitesse de déplacement augmentée. Prison aqueuse envoie une bulle d'eau à l'endroit visé, qui blesse et projette en l'air les ennemis touchés. Cela permet aussi d'appliquer Déferlantes aux alliés et donc d'accélérer les alliés en même temps que vous bloquez un ennemi. En solo sur les voies, Nami a intérêt à maximiser Prison aqueuse en premier. Sinon, il suffit d'y investir un point et de terminer par elle. Flux et reflux rebondit alternativement entre champions alliés et ennemis, blessant les ennemis et soignant les alliés. Flux et reflux ne peut toucher que trois cibles au total, et chaque cible une seule fois. Ainsi, vous devez choisir entre soigner deux alliés, en visant d'abord un allié, ou blesser deux ennemis, en visant d'abord un ennemi. Nami a intérêt à maximiser Flux et reflux en premier lorsqu'elle joue en duo sur une voie, et en second si elle joue en solo. Bénédiction de l'Aquamancienne permet de buffer un champion allié pendant quelques secondes, durant lesquelles ses attaques de base infligent des dégâts magiques supplémentaires et ralentissent leur cible. Nami devrait mettre rapidement 1 point dans Bénédiction de l'Aquamancienne et chercher à maximiser cette compétence après Flux et reflux. Raz-de-marée est l'ultime de Nami. La vague fonce devant elle, blessant tous les ennemis touchés, les projetant dans les airs et les ralentissant. Lorsque Raz-de-marée passe sur les coéquipiers de Nami, il applique également Déferlantes, ce qui les aide à pourchasser leurs ennemis. Comme avec tous les ultimes, Nami devrait maximiser le sien aussi vite que possible. En duo sur les voies, harcelez l'ennemi avec des attaques de base et Flux et reflux. Réfléchissez s'il vaut mieux soigner ou infliger des dégâts avec votre dernier rebond : visez l'un des champions ennemis pour infliger 2x des dégâts, ou visez un allié pour soigner 2x. Les compétences de Nami fonctionnent toutes bien ensemble lors d'un gank. Elle utilise Bénédiction de l'Aquamancienne sur elle-même, ralentissant Katarina. Cela permet à Udyr de la rattraper facilement, puis Prison aqueuse permet d'éliminer aisément Katarina avec Flux et reflux. Les alliés de Nami peuvent devenir des relais pour Flux et reflux. L'Aquamancienne ennemie est hors de portée, alors Nami lance Flux et reflux sur Lulu pour blesser le champion adverse. Nami doit penser à utiliser Bénédiction de l'Aquamancienne sur l'attaquant principal, Flux et reflux dès que possible et à bloquer les ennemis ralentis avec Prison aqueuse. Si vous arrivez à une telle maîtrise, vous ne ferez qu'une bouchée de vos ennemis. Les affrontements prolongés sont la spécialité de Nami. Après avoir subi quelques dégâts, Flux et reflux remet Nami en bonne condition, puis Prison aqueuse et Bénédiction de l'Aquamancienne permettent à Vayne d'attaquer Leona. Quand Vayne suit sa cible, Nami reste en retrait et attend la fin de ses délais de récupération. Flux et reflux soigne de nouveau Vayne et une nouvelle Bénédiction de l'Aquamancienne l'aide à continuer son assaut. Pour réussir l'élimination finale, Prison aqueuse suivie de Flux et reflux permettent à Vayne de survivre au tower dive. Raz-de-marée permet à Nami d'engager le combat d'extrêmement loin. Une fois le dragon tué, Raz-de-marée frappe la Leona ennemie. L'énorme ralentissement facilite beaucoup la Prison aqueuse, offrant une élimination facile à l'équipe. Rammus doit ensuite fuir avec Saut éclair et parvient à quitter le combat. Bénédiction de l'Aquamancienne aide toutefois Gangplank à le rattraper et à le ralentir, tandis que Prison aqueuse empêche Garen et Graves de riposter. Nami est idéale pour pourchasser les fuyards et enchaîner les éliminations. Elle accélère ses alliés avec Raz-de-marée, ce qui divise aussi l'équipe ennemie. Ainsi, Nami et ses alliés tombent sur l'Aquamancienne adverse et la tuent rapidement. Quand Warwick et Jax pourchassent Sivir, Prison aqueuse et Flux et reflux l'aident à s'éloigner. Elle utilise Bénédiction de l'Aquamancienne sur Udyr, ce qui empêche les ennemis de fuir. Le score pour ce combat est alors de 3 à 1. Après la mort de Jax, Katarina essaie à tort d'aider. Bénédiction de l'Aquamancienne puis Flux et reflux permettent à Udyr de la tuer sans difficulté. Ils se tournent ensuite vers Graves qui est sous sa tourelle. Udyr fonce tandis que Nami reste derrière lui. Bénédiction de l'Aquamancienne ralentit l'ennemi, pendant que Flux et reflux soigne l'équipe pendant le combat. Au bout du compte, Graves ne parvient pas à fuir et Udyr réussit la cinquième élimination. Voici un exemple de build pour Nami, qui se concentre sur le soutien d'un allié et le harcèlement des ennemis : Prenez des marques de dégâts d'attaque, des sceaux d'armure, des glyphes de résistance magique et des quintessences de PO. En maîtrises : 0/9/21, avec les PV et l'armure en Défense, plus la génération de PO et la réduction des délais dans Utilitaire. En sorts d'invocateur, prenez Soins ou Fatigue plus Saut éclair, pour aider votre équipe à survivre, neutraliser les ennemis et vous repositionner pendant les combats. Merci d'avoir suivi ce Focus sur Nami. Abonnez-vous ci-dessus à la chaîne YouTube de Riot Games et laissez vos commentaires sous la vidéo !

titre: The Ultimate League of Legends Iceberg Explained
transcription:
The Ultimate League of Legends Iceberg
League of Legends has been one of the most popular games in the 
past decade and a half now so there's bound to be things that 
have been forgotten through time but not today today we're going 
to be exploring the deep end and shining a light on forgotten 
League of Legends content and topics and in case of you guys don't know how icebergs   work we're going to start at the 
very top and work our way down the deeper we go the more obscure it gets 
so you guys are bound to learn something new or something you never heard about or remember something that 
you totally forgot about and for this iceberg I use a multitude of   different resources including an 
old Reddit post from 3 years ago my own topics that I found very interesting as well as my own YouTube comments so if you see yourself in this 
video congratulations you made it so without further ado grab a snack 
a drink some popcorn and get comfy because I present to you guys 
the ultimate League of Legends  iceberg Better Nerf Irelia this meme or joke was very popular 
in the old days of League of Legends and even for me I didn't even know 
why I was saying Better Nerf Irelia this community joke started because 
the day before Irelia was even released she got nerfed and I'm not even talking about like   a hot fix after it was released and 
they're seeing the win percentage NO before she even got onto 
live servers they nerfed her and the reason why they nerfed her was because Riot Morello said quote unquote she was too annoying to play against and that was in 2010 imagine if you 
saw some of the **** champions now and ever since she got released especially 
during the early days of League of Legends she would get the most random nerfs like Kassadin would have a 99% ban rate Malphite and Amumu and Shen 
will all be pick and banned but Irelia would just get a random nerf to her   base stats or a random nerf to her 
ultimate or a random nerf to her W it was really just a meme that the community   coined because they would 
just randomly nerf Irelia and Irelia wasn't known as like a broken 
broken champion back then (she was lowkey) Don't get it twisted she was [ __ ] annoying 
to play against especially with that E but compared to some of the

Meet fellow adventurers. Help people in need... And vanquish the bad guys before they hurt anyone else! We can be Freljords greatest heroes! And... BOOM! Saved the day. Ahhh... I wish we could choose more than one thing Willump. Whoa! What are we waiting for? Go Willump, go! Oh! What if we were armored? Or we were elves? Or zombies?! No... wait. Robots! That's what people want. ha. ha. ha. ha. ha. ha.

titre: Redesigning League of Legends' Boring Champs: Elise
transcription: hey what's up guys we're back and today we have some more animal crossing content no I'm just kidding actually today we're gonna take a pretty big leap from the last two videos you've seen from us on this channel we're all still locked inside and they're really only so many hours one can spend playing just animal crossing though I'm sure a lot of you will disagree anyway I've been occupying my time with a few other games as well including my go-to late night gaming treat slash punishment League of Legends okay I know it's not a lot of people's favorite game and honestly it's not mine either but if you have a group of friends that are alright goofing off and don't mind getting absolutely destroyed by a fifteen-year-old yeah so well one trick it's actually a pretty good time you guys probably already know how I feel about the character design it's like the biggest range of quality and art I've seen in any video game to be fair league is ten years old and a lot has changed since the game's conception the new characters that have been released over the past couple of years are novel interesting and fun to play and they have designed the tie in beautifully with both their lore and their gameplay styles and even the gross old characters from a decade ago are starting to get some love the League of Legends art team does an amazing job reimagining and redesigning characters so that they're not only visually more appealing but they also translate better to a player how the character will interact in-game as well as tie in more of the characters lore yeah did you know that league has lore I've been playing this game for about six years now and not once that I think that there was even an ounce of thought put into the backstories of some of these characters the old ones especially are so uninspired so generic that even if I knew that there were pages and pages of backstory written by the creative team I don't think I would care to read it which is a shame because a lot of the lore is actually pretty interesting let me give you an example I just started playing Elise a jungling champion whose default design is well less than exciting I like jungling but more than that I love inhuman characters most of my mains can be described as such I picked her up because I wanted to see what it might feel like to play as a sorceress who could freely transform from a humanoid to a full-on giant spider and to my surprise it was exactly as fun as I expected it to be in fact I had so much fun playing this character that I had to ask myself why have I never tried playing her before well the short answer is her design never interested me and when I say design I just mean her visual character design not her gameplay not how she plays just how she looks it's a big deciding factor for me when trying a new champion is this champion cool and Elise to me just wasn't the concept of an evil Spider Queen is just so overdone and nothing about her default look tells me that she'll offer anything new to the trope she's just a scary-looking lady with red eyes big boobs and a couple of spider legs growing out of her back big whoop to be fair I like her blood moon skin a lot especially the spider form it's great I just want it but focusing purely on her basic skin the one that every player sees when they first learn about this character there's just nothing there the most disappointing thing about all of this other than the fact that a poor design completely turned me off to a character I ended up really enjoying is that her story is actually way more interesting than her design implies I mean it's not groundbreaking it's not amazing but it introduces some new ideas to the trope of the evil Spider Queen and if you want to design a character with influences dating back to ancient Greece whose basic concept has been explored over and over and over again over hundreds of years you really need to be promising an audience that they're gonna be getting something new from that character for those of you unfamiliar with Elise's lore I'll give you the cliff notes basically Elise was born into a family of aristocrats in the noxus Empire raised to believe that a woman's power came from seduction she sought a wealthy powerful husband and wielded his family's influence to bring herself fame and fortune after a while though her husband got sick of being manipulated and tried to poison her over dinner it almost worked but as the poison rotted her flesh from the inside out at least managed to stab her husband and drink the antidote he was carrying moments before she succumb to the toxins she now had his entire family's estate to herself but was robbed of her beauty and charm something she accredited all of her success to she withdrew to the now-empty halls of this great mansion leaving her home only at night in fear that someone might see her now massage after a while though she fell into the favor of a group of individuals known as the Black Rose and was accepted into their fold despite her physical appearance she was sent on a mission to the shadow Isles to retrieve an enchanted dagger from an evil spider god shocker and while she was there Elise was asked to feed the God a couple of poor suckers who shanghaied into coming with her it worked out pretty well at first the spider God ate the losers she grabbed a knife but then if you can believe it she managed to get bitten by the spider and fall onto the knife at the same time crazy right well as it turned out it wasn't the worst thing that could have happened to her the venom of the spider mixed with the poison already in her body and all that reacted quite nicely to the magics of the knife that she poked herself with her beauty was restored her skin smoothed and polished like porcelain and her youth rekindled side effects spider legs growing out of her back and an insatiable hunger for blood that required her to constantly consume human victims in order to retain her new look it's a fun read I'll link the full story in the description of this video if you guys want to check it out for yourself the most interesting part of it to me and I think the part that is most lost in her official design is the importance she puts on beauty and how that vise influenced so many aspects of her life so with that in mind I decided that I should give this character a new look of my own design one that's not only visually more exciting but fresh new and more connected to her character and backstory I'll be including the process for my concept brainstorming as well as the time lapse for my final turnaround with this design now remember I'm not changing anything about how this character plays in game this is just how she looks I hope you enjoy my subjective critique and rework of Elise the Spider Queen and if you have any ideas of your own or just comments in general about her design let me know down in the comments well let's get into it enjoy the first thing that I wanted to change about Elise right off the bat was her face nothing about this face is unique or exciting there are so many pale seductresses out there with the red lipstick in the black hair even the little accents on her jaw feel reused reading her lore I kept thinking about masks and how Elise in a way is always wearing one either she's manipulating her husband and the people he influences or she's guiding a troupe of naive lost souls to their doom or even in a physical sense when she regains her beauty through the venom of the spider God the line describing her porcelain skin was the key influencer for my starting visual and I immediately thought of Japanese geishas the imagery of these painted ladies has been reappropriation time and again in a very subversive way and it's kind of like how clowns have become more of a subject of horror than children's entertainment they're unattainable Beauty almost certainly implies something lying beneath the mask some secret that remains untold what's more it introduces that element of seduction that is a key part of Elise's personality the source material gave me a few options to play with in terms of hair and I settled on something that looked like a large spider resting on the top of her head with hair pins that mimic twitching legs I played with the idea of a face that looked as if it was made up like a geishas porcelain white with painted highlights that are actually part of Elise's skin but I wanted this mask to conceal something physically disturbing underneath so I segmented her face in ways that at first could just look like contouring or patterns with drawn makeup but the closer you get the more you see the cracks on the porcelain what looks like drawn on eyebrows are actually another set of eyes bobbles in the hair yep more eyes the seams of the face would open up in several places revealing rows of teeth and folding mouth parts and I imagine this reveal would occur in game when she uses her abilities in her human form I felt this whole idea of a living mask so to speak paid more homage to her lore and personality trying so hard to maintain her outer beauty that it almost becomes uncanny and if you can't catch it quick enough it's already too late spiders in real life take advantage of this tactic for luring in prey as well with false eyes camouflage and the ability to mimic the behavior of the animal that they're hunting it's that morbid ingenuity that I think people most associate with spiders cunning manipulative and resourceful just like Elise [Music] I should mention at this point that I didn't really make any changes to Elise's actual spider form I feel like her spider form works well for the role it plays it doesn't really need anything extra I find that with things like fantasy bugs the more you add on to them the less they can be associated with the actual real-life animals and the connotations that they carry with them at least looks like a big magical spider save for some changes to the color palette I feel like this design could remain the same if I were to make any changes I would probably look at her blood moon skin for inspiration that look is really cool I would also like to throw some quick props of the animation team of League of Legends for creating an actual spider walk cycle it looks really great and it feels appropriate for her speed in size now with her full body design there were a few things that I wanted to change I wanted to continue with the theme of geisha to reimagine how seduction could look and I wanted her silhouette overall to be more like a spider and in general I wanted her to look like a character no one had ever seen before now you guys have heard me rant about this before but so many of the female characters in league have big exposed boobies and while there isn't an inherent problem with that I feel like it's something that can be rethought in modern designs Elise is one of the characters where her sexuality is actually a big part of her lore but there are so many other ways to convey seduction than just having prominent breasts I felt a more subtle and fitting depiction of seduction was implied wealth power control and on a more physical side some exposed leg I mean she's a spider how are the legs not more of a focal point I wanted to convey a little bit more just how scheming and manipulating Elise was so I posed her weaving a web of enchanted silk as I finalized the pose I realized that an extra set of arms worked a lot better than I thought they would and I could imagine them working the same way the face does a second set concealed under her layers of clothing until the moment was right to strike part of her story explicitly described spider legs breaking out of her back but I wanted to incorporate them in a somewhat different way from her original design I already had her with six limbs so I decided that I only needed one more set to sell the spider look I played around with a few different silhouettes until I settled on a placement that I thought worked best i shaped her robe so that her overall form would be much more spider like with the abdomen implied with the roundness of her coattails as far as color goes my main goal was to get away from the whole black widow theme the Black Widow is an overdone theme with spider characters you know with the killing of the husband in the seduction and the hourglass mark we've all seen it so many times before there are so many interesting spider and patterns and colors out there that haven't been explored yet and I wanted to focus in a little bit more on those [Music] [Music] [Music] [Music] for the last part of this video I'll include the process of this amazing splash art that Claire did of my Elise design all of the League of Legends characters have a fully painted piece of splash art that accompanies them on their selection screen I'm not that great at painting though but fortunately Claire is enjoy the time lapse of her process [Music] [Music] [Music] [Music] [Music] let's wrap it up by talking again about what I wanted to change about Elise's design and how I went about doing it firstly I wanted to introduce some new visuals to the tired Spider Queen trope using influences from Japanese culture as well as some alternative imagery from spiders other than black widows I created something relatively novel second I wanted to give new form to the theme of seduction instead of just being a curvy woman with big hips and exposed breasts I thought a little bit more about what other things can seduce people luxury pampering mystery and so on and legs for you like people out there lastly I really wanted to give her whole look more of a spider-like shape by changing her silhouette from just a woman's body with spiders legs to a form changing robe in the shape of a spider and I think I accomplished this but I want to hear what you guys think what would you change or add to Elise's design or do you think she's fine the way she is let me know down in the comments I was also thinking about perhaps making this a series picking other league characters I find boring and changing them up so if you have any suggestions for new characters let me know thanks for watching I hope you guys all enjoyed don't forget to Like and subscribe if you did and I'll see you all in the next video how long are we going to can-can because this is hurting and you don't tell you die
